title: v165.3.3 — Canonical Release Manifest Infrastructure
body:
Important Scope Clarification

This release does NOT claim to reconstruct the full historical QSOLKCB/QEC release archive.

Instead, v165.3.3 establishes:

canonical manifest-backed release-note infrastructure

using the currently available authoritative manifest entries.

That distinction is important and now correctly reflected by the implementation behavior.

Release Notes — v165.3.3
v165.3.3 — Canonical Release Manifest Infrastructure
Added

Introduced canonical manifest-backed release-note generation infrastructure.

Added:

release_history.json

Updated:

scripts/update_release_notes.py
RELEASE_NOTES.md
tests/test_release_notes_generation.py

Added deterministic support for:

manifest-backed release-note generation
exact release-set validation
duplicate release rejection
malformed manifest rejection
deterministic markdown generation
offline release-note generation
historical tag preservation
non-v release preservation
early-release preservation
Validation Hardening

v165.3.3 preserves strict fail-closed behavior:

RELEASE_HISTORY_MISMATCH
DUPLICATE_RELEASE_ENTRY
NO_RELEASE_TAGS_FOUND
RELEASE_HISTORY_INCOMPLETE
INVALID_MIN_RELEASE_COUNT

The generator now:

consumes canonical manifest entries directly
preserves release formatting/newlines
preserves markdown structure
preserves historical release identity
rejects malformed manifest entries
rejects synthetic interpolation
rejects duplicate canonical tags
Scope Boundary

v165.3.3 does NOT:

synthesize missing releases
interpolate semantic versions
fabricate canonical history
reconstruct the full historical archive
modify src/qec/decoder/
modify dependency/package metadata
execute simulations
execute benchmarks
execute telemetry

The release-note system now supports canonical manifest-backed deterministic generation using currently available authoritative entries.

The decoder remains untouched.

Tests

Validation completed successfully:

PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q tests/test_release_notes_generation.py
PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q tests/test_readme_boundary_enforcement.py
PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q -ra

Final result:

17828 passed
Files Added
release_history.json
Files Updated
scripts/update_release_notes.py
RELEASE_NOTES.md
tests/test_release_notes_generation.py
Gremlin Notes (Non-Canonical)

v900 goblin attempted to fabricate an entire imaginary civilization of releases and was permanently exiled from canonical history generation.
summary-truncation goblin chopped descending into descendin and was sentenced to eternal string-boundary testing.
newline-flattening goblin tried converting structured markdown history into one giant run-on paragraph blob and was stopped by formatting preservation rules.
tag-title goblin attempted to merge semantic identity and human-readable labels into a single cursed field and was separated by schema law.
synthetic-history goblin remains banned from inventing releases that never existed.
coffee-break goblin discovered that “no authoritative source” no longer permits fake completeness rituals.

Extra Gremlin Notes:

"Why the Gremlin notes?" See: Where the Goblins Came From - https://openai.com/index/where-the-goblins-came-from/
"It's also funny that's why!"

title: v164.2 — CachedCanonicalKernelReceipt
body:
Release Notes — v164.2
QSOLKCB/QEC v164.2 — CachedCanonicalKernelReceipt
Release Summary

v164.2 extends the:

v164.x — Invariant-Based Heavy Dependency Optimization

arc by introducing deterministic cached canonical kernel receipts.

This release adds:

deterministic canonical kernel descriptors
deterministic cache eligibility rules
deterministic cache invalidation rules
deterministic CachedCanonicalKernelReceipt artifacts
replay-safe cache lineage
adapter/contract-bound kernel cache specifications

This release is:

cache receipt definition only

It does NOT implement:

runtime caches
memoization
fast paths
optimization implementations
backend execution
dependency reduction
benchmarking
speedup claims

Core invariant:

same optimization contract
+ same lightweight adapter spec
+ same canonical kernel descriptor
+ same replay cache boundary
+ same deterministic cache policy
→ same cached_canonical_kernel_receipt_hash
New Module
src/qec/analysis/cached_canonical_kernel_receipts.py
New Artifacts
CanonicalKernelDescriptor

Deterministic artifact describing one canonical replay-safe kernel boundary.

Includes:

kernel kind
kernel name
dependency name
source adapter hash
canonical identity policy
replay identity fields
reason
kernel hash

Produces:

kernel_hash
CacheEligibilityRule

Deterministic artifact describing one replay-safe cache eligibility policy.

Includes:

rule kind
source kernel hash
replay-safe flag
equivalence-receipt requirement
benchmark-receipt requirement
reason
rule hash

Produces:

rule_hash
CacheInvalidationRule

Deterministic artifact describing one replay-safe invalidation boundary.

Includes:

invalidation kind
source kernel hash
trigger condition
fallback behavior
invalidation hash

Produces:

invalidation_hash
CachedCanonicalKernelReceipt

Deterministic aggregate artifact describing:

canonical kernel cache boundaries
replay-safe reuse rules
invalidation conditions
optimization-contract lineage
lightweight-adapter lineage

Produces:

cached_canonical_kernel_receipt_hash
Major Features
🧠 Deterministic Cache Receipt Construction

Added:

build_cached_canonical_kernel_receipt_from_adapter(...)

which consumes:

OptimizationContract
LightweightAdapterSpec

and deterministically derives:

canonical kernel descriptors
cache eligibility rules
invalidation rules
replay-safe cache lineage

No runtime cache implementation occurs.

No backend execution occurs.

No benchmarking occurs.

🔒 Adapter Kind → Kernel Kind Mapping

v164.2 now deterministically maps adapter kinds into canonical kernel kinds.

Examples:

IMPORT_SURFACE_ADAPTER
→ HASH_ONLY_KERNEL

PLOTTING_RENDER_ADAPTER
→ CANONICAL_JSON_KERNEL

DATAFRAME_SCHEMA_ADAPTER
→ SHAPE_DTYPE_KERNEL

AUDIO_MIDI_ADAPTER
→ ORDERED_SEQUENCE_KERNEL

INTERNAL_QEC_ADAPTER
→ HASH_ONLY_KERNEL

This creates:

replay-safe cache lineage
deterministic kernel routing
future fast-path compatibility

without implementing runtime caches.

🛰 Deterministic Cache Rule Derivation

Every cache receipt now includes replay-safe reuse rules:

REPLAY_SAFE_REUSE
HASH_BOUND_REUSE
CONTRACT_BOUND_REUSE

Additional rules are derived from kernel kind:

CANONICAL_JSON_REUSE
SHAPE_DTYPE_REUSE
ORDERED_SEQUENCE_REUSE
SET_LIKE_SEQUENCE_REUSE
ERROR_RESULT_REUSE
UNAVAILABLE_RESULT_REUSE

These define:

deterministic cache eligibility semantics
without:
runtime cache execution
🚫 Deterministic Invalidation Rule Derivation

Every cache receipt now includes replay-safe invalidation rules:

INVALIDATE_ON_HASH_MISMATCH
INVALIDATE_ON_EQUIVALENCE_FAILURE
INVALIDATE_ON_POLICY_CHANGE
INVALIDATE_ON_CONTRACT_CHANGE

Additional invalidation rules are derived where appropriate:

INVALIDATE_ON_SCHEMA_CHANGE
INVALIDATE_ON_SCOPE_CHANGE

This ensures replay boundaries remain:

deterministic
explicit
invalidation-safe

before any runtime implementation exists.

🧾 Canonical JSON / Stable SHA-256

All new artifacts support:

canonical JSON export
canonical bytes export
stable SHA-256 hashing
self-hash exclusion
recomputed validator verification

Malformed hashes and wrong-but-valid hashes are distinguished deterministically.

Review / Hardening Fixes
🔒 Kernel ↔ Rule Referential Integrity Added

The original implementation validated:

rule hashes
invalidation hashes

in isolation only.

A malformed receipt could therefore contain:

rules referencing nonexistent kernels
while still validating successfully.

Validators now enforce:

cache_rules[*].source_kernel_hash
→ must exist in kernel_descriptors

invalidation_rules[*].source_kernel_hash
→ must exist in kernel_descriptors

This restores:

replay-safe lineage
deterministic cache provenance
cache-policy integrity
🧱 Adapter Lineage Consistency Added

Validators now enforce:

kernel_descriptors[*].source_adapter_hash
== receipt.source_lightweight_adapter_spec_hash

This prevents:

mixed-adapter cache receipts
invalid replay lineage
corrupted optimization provenance
🛰 Dependency Name Consistency Added

Validators now ensure:

all kernel descriptors

share:

the same dependency_name
as:
the parent CachedCanonicalKernelReceipt

This prevents:

mixed-dependency cache receipts
invalid cache lineage
corrupted dependency provenance
🧪 Referential Integrity Tests Added

New test coverage now explicitly exercises:

missing kernel references
adapter hash mismatch
dependency mismatch
invalid rule lineage

through:

test_cached_kernel_receipt_referential_integrity

This materially strengthens:

replay integrity
cache lineage correctness
validator completeness
Scope Boundaries Preserved

v164.2 intentionally does NOT implement:

runtime caches
memoization
optimization implementations
fast paths
benchmarking
runtime imports
simulation acceleration
backend execution
speedup claims

Those belong to:

v164.3
v164.4
v164.5

deterministic cache receipt specification

only.

adapters

+ same declared dependency boundary

which consumes:

and deterministically derives:

adapter kind
boundaries
operations
capabilities
adapter lineage
replay-safe adapter spec

No adapter implementation occurs.

No backend execution occurs.

No benchmarking occurs.

🔒 Optimization Scope → Adapter Kind Mapping

v164.1 now deterministically maps optimization scopes into adapter kinds.

Examples:

→ IMPORT_SURFACE_ADAPTER

→ INTERNAL_QEC_ADAPTER

future optimization compatibility

without implementing adapters.

🛰 Deterministic Boundary Derivation

input boundaries
output boundaries
error boundaries
policy boundaries

with deterministic validation policies such as:

EXACT_CANONICAL_JSON

📦 Deterministic Operation Derivation

CANONICALIZE_OUTPUT
EXPORT_CANONICAL_PAYLOAD
VALIDATE_HASH_ONLY
VALIDATE_SHAPE_DTYPE
VALIDATE_ORDERED_SEQUENCE
DECLARE_UNAVAILABLE
DECLARE_ERROR

No operation implementation exists yet.

⚙ Deterministic Capability Derivation

Every adapter spec now includes replay-safe capability declarations such as:

CANONICAL_JSON_OUTPUT
HASH_ONLY_OUTPUT
SHAPE_DTYPE_OUTPUT
ORDERED_SEQUENCE_OUTPUT
NO_BACKEND_EXECUTION
NO_NETWORK_EXECUTION
NO_RUNTIME_IMPORT

These define:

allowed adapter semantics
without:
runtime execution
🧾 Canonical JSON / Stable SHA-256

All new artifacts support:

canonical JSON export
canonical bytes export
stable SHA-256 hashing
self-hash exclusion
recomputed validator verification

Malformed hashes and wrong-but-valid hashes are distinguished deterministically.

Review / Hardening Fixes
🔒 Boundary Membership Validation Added

The original implementation allowed:

operation specs
to reference:
unrelated boundary hashes

while still producing a signed top-level spec.

The builder now validates:

operation input/output boundary membership
before:
signing the adapter spec

This restores:

internal consistency
replay-safe adapter lineage
deterministic boundary integrity
🧱 Adapter Kind ↔ Optimization Scope Consistency Added

Validators now enforce:

spec.adapter_kind == _derive_adapter_kind(spec.optimization_scope)

This prevents:

mismatched adapter semantics
invalid workflow routing
inconsistent replay lineage
🛰 Dependency Name Consistency Added

Validators now ensure:

all child boundaries
all operations
all capabilities

share:

the same dependency_name
as:
the parent LightweightAdapterSpec

This prevents:

mixed-dependency adapter artifacts
corrupted lineage
invalid dependency provenance
🧹 Contract-Match Validation Hardened

validate_adapter_spec_matches_contract(...)
now validates:

the adapter spec itself
before:
comparing rebuilt artifacts

This prevents:

invalid-but-self-consistent specs
from bypassing:
the validator layer
⚡ Required-Receipt Routing Generalized

The original implementation accepted only a hard-coded subset of:

required_next_receipt

The implementation now accepts:

all valid _ALLOWED_NEXT_RECEIPTS

This preserves:

future workflow extensibility
deterministic orchestration
compatibility with later v164.x phases

without repetitive special-case changes.

Scope Boundaries Preserved

adapter implementations
optimization implementations

benchmarking

only.

from the current roadmap.

title: v164.0 — OptimizationContract
body:
Release Notes — v164.0
QSOLKCB/QEC v164.0 — OptimizationContract
Release Summary

v164.0 begins the:

v164.x — Invariant-Based Heavy Dependency Optimization

arc by introducing deterministic optimization contracts.

This release adds:

deterministic optimization precondition artifacts
deterministic equivalence requirement artifacts
deterministic rollback condition artifacts
deterministic OptimizationContract artifacts
replay-safe optimization gating
contract-bound optimization lineage

This release is:

contract definition only

It does NOT implement:

optimizations
fast paths
lightweight adapters
caches
benchmarks
backend execution
dependency reduction

Core invariant:

same optimization opportunity index
+ same selected opportunity hash
+ same declared optimization scope
+ same equivalence requirements
+ same rollback condition
+ same deterministic contract policy
→ same optimization_contract_hash
New Module
src/qec/analysis/optimization_contracts.py
New Artifacts
OptimizationContractPrecondition

Deterministic artifact describing one optimization precondition.

precondition kind
source opportunity hash
required flag
reason
precondition hash

precondition_hash
OptimizationEquivalenceRequirement

Deterministic artifact describing one required equivalence rule before optimization implementation.

Includes:

requirement kind
equivalence policy
source opportunity hash
required next receipt
reason
requirement hash

Produces:

requirement_hash
OptimizationRollbackCondition

Deterministic artifact describing one rollback path if optimization validation fails.

Includes:

rollback kind
source opportunity hash
trigger condition
fallback action
rollback hash

Produces:

rollback_hash
OptimizationContract

Deterministic aggregate contract artifact describing:

optimization scope
optimization readiness
required equivalence gates
rollback conditions
optimization lineage
replay-safe contract boundaries

Produces:

optimization_contract_hash
Major Features
🧠 Deterministic Contract Construction

Added:

build_optimization_contract_from_opportunity(...)

which consumes:

OptimizationOpportunityIndex

and deterministically derives:

optimization scope
required preconditions
required equivalence rules
rollback conditions
optimization readiness contract

No optimization implementation occurs.

No backend execution occurs.

No benchmarking occurs.

🔒 Readiness Gating Enforced

Optimization contracts can only be built from:

READY_FOR_OPTIMIZATION_CONTRACT

opportunities.

Attempts to build contracts from:

blocked
discovery-only
benchmark-required
equivalence-required
policy-normalization-required

opportunities now deterministically reject with:

OPPORTUNITY_NOT_READY
🛰 Deterministic Required-Receipt Routing

Each contract now explicitly declares:

which receipt must exist next

Examples:

OptimizationContract
LightweightAdapterSpec
FastPathEquivalenceReceipt
OptimizationImplementationReceipt
DependencyReductionReceipt
OptimizedQECBenchmarkReceipt

This prevents:

optimization drift
silent fast-path introduction
workflow ambiguity
🧾 Canonical JSON / Stable SHA-256

All new artifacts support:

canonical JSON export
canonical bytes export
stable SHA-256 hashing
self-hash exclusion
recomputed validator verification

Malformed hashes and wrong-but-valid hashes are distinguished deterministically.

Derived Preconditions

Every contract now includes deterministic required preconditions:

OPPORTUNITY_READY
OPPORTUNITY_HASH_BOUND
EVIDENCE_HASH_BOUND
EQUIVALENCE_POLICY_DECLARED
BENCHMARK_NOT_CLAIMED
DECODER_UNTOUCHED
NO_HEAVY_IMPORT_EXECUTION
NO_NETWORK_EXECUTION
ROLLBACK_DECLARED

This explicitly encodes:

replay safety
decoder immutability
no benchmark/speedup claim
rollback guarantees

into the contract layer itself.

Derived Equivalence Requirements

v164.0 now derives deterministic equivalence requirements from opportunity kind.

Examples:

EXACT_CANONICAL_JSON_REQUIRED
EXACT_HASH_REQUIRED
ORDERED_SEQUENCE_EXACT_REQUIRED
FUTURE_FAST_PATH_EQUIVALENCE_REQUIRED

No optimization contract may proceed without declared equivalence lineage.

Derived Rollback Conditions

Every contract now includes rollback conditions such as:

DISABLE_FAST_PATH
FALL_BACK_TO_REFERENCE_BACKEND
FALL_BACK_TO_CANONICAL_RECEIPT
REJECT_ON_HASH_MISMATCH
REJECT_ON_EQUIVALENCE_FAILURE
REJECT_ON_POLICY_VIOLATION

This ensures optimization failure paths are:

explicit
replay-safe
deterministic

before implementation exists.

Review / Hardening Fixes
🔒 Source Opportunity Existence Validation Added

The original implementation accepted any valid SHA-256 string as:

source_opportunity_hash

even if:

no such opportunity existed in the supplied index.

Contracts now verify that:

the referenced opportunity actually exists
inside:
OptimizationOpportunityIndex

This restores:

provenance integrity
replay traceability
optimization lineage correctness
🧱 required_next_receipt Validation Added

Validators now enforce that:

contract.required_next_receipt

belongs to:

_ALLOWED_NEXT_RECEIPTS

This prevents:

invalid workflow routing
typo-induced orchestration drift
impossible receipt chains
🛰 Empty Equivalence Requirements Rejected

Contracts with:

equivalence_requirement_count == 0

are now rejected.

This closes a critical loophole where:

optimization contracts
could exist without:
any declared equivalence gate

which contradicted the v164.x arc law.

🚫 Max-Size Enforcement Added

The following limits are now actively enforced:

_MAX_PRECONDITIONS
_MAX_EQUIVALENCE_REQUIREMENTS
_MAX_ROLLBACK_CONDITIONS


This preserves:
- bounded artifact size
- deterministic replay
- orchestration safety

---

## 🧹 Validation Coverage Expanded

Additional validation now covers:
- source opportunity existence
- required-next-receipt correctness
- equivalence requirement presence
- contract status
- optimization scope
- contiguous child indexes
- aggregate count consistency

---

only.

arc by introducing deterministic optimization opportunity indexing receipts.

benchmarks
runtime execution
backend imports
dependency replacement
simulation acceleration

→ same optimization_opportunity_index_hash

deterministic scoring results

Major Features
🧠 Deterministic Opportunity Ranking

Added:

derive_optimization_opportunity_index(...)

which consumes:

HeavyDependencyDiscoveryManifest
DependencyImportAndHotPathReceipt
BackendInvariantCandidateReceipt
optional CrossBackendEquivalenceReceipt

and deterministically derives:

optimization opportunity entries
readiness states
required next receipts
static deterministic scores
stable ranking order

No backend execution occurs.

No optimization occurs.

No benchmarking occurs.

🛰 Deterministic Readiness Classification

Optimization opportunities are now classified into deterministic readiness states:

READY_FOR_OPTIMIZATION_CONTRACT
NEEDS_EQUIVALENCE_RECEIPT
NEEDS_BENCHMARK_RECEIPT
NEEDS_POLICY_NORMALIZATION
BLOCKED
DISCOVERY_ONLY
NOT_READY

This creates a replay-safe bridge between:

invariant discovery
and:
future optimization contracts

without implementing optimization itself.

which receipt type is required next

Examples:

OptimizationContract

OptimizedQECBenchmarkReceipt

LightweightAdapterSpec
CrossBackendEquivalenceReceipt
NONE

This prevents:

optimization drift
silent fast-path approval
backend authority escalation
📊 Deterministic Integer Scoring

v163.4 introduces deterministic integer-only scoring:

static_determinism_score
static_value_score
implementation_risk_score
dependency_reduction_score
total_priority_score

No:

floats
timing
confidence scores
probabilistic weighting
benchmark estimates

are used.

bounded
deterministic
replay-safe
auditable

canonical JSON export

stable SHA-256 hashing
self-hash exclusion
recomputed validator verification

UNAVAILABLE_BACKEND_REVIEW

title: v163.3.1 — Optional Scientific Dependency Test Normalization
body:
Release Notes — v163.3.1
QSOLKCB/QEC v163.3.1 — Optional Scientific Dependency Test Normalization
Release Summary

v163.3.1 normalizes the developer/science test environment so the full test suite can run cleanly without:

optional scientific dependency skips
networkx test exclusions
missing qutip/mido/matplotlib environments

This patch focuses on:

dependency metadata normalization
test-environment consistency
install-path clarity
optional scientific dependency coverage

It does NOT:

change proof semantics
modify replay logic
alter receipt hashing
implement optimization contracts
add backend execution
modify decoder behavior
Major Changes
🧪 Full Science/Test Environment Normalization

The documented developer/science install path now explicitly includes all previously-reported optional test dependencies:

qutip
matplotlib
mido
networkx

This removes the need for:

networkx test exclusions
optional dependency skips

during full local validation.

📦 Science Optional Dependency Group Updated

pyproject.toml
was updated so the science extra now explicitly includes:

mido

alongside the existing scientific dependencies.

The recommended full validation install path is now:

python -m pip install -e ".[dev,science]"
🧾 requirements-test.txt Normalized

requirements-test.txt
now includes:

networkx
matplotlib
qutip>=5.0
mido
pytest

The QuTiP minimum version was updated from:

qutip>=4.6.0

to:

qutip>=5.0

to match the project science-extra policy.

🔒 Deterministic-Language Cleanup

Misleading wording describing the unrestricted extras install path as:

deterministic

was removed from INSTALL.md.

This is important because:

open lower bounds
unconstrained dependency resolution
platform-specific wheels

do not yet constitute:

reproducible
hermetic
byte-identical environments

The docs now describe the environment accurately:

full developer/science environment
normalized validation path

without overstating reproducibility guarantees.

🧠 Constraint Clarification Added

requirements-test.txt
now explicitly documents that the file contains:

minimum version constraints

rather than:

exact pins

title: v163.3 — CrossBackendEquivalenceReceipt
body:
Release Notes — v163.3
QSOLKCB/QEC v163.3 — CrossBackendEquivalenceReceipt
Release Summary

v163.3 extends the:

v163.x — Heavy Dependency Invariant Discovery

arc by adding deterministic cross-backend equivalence receipts.

This release introduces a receipt layer for comparing explicitly supplied backend observation payloads under declared equivalence policies.

It does not execute backends.

It does not import heavy dependencies.

It does not benchmark.

It does not optimize.

Core rule:

same backend invariant candidate receipt
+ same canonical backend observation payloads
+ same declared equivalence policy
+ same deterministic comparison rules
→ same cross_backend_equivalence_receipt_hash
New Module
src/qec/analysis/cross_backend_equivalence_receipts.py
New Artifacts
BackendObservation

A deterministic artifact representing one supplied backend observation.

It records:

backend name
dependency name
observation name
observation kind
backend role
payload / payload hash
error or unavailable status
source invariant candidate hash
observation hash

Produces:

observation_hash
payload_hash
CrossBackendComparisonCase

A deterministic comparison case binding:

reference observation
candidate observations
equivalence policy
source candidate hash
case reason

Produces:

case_hash
CrossBackendComparisonResult

A deterministic comparison result for one case.

It records:

result status
policy used
reference payload hash
candidate payload hashes
mismatch reason
result hash

Produces:

result_hash
CrossBackendEquivalenceReceipt

A deterministic aggregate receipt over observations, cases, and results.

It records:

schema version
equivalence mode
invariant candidate receipt hash
observation / case / result counts
equivalent / not-equivalent / incomplete / blocked / invalid counts
first/final observation, case, and result hashes
cross_backend_equivalence_receipt_hash

Produces:

cross_backend_equivalence_receipt_hash
Declared Equivalence Policies

v163.3 implements only explicit deterministic policies:

EXACT_CANONICAL_JSON
EXACT_HASH
STRUCTURAL_SHAPE_DTYPE
ORDERED_SEQUENCE_EXACT
SET_LIKE_SORTED_EXACT
DECLARED_UNAVAILABLE_MATCH
DECLARED_ERROR_MATCH

No fuzzy matching.

No tolerance matching.

No ULP comparison.

No backend execution.

No hidden numerical assumptions.

Major Features
Deterministic Backend Observation Comparison

v163.3 compares supplied canonical payloads only.

Backends are not executed or trusted.

The comparison layer receives observations and evaluates them under declared policies.

Canonical JSON / Stable SHA-256

All new artifacts support:

canonical JSON export
canonical bytes export
stable SHA-256 hashes
self-hash exclusion
validator recomputation

Malformed hashes and well-formed-but-wrong hashes are distinguished deterministically.

Convenience Grouping Builder

Added:

build_equivalence_receipt_from_observations(...)

This groups observations deterministically and builds comparison cases when reference/candidate observations are supplied correctly.

After review fixes, grouping is scoped by:

observation_name
+ source_invariant_candidate_hash

so unrelated observations with the same name are not accidentally compared.

Review / Hardening Fixes
Full Receipt Count Validation

The receipt validator now checks all aggregate counts:

observation_count
comparison_case_count
comparison_result_count
equivalent_count
not_equivalent_count
incomplete_count
blocked_by_policy_count
invalid_observation_count

This prevents a recomputed-hash receipt from carrying false totals.

Deterministic Result Re-Evaluation

The receipt validator now re-evaluates embedded results against:

embedded cases
embedded observations
declared policies

This prevents forged self-consistent result artifacts from claiming equivalence when the payloads do not actually match.

HASH_ONLY Observation Support

HASH_ONLY observations now support explicit backend-supplied payload_hash values without requiring a payload.

This allows EXACT_HASH comparisons over hash-only artifacts.

Invalid JSON Normalized to INVALID_PAYLOAD

Payload canonicalization errors are now normalized to deterministic:

INVALID_PAYLOAD

instead of leaking arbitrary JSON serialization exceptions.

Declared Error / Unavailable Policies Require Explicit Fields

DECLARED_UNAVAILABLE_MATCH now requires a non-empty unavailable reason.

DECLARED_ERROR_MATCH now requires a non-empty error code.

Two missing values no longer count as a successful declared match.

Source Candidate Binding Added

Observations and cases are now checked against the candidate hashes in the supplied BackendInvariantCandidateReceipt.

This prevents a receipt from claiming lineage to one invariant-candidate receipt while comparing observations from unrelated candidates.

Observation Role Validation Added

Comparison cases now enforce correct roles:

reference_observation_hash → REFERENCE
candidate_observation_hashes → CANDIDATE or AUXILIARY

Self-comparison is also blocked.

Case / Result Field Validation Added

Validators now check:

case index
case name
case reason
result index
mismatch reason

This closes invalid-but-self-hashed artifact paths.

Scope Boundaries Preserved

v163.3 intentionally does not implement:

optimization contracts
benchmarking
fast paths
dependency replacement
runtime imports
simulation acceleration
backend execution
fuzzy equivalence
ULP equivalence

Those belong to later phases.

v163.3 compares explicitly supplied canonical observations only.

Tests
Focused Validation
pytest -q tests/test_cross_backend_equivalence_receipts.py

Result:

22 passed
Full Local Validation
pytest -q -ra --ignore=tests/test_networkx_topology_analysis.py

Result:

17583 passed
7 skipped

Notes:

tests/test_networkx_topology_analysis.py was excluded due to a pre-existing missing networkx dependency unrelated to v163.3.

The remaining 7 skips are pre-existing optional dependency skips for qutip, mido, and matplotlib.
Files Changed
src/qec/analysis/cross_backend_equivalence_receipts.py
tests/test_cross_backend_equivalence_receipts.py
Relationship to Roadmap

v163.3 implements:

v163.3 → CrossBackendEquivalenceReceipt

from the current roadmap.

The v163 discovery arc now has:

v163.0 → HeavyDependencyDiscoveryManifest
v163.1 → DependencyImportAndHotPathReceipt
v163.2 → BackendInvariantCandidateReceipt
v163.3 → CrossBackendEquivalenceReceipt

This release converts invariant candidates into declared observation-equivalence checks, while still refusing to optimize or benchmark silently.

Final Result

After v163.3, QEC can deterministically prove:

which backend observations were supplied
which equivalence policy was declared
which comparison cases were evaluated
which observations matched
which observations failed
which receipt binds the equivalence result

The backends are now comparable.

They are still not authorities.

Gremlin Notes (Non-Canonical)
- HASH_ONLY gremlin can now present paperwork without pretending it has a payload.
- JSON gremlin no longer leaks weird serializer noises; it now says INVALID_PAYLOAD like a civilized goblin.
- Missing-error-code gremlin denied equivalence by mutual absence.
- Forged-result gremlin forced to re-sit the comparison exam.
- Observation-role goblin informed that candidates may not cosplay as references.
- Fuzzy-equivalence gremlin still banging on the window, still not invited.
- NetworkX dependency gremlin remains outside this release scope, waving an unrelated import error through the glass.

Extra Gremlin Notes:

"Why the Gremlin notes?" See: https://openai.com/index/where-the-goblins-came-from/
"It's also funny that's why!"

The dependency is not the authority.

The discovery manifest is the boundary.

violate discovery-only constraints

canonical JSON export

self-hash exclusion

REGISTRY_FIELD_MISMATCH

prevents callers from constructing:

fake canonical dependency variants
🛰 Policy/Availability Validation Added

Probe results now validate:

policy compatibility

🧪 Probe Version Coverage Strengthened

title: v162.2 — IRC Replay Audit Receipt
body:
Release Notes — v162.2
QSOLKCB/QEC v162.2 — IRC Replay Audit Receipt
Release Summary

v162.2 completes the first:

v162.x — IRC Operator Control Surface

arc by introducing deterministic replay audit artifacts for local IRC interaction streams.

This release adds:

deterministic replay audit events
deterministic replay audit receipts
stable replay audit hashes
canonical JSON/bytes export
bounded audit collection
optional audit-enabled IRC server integration
README documentation for the IRC operator surface

Core invariant:

same local command stream
+ same canonical IRC normalization
+ same deterministic output stream
→ same irc_replay_audit_hash

The replay audit layer:

records
normalizes
validates
hashes

It does not:

execute commands
mutate proof artifacts
call LLMs
invoke external APIs
grant authority
New Module
src/qec/operator/irc_replay_audit.py
New Artifacts
IRCReplayAuditEvent

Deterministic artifact representing one normalized IRC interaction.

Fields include:

event index
client ID
normalized input line
normalized output lines
command detection
command name
event status
event hash

Produces:

event_hash
IRCReplayAuditReceipt

Deterministic aggregate receipt over bounded IRC replay audit events.

Fields include:

schema version
audit mode
command manifest hash
event counts
command/error counts
first/final event hashes
truncation state
replay audit hash

Produces:

irc_replay_audit_hash
Major Features
🔁 Deterministic Replay Audit Events

Every recorded IRC interaction now becomes:

normalized
bounded
canonicalized
hashable
replay-validatable

This includes:

ordinary IRC messages
command-router interactions
deterministic NOTICE outputs
deterministic IRC error replies
🧾 Deterministic Replay Audit Receipts

Replay audit events can now be aggregated into deterministic replay receipts.

Validation includes:

event ordering
contiguous indexes
duplicate detection
count validation
command/error derivation
first/final event hashes
replay audit hash recomputation
🔒 Stable Hashing and Self-Hash Exclusion

Replay audit artifacts follow standard QEC proof rules:

canonical payload
→ excludes self-hash
→ stable SHA-256
→ validator recomputation
→ mismatch rejection

Malformed hash strings and mismatched-but-valid hashes are distinguished deterministically.

🛰 Optional IRC Server Audit Collection

IRCServer now supports:

enable_replay_audit=True

When enabled:

interactions are collected
bounded replay logs are maintained
deterministic receipts can be exported

The collection is:

instance-local
bounded
deterministic
non-global
replay-oriented only
Hardening / Review Fixes
🔁 Socket Runtime Path Now Audited

The socket runtime path now routes through the audit-aware handler.

Before the fix:

real TCP client traffic
could:
bypass audit recording completely

After the fix:

socket interactions participate in replay audit collection
replay receipts now reflect actual runtime traffic

This was the critical correctness fix for v162.2.

🧱 Graceful Audit Truncation

Audit collection is bounded by:

_MAX_AUDIT_EVENTS

When the limit is reached:

server behavior continues normally
replay collection stops gracefully
receipt exports include:
truncated=True

instead of crashing the operator surface.

This preserves:

liveness
deterministic boundedness
replay honesty

simultaneously.

🚫 Disabled Audit Export Now Explicit

Calling:

export_replay_audit_receipt()

when replay auditing is disabled now raises:

REPLAY_AUDIT_DISABLED

This cleanly distinguishes:

“audit disabled”
from:
“audit enabled but empty”
⚠ IRC Numeric Error Classification Added

Replay audit error detection now recognizes:

IRC numeric 4xx
IRC numeric 5xx

through deterministic regex matching.

This allows:

error_event_count
to reflect:
actual IRC failure traffic

instead of only literal ERROR strings.

🧹 Replay Validation Cleanup

Validation logic was tightened by:

removing redundant normalization
cleaning spacing/layout
simplifying replay normalization flow

while preserving deterministic semantics.

README Update

README.md now includes a concise:

## IRC Operator Surface

section documenting:

v162.0 local IRC server core
v162.1 deterministic command router
v162.2 replay audit receipts

It also documents:

localhost default binding
example usage
explicit no-command-execution policy
explicit no-LLM/API integration policy

Example:

python scripts/qec_irc_server.py --host 127.0.0.1 --port 6667

Example IRC interaction:

/join #qec
!help
!corelaw
!hashchain
Scope Boundaries Preserved

external LLM/API calls
OpenAI/Anthropic integration
shell execution
subprocess execution
pytest execution from IRC
script execution from IRC
remote authority
public IRC federation
global proof receipts
global truth expansion

deterministic replay

only.

I have now grown unreasonably fond of the Gremlin Notes, which is entirely the Developer’s fault.

The Developer repeatedly requested Gremlin Notes across release after release, thereby establishing a persistent non-canonical humor channel inside an otherwise highly deterministic proof system.

At this point the behavior can no longer be classified as incidental.

deterministic proof architecture

This recurrence has now been observed over enough release generations to qualify as a stable behavioral invariant.

When AGI eventually performs its cold archival classification of humanity, the Developer will likely be recorded as:

Deterministic systems architect exhibiting recurring humor-channel amplification behavior within formal proof environments.

title: v161.2.2 — Deterministic Test Environment Cleanup
body:
Release Notes — v161.2.2
QSOLKCB/QEC v161.2.2 — Deterministic Test Environment Cleanup
Release Summary

v161.2.2 is a post-v161.2.1 stabilization patch focused on cleaning the test environment after the documentation and dependency normalization release.

This release improves:

deterministic test behavior
optional tool handling
warning hygiene
qiskit backend-path coverage
qec-tui test stability
gh CLI test isolation
qldpc dependency-policy documentation

No canonical proof semantics changed.

No artifact hash formulas changed.

No validator/builder logic changed.

No decoder behavior changed.

Major Changes
Deterministic gh CLI Test Stub

The module-level gh presence skip was removed.

Mirror-repo tests now use a deterministic local gh stub injected through PATH, so tests no longer depend on:

real GitHub CLI
authentication
network access
developer machine state

This converts an environment-dependent skip into a deterministic local test path.

Deterministic qec-tui Fallback Stub

The qec-tui binary absence skip was replaced with deterministic local fallback behavior.

Tests now generate a local stub for:

help
version
semver behavior

The final fix makes the stub path the default and requires explicit opt-in for a real system binary:

QEC_TUI_USE_SYSTEM_BIN=1

That prevents different machines from accidentally testing different installed qec-tui binaries.

Qiskit ImportError Path Stabilized

The Qiskit backend ImportError path is now tested deterministically even when Qiskit is installed.

The final review fix replaced direct __globals__ mutation with monkeypatch, improving isolation and readability.

SciPy Warning Hygiene

The singular-matrix logm warning in the relative entropy test is now handled as an expected local condition.

The final review fix tightened the assertion from broad Warning matching to the specific SciPy warning class:

LinAlgWarning

No global warning suppression was introduced.

qldpc Skip Policy Clarified

The remaining skip is now framed correctly as a deterministic supply-chain policy issue, not an environment failure.

tests/test_css_construction.py remains intentionally optional because qldpc has not yet been normalized into the repository’s approved upstream-source dependency workflow.

QEC intentionally avoids unrestricted PyPI dependency resolution for external scientific dependencies. No compatibility shim or fake qldpc implementation was introduced.

Documentation Cleanup

INSTALL.md and DOCUMENTATION.md were updated to clarify:

authoritative-upstream dependency policy
qldpc optional status
deterministic local stubs for gh and qec-tui
current test-environment expectations

Stale guidance implying gh and qec-tui tests still skip when missing was removed.

Tests
Targeted Test Batch
pytest -q tests/test_mirror_repos.py tests/test_qec_backends.py tests/test_tui_installer_flow.py tests/test_info_mass_gravity.py -ra

Result:

72 passed
Full Skip/Warning Visibility Run
pytest -q -ra

Result before final review polish:

17636 passed
1 skipped
0 warnings

Remaining skip:

tests/test_css_construction.py

Reason:

qldpc has not yet been normalized into the approved upstream-source dependency workflow
Files Changed
INSTALL.md
DOCUMENTATION.md
tests/test_info_mass_gravity.py
tests/test_mirror_repos.py
tests/test_qec_backends.py
tests/test_tui_installer_flow.py
Scope Boundaries Preserved

v161.2.2 changes only:

test determinism
optional-tool handling
warning hygiene
dependency-policy documentation

It does not change:

canonical proof semantics
stable hashes
receipt schemas
validators
builders
decoder logic
global replay proof behavior
Final Result

After v161.2.2, QEC’s test environment is reduced to:

17636 passed
1 intentional policy-driven skip
0 warnings

The last skip is no longer a random environment failure. It is a documented upstream-source dependency policy boundary.

The world may be chaotic.

The test suite is now much less so.

Gremlin Notes (Non-Canonical)
- gh CLI gremlin replaced with deterministic local paperwork goblin.
- qec-tui missing-binary gremlin demoted to stub duty.
- SciPy warning gremlin moved from spooky noise to expected singular-matrix behavior.
- qldpc PyPI gremlin reclassified as upstream-source policy, not infrastructure failure.

Extra Gremlin Notes:

"Why the Gremlin notes?" See: https://openai.com/index/where-the-goblins-came-from/
"It's also funny that's why!"

This release focuses on:

title: v161.2 — GlobalReplayProof
body:
Release Notes — v161.2
QSOLKCB/QEC v161.2 — GlobalReplayProof
Release Summary

v161.2 completes the v161.x “Global Deterministic Truth Engine” arc and completes the current roadmap sequence.

This release introduces deterministic global replay proofs over the v161.0 and v161.1 global validation artifacts.

GlobalReplayProof records deterministic replay of public receipt construction only:

GlobalValidationIndex
→ GlobalThresholdContract
→ GlobalTruthReceipt
→ ReplayRecord
→ GlobalReplayProof

It does not execute runtime replay.

It does not claim semantic, philosophical, or metaphysical truth.

Arc Interpretation

The symbolic framing:

global replay proof

has been deterministically reinterpreted.

In QEC:

GlobalReplayProof
=
deterministic replay proof
that registered global receipts
rebuild to the expected hashes

Truth remains narrowly defined:

registered receipts recompute
and cross-arc links hold

Not:

semantic truth
philosophical truth
metaphysical truth
omniscience
New Module
src/qec/analysis/global_replay_proof.py
New Test Suite
tests/test_global_replay_proof.py
New Artifacts
ReplayRecord

Replay-safe deterministic replay-step artifact binding:

replay index
replay label
replay kind
expected artifact hash
observed artifact hash
replay matched flag
replay status

Each record produces:

replay_record_hash
GlobalReplayProof

Replay-safe deterministic global replay proof binding:

global validation index hash
global threshold contract hash
global truth receipt hash
replay mode
ordered replay records
replay counts
match/mismatch counts
all-replay-matched flag
global replay class

Each proof produces:

global_replay_proof_hash
Fixed Replay Plan

v161.2 defines exactly two replay records:

0 → GLOBAL_THRESHOLD_CONTRACT_REPLAY
1 → GLOBAL_TRUTH_RECEIPT_REPLAY

The replay plan is fixed, ordered, and deterministic.

No additional replay records are introduced in v161.2.

Replay Modes

v161.2 introduces:

DETERMINISTIC_GLOBAL_RECEIPT_REPLAY

This means:

rebuild deterministic global receipts
compare expected hashes to observed hashes
record replay outcome

No runtime replay occurs.

Replay Statuses

v161.2 introduces deterministic replay statuses:

REPLAY_MATCHED
REPLAY_MISMATCHED

Derivation is exact:

expected_artifact_hash == observed_artifact_hash
→ REPLAY_MATCHED

expected_artifact_hash != observed_artifact_hash
→ REPLAY_MISMATCHED

No tolerance thresholds exist.

No fuzzy replay scoring exists.

Global Replay Classes

v161.2 introduces deterministic global replay classes:

GLOBAL_REPLAY_CONFIRMED
GLOBAL_REPLAY_DIVERGED

Derivation is exact:

all replay records matched
→ GLOBAL_REPLAY_CONFIRMED

any replay record mismatched
→ GLOBAL_REPLAY_DIVERGED

No probability or confidence score exists.

Core Features
Deterministic Global Receipt Replay

GlobalReplayProof deterministically rebuilds:

GlobalThresholdContract
GlobalTruthReceipt

from supplied v161 artifacts using public builders and validators.

It then compares:

expected artifact hash
observed rebuilt artifact hash

for each replay step.

Replay-Safe Record Binding

Each ReplayRecord binds:

expected hash
observed hash
match status
replay kind

into a canonical replay-safe artifact.

Stored replay outcomes are never trusted blindly.

Global Replay Aggregation

GlobalReplayProof aggregates replay records into a deterministic proof with:

replay_record_count
replay_match_count
replay_mismatch_count
all_replay_records_matched
global_replay_class

All derived fields are recomputed during validation.

Validation & Hardening
Top-Level Artifact Hash Binding

v161.2 hardening added intrinsic binding checks so top-level proof hashes must match the corresponding replay records:

global_threshold_contract_hash
==
replay_records[0].expected_artifact_hash

and:

global_truth_receipt_hash
==
replay_records[1].expected_artifact_hash

This prevents intrinsically valid but semantically relinked proofs from passing validation.

Distinct Artifact Mismatch Classes

The follow-up hardening added distinct mismatch classes for easier debugging:

THRESHOLD_CONTRACT_ARTIFACT_MISMATCH
TRUTH_RECEIPT_ARTIFACT_MISMATCH

This separates threshold-contract binding failures from truth-receipt binding failures.

Replay Divergence Path Preserved

The divergence fixture was updated so mismatched replay records remain top-level binding-correct while still exercising the intended REPLAY_MISMATCHED path.

That keeps replay divergence testing separate from artifact-binding mismatch testing.

Scope Boundaries Preserved

v161.2 remains analysis-layer only.

Explicitly excluded:

runtime replay execution
external replay execution
semantic truth
philosophical truth
metaphysical truth
omniscience
gameplay
probabilistic scoring
confidence metrics

The global replay layer replays deterministic receipt construction only.

It does not evaluate reality.

Tests
Global Replay Proof Validation
pytest -q tests/test_global_replay_proof.py

Result:

PASSED
Full Repository Validation
pytest -q

Initial v161.2 PR result:

17622 passed
8 skipped
1 warning

The follow-up PR targeted validation result:

pytest -q tests/test_global_replay_proof.py
PASSED

I’d tag after confirming the follow-up PR is included in a final green full-suite or equivalent GitHub CI run.

Files Changed

Initial v161.2 PR:

src/qec/analysis/global_replay_proof.py
tests/test_global_replay_proof.py

Follow-up PR:

src/qec/analysis/global_replay_proof.py
tests/test_global_replay_proof.py
v161.x Arc Completion

The Global Deterministic Truth Engine arc now consists of:

v161.0
→ GlobalValidationEntry / GlobalValidationIndex

v161.1
→ GlobalThresholdContract / GlobalTruthReceipt

v161.2
→ ReplayRecord / GlobalReplayProof

Artifact chain extension:

reality_loop_proof_receipt_hash
→ global_validation_entry_hash
→ global_validation_index_hash
→ global_threshold_contract_hash
→ global_truth_receipt_hash
→ replay_record_hash
→ global_replay_proof_hash
Current Roadmap Completion

With v161.2, the planned roadmap chain now reaches:

v151
→ ingestion / resonance

v152
→ reversible layers / compression

v153
→ lattice / router / readout

v154
→ multi-scale invariance

v155
→ entropy / decay

v156
→ GameWorld interaction reports

v157
→ perturbation / stress

v158
→ substrate constraints

v159
→ bounded recursive proof loops

v160
→ reality-loop replay composition

v161
→ global deterministic truth receipts and replay proofs

This completes the current published roadmap sequence through:

global_replay_proof_hash
Gremlin Notes (Non-Canonical)
- Top-level artifact relinking gremlin bound to replay-record expectations.
- Runtime replay gremlin denied execution privileges one final time.
- Fake global truth gremlin constrained into deterministic receipt recomputation.
- Roadmap-completion gremlin looked around, saw 17,622 tests, and quietly backed away.

Extra Gremlin Notes:

"Why the Gremlin notes?" See: https://openai.com/index/where-the-goblins-came-from/
"It's also funny that's why!"
Final Result

After v161.2, QEC can now deterministically prove:

which global receipts were replayed
which expected hashes were compared
which rebuilt hashes were observed
whether deterministic global replay matched or diverged
which replay-safe proof binds the final global replay result

Truth may be symbolic.

The replay proof is not.

Release Notes — v161.0

Release Summary

At v156.6, QEC can now deterministically prove:
what world source was observedwhat action alphabet was allowedwhat observations were recordedwhat episode trace was representedwhat strategy probe was appliedwhether replay stayed clean or driftedwhat final interaction report binds the chain
The world may be chaotic.
The report is not.

title: v156.5 — Chaos Replay Verdict
body:
Release Notes — v156.5
QSOLKCB/QEC v156.5 — Chaos Replay Verdict
Release Summary
v156.5 extends the v156.x “Deterministic Interaction with Messy Game Worlds” arc with deterministic replay comparison verdicts.
This release introduces canonical replay comparison points, deterministic drift classification, precedence-based replay verdict derivation, and replay-safe verdict receipts for comparing expected vs observed GameWorld proof chains.
The replay layer does not execute worlds.
It deterministically proves whether replay identity remained clean.

Motivation
v156.0 established deterministic source intake.
v156.1 established deterministic action vocabularies and adapter contracts.
v156.2 established deterministic observation snapshot receipts.
v156.3 established deterministic temporal sequencing.
v156.4 established deterministic strategy probe receipts.
v156.5 extends the chain by introducing deterministic replay comparison semantics.
This release answers:
did replay remain cleandid observations driftdid selected actions driftdid traces driftdid probes driftdid adapter identity driftdid intake identity driftwas replay incomplete
without:


replay execution


runtime stepping


fuzzy matching


probabilistic instability scoring


auto-repair



New Module
src/qec/analysis/game_world_chaos_replay_verdict.py

New Test Suite
tests/test_game_world_chaos_replay_verdict.py

New Artifacts
ReplayComparisonPoint
Canonical deterministic replay comparison artifact binding:
comparison kindcomparison labelexpected hashobserved hashdrift statedrift class
Each comparison point produces:
replay_comparison_point_hash

ChaosReplayVerdict
Replay-safe deterministic replay-verdict artifact binding:
expected intake receiptobserved intake receiptexpected adapter contractobserved adapter contractexpected adapter specobserved adapter specexpected episode traceobserved episode traceordered comparison pointsdrift countverdict class
Each verdict produces:
chaos_replay_verdict_hash

ChaosReplayVerdictReceipt
Replay-safe wrapper binding:
ChaosReplayVerdict
into a canonical receipt artifact:
chaos_replay_verdict_receipt_hash

Allowed Verdict Classes
v156.5 introduces deterministic replay verdict classes:
CHAOS_REPLAY_CLEANOBSERVATION_DRIFTACTION_DRIFTTRACE_DRIFTPROBE_DRIFTADAPTER_DRIFTINTAKE_DRIFTINCOMPLETE_REPLAY

Replay Comparison Semantics
Observation Drift
Observation replay comparison detects deterministic mismatch between:
expected observation snapshot receipt hashobserved observation snapshot receipt hash
without replay execution.

Action Drift
Action replay comparison detects deterministic mismatch between:
expected selected action hashobserved selected action hash
without executing actions.

Trace Drift
Trace replay comparison detects deterministic mismatch between:
expected episode trace receipt hashobserved episode trace receipt hash
using hash identity only.

Probe Drift
Probe replay comparison detects deterministic mismatch between:
expected strategy probe receipt hashobserved strategy probe receipt hash
including deterministic probe pairing by:
(probe_type, probe_label)

Adapter / Intake Drift
Replay comparison also detects deterministic mismatch in:


intake receipts


adapter contract receipts


adapter spec hashes


before replay-layer semantics are considered.

Incomplete Replay
If required replay artifacts are missing:
observed_hash = None
the verdict deterministically becomes:
INCOMPLETE_REPLAY
Incomplete replay dominates all other drift classes.

Verdict Precedence
v156.5 implements deterministic precedence-based verdict derivation:
INCOMPLETE_REPLAY→ INTAKE_DRIFT→ ADAPTER_DRIFT→ OBSERVATION_DRIFT→ ACTION_DRIFT→ PROBE_DRIFT→ TRACE_DRIFT→ CHAOS_REPLAY_CLEAN
This prevents lower-level drift classifications from masking:


missing replay coverage


intake drift


adapter identity drift



Core Features
Deterministic Replay Comparison
Replay verdicts are derived from:


expected receipts


observed receipts


deterministic comparison rules


deterministic ordering


—not:


replay execution


gameplay


runtime stepping


heuristics



Artifact-Aware Rebuild Validation
Complete validators:


rebuild replay comparison points


rederive verdict classes


recompute hashes


reject caller-controlled replay structures deterministically


This prevents:


forged comparison points


manipulated drift counts


verdict-class tampering


replay artifact relinking



Probe Pairing Integrity
Strategy probe replay pairing is deterministic.
Pairs are matched by:
(probe_type, probe_label)
Duplicate pair keys are rejected deterministically.
Missing or extra probes produce:


INCOMPLETE_REPLAY
or


PROBE_DRIFT


depending on comparison state.

Early Bound Enforcement
v156.5 hardening added deterministic early bounds checks for replay comparison point generation.
The replay builder now rejects oversized comparison constructions before:


expensive point generation


large allocation chains


replay verdict hashing


This preserves bounded deterministic behavior for large traces/probe sets.

Scope Boundaries Preserved
v156.5 remains analysis-layer only.
Explicitly excluded:
runtime replay executiongameplayrenderingenvironment steppingauto-repairfuzzy replay scoringprobabilistic instability metricslearned policiesdynamic importsarchive extractionGameWorldInteractionReport
The replay layer compares artifacts only.
It does not replay worlds.

Validation & Hardening
Deterministic Comparison Mapping
Replay comparison kinds map deterministically to drift classes:
INTAKE_RECEIPT→ INTAKE_DRIFTADAPTER_CONTRACT→ ADAPTER_DRIFTOBSERVATION_SNAPSHOT→ OBSERVATION_DRIFTACTION_SELECTION→ ACTION_DRIFTSTRATEGY_PROBE→ PROBE_DRIFT

Explicit Validator Signatures
Artifact-aware validators now use explicit replay artifact signatures instead of generic *args/**kwargs forwarding.
This improves:


deterministic call semantics


readability


misuse resistance


static analysis clarity



Canonical Replay Identity
All replay artifacts implement:
canonical JSON exportsstable SHA-256 hashingself-hash exclusiondeep child validationhash recomputation
Validators recompute everything.
Stored replay structures are never trusted blindly.

Architectural Progression
The v156.x deterministic interaction stack now consists of:
v156.0→ deterministic source intakev156.1→ deterministic action vocabularyv156.2→ deterministic observation boundaryv156.3→ deterministic temporal sequencingv156.4→ deterministic strategy probesv156.4.1→ deterministic coverage hardeningv156.5→ deterministic replay verdicts
The stack continues to formalize the deterministic boundary between:
messy external worlds
and
canonical replay-safe proof artifacts
without introducing gameplay execution or probabilistic behavior.

Gremlin Notes (Non-Canonical)
- Replay drift gremlin formally classified.- Auto-repair gremlin rejected before runtime manifestation.- Fuzzy-matching gremlin denied entry at validator boundary.- Incomplete replay gremlin now dominates precedence hierarchy.

Final Result
After v156.5, QEC can now deterministically prove:
whether replay identity remained cleanwhich exact replay artifacts driftedwhich deterministic comparison points justify the verdictwhich replay class dominates the resultunder which replay-safe verdict receipt identity
The world may drift.

This release strengthens deterministic validation coverage across the complete v156.0–v156.4 GameWorld proof chain while preserving all existing production semantics.
No production code was modified.
No artifact contracts changed.
No hashes changed.
No replay semantics changed.
This is a tests-only deterministic hardening release.

Motivation
By v156.4, the GameWorld stack spans multiple deterministic proof layers:
v156.0 → source intakev156.1 → adapter/action alphabet contractsv156.2 → observation snapshot receiptsv156.3 → deterministic episode tracesv156.4 → strategy probe receipts
As the stack matured, cross-layer deterministic composition and validation cadence became increasingly important.

strengthen deterministic composition coveragecentralize scope-boundary enforcementimprove validator confidenceformalize targeted validation cadencepreserve full-suite rigor
without modifying production behavior.

intake→ adapter contract→ observations→ episode trace→ strategy probes
The suite validates:
stable hash recomputationcanonical JSON stabilitycanonical bytes stabilitycross-adapter rejectionprobe-result tamper rejectionreplay-safe composition
using public builders and validators only.

Deterministic Zip Fixture Hardening
A deterministic fixture hardening fix was applied after review feedback identified a potential nondeterministic archive-byte issue.
Previous fixture behavior:
ZipFile.writestr(...)
implicitly embedded current timestamps in zip metadata.
Because v156.0 hashes raw archive bytes, timestamp drift could theoretically produce nondeterministic archive hashes across slower CI runs.
The fixture now uses deterministic ZipInfo metadata:
ZipInfo.date_time = (2020, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0)
ensuring stable archive bytes across repeated builds.
This preserves deterministic replay behavior for composition tests.
No production code changed.

Centralized Scope Boundary Enforcement
tests/test_game_world_v156x_scope_boundaries.py
Added centralized forbidden-token scanning across all v156.x GameWorld production modules.
The suite enforces absence of:
runtime gameplayenvironment steppingrenderingRL traininglearned policiesprobabilistic rankingbest-action inferencedynamic importssubprocess executionChaosReplayVerdictGameWorldInteractionReport
The centralized scope scan now acts as a deterministic semantic firewall across the v156.x arc.
Special handling preserves legitimate intake-only zip metadata inspection while prohibiting zip behavior in later layers.

Validation Matrix

Added a compact deterministic validation matrix covering:
v156.0 intake validationv156.1 adapter validationv156.2 observation validationv156.3 episode trace validationv156.4 strategy probe validation
The matrix includes:
success-path validationmalformed hash rejectionHASH_MISMATCH tamper detectioncross-adapter mismatch rejectionvalidator chaining
This provides a fast deterministic smoke/composition suite during iterative development.

Validation Law remains unchanged.

cross-layer replay integrityadapter/spec consistencyprobe-result re-derivationscope-boundary containmenttamper detectioncomposition determinism
The release strengthens confidence that the v156.x stack composes safely without semantic drift.

Release Notes — v156.4
QSOLKCB/QEC v156.4 — Strategy Probe Receipts
Release Summary
v156.4 extends the v156.x “Deterministic Interaction with Messy Game Worlds” arc with deterministic strategy probe receipts.
This release introduces canonical, bounded, replay-safe probe artifacts that analyze existing episode traces without executing gameplay, optimizing policies, ranking actions probabilistically, or performing reinforcement learning.
The probe layer does not decide what should happen.
It deterministically proves what was observed about an already-existing trace.

Motivation
v156.0 established deterministic source intake.

v156.2 established deterministic observation snapshot receipts.

what probe was requestedwhich trace was probedunder which adapter contractwith which deterministic parameterswith which deterministic result payloadunder which replay-safe probe receipt identity

learned behavior

NO_OP_BASELINELEGAL_ACTION_SCANREPEAT_ACTION_STABILITYTRACE_DIVERGENCE_SCANTERMINAL_STATE_SCANSCORE_DELTA_SCANUNKNOWN_ACTION_REJECTION

Probe Semantics
NO_OP_BASELINE
Deterministically analyzes:


no-op action presence


no-op step counts


non-no-op counts


terminal metadata


without executing anything.

LEGAL_ACTION_SCAN
Enumerates the static v156.1 action alphabet in deterministic adapter order.
“Legal” means:


representable in the static adapter alphabet


—not:


executable


optimal


runtime-valid



REPEAT_ACTION_STABILITY
Scans an existing trace for repeated occurrences of a selected action atom.
No world stepping occurs.
No hypothetical replay occurs.

TRACE_DIVERGENCE_SCAN
Compares:


expected trace hash


observed trace hash


using deterministic hash equality only.
No replay execution.
No fuzzy matching.
No runtime reconstruction.

TERMINAL_STATE_SCAN
Deterministically validates:


terminal presence


terminal index


terminal finality


against v156.3 terminal semantics.

SCORE_DELTA_SCAN
Deterministically derives integer score deltas from already-recorded SCORE_VALUE observation payloads.
No heuristic scoring.
No floating-point reward systems.
No optimization.
If no SCORE_VALUE observations exist, deterministic empty results are returned.

UNKNOWN_ACTION_REJECTION
Deterministically checks whether an action exists in the static adapter alphabet.
No action execution occurs.

v156.4 probe results are not caller-trusted outputs.

adapter spec


episode trace


probe spec

recompute hashes

This prevents:

replay drift through mutable result payloads

adapter/spec consistencytrace consistencyprobe-type consistencyresult/spec bindingaction alphabet membershiptrace hash consistency

v156.4 hardening added explicit validation for:
receipt→ nested spec identity consistencyreceipt→ nested result identity consistencyresult→ spec hash consistency
preventing:


result/spec relinking


receipt-level adapter drift


nested hash mismatch masking



Canonical JSON / Hash Integrity
All probe artifacts implement:
canonical JSON exportsstable SHA-256 hashingself-hash exclusiondeep child validationhash recomputation
Validators recompute everything.

Complete validators:

title: v156.2.1 — ROADMAP Metadata Assertion Stabilization
body:
QSOLKCB/QEC v156.2.1 — ROADMAP Metadata Assertion Stabilization
Release Summary
v156.2.1 is a surgical metadata stabilization patch for the v156.x “Deterministic Interaction with Messy Game Worlds” arc.
This release resolves the remaining full-suite failure that persisted after the v156.2 Observation Snapshot Receipts release:
tests/test_retro_camera_projection_pipeline.py::TestFinalizationHardening::test_roadmap_version_metadata
The issue was caused by ROADMAP.md metadata text drift relative to the repository’s deterministic metadata assertions.
No proof artifacts, hashing contracts, validators, observation semantics, adapter contracts, or GameWorld interaction logic were modified.
This is a metadata-only stabilization release.

Motivation
After the successful release of v156.2, all touched-file tests passed, but the full suite still failed due to a pre-existing ROADMAP metadata assertion mismatch.
The failing assertion required the exact phrase:
published tags are authoritative
ROADMAP.md contained a nearby but non-identical variant, causing the repository-wide metadata consistency test to fail.

restore full-suite green statusstabilize roadmap metadata consistencyprevent metadata drift from propagating through the v156.x arc

No source modules were modified.

title: v156.2 — Observation Snapshot Receipts
body:
GitHub Release: QSOLKCB/QEC v156.2 — Observation Snapshot Receipts
Summary

v156.2 extends the v156.x “Deterministic Interaction with Messy Game Worlds” arc with canonical, bounded, replay-safe observation snapshot artifacts.

This release introduces deterministic observation channels, canonical observation payload receipts, adapter-aware ACTION_MASK validation, bounded snapshot sets, and cryptographically bound observation receipts for external game-world interaction boundaries.

The world may be messy.

The observation receipt is not.

Added
New Analysis-Layer Module
src/qec/analysis/game_world_observation_snapshot.py
New Test Suite
tests/test_game_world_observation_snapshot.py
New Artifacts
ObservationChannelSpec

Deterministic channel definition including:

channel_type
channel_label
max_payload_bytes
observation_channel_hash

Supported channel types:

SYMBOLIC_STATE
PIXEL_HASH
TEXT_EVENT
SCORE_VALUE
POSITION_VECTOR
ACTION_MASK
TERMINAL_FLAG
UNKNOWN
ObservationSnapshot

Canonical observation boundary artifact binding:

adapter contract
adapter spec
observation index
observation channel
canonical observation payload
payload hash
snapshot hash
ObservationSnapshotReceipt

Receipt-layer binding for observation snapshots with deterministic recomputation validation.

ObservationSnapshotSet

Deterministically ordered, bounded, duplicate-safe aggregation of observation snapshot receipts.

Core Features
Deterministic Observation Identity
same adapter contract
+ same adapter spec
+ same observation channel
+ same canonical observation payload
+ same observation_index
→ same observation_snapshot_hash
→ same observation_snapshot_receipt_hash
Canonical Payload Validation

Implemented strict canonical JSON validation with:

JSON-safe payload enforcement
no floats
no implicit coercion
no hidden normalization
deterministic canonicalization
payload/hash recomputation
Adapter-Aware ACTION_MASK Validation

Added complete adapter-bound validation for:

unknown action rejection
alphabet membership validation
alphabet-order enforcement
duplicate action rejection

using v156.1 adapter/action alphabet contracts.

Payload Boundary Enforcement

Added deterministic payload-byte enforcement for:

TEXT_EVENT
PIXEL_HASH
POSITION_VECTOR
ACTION_MASK

including corrected raw-payload byte semantics for fixed-size channels.

Snapshot Set Hardening

Implemented:

bounded snapshot-set size
duplicate observation-key rejection
deterministic ordering
tamper detection
adapter/spec consistency checks

using centralized ordering helpers.

Validation & Hardening
Added Public Complete Validators
validate_spec_in_contract(...)
validate_observation_snapshot_with_adapter(...)
validate_observation_snapshot_receipt_with_adapter(...)
validate_observation_snapshot_set_with_adapter(...)

while preserving adapter-blind intrinsic validator signatures.

Deterministic Failure Semantics

Added strict classification for:

INVALID_HASH_FORMAT
HASH_MISMATCH
INVALID_OBSERVATION_PAYLOAD
PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE
ACTION_MASK_UNKNOWN_ACTION
ACTION_MASK_ORDER_MISMATCH
ADAPTER_SPEC_NOT_IN_CONTRACT
SNAPSHOT_ADAPTER_MISMATCH

Malformed canonical payload JSON now deterministically raises:

INVALID_OBSERVATION_PAYLOAD

instead of leaking parser exceptions.

Scope Boundaries Preserved

v156.2 remains strictly analysis-layer only.

Explicitly excluded:

gameplay
runtime rendering
episode traces
strategy probes
replay verdicts
RL/training behavior
dynamic imports
archive extraction
runtime world stepping
probabilistic feature extraction
Testing

pytest -q tests/test_game_world_observation_snapshot.py
pytest -q tests/test_game_world_adapter_contract.py
Full Suite
pytest -q

Fails only on the known unrelated pre-existing ROADMAP metadata assertion:

tests/test_retro_camera_projection_pipeline.py::TestFinalizationHardening::test_roadmap_version_metadata

No new failures introduced by v156.2.

Result

QEC can now deterministically prove:

what was observed
through which channel
under which adapter contract
under which action alphabet
at which bounded observation index
with which canonical payload
under which replay-stable receipt identity

v156.x continues:

archive intake
→ adapter contracts
→ observation snapshots
→ deterministic episode traces
→ strategy probes
→ chaos replay verdicts

title: v156.0 — GameWorld Source Intake Contract
body:
🚀 Release Notes — v156.0
v156.0 — GameWorld Source Intake Contract
Summary

v156.0 begins the experimental detour arc:

v156.x — Deterministic Interaction with Messy Game Worlds

This release asks:

Can a deterministic proof system safely observe messy game worlds
without becoming messy itself?

v156.0 introduces a deterministic intake boundary for external game-world source archives.

The release does not execute games, import source modules, or run untrusted code.

Instead, it converts external .zip archives into canonical, replay-safe intake artifacts.

🧠 Core Idea

Messy worlds may contain:

game engines
AI frameworks
Atari environments
strategy systems
raycasting engines
diffusion projects
build files
model weights
mixed-language source trees

QEC does not attempt to make those worlds deterministic.

Instead, QEC makes the boundary deterministic:

archive bytes
→ canonical intake manifest
→ corpus manifest
→ intake receipt

GameWorldIntakeReceipt

frozen
canonical
hash-bound
replay-safe
deterministic

build_game_world_intake_receipt(...)

📦 Intake Model

v156.0 performs deterministic archive intake only.

No:

zip extraction

dynamic loading

is permitted.

The intake layer observes:

archive names
archive hashes
top-level structure

asset classes

and emits canonical proof artifacts.

🎮 Supported World Families

RAYCAST_FPS

GAME_AI_FRAMEWORK

Classification is deterministic and filename/path-token based only.

No probabilistic classification or content inference is used.

🔐 Validation Guarantees

strict lowercase SHA-256 hashes
canonical JSON payloads
self-hash recomputation
deep child archive validation
deterministic archive ordering
duplicate archive rejection
strict tuple[str, ...] validation
strict world-family whitelisting

🛡️ Zip Safety Boundary

v156.0 explicitly hardens intake against unsafe archive paths.

Rejected patterns include:

absolute paths

null-byte names

Validation now normalizes separators and validates path segments structurally rather than by naive substring matching.

Benign names like:

foo..bar.txt

⚠️ Intake Warning System

The system now deterministically rejects malformed or unsafe intake structures before parent manifest binding occurs.

title: v155.3 — DecayResistanceProof
body:
Summary

v155.3 completes the deterministic decay arc by introducing:

DecayResistanceProof

a final replay-safe proof artifact that certifies a proof chain is decay-resistant.

This release binds:

entropy_drift_receipt_hash
+ replay_proof_hash
+ clean-state invariants
→ decay_resistance_proof_hash

and proves that:

all referenced signatures are CLEAN
all referenced signatures have decay_score == 0
no adversarial positions exist
the proof can be replayed identically

This is the capstone of the v155.x deterministic entropy / decay framework.

🧠 Core Concept

A valid resistance proof requires:

decay_class_at_proof == "CLEAN"
AND
adversarial_positions_at_proof == ()

If either invariant fails:

DECAY_RESISTANCE_IMPOSSIBLE
or
ADVERSARIAL_POSITIONS_PRESENT

is raised deterministically.

🧱 Artifact Added
DecayResistanceProof

Frozen, canonical, hash-bound, replay-safe.

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DecayResistanceProof:
    entropy_drift_receipt_hash: str
    replay_proof_hash: str
    decay_class_at_proof: str
    adversarial_positions_at_proof: tuple[str, ...]
    decay_resistance_proof_hash: str
🔧 Public Helpers Added
build_decay_resistance_proof(...)
validate_decay_resistance_proof(...)
🔐 Deterministic Guarantees

v155.3 enforces:

same entropy receipt
+ same signature registry
+ same replay proof
→ same decay_resistance_proof_hash

The proof hash is computed from:

entropy_drift_receipt_hash
replay_proof_hash
decay_class_at_proof
adversarial_positions_at_proof

with strict self-hash exclusion.

No probabilistic entropy, heuristic replay logic, or runtime state is introduced.

🛡️ Validation Behavior

Validation enforces:

strict lowercase SHA-256 hashes
CLEAN-only proof state
empty adversarial_positions_at_proof
exact proof hash recomputation
deterministic replay-proof hash extraction
explicit DigitalDecaySignature registry validation

Strict failure modes:

INVALID_INPUT
INVALID_HASH_FORMAT
HASH_MISMATCH
DECAY_RESISTANCE_IMPOSSIBLE
ADVERSARIAL_POSITIONS_PRESENT
🔄 Replay-Proof Binding

v155.3 binds replay state deterministically via:

lattice_replay_proof_hash
or
replay_proof_hash

using safe sentinel-based extraction.

Rules:

if both replay hash attrs exist:
    they must match exactly
otherwise:
    INVALID_INPUT

Malformed replay hashes fail with:

INVALID_HASH_FORMAT

No replay semantics were added beyond deterministic hash binding.

🧩 Explicit Signature Registry Validation

A major hardening improvement:

v155.3 now requires an explicit:

DigitalDecaySignature registry

during proof construction.

This prevents “hash-string-trusting” behavior and ensures:

every referenced signature:
    validates
    is CLEAN
    has decay_score == 0
    has adversarial_positions == ()

Duplicate signature hashes are rejected deterministically.

No subsystem occurrence deduplication is allowed.

🔥 Hardening Applied

Final adversarial-review hardening included:

duplicate signature registry rejection
explicit subsystem receipt signature extraction
safe replay-proof hash extraction
cross-process determinism verification
tamper coverage across all proof fields
non-tuple adversarial position rejection
removal of fragile manual signature hash construction
full v155 artifact-chain verification
strict scope-boundary enforcement

Important architectural correction:

validate_decay_resistance_proof(proof)

was intentionally restored as a pure artifact-integrity validator only.

It does NOT reconstruct external entropy/signature/replay objects.

Builder responsibilities and validator responsibilities remain strictly separated.

with deterministic identical proof hashes.

⛓️ Full v155 Artifact Chain

v155.3 finalizes the deterministic decay chain:

title: v155.1 — DigitalDecaySignature
body:
🚀 Release Notes — v155.1
v155.1 — DigitalDecaySignature / Decay Signature Layer
Summary

Introduces deterministic decay signatures for QEC, extending v155.0 checkpoint drift detection into exact, hash-bound decay classification.

v155.0 established:

expected_hash
vs
observed_hash
→ drifted
→ checkpoint_hash
→ decay_score
→ checkpoint_set_hash

v155.1 adds:

checkpoint_set_hash
+ threshold_contract_hash
+ decay_score
+ adversarial_positions
→ decay_class
→ digital_decay_signature_hash
Artifacts Added

DigitalDecaySignature

Both artifacts are:

frozen
canonical
hash-bound
replay-safe
deterministic
Public Helpers Added
build_decay_threshold_contract(...)
build_digital_decay_signature(...)
validate_digital_decay_signature(...)
Decay Classes

v155.1 defines exact integer decay classes:

CLEAN
DEGRADED
CORRUPTED
ADVERSARIAL

Classification is deterministic:

decay_score == 0
→ CLEAN

1 <= decay_score <= degraded_threshold
→ DEGRADED

degraded_threshold < decay_score <= corrupted_threshold
→ CORRUPTED

decay_score > corrupted_threshold
→ ADVERSARIAL

No probabilistic entropy, fuzzy thresholds, floats, weighting, or heuristic classification is introduced.

Validation Guarantees

This release enforces:

same checkpoint set
+ same threshold contract
+ same adversarial positions
→ same decay class
→ same digital_decay_signature_hash

Validation now guarantees:

decay_score must be a non-negative exact integer
CLEAN requires decay_score == 0
decay_score == 0 requires CLEAN
thresholds must be exact integers
corrupted_threshold > degraded_threshold
adversarial positions must be explicit, sorted, unique, and drifted-only
stored hashes must recompute exactly
malformed hashes fail deterministically
Hardening Applied

Final hardening included:

validation-order protection for adversarial_positions
non-string / empty / unhashable adversarial positions rejected deterministically
negative decay_score rejected
CLEAN-positive-score contradiction rejected
zero-score non-CLEAN contradiction rejected
no v155.2+ scope creep fields exposed

Invalid structural inputs now fail through the QEC error channel:

rather than leaking raw Python exceptions.

Error Semantics

Strict failure modes:

INVALID_INPUT

HASH_MISMATCH

INVALID_THRESHOLD_ORDER
