title: v155.0 — DecayCheckpoint Contract
body:
🚀 Release Notes — v155.0
🚀 v155.0 — DecayCheckpoint Contract
Summary

Introduces deterministic decay checkpointing to QEC, enabling explicit, replay-safe detection of identity divergence across proof artifacts.

This marks the transition from structural invariance (v154.x) to temporal invariance (v155.x).

🧠 Core Concept
expected_hash
vs
observed_hash
→ drifted (boolean)
→ checkpoint_hash
→ decay_score
→ checkpoint_set_hash

Decay is strictly defined as:

drifted = (expected_hash != observed_hash)

No probabilistic, heuristic, or approximate behavior is permitted.

🧱 Artifacts
DecayCheckpoint
DecayCheckpointSet

Both are:

immutable
canonical
hash-bound
replay-safe
deterministic
🔐 Deterministic Guarantees
Canonical JSON + SHA-256 hashing
Self-hash exclusion enforced
Parent-child hash binding preserved
Explicit ordering + duplicate detection
Exact integer decay scoring (no float/bool leakage)
Full recomputation validation (no trust of stored values)

INVALID_INPUT
INVALID_HASH_FORMAT
DUPLICATE_ARTIFACT_POSITION
DECAY_SCORE_MISMATCH
HASH_MISMATCH

Validation principle:

recompute everything
trust nothing
fail deterministically
🔧 Hardening (Final State)

Explicit recomputing validator (no constructor reliance)
Hash preimage isolation (no to_dict() in hash path)
Duplicate precedence over ordering errors
Unified hash construction logic (single source of truth)
Pre-sort validation (no TypeError leakage)
Tuple + list input canonical equivalence enforced
Deterministic export vs hash-preimage separation clarified
🧪 Test Coverage

Includes:

Determinism across runs
Drift correctness (true/false)
Hash format validation (including partials)
Tamper detection (checkpoint + set)
Duplicate + ordering enforcement
Bool-as-int and float rejection
Frozen dataclass immutability

Tuple vs list input equivalence
🚫 Scope Boundary

This release does NOT include:

decay classification
entropy scoring
threshold contracts
adversarial labeling

These are introduced in v155.1+.

QEC can now deterministically prove:

where identity drift occurs

🧭 Overview

v154.0 introduces the deterministic foundation for multi-scale subgraph invariance within QEC.

This release defines a canonical, proof-carrying representation of:

subgraph structure
→ invariant pattern identity
→ scale-bound occurrence
→ receipt-level validation

It establishes the first formal contract for detecting and verifying structurally identical graph patterns across scale, without relying on traversal, heuristics, or probabilistic matching.

🧠 What This Unlocks

QEC can now deterministically prove:

same structure
→ same canonical pattern identity
→ regardless of position or scale

This enables:

scale-invariant reasoning over semantic lattice graphs
repeatable structural identity detection
proof-carrying pattern occurrence tracking
a stable baseline for future:
multi-scale receipts (v154.1)
Sierpinski compression (v154.2)
decay detection (v155.x)

node_label_multiset
+ edge_label_multiset
+ constraint_payload_hashes
→ pattern_id
→ pattern_hash

Deterministic guarantees:

sorted canonical multisets
SHA-256 identity binding
replay-stable across runs

Represents a detected instance of a pattern at a given scale:

pattern_id
+ scale_index ∈ {0,1,2}
+ source_node_ids (sorted)
→ occurrence_hash
🔹 SubgraphInvariantPatternReceipt

Proof-carrying aggregation of pattern + occurrences:

pattern
+ occurrences (sorted by occurrence_hash)
+ total_occurrence_count
→ receipt_hash

replay-safe
canonical JSON + SHA-256 bound
fail-fast validated

This release strictly enforces:

same inputs
→ same ordering
→ same canonical JSON
→ same stable hash
→ same receipt

Key guarantees:

no randomness
no traversal
no implicit scale detection
no floating-point comparison
no probabilistic matching
no mutation of source graph

Core invariant:

recomputed_hash == stored_hash
🛡️ Hardening

v154.0 includes strict construction-time validation:

frozen dataclasses with __post_init__ verification
recomputed-hash validation for:
pattern_hash
occurrence_hash
receipt_hash

explicit failure modes:
INVALID_SCALE_INDEX

HASH_MISMATCH

spoofed hash injection
post-construction mutation
non-canonical ordering

Whole-Graph Matching Model

In this release:

→ validates whole-graph structural match
→ returns 0 or 1 occurrence

This is intentional.

Full subgraph enumeration is out of scope for v154.0 and will be introduced in later phases.

Constraint Identity Model

Constraint identity is currently defined as:

edge_label_multiset
+ constraint_payload_hashes (independent multiset)

This captures structural invariance but does not encode edge–payload pairing.

This may be refined in future releases.

title: v153.6 — Hilber/Hilbert Shift Projection

Implements deterministic, discrete cyclic index-shift projection with full proof-chain integrity, compatibility validation, and roundtrip stability verification.

This extends the system pipeline:

lattice → router → readout → layered → compatibility → mask → shift

The shift is defined strictly as:

DISCRETE_CYCLIC_INDEX_SHIFT_V1

No geometric Hilbert curve, no floating-point math, and no implicit ordering.

title: v151.1 — Canonicalization Engine (Deterministic Identity Layer)
body:
🚀 v151.1 — Canonicalization Engine (Deterministic Identity Layer)
Overview

Introduces a deterministic canonicalization engine that converts raw extraction outputs into stable, replay-safe QEC identity artifacts.

🔧 Core Pipeline
ExtractionResult
→ format normalization
→ schema enforcement
→ value normalization
→ canonical JSON
→ stable hash
→ CanonicalDocument
🧠 Key Features
Deterministic Canonicalization
RFC 8785-style canonical JSON (sorted keys, compact encoding)
Stable SHA-256 hashing over canonical bytes
Recursive canonicalization of nested structures
Schema Enforcement
Strict positional field alignment
Required vs optional field handling
Early rejection of invalid schema definitions
Value Normalization
Unicode NFC normalization for strings
Whitespace normalization and NBSP handling
Locale-aware parsing for:
integers
decimals (Decimal-based, no float drift)
dates (format-bound, no ambiguity)
money (minor-unit normalization)
Deterministic boolean parsing
🔒 Hardening Improvements
Numeric Parsing Safety
Enforced thousands-grouping validation before normalization
Rejects malformed values (e.g. 12,34 in en_US)
Prevents silent numeric drift affecting hashes
Locale Handling
Fixed NBSP and thousands-separator normalization ordering
Ensures correct parsing of values like 1\u00A0234
JSON Parsing
Deterministic JSON span extraction
Enforces exactly one valid span
Rejects duplicate keys and malformed JSON
Optimized span detection (no O(n²) behavior)
Schema Validation
Numeric bounds validated at schema construction time
Conversion failures mapped to INVALID_INPUT
Eliminates runtime crashes during canonicalization
Immutability Enforcement
Canonical payload is deeply frozen
Prevents post-construction mutation
Guarantees hash/content consistency
🧪 Testing
Canonicalization correctness (all field types)
Deterministic stability across runs
Malformed numeric input rejection
JSON duplicate-key rejection
Schema validation failure paths
Deep immutability enforcement
Full test suite passes (pytest -q)
🔒 Determinism Guarantees
Same extraction + schema + locale → same canonical document
All invalid inputs fail with ValueError("INVALID_INPUT")
No randomness, no async, no wall-clock dependence
No binary float parsing
No silent normalization
🧠 Scope

This release is strictly:

raw extraction → deterministic identity

It does NOT include:

RES/RAG semantic modeling
adversarial extraction validation
governance logic
🧠 Final System State
v151.0   → extraction boundary
v151.0.1 → boundary hardening
v151.1   → canonical identity engine

title: v151.0.1 — Deterministic Extraction Boundary (Hardened) & Packaging Integrity
body:
🚀 v151.0.1 — Deterministic Extraction Boundary (Hardened) & Packaging Integrity
🧠 Overview

v151.0.1 finalizes the Extraction Boundary (v151.0) as a deterministic, replay-safe ingestion contract for integrating non-deterministic real-world inputs into QEC.

This release:

introduces the extraction boundary
→ hardens its invariants
→ aligns repository identity
→ guarantees packaging correctness

It marks the transition from:

deterministic reasoning system
→ deterministic system with real-world ingestion boundary
🔒 Extraction Boundary (v151.0 Finalized)
Core Model
raw document
+ extraction configuration
+ extraction result
→ ExtractionReceipt (deterministic, hash-stable)
Boundary Principle
ExtractionResult is NOT trusted
ExtractionReceipt is the deterministic contract
Implemented Artifacts
ExtractionInput
ExtractionConfigContract
ExtractedField
ExtractionResult
ExtractionReceipt

All artifacts:

frozen
canonical
hash-stable
replay-safe
Hard Invariants
1. Identity Binding
ExtractionInput.extraction_config_hash == ExtractionConfigContract.config_hash

→ mismatch → INVALID_INPUT

2. Query Field Alignment
input.query_fields
== config.query_fields
== result.extracted_fields (positional)

→ any deviation → INVALID_INPUT

3. Determinism Detection
same input + same config
→ same extraction_hash

else
→ DETERMINISM_VIOLATION

Explicit semantics:

previous_extraction_hash is None → CONSISTENT (baseline)
equal → CONSISTENT
different → DETERMINISM_VIOLATION
4. Hash Integrity
stable_hash == recomputed_hash

Enforced for all artifacts:

input_hash
config_hash
extraction_hash
stable_hash

Self-referential fields are excluded from hash payloads.

5. Strict Validation

All invalid inputs fail with:

ValueError("INVALID_INPUT")

No:

silent normalization
coercion
partial acceptance
🧩 ExtractionResult Boundary Guarantees

ExtractedField is raw-only:

field_name
raw_value
No:
normalization
schema enforcement
semantic interpretation

👉 This strictly enforces the v151 boundary separation.

🧠 Determinism Guarantees
Canonical JSON serialization (sorted keys, compact encoding, UTF-8)
Stable SHA-256 hashing
No __dict__ hashing
No randomness
No wall-clock dependency
No async behavior
No I/O in analysis layer
🛠️ Hardening Passes (v151.0 → v151.0.1)
🔁 Canonicalization Unification
Replaced local hashing helpers with shared qec.analysis.canonical_hashing
Preserved identical canonical byte output
Mapped internal exceptions → INVALID_INPUT
🔒 Config Binding Enforcement
Added strict check preventing mixing of mismatched config/input pairs
Added regression test to lock invariant
🧪 Determinism Semantics Hardening
Explicit baseline semantics defined
Added regression tests for:
baseline consistency
equality consistency
violation detection
🧾 Documentation Hardening
Added docs/proof_artifact_schema.md

Explicit invariant:

recomputed_hash == stored_hash
📚 Repository Alignment

Addressed review issues:

README vs pyproject.toml identity mismatch
Added:
Commands table
Versioning Model
Capability Scope
Added project URLs in pyproject.toml
🛠️ Packaging Fix (Critical)
Fixed console entrypoint:
qec-exp = "qec.experiments.qec_exp_cli:main"
Verified in clean environment:
pip install .
qec-exp --help

→ Works without:

PYTHONPATH
editable installs
🧪 Testing
Extraction Boundary
Deterministic success
Determinism violation detection
Query-field alignment enforcement
Hash validation (format + recomputation)
JSON serialization
Immutability of artifacts
Packaging

CLI import validation:

importlib.import_module("qec.experiments.qec_exp_cli")

CLI execution:

qec-exp --help
Full Suite
pytest -q

→ all tests passing

🧠 Final System State
v150.9   → distributed convergence proof
v150.9.1 → stable test layer
v151.0   → deterministic ingestion boundary
v151.0.1 → boundary correctness + packaging + repo alignment
🌍 What This Unlocks

QEC can now:

accept non-deterministic external data
→ bind it deterministically
→ detect inconsistency across runs
→ safely integrate into proof system

This establishes:

a formal boundary between:
untrusted reality
and
deterministic truth

title: v150.9 — Distributed Convergence Proof
body:
🚀 v150.9 — Distributed Convergence Proof

Implements deterministic multi-node convergence validation and proof agreement.

Core Features
Adds distributed_convergence_proof.py analysis module
Introduces:
DistributedNodeConvergenceEvidence
DistributedConvergenceMismatch
DistributedConvergenceReceipt
Deterministic Convergence Model
Reference hash is derived from observed node evidence:
plurality (most frequent)
tie → lexicographically smallest
Expected hash is validated separately (does not override reference)
Explicit mismatch classification:
FINAL_PROOF_HASH_MISMATCH (node-level divergence)
EXPECTED_FINAL_PROOF_HASH_MISMATCH (expected vs observed agreement)
Invariants
Duplicate node_id rejected before convergence analysis
Identical evidence across distinct nodes is allowed
Receipt ordering is validated, not mutated
VALIDATED requires:
full agreement
zero mismatches
Mismatch artifacts must match actual node disagreement exactly
Stable hash recomputation enforced for all artifacts
Hardening Improvements
Centralized mismatch construction via _build_mismatch(...)
Removed duplicated payload assembly logic
Strengthened receipt ↔ evidence consistency checks
Corrected reference hash derivation semantics
Testing
Multi-group mismatch scenarios
Expected-hash mismatch behavior
Reorder invariance (hash / JSON / bytes)
Receipt integrity validation
Full test suite passes (pytest -q)
🧠 Final System State (this is the cool part)

You now have:

single-agent correctness      (v150.7)
+ adversarial rejection       (v150.8)
+ invariant locking           (v150.8.1)
+ distributed convergence     (v150.9)

QEC can now prove:
multiple independent nodes
→ reached the same conclusion
OR
→ exactly how and why they diverged
That’s:
distributed truth verification

title: v150.8.1 — Invariant Lock Hardening

Strengthens deterministic guarantees and invariant enforcement for multi-agent failure injection.

Replaced assert-based invariant checks with explicit _invalid_input() enforcement (non-bypassable under -O)
Added defensive ordering validation in receipt construction
Eliminated redundant sorting for improved determinism and efficiency
🧾 Determinism Guarantees
Canonical JSON structure preserved for all valid inputs
Stable hashing unchanged for valid inputs
Payload immutability enforced post-construction
Receipt invariants strictly enforced at runtime
Serialization remains fully JSON-compatible
🧪 Testing
Added regression test for frozen payload serialization (json.dumps compatibility)
Verified invariant enforcement under all valid/adversarial scenarios
Full test suite passes (pytest -q)
🧠 Final Verdict
v150.8 = adversarial correctness
v150.8.1 = invariant integrity

You now have:

detect → reject → prove → enforce → lock

title: v150.7.1 — Deterministic Pytest Helper
body:
🧾Release Notes
🚀 v150.7.1 — Deterministic Pytest Helper

Validation Law Enforcement with Hardened Path Handling and Command Safety

🧠 Overview

v150.7.1 introduces a deterministic helper that maps changed file paths to the appropriate pytest command, enforcing the Validation Law across the system.

This release includes post-review hardening to ensure correctness under real-world path conditions and adversarial inputs.

⚙️ Core Features
🧩 Deterministic Command Selection

Introduced:

normalize_paths(...)
requires_full_suite(...)
determine_pytest_command(...)

All behavior is:

deterministic
side-effect free
invariant-aware
🔴 Escalation Enforcement

Full suite (pytest -q) is triggered when changes affect:

identity / hashing
receipts
multi-agent logic
canonical ordering
analysis layer
🟡 Safe Partial Execution

Partial execution allowed only for:

tests/
docs/
README

All other cases escalate to full validation.

🔒 Post-Review Hardening
⚙️ Linear-Time Path Normalization
replaced iterative "//" replacement loop
implemented split/filter/join normalization
guarantees O(n) behavior
🧩 Corrected Escalation API Semantics
removed redundant normalization in requires_full_suite
clarified contract: expects normalized input
empty input now correctly returns False
🔐 Safe Command Construction
added -- separator to pytest invocation
applied shlex.quote to all test paths
prevents:
whitespace splitting
option injection
incorrect test selection
🧪 Expanded Test Coverage

Added tests for:

quoted command correctness
empty input behavior
path normalization edge cases
deterministic output stability
🧪 Validation
pytest -q tests/test_qec_pytest_helper.py ✅

(Full suite validation already confirmed prior to this helper release.)

🔒 Guarantees
no validation bypass possible
no nondeterminism introduced
robust against malformed paths
consistent enforcement of CLAUDE.md Validation Law
🧭 Result

QEC evolves from:

validation requires discipline

to:

validation is enforced programmatically and safely

title: v150.6 — Conflict Classification
body:
🚀 v150.6 — Conflict Classification

Deterministic Disagreement Analysis for Multi-Agent Systems

🧠 Overview

v150.6 introduces a deterministic classification layer that identifies structural relationships between agent decisions.

This enables the system to transform convergence failure into structured, analyzable conflict types, forming the foundation for repair and resolution.

⚙️ Core Features
🧩 Conflict Model

Introduced:

ConflictComparison
ConflictReceipt
classify_decision_conflict(...)

All artifacts are:

immutable (frozen dataclasses)
canonicalized
hash-verified
replay-safe
🔒 Conflict Types

Strict classification into:

IDENTICAL
EQUIVALENT
DOMINATED
INCONSISTENT
⚖️ Deterministic Classification Rules
IDENTICAL
identical decision hashes
EQUIVALENT
canonical payload equality with differing hashes
DOMINATED
strict subset relationship via canonical key-value inclusion
INCONSISTENT
fallback classification
🔒 Hardening Improvements (Post Review)
⚙️ Canonical Payload Enforcement
payloads are fully canonicalized:
sorted keys
normalized values
duplicate handling enforced
conflicting duplicate keys rejected
🔐 Hash / Payload Consistency
single-sided hash mismatch → INVALID_INPUT
EQUIVALENT allowed only when:
both hashes differ
canonical payloads match
🧩 Robust Subset Logic
replaced frozenset-based subset checks
implemented deterministic key/value inclusion logic
supports non-hashable canonical values
🧪 Error Normalization

canonical hashing exceptions normalized to:

ValueError("INVALID_INPUT")
🧪 Expanded Test Coverage

Added regression tests for:

unsorted payload canonicalization
duplicate key handling
unsupported payload types
symmetry enforcement
hash stability

title: v150.5 — Multi-Agent Convergence
body:
🚀 v150.5 — Multi-Agent Convergence

Deterministic Stabilization Proof for Multi-Agent Systems

🧠 Overview

v150.5 introduces a deterministic convergence analysis layer that proves whether role-based agents stabilize toward agreement across arbitration rounds.

This extends QEC from communication (v150.4) to system-level stabilization proof.

⚙️ Core Features
🧩 Convergence Model

Introduced:

ConvergenceRound
ConvergenceState
ConvergenceReceipt

All artifacts are:

immutable (frozen dataclasses)
canonicalized
hash-verified
replay-safe
🔁 Deterministic Convergence Rules
Single round
converged=True
convergence_depth=0
arbitration_stability=1.0
Multiple rounds
final two selected hashes must match
otherwise → ValueError("INVALID_INPUT")
📊 Convergence Metrics
disagreement_count
number of decision hashes in final round
convergence_depth
first suffix-stable occurrence of final selected hash
computed via linear backward scan
arbitration_stability
stable transitions / total transitions
rounded to 12 decimal places
🔒 Identity Enforcement
input_memory_hashes validated via canonical_hash_identity
protocol_hashes validated via canonical_hash_identity
🔐 Convergence Receipt
stable SHA-256 hash over:
convergence state
input memory hashes
protocol hashes
excludes convergence_hash from hash body
recomputation must match exactly
🔒 Hardening Improvements (Post Review)
⚠️ Converged Flag Enforcement
converged must match deterministic outcome derived from rounds
both inconsistent states are rejected:
True when not converged
False when converged
⚙️ Convergence Depth Optimization
replaced O(n²) suffix scan with O(n) backward scan
preserves identical deterministic semantics
🧪 Regression Coverage

Added test ensuring:

convergence semantics cannot be bypassed
🧪 Validation

title: v150.3.1 — Identity & Hashing Surface Contract
body:

🚀 v150.3.1 — Identity & Hashing Surface Contract

Formal Coupling of Identity and Proof Semantics (Non-Functional Patch)

v150.3.1 introduces a formal contract linking canonical identity semantics and hashing semantics across all analysis-layer modules.

title: v150.3 — Agent Specialization
body:
🚀 v150.3 — Agent Specialization

Deterministic Role-Constrained Reasoning

🧠 Overview

v150.3 introduces role-based agent specialization, enabling structured multi-agent reasoning while preserving full determinism and proof integrity.

agent role
→ constrained reasoning space
→ deterministic decision
→ role-consistent decision set
⚙️ Core Features
🧩 Agent Roles

Defined deterministic roles:

control
validation
repair
adversarial
compression

Each role represents a restricted reasoning surface.

🧠 Role-Constrained Decisions
extended AgentDecision → RoleAgentDecision
decision must satisfy:
same role + same input → same decision

canonical_hash_identity(...)

mapped into canonical compression segments

stable segment_id
canonical segment_hash
deterministic compression ratio

ARCHIVAL  → 0.55
RELEASE   → 0.65
SESSION   → 0.80

ARCHIVAL → ARCHIVAL
RELEASE  → COLD
SESSION  → WARM
EPHEMERAL → HOT

EMPTY
COMPRESSED
NO_GAIN

All states enforce strict size/ratio invariants.

storage_integrity_hash

Derived from:

artifact hashes
segment hashes
size totals
compression ratio

Guaranteeing replay-safe storage planning.

NO_OP

consistency_score = effect_strength (if VALID)
otherwise = 0.0

mean(consistency_scores)

bounded [0.0, 1.0]
rounded to 12 decimals
replay-stable

title: 🚀 v149.1.1 — Hierarchical Memory Hardening
body:
🚀 v149.1.1 — Hierarchical Memory Hardening
Summary

Surgical hardening patch for v149.1 improving performance, invariant enforcement, and deterministic error-surface stability.

No changes to public API, memory model, or arbitration semantics.

🔧 What Changed
1. Linear-Time Memory Grouping

Replaced per-key filtering with deterministic grouped construction:

O(n²) → O(n) + sorted key iteration
Preserves deterministic ordering
Improves scalability for large memory sets
2. Fail-Fast Validation Before Sorting

All projection inputs are now validated before any sorting occurs.

Prevents:

AttributeError leakage
inconsistent error types

Guarantees:

invalid input → ValueError (deterministic)
3. Contributor Pairing Invariants

Strengthened validation in GlobalMemoryProjection:

Reject non-empty projections with zero contributors
Enforce consistent agent↔evidence pairing
Preserve deterministic contributor ordering
4. Projection Ordering Contract Locked

Added explicit invariant:

projections must be sorted by memory_key
Fails fast on violation
Prevents silent drift in future refactors
5. Deterministic Tie-Break Regression Test

Added:

test_global_same_recommendation_priority_tie_break

Locks behavior when:

multiple memory keys
same recommendation
different priority/confidence

Ensures selection follows:

aggregate_priority desc
aggregate_confidence desc
severity desc
memory_key asc
agent_id asc
stable_hash asc
🔒 Guarantees Preserved
Canonical JSON unchanged
Stable SHA-256 hashing unchanged
Replay identity unchanged
No randomness / async / wall-clock
Decoder untouched
Analysis-layer only

🚀 v149.1 — Hierarchical Memory / Shared Memory Arbitration
Summary

Introduces a deterministic, replay-safe hierarchical memory arbitration layer that promotes local agent memory into canonical global memory projections and determines when recursive governance is required.

This extends v149.0 multi-agent governance into structured memory formation:

local memory
→ global memory projection
→ recursive governance decision

All outputs are canonical, immutable, and replay-stable.

🧠 What This Adds
1. Local → Global Memory Promotion

Multiple agent-local memory states are:

grouped by memory_key
deterministically ordered
resolved into a single GlobalMemoryProjection

With explicit outcomes:

EMPTY
CONSENSUS_PROMOTED
PRIORITY_PROMOTED
CONFIDENCE_PROMOTED
RECURSIVE_ESCALATION
2. Recursive Governance Detection

After projection:

If conflicts remain structurally unresolved → no silent tie-breaking
System emits:
RECURSIVE_GOVERNANCE_REQUIRED

Otherwise:

GLOBAL_MEMORY_READY

This preserves:

conflict visibility instead of artificial determinism

3. Full Hierarchical Receipt Model

LocalMemoryState

✅ Finite Float Enforcement
Rejects NaN, inf, -inf for:
confidence
consensus_score

"NONE" is reserved
Cannot be used as a real agent_id

are now paired and preserved

→ No ambiguity in provenance

✅ Consensus Semantics Fixed
Unanimous agreement now yields:
consensus_score = 1.0

(Not averaged confidence)

✅ Recursive Governance Consistency
Enforces:
RECURSIVE_GOVERNANCE_REQUIRED ⇔ recursive_governance_required = True

No contradictory states allowed

✅ Receipt Self-Consistency

Prevents internally inconsistent but hash-valid receipts

aggregate_priority desc

title: v147.1.0 — Retro Trace Intake Bridge
body:
v147.1.0 — Retro Trace Intake Bridge
Motivation

Introduce a deterministic, fail-fast ingestion boundary that transforms heterogeneous retro trace inputs into a canonical, replay-safe QEC trace representation.

This module establishes a strict contract:

same input → same ordering → same canonical JSON → same stable hash → same bytes

It enforces:

schema correctness
canonical ordering
integer-cycle timing normalization
immutable tuple-based structures
stable SHA-256 integrity binding

The goal is to eliminate ambiguity at the ingestion layer and protect all downstream analysis, control, and governance systems from malformed or non-canonical inputs.

Description

Adds a new analysis-layer module:

src/qec/analysis/retro_trace_intake_bridge.py
Core API
build_retro_trace(...) — single deterministic entry point
Core Data Structure
RetroTraceReceipt (frozen dataclass)
canonical JSON export (to_dict, to_canonical_json, to_canonical_bytes)
stable SHA-256 hashing (stable_hash)
full fail-fast validation in __post_init__
Canonicalization & Determinism

The intake bridge enforces strict canonical structure:

Primitive-only payloads
Sorted key/value pairs stored as tuples
Tuple-only event sequences (no mutable containers)
Deterministic event ordering with stable tie-breaking
No silent coercion (fail-fast on invalid input)
Timing Normalization
All timing inputs normalized to integer cycle units
Enforced properties:
non-negative
monotonic non-decreasing
Canonical timing representation:
{"cycle": int}
Raw float or "cycles" fields are not permitted in canonical payloads
Integrity Binding
Stable SHA-256 computed over canonical JSON bytes
Hash field excluded from hash computation
Guarantees cross-environment reproducibility
Validation & Safety Guarantees
Rejects:
malformed schemas
duplicate keys (no silent dict() collapse)
non-canonical tuple ordering
missing timing fields
invalid or non-integer timing values
Verifies:
target_receipt hash integrity
canonical structure before hashing
replay-safe immutability
Metrics

Computes bounded, deterministic metrics (rounded to 12 decimal places):

trace_completeness
event_order_integrity
timing_observability
input_sparsity
replay_consistency

Stored as a sorted tuple of key/value pairs for canonical stability.

title: v146.1 — Action Capsule Integrity & Replay Certification Hardening
body:
v146.1 — Action Capsule Integrity & Replay Certification Hardening
🔧 Summary

Hardening pass over the v146.0 Execution Bridge to fully close all remaining integrity, determinism, and replay-certification gaps in proof-carrying action capsules.

This release enforces strict lineage binding, canonical serialization guarantees, replay identity correctness, and tamper-proof validation across all artifact layers.

🔐 Core Improvements
1. Lineage Binding — Cryptographically Closed

Enforced strict equality across all lineage layers:

descriptor lineage
== top-level capsule fields
== *_receipt_hash values
Eliminates all divergence paths
Binds descriptor payload directly to certified receipts
Prevents attacker-controlled hash substitution
2. Three-Layer Tamper Rejection

Validation now rejects any single-layer tampering, including:

descriptor payload mutation
top-level hash mutation
receipt hash mutation

All cases fail fast with ValueError, even after recomputation attempts.

3. Replay Identity — Versioned & Deterministic
Introduced version-aware replay identity validation
v146.0 capsules: backward-compatible acceptance
v146.1+: strict canonical replay identity enforcement

Replay identity is now:

transition_hash | refinement_hash | governance_hash
→ UTF-8
→ SHA-256
4. Canonical Serialization Guarantees
Enforced canonical JSON round-trip validation
Eliminated representation drift (e.g., 1 vs 1.0)
Added float normalization enforcement (pre-rounded inputs only)
5. Nested Hash Re-derivation Checks
All nested artifacts now validated via:
stored hash equality
recomputed canonical hash

No trust in stored values.

6. Mutation Safety
Input snapshot protection during build_action_capsule
Detection of in-place mutation during construction
Ensures build process is side-effect free
🧪 Testing

Expanded test suite to enforce strict invariants:

Three-layer lineage binding test
Single-layer tampering rejection (parametrized)
Replay identity recomputation checks
Canonical JSON round-trip stability
Descriptor ↔ capsule cross-field consistency
Float normalization enforcement
Deep schema validation
Input mutation detection
Result: 21 / 21 tests passed
🧱 Files Modified
File	Change
src/qec/analysis/action_capsule_kernel.py	Hardened validation logic, replay identity, canonical checks, lineage binding
tests/test_action_capsule_kernel.py	Added strict lineage + tamper tests, expanded invariant coverage
⚠️ Breaking / Behavioral Notes
v146.1 introduces strict lineage binding enforcement
v146.1 enforces canonical replay identity derivation
v146.0 capsules remain backward-compatible under version-gated validation
🧠 Architectural Position

v146.1 completes the Execution Bridge hardening phase.

The system now guarantees:

proof-carrying action representation
+ canonical serialization
+ deterministic replay identity
+ cryptographically bound lineage
+ tamper-proof validation

No execution semantics introduced.
No decoder modifications.
No nondeterminism introduced.

title: v145.3 — Policy Family Benchmarking Kernel (PFBK)
body:
🚀 v145.3 — Policy Family Benchmarking Kernel (PFBK)
Overview

This release introduces the Policy Family Benchmarking Kernel (PFBK) — a deterministic analysis-layer system for evaluating structured families of governance policies across bounded parameter sweeps.

It provides:

baseline policy
+ parameter sweep axes
→ deterministic policy family
→ governed closed-loop run per policy
→ pairwise benchmark relations
→ family-level summary
→ canonical benchmark receipt
🧠 Core Capability

Given:

SimulationConfig
a baseline GovernancePolicy
a bounded PolicyFamilySpec

PFBK:

generates a deterministic policy family via cartesian sweep
evaluates each policy via v145.1 governed closed-loop simulation
compares outcomes across all family members
classifies:
permissiveness
restrictiveness
convergence behavior
family-level variation
emits a fully canonical, replay-safe benchmark artifact
🔬 Key Features
1. Deterministic Policy Family Generation
sorted axes → cartesian product → baseline overrides → canonical policies
axes sorted by parameter name
overrides applied deterministically
policies sorted by stable_hash
strict family size enforcement
2. Quantized Axis Normalization (Critical Fix)

Numeric sweep values are now:

round12(validate_unit_interval(value))

before:

duplicate detection
storage
hashing

Impact

in-memory values == canonical export == stable hash

Prevents:

duplicate collapse after serialization
non-round-trippable payloads
hidden hash inconsistencies
3. Governed Evaluation Reuse

Each generated policy is evaluated via:

run_governed_closed_loop(config, policy)
no duplicated simulation logic
full reuse of v145.1 pipeline
strict linkage validation
4. Deterministic Pairwise Benchmarking

For all unordered pairs:

n policies → nC2 comparisons

Each comparison computes:

admissible delta
reject delta
convergence delta
relation classification:
equivalent
more_permissive
more_restrictive
mixed
5. Family-Level Classification

Family behavior classified as:

stable_family
mixed_family
high_variation_family

Based on deterministic variation metrics derived from pairwise deltas.

6. Canonical Benchmark Artifacts

All dataclasses:

immutable
self-validating
canonical JSON serialized
SHA-256 stable hashed

Ensuring:

same inputs → same receipt → same bytes

title: v145.1 — Governed Closed-Loop Simulation Kernel (GCLSK)

🚀 v145.1 — Governed Closed-Loop Simulation Kernel (GCLSK)
Overview

This release integrates governance evaluation into the deterministic closed-loop simulation pipeline, producing a fully policy-certified simulation system.

It extends:

simulate → observe → rank → select → refine

into:

simulate → observe → rank → select → refine → govern → record

All within a fully deterministic, replay-safe framework.

🧠 Core Capability

Given:

SimulationConfig (v144.5)
GovernancePolicy (v145.0)

GCLSK:

executes the full SCOL pipeline per cycle
evaluates governance on each transition
records governance verdicts without affecting loop progression
emits a canonical, self-validating simulation receipt
🔬 Key Features
1. Governance-Integrated Simulation Loop

Each cycle performs:

stress → state → mesh → transition → refinement → governance

Outputs are linked via:

mesh receipt
transition receipt
refinement receipt
governance receipt

All hash-validated and immutable.

2. Governance Invariant Enforcement (P1 Fix)

Enforced:

governance_admissible == (governance_verdict == "allow")

Impact

prevents inconsistent but hash-valid artifacts
restores true “self-validating receipt” guarantees
3. Policy Consistency Validation (P1 Fix)

Enforced:

governance_receipt.policy_hash == input policy_hash

Impact

prevents silent policy drift
guarantees audit/replay correctness
4. Correct Admissibility Aggregation (P2 Fix)

Replaced:

admissible_count = allow_count

with:

admissible_count = sum(record.governance_admissible)

Impact

summary derived from actual recorded data
robust against future invariant changes
5. Shared Canonical Helper Unification

Promoted CLSK helpers to public API:

round12
validate_sha256_hex
validate_unit_interval
ensure_stable_hash
derive_state_from_stress_point

Impact

single source of truth → no drift → stable hashing
6. Removed Private Helper Coupling

Replaced _derive_state_from_stress_point with public API usage.

Impact

removes fragile internal dependency
improves module stability under refactor
7. Canonical Governed Cycle Recording

Each GovernedCycleRecord now enforces:

strict enum validation
invariant correctness
hash linkage across all pipeline artifacts

title: v145.0 — Governed Orchestration Layer (GOL)
body:
🚀 v145.0 — Governed Orchestration Layer (GOL)
Overview

This release introduces the Governed Orchestration Layer (GOL) — the first deterministic policy gate for SCOL.

It provides:

TransitionPolicyReceipt + RefinementReceipt
→ deterministic governance checks
→ bounded orchestration verdict
→ canonical governance receipt

This marks the transition from:

deterministic simulation → governed orchestration
🧠 Core Capability

GOL evaluates whether a proposed transition is:

admissible
sufficiently confident
sufficiently converged
compliant with explicit policy

and emits a deterministic verdict:

allow | hold | reject
🔬 Key Features
1. Deterministic Governance Evaluation

Fixed evaluation order:

score → confidence → margin → tie_break → stability → convergence → refinement_class

Each produces a canonical GovernanceCheck.

2. Corrected Threshold Semantics (Critical Fix)

Replaced inverted bounds:

max_allowed_score   → min_required_score
max_allowed_margin  → min_required_margin

Now:

score ≥ min_required_score
margin ≥ min_required_margin

Impact

strong candidates are accepted
weak/ambiguous candidates are rejected
policy aligns with SCOL signal semantics
3. Explicit Verdict Model
allow  → all checks pass
hold   → soft failures only
reject → hard constraint violation

With deterministic reason codes:

score_too_low
margin_too_low
tie_break_disallowed
unstable_transition
low_convergence
no_improvement_disallowed
4. Strict Linkage Validation
RefinementReceipt.input_policy_hash
== TransitionPolicyReceipt.stable_hash

Impact

prevents cross-artifact corruption
enforces pipeline integrity boundary
5. Canonical Policy Model

GovernancePolicy now:

uses minimum thresholds (aligned with signal meaning)
rejects bool / invalid numeric inputs
remains fully canonical and hash-stable
6. Deterministic Check Artifacts

Each check records:

observed value
threshold
pass/fail
canonical message

All:

immutable
hash-valid
replay-safe

Overview

This release introduces the Closed-Loop Simulation Kernel (CLSK) — the first deterministic, end-to-end orchestration harness for the SCOL pipeline.

It executes the full pipeline:

stress → observe → rank → select → refine → repeat

in bounded, replay-safe simulation cycles.

Given a SimulationConfig, CLSK:

derives bounded state representations

bounded

v144.4 → bounded refinement
v144.5 → CLOSED LOOP

Each cycle:

contributes to a canonical simulation trace

decision_type != "tie_break"

with:

transition_classification ∈ {"stable_transition", "uncertain_transition"}

Impact

eliminates hash drift from input ordering
ensures replay stability across runs
4. Recurrence Signal Integration (P1 Functional Fix)

Mapped periodicity output into filter mesh:

otherwise → "aperiodic"

Impact

activates previously dormant scoring path
correctly integrates v144.0 → v144.2

decision_type
refinement_classification

enforces cross-module invariants
guarantees downstream compatibility

derived state

hash-linked

derived from canonical cycle data

title: v144.4 — Bounded Refinement Kernel (BRK)
body:
🚀 v144.4 — Bounded Refinement Kernel (BRK)
Overview

This release introduces the Bounded Refinement Kernel (BRK) — the final component completing the deterministic SCOL control loop.

It provides:

TransitionPolicyReceipt
→ bounded deterministic refinement
→ convergence trace
→ refined control vector
→ canonical refinement receipt

BRK is a deterministic, bounded translation of Newton-style refinement designed for:

strict replay safety
fixed iteration ceilings
canonical state evolution
zero divergence risk
🧠 Core Capability

Given a TransitionPolicyReceipt:

derives a deterministic initial control vector
applies bounded iterative updates
computes convergence metrics
emits a fully canonical refinement trace

No:

stochastic behavior
adaptive step sizing
floating-point instability
unbounded convergence
🔬 Key Features
1. Deterministic Initialization
ordering_signature → stable hash → bounded vector ∈ [0,1]
reproducible across runs
independent of runtime
no hidden entropy
2. Bounded Iterative Refinement
x_{t+1} = clamp01(x_t - α * (x_t - target))
fixed MAX_ITERATIONS
fixed STEP_SIZE
deterministic gradient
early stopping via threshold
3. Convergence Detection
delta = mean(|x_new - x_old|)
early termination when ≤ threshold
deterministic convergence trace
4. Deterministic Classification
converged
bounded
no_improvement

Derived purely from bounded metrics.

🔒 Hardening & Integrity (Final Pass)
Nested Decision Validation (Critical)

Before refinement:

TransitionDecision must self-validate via stable hash
decision_rank must be valid (int ≥ 1)
decision_type must be one of:
clear_winner
narrow_margin
tie_break
selected_score, decision_confidence validated via _validate_unit_interval
margin_to_next must be finite and within [0, 1]
Final Fix — Margin Bound Enforcement
margin_to_next ∈ [0,1]
rejects values > 1.0
rejects negative values
rejects non-finite values

Impact

no impossible upstream policy state can enter refinement
🧪 Test Coverage
New Hardening Coverage
tampered nested decision rejection
invalid decision_type
invalid decision_rank (0, negative)
invalid numeric fields (score/confidence bounds)
invalid margin_to_next (−0.1, 1.1, 2.0)
Existing Guarantees Maintained
deterministic replay (byte-identical)
bounded iteration ceiling
vector bounds enforcement
float stability (12-decimal rounding)
immutability of artifacts
hash self-validation
📦 Files Added
src/qec/analysis/bounded_refinement_kernel.py
tests/test_bounded_refinement_kernel.py
🔒 Determinism Guarantees (Revalidated)
same input
→ same ordering
→ same canonical JSON
→ same stable hash
→ same refinement trace
→ same final vector
→ same bytes

Specifically enforced:

no float precision leakage
no invalid nested input

title: v144.3 — Deterministic Transition Policy (DTP)
body:
🚀 v144.3 — Deterministic Transition Policy (DTP)
Overview

This release introduces the Deterministic Transition Policy (DTP) — the first canonical decision-selection layer in the SCOL arc.

It transforms ranked proposals into a single deterministic transition decision:

FilterMeshReceipt
→ canonical margin evaluation
→ deterministic selection
→ bounded confidence
→ canonical transition receipt

This replaces stochastic policy selection with a fully replay-safe, margin-based decision rule.

🧠 Core Capability

Given a FilterMeshReceipt:

selects one ordering deterministically
computes margin between top candidates
derives decision type
computes bounded confidence
emits canonical, self-validating receipt

No:

re-ranking
re-scoring
probabilistic behavior
🔬 Key Features
1. Deterministic Selection Rule
ordered_scores
→ (s1, s2)
→ margin = s1 - s2
→ decision_type
→ decision_confidence

Decision types:

clear_winner
narrow_margin
tie_break
2. Canonical Margin Computation (Critical Fix)
margin = round12(round12(s1) - round12(s2))

Includes:

rounding before subtraction
normalization of -0.0 → 0.0

Impact

margin reflects canonical ordering
eliminates float drift
guarantees cross-platform consistency
3. Canonical Confidence Derivation (Critical Fix)
confidence = round12(clamp01(first_score * (1 + margin)))

Where:

first_score = round12(s1)

Impact

decision = function(canonical data ONLY)
no hidden precision dependency
replay-safe after serialization/deserialization
prevents classification flips
4. Strict Receipt Integrity Validation

Added enforcement:

input_receipt_hash must be:
64-char SHA-256 hex
candidate_count must be strict int (no bool/float)
ordered_scores must be tuple[OrderingScore]

Dominant consistency:

dominant_ordering_signature == ordered_scores[0]
dominant_score == ordered_scores[0].total_score
5. Strict Numeric Validation

Replaced loose bounds with:

_validate_unit_interval(...)

Impact

rejects bool, NaN, inf
enforces numeric correctness across decision path
6. Immutable, Canonical Artifacts
no mutation after construction
canonical JSON serialization
SHA-256 stable hashing
self-validating receipts
🧪 Test Coverage
New Hardening Tests
canonical margin behavior
negative-zero normalization
confidence replay stability
invalid score types (bool / NaN / inf)
invalid input_receipt_hash
strict candidate_count typing
Existing Guarantees Maintained
deterministic replay (byte-identical)
stable hashing
canonical ordering
classification correctness
immutability enforcement

+30 / -9

tests/test_deterministic_transition_policy.py
+58 / -0
🔒 Determinism Guarantees (Revalidated)
same input
→ same ordering
→ same canonical JSON
→ same stable hash
→ same decision
→ same bytes

Specifically enforced:

no margin ambiguity
no hidden state
no invalid numeric acceptance

DTP:

title: v144.2 — State-Conditioned Filter Mesh (TSM)
body:
🚀 v144.2 — State-Conditioned Filter Mesh (TSM)
Overview

This release introduces the State-Conditioned Filter Mesh (TSM) — the first deterministic scoring layer in the SCOL arc.

It provides:

state
→ deterministic scoring of candidate orderings
→ canonical ranking
→ dominant proposal
→ replay-safe receipt

TSM is the QEC-compliant translation of a “Markov transition matrix”:

no probabilities
no sampling
no stochastic transitions
bounded scoring + deterministic argmax/ranking only
🧠 Core Capability

Given:

a bounded FilterMeshState
a finite set of FilterOrdering candidates

TSM computes:

per-ordering score components
weighted total score ∈ [0,1]
canonical ranking (stable tie-break)
dominant proposal + classification
canonical receipt with stable hash
🔬 Key Features
1. Deterministic Scoring (No Stochasticity)

Score model:

score(s, f) =
  w_inv  * invariant_alignment +
  w_hw   * hardware_alignment +
  w_rec  * recurrence_avoidance +
  w_stab * stability_alignment
fixed published weights (no learning)
bounded outputs ∈ [0,1]
clamp + normalize
fully deterministic
2. Canonical Ordering & Tie-Breaking
sort by:
1. rounded total_score (12 decimals)
2. ordering_signature
3. stable_hash

Impact

eliminates float drift across platforms
guarantees ordering == canonical export == hash identity
3. Injective Ordering Signatures (Critical Fix)

Replaced delimiter-based encoding with:

canonical JSON serialization

Impact

eliminates collisions (e.g. "a,b" vs ["a","b"])
ensures 1:1 mapping between structure and signature
preserves determinism and correctness
4. Strict Fail-Fast Validation

Added explicit validation:

state must be FilterMeshState
candidates must be valid FilterOrdering

Impact

prevents hidden AttributeError failures
enforces deterministic failure semantics
5. Canonical Receipt Validation Alignment
sorting uses _round12(total_score) in BOTH:
runtime ranking
receipt validation

Impact

runtime ordering == canonical JSON == stable hash

No divergence possible.

🧱 Data Model
FilterMeshState
invariant_class
geometry_class
spectral_regime
hardware_class
recurrence_class
bounded metrics ∈ [0,1]

input_filters (tuple)

stable_hash (self-validating)

component scores (bounded)
total_score ∈ [0,1]
rank
stable_hash
FilterMeshReceipt
state
ordered_scores (canonical tuple)
dominant_ordering_signature
dominant_score
classification:
stable_preference
weak_preference
conflicted
stable_hash

signature injectivity (no collisions)
invalid state rejection (fail-fast)
rounding-stable sorting behavior

v143.3 — Self-Determination Kernel
SPHAERA Phase 4 — Deterministic Transition Selection
🧠 Summary

Introduces the Self-Determination Kernel, enabling deterministic, constraint-driven transition selection from ensemble- and spectral-constrained system state.

This module defines a finite transition space, filters it using invariant, ensemble, and spectral constraints, and selects a transition deterministically using bounded scoring and stable tie-breaking.

All behavior remains:

advisory-only (no execution)
deterministic
replay-safe
canonical and hash-stable
⚙️ Implementation Overview
🔹 New Module

spectral-constrained state
→ transition space
→ constraint filtering
→ deterministic scoring
→ stable selection

TransitionOption (frozen)

Represents a candidate transition:

transition_id
description

SelfDeterminationReceipt (frozen)

🔐 Determinism & Replay Guarantees
no randomness
fixed scoring weights
deterministic ordering + lexicographic tie-break
stable float rounding (round(..., 12))
canonical JSON serialization
SHA-256 stable hashing
identical input → identical output
📊 Decision Model (Corrected)
🔹 Transition Prioritization (Fixed)

Previously degenerate (all transitions equal), now:

base_priority × per-transition weight
_TRANSITION_WEIGHTS introduces deterministic differentiation
base_priority computed once per evaluation
ensures scoring signal drives selection
🔹 Constraint Filtering

Transitions rejected if violating:

invariant preservation
ensemble consistency thresholds
spectral regime constraints
🔹 Deterministic Selection
1. sort by score (descending)
2. tie-break by transition_id (lexicographic)
3. select first admissible
🔹 Confidence (Corrected)
selection_confidence =
normalized score within admissible subset
excludes inadmissible transitions
fallback: 0.0 if none admissible
🧹 Critical Fixes (Pre-Merge Hardening)
🔴 Priority Degeneracy Fix (P1)

Resolved issue where all transitions received identical scores:

selection → lexicographic fallback ❌

Now:

selection → score-driven ✅
🔴 Confidence Semantics Fix (P1)

Confidence now computed using:

admissible transitions only

Improves interpretability and correctness.

🔴 Invariant Lineage Preservation (P1)

Always propagates:

invariant_receipt_stable_hash

even when optional invariant receipt is not passed.

Ensures:

same upstream state → identical stable_hash
🟡 Receipt Integrity Enforcement

Added validation:

selected_transition_id ∈ allowed_transitions
OR sentinel "no_admissible_transition"

Prevents construction of invalid receipts.

🟡 Efficiency Fix
base_priority computed once (not per loop)
avoids redundant recomputation

title: v142.4.1 — Structural Redundancy Cutoff Patch (Hardening)
body:
v142.4.1 — Structural Redundancy Cutoff Patch (Hardening)
IRIS (Invariant Runtime) — Deterministic Redundancy Refinement
🧠 Summary

This patch upgrades the v142.4 benchmark model from a convergence-derived estimate to a structurally justified redundancy measurement.

Redundancy is now derived from the observed execution trace, using deterministic cutoff detection based on:

convergence threshold
plateau detection
fixed-point stabilization

An explicit oscillation guard prevents false redundancy claims.

⚙️ Implementation Overview
🔹 Core Update
src/qec/analysis/cross_domain_convergence_benchmarks.py

Replaces prior redundancy estimation with:

trace
→ structural cutoff detection
→ effective iterations
→ structural redundancy ratio
🔬 Structural Cutoff Model

Cutoff is determined in strict order:

1. Convergence Threshold

First snapshot where:

convergence_metric ≥ CONVERGENCE_THRESHOLD
2. Plateau Detection (Corrected)

Now aligned with repository semantics:

3 consecutive snapshots
→ 2 adjacent deltas < STABILIZE_DELTA_THRESHOLD

Fixes prior off-by-one behavior that missed short plateaus.

3. Fixed-Point Detection

First repeated state_id with transition label:

{"stabilize", "converge"}
4. Fallback
cutoff_step = total_steps - 1
⚠️ Oscillation Guard (Critical)

If:

convergence_label == "oscillating"

Then:

cutoff_step = final step
structural_redundancy_ratio = 0.0

Prevents false redundancy claims in non-convergent regimes.

📊 Updated Metrics
New Fields
cutoff_step
structural_redundancy_ratio
Updated Fields (Backward-Compatible)
iterations_effective = cutoff_step + 1
redundancy_ratio = structural_redundancy_ratio
🔐 Determinism Guarantees
no randomness or time dependence
deterministic scan order
canonical JSON serialization
stable_hash unchanged for identical inputs
🧹 Hardening & Fixes
🔹 Plateau Semantics Fix
aligned with 3-snapshot definition
corrected short-trace behavior
🔹 Public Constant Usage

Replaced private imports:

_CONVERGENCE_THRESHOLD ❌
_STABILIZE_DELTA_THRESHOLD ❌

with stable, module-local constants:

CONVERGENCE_THRESHOLD
STABILIZE_DELTA_THRESHOLD

Improves encapsulation and future-proofing.

🔹 Test Helper Validation

Added explicit validation for convergence_label:

invalid labels now raise ValueError
prevents ambiguous KeyError failures
🔹 Regression Coverage

Added/updated tests for:

exact 3-snapshot plateau detection
convergence cutoff correctness
oscillation guard behavior
fallback path correctness
deterministic replay + hash stability

iterations_effective
redundancy_ratio
invariant_density
convergence_speedup
early_termination_rate
efficiency_gain
🧠 Redundancy Model (Updated)
🔹 Observed Execution Approximation (Fix)

Replaced previous forward-plan-based model:

min(iterations_total, allowed_next_steps) ❌

with convergence-derived estimation:

iterations_effective =
    int(iterations_total * (1.0 - convergence_pressure))
redundancy_ratio =
    1 - (iterations_effective / iterations_total)
🔹 Impact
removes forward-budget inflation
ties redundancy to observed convergence behavior
produces stable and monotonic estimates
🔢 Classification
efficiency_gain < 0.25 → low  
< 0.50 → moderate  
< 0.75 → high  
≥ 0.75 → extreme

benchmark_label

optimization_viable
confidence
rationale

execution_receipt.version == v142.0

convergence_receipt.version == v142.2  
wrapper_receipt.version == v142.3  

Prevents:

schema drift → silent misinterpretation ❌

title: v142.2 — Convergence Engine
body:
v142.2 — Convergence Engine
IRIS (Invariant Runtime) — Phase 3 (Convergence Classification & Early-Termination Advisory)
🧠 Summary

Introduces the third module in the v142.x — IRIS (Invariant Runtime) arc.

This release adds a deterministic convergence engine that consumes:

iterative execution traces (v142.0)
invariant diagnostics (v142.1)

and produces:

convergence classification
early-termination advisory signals
execution efficiency metrics

All outputs are bounded, deterministic, and replay-safe, with no mutation or execution side effects.

⚙️ Implementation Overview
🔹 Core Module
src/qec/analysis/convergence_engine.py (new)

Implements:

deterministic convergence scoring
strict classification logic (ordered rules)
advisory-only early termination decisions
efficiency and pressure metrics
canonical receipt generation with stable hashing
🔹 Convergence Pipeline
IterativeExecutionReceipt
+ InvariantDetectionReceipt
→ signal composition
→ convergence classification
→ termination advisory
→ efficiency metrics
→ canonical receipt
🔹 Frozen Dataclasses
ConvergenceSignal
ConvergenceDecision
ConvergenceReceipt

Each provides:

to_dict()
canonical JSON serialization
deterministic structure
stable SHA-256 hashing (stable_hash computed internally)
🔐 Determinism & Replay Guarantees
no randomness or time dependence
no mutation of upstream receipts
canonical JSON via shared helpers
stable_hash derived internally

Guarantee:

same inputs → same classification → same hash
📊 Signal Model (All Bounded [0,1])
mean_convergence
terminal_convergence
invariant_pressure
plateau_component
oscillation_component
convergence_pressure
efficiency_score
🧠 Convergence Model
convergence_pressure =
0.45 * terminal_convergence
+ 0.30 * mean_convergence
+ 0.15 * plateau_component
+ 0.10 * invariant_pressure
efficiency_score =
0.6 * convergence_pressure
+ 0.4 * (1 - oscillation_component)
🚨 Classification Rules (Strict Order)
oscillating   → oscillation_component ≥ 0.5
converged     → trace.converged == True
near_converged → terminal_convergence ≥ 0.90
progressing   → convergence_pressure ≥ 0.40
unconverged   → fallback
🔢 Rank Mapping
unconverged → 0
progressing → 1
near_converged → 2
converged → 3
oscillating → 4
⛔ Early-Termination Advisory
near_converged | converged
AND not oscillating
→ early_termination_advised = True

Advisory only — no execution.

📈 Termination Confidence
0.5 * terminal_convergence
+ 0.3 * mean_convergence
+ 0.2 * (1 - oscillation)

Oscillation reduces confidence.

🧾 Rationale Model

Strict, fixed strings:

unconverged_detected
progressing_detected
near_converged_detected
converged_detected
oscillating_detected
🧹 Hardening & Fixes
🔹 Canonical Serialization Test Fix (Critical)
removed tautological self-comparison
replaced with independent recomputation:
to_canonical_json() == canonical_json(to_dict())
now meaningfully validates deterministic serialization
🔹 Deterministic Validation Coverage
replay-safe serialization enforced
stable_hash consistency verified
classification paths fully covered
🔹 Clean Code Fix
removed unused variable assignments flagged by linting
🧪 Testing
Test File

title: v142.1 — Generalized Invariant Detector

v142.1 — Generalized Invariant Detector
IRIS (Invariant Runtime) — Phase 2 (Structural Invariant Detection)
🧠 Summary

Introduces the second module in the v142.x — IRIS (Invariant Runtime) arc.

This release adds a deterministic invariant detection layer that analyzes iterative execution traces (from v142.0) and identifies core structural patterns:

repeated states
fixed points
convergence plateaus
simple oscillations (A/B)

All outputs are bounded, deterministic, and replay-safe, producing canonical invariant diagnostics for downstream convergence optimization.

⚙️ Implementation Overview
🔹 Core Module
src/qec/analysis/generalized_invariant_detector.py (new)

Implements:

deterministic invariant scoring
strict invariant classification + ranking
ordered invariant pattern emission
canonical receipt generation with stable hashing
🔹 Invariant Detection Pipeline
IterativeExecutionReceipt
→ trace analysis
→ invariant scoring
→ dominant invariant selection
→ ordered pattern emission
→ canonical receipt
🔹 Frozen Dataclasses
InvariantSignal
InvariantDecision
InvariantPattern
InvariantDetectionReceipt

Each provides:

to_dict()
canonical JSON serialization
deterministic structure
stable SHA-256 hashing (stable_hash computed internally)
🔐 Determinism & Replay Guarantees
no randomness or time dependence
no mutation of input trace
strict canonical JSON serialization
shared hashing helpers used across analysis layer

Guarantee:

same trace → same invariants → same hash
📊 Invariant Signal Model (All Bounded [0,1])
repeated_state_score
fixed_point_score
plateau_score
oscillation_score
invariant_pressure
🧠 Detection Logic
🔹 Repeated State
1 - (unique_state_ids / total_steps)
🔹 Fixed Point
final_state == previous_state
AND transition ∈ {stabilize, converge}
🔹 Convergence Plateau
≥ 3 consecutive steps with Δ convergence < threshold
🔹 Oscillation (A/B)
alternating state pattern (A,B,A,B,...)
length ≥ 4
🚨 Dominant Invariant Selection

Highest score wins.

Tie-break order:

fixed_point > repeated_state > plateau > oscillation
🔢 Invariant Rank Mapping
none           → 0
fixed_point    → 1
repeated_state → 2
plateau        → 3
oscillation    → 4
🔁 Pattern Emission (Deterministic Order)
1. fixed_point
2. repeated_state (sorted)
3. plateau
4. oscillation
🧾 Rationale Model

Strict, fixed strings:

no_invariant
fixed_point_detected
repeated_state_detected
plateau_detected
oscillation_detected
🧹 Hardening & Fixes
🔹 Non-Finite Value Rejection (P1 Fix)
added math.isfinite check in _bounded
rejects NaN / Inf at validation time
prevents delayed failure during canonical serialization
🔹 Canonical Hashing Alignment
replaced local JSON/hash helpers
now uses shared canonical_json + sha256_hex
ensures cross-module consistency
🔹 Oscillation Key Correction
replaced hardcoded "A<->B"
now emits actual detected pair:
X<->Y
sorted deterministically
🔹 Validation Message Fix
corrected ambiguous "invalid invariant label"
now uses precise "invalid pattern_type"
🔹 Test Typing Consistency
added missing return type annotation to helper
aligns with repo test conventions
🧪 Testing

title: v142.0 — Iterative System Abstraction Layer

v142.0 — Iterative System Abstraction Layer
IRIS (Invariant Runtime) — Phase 1 (Deterministic Iterative Scaffold)
🧠 Summary

Introduces the first module in the v142.x — IRIS (Invariant Runtime) arc.

This release establishes a deterministic abstraction layer for iterative systems, providing canonical representations of:

state snapshots
transitions
execution traces
convergence behavior

All outputs are bounded, immutable, and replay-safe, forming the foundation for invariant detection and compute optimization.

⚙️ Implementation Overview
🔹 Core Module

Implements:

deterministic transition construction
convergence tracking and aggregation
canonical execution trace representation
replay-safe receipt generation with stable hashing
🔹 Iterative Abstraction Pipeline

→ transition construction
→ convergence aggregation
→ execution trace
→ canonical receipt
🔹 Frozen Dataclasses
IterativeStateSnapshot
IterativeTransition
IterativeExecutionTrace
IterativeExecutionReceipt

Each provides:

to_dict()
canonical JSON serialization
deterministic structure
stable SHA-256 hashing (stable_hash as computed attribute)
🔐 Determinism & Replay Guarantees
no randomness or time dependence
no mutation after construction
canonical JSON used for all serialization
stable_hash derived internally

Guarantee:

same input → same trace → same hash
📊 Core Model
State Snapshot
step_index + state_id + payload + convergence_metric → immutable state
Transition Model
state_t → state_t+1
→ delta_magnitude
→ transition_label

Labels:

converge | stabilize | advance | stall
Trace Aggregation
snapshots
→ transitions
→ mean_convergence
→ final_state
→ convergence flag
🧠 Convergence Model
mean_convergence =
average(snapshot.convergence_metric)
converged =
final convergence_metric ≥ threshold
🧹 Hardening & Fixes (Critical)
🔹 Deep Payload Immutability (P1 Fix)
introduced _deep_freeze for recursive sealing
nested dicts → _FrozenDict
lists → tuples
prevents all post-construction mutation

This closes:

nested mutation → hash drift ❌
🔹 Mutation Leak Fix (P1 Fix)
blocked in-place union (|=) via:
__ior__ = _immutable

Prevents:

silent mutation before exception ❌
🔹 stable_hash API Standardization
converted all dataclasses to:
stable_hash: str = field(init=False)
computed in __post_init__
aligns API across all IRIS/QEC modules
🔹 Threshold Constant Centralization

Replaced duplicated magic numbers with:

_CONVERGENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.999
_STABILIZE_DELTA_THRESHOLD = 0.01

Used consistently in:

transition labeling
convergence detection

title: v141.4 — Self-Healing Integration Bridge

v141.4 — Self-Healing Integration Bridge
Autonomous Recovery & Self-Healing — Phase 5 (Unified Directive Integration)
🧠 Summary

Introduces the final module in the v141.x — Autonomous Recovery + Self-Healing Runtime arc.

This release adds a deterministic integration bridge that composes anomaly detection, rollback planning, policy adaptation, and recovery validation into a single bounded, replay-safe self-healing directive.

The system now performs:

anomaly
+ rollback plan
+ policy adaptation
+ validation
→ unified recovery directive
→ bounded self-healing decision
→ canonical receipt
⚙️ Implementation Overview
🔹 Core Module
src/qec/analysis/self_healing_integration_bridge.py (new)

Implements:

deterministic signal composition across all recovery stages
validation-first directive classification
explicit rollback → directive mapping
coherence and integration pressure scoring
canonical receipt generation with stable hashing
🔹 Integration Pipeline
AutonomousAnomalyReceipt
+ RollbackPlanReceipt
+ PolicyAdaptationReceipt
+ RecoveryValidationReceipt
→ signal composition
→ integration_pressure + coherence scoring
→ directive classification
→ canonical receipt
🔹 Frozen Dataclasses
SelfHealingInputs
SelfHealingSignal
SelfHealingDecision
SelfHealingReceipt

Each provides:

to_dict()
canonical JSON serialization
deterministic structure
stable SHA-256 hashing (stable_hash internally derived, init=False)
🔐 Determinism & Replay Guarantees
fixed logic, no branching ambiguity
no randomness, time, or hidden state
canonical JSON shared between serialization and hashing
stable_hash derived internally only

Guarantee:

same input → same directive → same hash
📊 Signal Composition (All Bounded [0,1])
anomaly_score
rollback_strength
policy_bias
control_gain
validation_pressure
recovery_alignment
integration_pressure
coherence_score
🧠 Integration Model
integration_pressure =
0.35 * anomaly_score
+ 0.25 * rollback_strength
+ 0.20 * policy_bias
+ 0.20 * validation_pressure
coherence_score =
1 - (
|anomaly - policy_bias|
+ |rollback_strength - validation_pressure|
) / 2
🚨 Directive Classification

Validation-first decision model:

recovery_failed → abort
stable          → no_action
monitor         → observe
validate_recovery → apply_* (based on rollback plan)
Apply Mapping
soft_reset        → apply_soft
partial_rollback  → apply_partial
full_rollback     → apply_full
🔢 Directive Rank Mapping
no_action     → 0
observe       → 1
apply_soft    → 2
apply_partial → 3
apply_full    → 4
abort         → 5
🔁 Recovery Permission
recovery_permitted =
validation_receipt.decision.recovery_viable
📈 Integration Confidence
integration_confidence =
0.5 * validation_confidence
+ 0.5 * coherence_score
🧾 Rationale Model

Strict deterministic format:

<directive>::<anomaly>::<adaptation>::<validation>

Examples:

apply_partial::recover::constrain::validate_recovery
abort::critical::harden::recovery_failed
🧹 Hardening & Fixes
🔹 Explicit Mapping Enforcement (Critical Fix)
removed implicit fallback to apply_full
added explicit handling for full_rollback
invalid combinations now raise ValueError

This prevents:

validate_recovery + none → apply_full ❌
🔹 Regression Protection

Added test:

test_validate_recovery_rejects_none_action

Ensures:

invalid selected_action="none" is rejected
no future silent escalation regressions
🔹 Deterministic Contract Integrity
all directive mappings explicit
no default fallthrough paths
all invalid states fail fast

This release adds a deterministic recovery validation kernel that evaluates whether the proposed recovery posture (derived from anomaly detection and policy adaptation) is stabilizing, uncertain, or failing, producing a bounded, replay-safe validation receipt.

anomaly + policy adaptation
→ recovery alignment analysis
→ validation scoring
→ classification (stable → recovery_failed)
→ canonical receipt

anomaly_component
confidence_component
policy_bias_component
control_gain_component
validation_pressure
recovery_alignment
🧠 Scoring Model
validation_pressure =
0.45 * anomaly_component
+ 0.25 * policy_bias_component
+ 0.20 * control_gain_component
+ 0.10 * (1 - confidence_component)
recovery_alignment =
1 - |policy_bias - anomaly_score|
🚨 Classification Model
critical + poor alignment → recovery_failed

low pressure + not recovery_ready → stable
< 0.50 → monitor
< 0.75 → validate_recovery
else → recovery_failed
🔢 Validation Rank Mapping
stable             → 0
monitor            → 1
validate_recovery  → 2
recovery_failed    → 3
🔁 Recovery Viability
stable | monitor | validate_recovery → True
recovery_failed                     → False
🧾 Rationale Model (Final Fix Applied)

Rationale now follows a strict, uniform schema:

<validation_label>::<anomaly_label>::<adaptation_label>

Examples:

stable::nominal::hold
monitor::watch::tune
validate_recovery::recover::constrain
recovery_failed::critical::harden

✔ resolves prior inconsistent prefix issue
✔ guarantees parseable, contract-safe output

anomaly_score * (0.5 + 0.5 * anomaly_confidence)

Introduces the first module in the v141.x — Autonomous Recovery + Self-Healing Runtime arc.

This release adds a deterministic anomaly detection kernel that converts the global hardware consensus signal into a bounded anomaly classification, a recovery readiness signal, and a canonical replay-safe receipt.

The system now performs:

consensus control truth
→ anomaly signal decomposition
→ bounded anomaly scoring
→ classification (nominal → critical)
→ recovery readiness decision
→ canonical receipt
⚙️ Implementation Overview

Implements:

deterministic structure

🔐 Determinism & Replay Guarantees

canonical JSON shared between serialization + hashing
no randomness, time, or hidden state
deterministic rationale generation

Guarantee:

same input → same receipt → same hash
📊 Anomaly Signal Model (All Bounded [0,1])
pressure_component
instability_component
conflict_component
anomaly_score
anomaly_confidence
🧠 Scoring Model
anomaly_score =
0.45 * pressure
+ 0.35 * instability
+ 0.20 * conflict
anomaly_confidence =
0.5 * consensus_confidence
+ 0.5 * (1 - conflict_component)
🚨 Classification Model
emergency_align → critical (override)

score < 0.25 → nominal
< 0.50 → watch
< 0.75 → recover
else → critical
🔢 Escalation Mapping
nominal  → 0
watch    → 1
recover  → 2
critical → 3
🔁 Recovery Readiness
recover | critical → recovery_ready = True
else → False
🧾 Rationale Model (Deterministic)

Rationale is strictly derived from:

(anomaly_label, dominant_signal)

Pattern:

stable_consensus::<signal>
watch_consensus::<signal>
recovery_required::<signal>
critical_recovery_required::<signal>

No dynamic text or non-deterministic content.

🧹 Validation & Safety

Strict fail-fast validation:

correct input type (HardwareConsensusReceipt)
bounded metric enforcement
valid labels and escalation ranks
valid dominant_signal
malformed inputs raise ValueError

No silent correction.

🔐 Determinism & Consensus Guarantees
Fixed Signal Ordering

Votes are always constructed in strict order:

thermal → latency → timing → power
Deterministic Tie-Breaking

severity_rank
→ pressure
→ fixed signal order

canonical JSON shared between serialization and hashing
identical inputs always produce identical outputs

same input → same receipt → same hash
🧠 Consensus Model
Signal Votes

Each subsystem contributes:

pressure ∈ [0,1]
stability ∈ [0,1]
severity rank (0–3)
mapped action label
Severity Mapping
stable           → 0
bias_adjust      → 1
reduce_load      → 2
emergency_align  → 3
Consensus Metrics
consensus_pressure → mean(signal pressures)
consensus_stability → mean(signal stability)
conflict_count → distinct action labels - 1
Confidence Model
confidence =
1.0
- 0.5 * conflict_penalty
- 0.5 * pressure_spread

Where:

conflict_penalty = conflict_count / (len(votes) - 1) ✅ (dynamic fix applied)
pressure_spread = max(pressure) - min(pressure)
Final Action Resolution
any emergency → emergency_align
else if high pressure or reduce present → reduce_load
else if moderate pressure or bias present → bias_adjust
else → stable
🧾 Receipt Structure
fixed signal_votes tuple
deterministic decision object
control_mode = "hardware_consensus_advisory"
observatory_only = True
canonical serialization + stable hash
🧹 Hardening & Fixes
🔹 Removed Hidden Coupling
replaced hardcoded 3.0 denominator with:
max(len(votes) - 1, 1)

This ensures:

no dependency on fixed signal count

title: v140.3 — Power-Aware Control Modulation
body:
v140.3 — Power-Aware Control Modulation
Bounded Hardware Feedback Control — Phase 4 (Resource Pressure and Energy-Aware Control)
Summary

Introduces the fourth module in the v140.x — Bounded Feedback Hardware Control Mesh arc.

This release extends QEC from timing coordination into resource-aware control, integrating node-level power signals with thermal, latency, and timing context to produce bounded modulation recommendations and a canonical replay-safe receipt.

The system now models:

power signals
+ thermal pressure
+ latency instability
+ timing drift
→ load classification
→ modulation decision
→ energy-aware control policy
→ canonical receipt
Implementation Overview
Core Module
src/qec/analysis/power_aware_control_modulation.py (new)

Implements:

node-local power signal ingestion
deterministic power pressure scoring
bounded classification (stable | balance | reduce | critical)
modulation and efficiency outputs
canonical receipt generation with stable hashing
Power Control Pipeline
power telemetry
+ thermal receipt
+ latency receipt
+ timing receipt
→ normalization (power, delta, utilization, excess)
→ power pressure computation
→ classification
→ bounded modulation outputs
→ canonical receipt
Frozen Dataclasses
PowerNodeSignal
PowerControlInputs
PowerNodeDecision
PowerControlReceipt

Each provides:

to_dict()
canonical JSON serialization
deterministic structure
stable SHA-256 hashing
Determinism and Hashing Guarantees
stable hashes are internally derived
canonical JSON is used for replay-safe serialization
node ordering is deterministic
duplicate node_id values are rejected
advisory outputs are fully bounded and reproducible

Guarantee:

same input → same receipt → same hash
Input Validation

Strict fail-fast validation includes:

finite numeric values only
max_power_capacity_w > 0
utilization ∈ [0,1]
expected receipt types required
duplicate node IDs rejected

No silent correction.

Metrics (All Bounded [0,1])
power_pressure
load_balance_score
modulation_strength
efficiency_score
Classification Model
pressure < 0.25 → stable
< 0.50 → balance
< 0.75 → reduce
else → critical
Control Model
Pressure Inputs
normalized power draw
positive-only power delta
utilization
overload excess
thermal mesh pressure
latency mesh instability
timing mesh drift
Important Final Fixes
Negative delta no longer increases pressure

The model now uses positive-only delta contribution:

max(0.0, power_delta_w)

So:

rising power draw increases pressure
falling power draw is treated as recovery, not overload
Overload severity is now represented

Added normalized_excess to distinguish:

slightly over capacity
vs
massively over capacity

This restores overload severity in the pressure model and removes the previous dead zero-weight excess term.

Aggregation
deterministic node_id sorting
mesh_power_pressure = max(node power_pressure)
mesh_efficiency_score = min(node efficiency_score)
overload_count = count(reduce | critical)
control_mode = "power_advisory"
observatory_only = True
Hardening and Fixes
Final Modeling Corrections
removed absolute-delta behavior that misclassified recovery as rising pressure
activated overload excess as a real scoring factor
removed dead + 0.0 * power_excess term
rebalanced weights while keeping total weight normalized

This makes the power model more physically meaningful without sacrificing determinism.

rising_delta > falling_delta

title: v138.9.2 — Multi-Code Orchestration Benchmark

🚀 v138.9.2 — Multi-Code Orchestration Benchmark
Overview

Introduce a deterministic orchestration benchmark engine that evaluates whether multi-code execution strategies provide measurable benefit over single-code baselines.

This release completes the third stage of the Multi-Code Adaptive Runtime:

code space
→ selection
→ migration
→ orchestration benchmark
→ policy adaptation
→ runtime optimization

v138.9.0 — deterministic selection
v138.9.1 — deterministic migration planning

v138.9.2 answers the critical question:

Was switching (or orchestrating) actually worth it?

🧠 Architectural Role

→ admissibility filtering
→ bounded metric evaluation
→ deterministic scoring
→ baseline comparison
→ orchestration verdict
→ benchmark receipt

This module evaluates — it does not execute.

reasons

→ canonical receipt
📊 Benchmark Metrics

All computed via bounded deterministic arithmetic:

stability_gain     = logical_stability
loss_reduction     = 1 - projected_loss
hardware_gain      = hardware_alignment
efficiency_gain    = execution_efficiency
overhead_penalty   = 1 - migration_overhead
confidence_gain    = avg(selection_confidence, migration_confidence)

Utility:

weighted mean (explicit weights only)
🧮 Deterministic Ranking

Tie-break order:

1. higher orchestration_utility
2. higher logical_stability
3. lower projected_loss
4. higher hardware_alignment
5. lexicographically smaller candidate_id
🧱 Baseline Requirement

At least one candidate must satisfy:

source_family == target_family
AND orchestration_depth == 0

This defines the baseline reference path.

title: v138.9.1 — Ternary + Surface Migration Engine

Overview

Introduce a deterministic cross-code migration planner for heterogeneous QEC systems.

This release implements the second stage of the Multi-Code Adaptive Runtime:

code space

→ policy adaptation
→ runtime optimization

Following v138.9.0 (selector), this module provides a replay-safe, analysis-layer migration engine that deterministically plans transitions between:

surface-code runtime targets

No runtime mutation. No decoder interaction. Pure advisory planning.

🧠 Architectural Role

selected code
→ migration planning
→ compatibility assessment
→ deterministic translation plan
→ admissibility validation
→ migration receipt

This module converts a selection decision into a provable migration plan.
