title: v138.9.0 — Surface / qLDPC Selector
body:
v138.9.0 — Surface / qLDPC Selector
Overview

Introduce a deterministic multi-code advisory selector for heterogeneous QEC systems.

This release establishes the first stage of the Multi-Code Adaptive Runtime:

code space
→ selection
→ migration
→ policy adaptation
→ runtime optimization

v138.9.0 implements the selection layer only — a strict, replay-safe, analysis-layer module that deterministically chooses the optimal code candidate (e.g. surface vs qLDPC) based on bounded metrics and explicit policy constraints.

No decoder changes. No runtime switching. No mutation.

🧠 Architectural Role

This module sits directly after:

noise modeling → control synthesis → candidate evaluation

and produces:

candidate set
→ admissibility filtering
→ deterministic scoring
→ stable ranking
→ selected code (receipt)

This is a pure advisory system — it does not execute migration.

📦 Files Added
src/qec/analysis/multi_code_selector.py
tests/test_multi_code_selector.py
🧱 Core Components
Frozen Dataclasses
CodeCandidateProfile
Strict validation of:
code_id
code_family
bounded metrics ∈ [0,1]
finite numeric values only
CodeSelectionPolicy
Enforces:
strictly positive weights
finite aggregate weight
explicit admissibility thresholds
CodeSelectionScore
Per-candidate:
admissibility verdict
weighted score
metric snapshot
CodeSelectionReceipt
Canonical output containing:
selected code
deterministic ranking order
per-candidate scores
policy snapshot
replay identity
stable hash

All dataclasses are frozen and support:

to_dict()
to_canonical_json()
to_canonical_bytes()
stable_hash()
⚙️ Selection Pipeline
candidate profiles
→ strict validation
→ admissibility filtering
→ weighted scoring
→ deterministic ranking
→ selection receipt
Admissibility Rules

Candidates are rejected if:

metric is NaN / inf / non-numeric
metric ∉ [0,1]
thresholds not satisfied:
noise_fit
hardware_alignment
convergence_confidence
unknown code_family
invalid structure

Fail-fast on any invalid input.

📊 Scoring Model

Weighted mean over bounded metrics:

score =
w1 * logical_stability
+ w2 * latency_efficiency
+ w3 * overhead_efficiency
+ w4 * hardware_alignment
+ w5 * noise_fit
+ w6 * convergence_confidence

Constraints:

weights > 0
total weight finite and > 0
no hidden normalization
deterministic arithmetic only
🧮 Deterministic Ranking

Tie-break order (strict):

1. higher weighted score
2. higher logical_stability
3. higher hardware_alignment
4. lexicographic code_family
5. lexicographic code_id

All ordering is explicit and stable.

🔒 Determinism Guarantees

This release fully preserves QEC system invariants:

same input → same bytes
canonical JSON serialization (sorted, compact)
stable SHA-256 hashing
deterministic ordering + tie-breaking
no randomness
no async
no I/O
no wall-clock dependence
frozen dataclass immutability
fail-fast validation (no silent correction)
🧾 Receipt Model

The selector produces a proof-carrying receipt:

selected_code_id
selected_code_family
ranking_order
candidate_scores
policy_snapshot
replay_identity
stable_hash

Guarantees:

byte-identical across identical inputs
fully auditable downstream
safe for governance + replay pipelines
🧪 Testing
pytest -q tests/test_multi_code_selector.py

Result:

✅ 8 passed
Coverage Includes
correct selection behavior
deterministic tie-breaking
invalid metric rejection (NaN / inf / out-of-range)
invalid policy rejection
no-admissible-candidate failure
canonical JSON stability across input permutations
stable hash determinism
frozen dataclass immutability
🛠 Hardening & Review Fixes

All review feedback addressed (no functional changes required):

✅ dynamic derivation of _ALLOWED_CODE_FAMILIES in error messages (no hardcoding)
✅ removal of unreachable weighted_score is not None branch
✅ explicit invariant enforcement for admissible scores
✅ aggregate weight finite validation (prevents inf/inf → NaN)
✅ duplicate candidate detection (code_id / (family, id))
✅ receipt ambiguity prevention via deterministic candidate identity

Final state required no additional commits after verification.

🚫 Explicit Non-Scope

This release intentionally does NOT include:

code-state migration
live runtime switching
adaptive policy learning
orchestration benchmarking
cross-code execution bridges

These are implemented in subsequent releases:

v138.9.1 — Migration Engine
v138.9.2 — Orchestration Benchmark
v138.9.3 — Policy Kernel
v138.9.4 — Multi-Code Bridge
🧭 Strategic Position

This release establishes:

multi-code awareness
+ deterministic selection
+ proof-carrying decision layer

Which upgrades QEC from:

single-code execution
→ multi-code adaptive runtime (foundation)
🔚 Summary

v138.9.0 introduces a minimal, rigorous, and fully deterministic selection layer that:

formalizes code choice as a validated, scored, and provable decision
integrates cleanly with existing control + noise pipelines
preserves all replay-safe invariants
prepares the system for safe cross-code migration

Deterministic code selection. No guesses. No drift. Just proofs.

title: v138.8.4 — Noise-Tailoring Hardware Bridge (Validation + Control Alignment)
body:
v138.8.4 — Noise-Tailoring Hardware Bridge (Validation + Control Alignment)

Files Modified

src/qec/analysis/noise_tailoring_hardware_bridge.py
tests/test_noise_tailoring_hardware_bridge.py
🧠 What this release does

Introduces a deterministic validation bridge that compares noise-tailored control outputs against hardware-like measurements, quantifying how well mitigation strategies align with observed system behavior.

Pipeline:

projected noise (v138.8.2)
→ control synthesis (v138.8.3)
→ hardware comparison
→ error + agreement metrics
→ validation decision
⚙️ Core Functionality

For each scenario:

derives control-adjusted noise using derive_noise_control_signal(...)
computes post-mitigation means for:
node weights
edge weights
compares against hardware-provided values

Metrics:

node_error
edge_error
node_agreement = 1 / (1 + error)
edge_agreement = 1 / (1 + error)
📊 Aggregate Output

Returns deterministic, 12-decimal rounded metrics:

mean_node_error
mean_edge_error
mean_node_agreement
mean_edge_agreement
🚨 Validation Gate (New Behavior)

Configured thresholds are now enforced:

mean_node_error ≤ max_mean_node_error
mean_edge_error ≤ max_mean_edge_error

If violated:

→ ValueError (fail-fast)

👉 Prevents silent validation passes

🛠️ Hardening Improvements
🔥 1) Strict Weight Validation (Critical Fix)

All tailored weights are now validated before aggregation:

must be numeric (int/float, not bool)
must be finite (math.isfinite)
must be ≥ 0

Invalid values raise:

ValueError("Invalid weight: values must be finite non-negative numbers")

👉 Ensures no NaN / inf / negative propagation

⚙️ 2) Single Validation Gate
_mean_from_values is now the sole validator for weight contents
compare_tailored_to_hardware handles structure only

Result:

clean separation:
shape validation → value validation → aggregation
🔁 3) No Silent Fallbacks
no .get(..., default) patterns
all required data must be present and valid

Result:

invalid input → explicit failure
🧪 4) Expanded Failure Tests

Added targeted tests for:

NaN weight → ValueError
infinite weight → ValueError
negative weight → ValueError

All failure paths now explicitly covered.

🧪 Testing
pytest -q tests/test_noise_tailoring_hardware_bridge.py

13 passed

🔒 Invariants Guaranteed
deterministic execution (same input = same output)
no randomness or time-based logic
finite numeric outputs only
strict validation (no silent degradation)
stable ordering and aggregation
reproducible CI-safe metrics
🧠 Interpretation

This release completes the noise control validation loop:

v138.8.0 → generate
v138.8.1 → structure
v138.8.2 → constrain
v138.8.3 → control
v138.8.4 → validate (hardware alignment)

You now have:

noise → structured → admissible → mitigated → validated

title: v138.8.2 — Honest Noise Approximation Pack (Hardened)
body:
🚀 v138.8.2 — Honest Noise Approximation Pack (Deterministic Projection + Hardening)
🧠 What this release does

This release introduces a deterministic projection layer that converts raw, structured noise into a:

bounded
physically admissible
comparable
replay-safe

representation.

Up to this point:

v138.8.0 → generate noise (SU(3 regimes)
v138.8.1 → structure noise (cluster expansion)

Now:

v138.8.2 → constrain noise (honest projection)
🔧 Core Functionality

The module enforces a strict projection pipeline:

raw noise
→ clamp (bounds enforcement)
→ contract (stability scaling)
→ finalize (deterministic output)
Result:
All weights are finite
All values are within defined bounds
Outputs are comparable across experiments
Behavior is fully deterministic (same input = same bytes)
🎯 Why “Honest Noise” matters

Without this layer:

different noise configurations → incomparable results ❌

With this layer:

all noise → projected into the same admissible space ✅

This enables:

meaningful benchmarking
fair comparisons across configurations
stable downstream control logic (v138.8.3+)
🛠️ Hardening Improvements (Critical)

This release also includes a focused correctness and determinism hardening pass.

🔥 1. Collision-Safe Edge Handling
Problem (before):
edge keys encoded as "src->dst"
→ collisions possible with valid node IDs
→ silent overwrites ❌
Fix:
internal representation switched to tuple keys (src, dst)
string form only used at API output boundary
added explicit ambiguity rejection
Result:
no collisions
no silent corruption
provable mapping correctness
⚙️ 2. Deterministic Edge Handling + Ordering
centralized edge-key generation
centralized deterministic ordering
ordering computed once and reused
Result:
consistent iteration
stable aggregation
replay-safe outputs
🔁 3. Single-Pass Validation Model
Before:
validate → compute → revalidate ❌
Now:
validate → normalize → compute ✅
added _project_to_honest_noise_validated(...)
runner validates once, then executes cleanly
Result:
no redundant validation
no drift between validation paths
improved performance clarity
🧹 4. Removal of Unreachable Logic
Problem:
clamp → contract → normalization (never triggered) ❌
Fix:
removed dead _normalize_if_needed path
pipeline now reflects actual behavior
Final pipeline:
clamp → contract → finalize
Result:
no hidden branches

title: v137.21.7 — Manifest Policy Knob Fix
body:
v137.21.7 — Manifest Policy Knob Fix

Targeted patch release for the v137.21.x benchmark custody and poisoning-resistance line.

Restores correct policy-controlled manifest validation semantics.

Fixed

Resolved a correctness bug in BenchmarkVaultKernel.evaluate where manifest field requirements were effectively enforced unconditionally before policy flags were evaluated.

Previously, artifacts could be rejected even when:

require_manifest_hash=False
require_lineage_hash=False

This made policy knobs ineffective.

Behavior restored

Effective required manifest fields are now derived dynamically from active policy state.

Validation now enforces:

always:
- benchmark_id

conditional:
- manifest_hash
- lineage_hash

depending on policy configuration.

Test hardening

Expanded regression coverage to verify both rejection reasons and final decisions.

Examples:

assert allow_report.decision == "allow"
assert reject_report.decision == "reject"

This strengthens protection against future evaluation regressions.

Code quality improvements

Applied readability hardening to effective field construction logic and removed redundant ordering work.

Architecture status
surgical patch
deterministic
stable hashing unchanged
no schema expansion
decoder untouched
review-hardened

title: v137.14.4 — Information Geometry Consensus Kernel
body:
## v137.14.4 — Information Geometry Consensus Kernel

Introduces a new deterministic Layer-4 consensus aggregation kernel
that fuses the full information-geometry supervisory stack into a
single bounded consensus manifold.

This release aggregates signals from:

- Jensen–Shannon divergence
- Fisher–Rao geometry
- Bregman / f-divergence correspondence
- optimal transport geometry

into a unified supervisory consensus control surface.

### Added
- `src/qec/analysis/information_geometry_consensus_kernel.py`
- `tests/test_information_geometry_consensus_kernel.py`

### New deterministic dataclasses
- `InformationGeometryConsensusConfig`
- `InformationGeometryConsensusResult`
- `InformationGeometryConsensusReport`
- `InformationGeometryConsensusReceipt`

All artifacts are frozen, replay-safe, canonically exported,
and lineage-stable via SHA-256.

### Exported metrics
All bounded `[0,1]`, quantized to 12dp:

- `geometry_consensus_score`
- `geometry_dispersion_score`
- `manifold_agreement_score`
- `geometry_stability_score`
- `global_information_consensus_score`

### Consensus semantics hardening
This release includes a critical semantic hardening pass.

Consensus is now correctly computed from alignment space:

    alignment = 1 - divergence

This fixes a prior inversion where larger divergence values
incorrectly increased consensus.

Correct convention is now:

- `1.0` = strongest agreement
- `0.0` = strongest disagreement

### Hash lineage hardening
Added `as_hash_payload()` methods across:

- result
- report
- receipt

to centralize hash generation and reduce payload drift risk.

### Numeric hardening
`_quantized_decimal` now avoids unnecessary float round-trips
for non-float numeric inputs, improving precision fidelity.

### Testing
`13/13 passing`

Coverage includes:

- same input same bytes
- same input same hash
- repeated-run byte identity
- bounded metrics
- zero-divergence → consensus 1.0
- maximal-divergence → consensus 0.0
- mixed divergence → expected midpoint
- manual pipeline equivalence
- negative input validation
- receipt integrity
- canonical export stability
- ASCII summary presence

### Merge risk
Low

Pure additive Layer-4 supervisory module.
Decoder untouched.
Semantic hardening complete.
Tag safe.

title: v137.14.2 — Bregman / f-Divergence Correspondence Engine
body:
v137.14.2 — Bregman / f-Divergence Correspondence Engine

Adds a deterministic Layer-4 correspondence engine that unifies multiple divergence families into a single bounded replay-safe comparison space.

This extends the v137.14.x information geometry stack:

137.14.0 → Jensen–Shannon
137.14.1 → Fisher–Rao
137.14.2 → Bregman / f-divergence correspondence

src/qec/analysis/bregman_f_divergence_correspondence_engine.py
tests/test_bregman_f_divergence_correspondence_engine.py
Dataclasses

DivergenceCorrespondenceReceipt

All support:

stable SHA-256 lineage
replay-safe receipt continuity
Public APIs

Metrics

All bounded in [0,1]

kl_divergence_score
total_variation_score
bregman_alignment_score
divergence_family_consistency_score
global_divergence_correspondence_score

Convention:

0 = identical
1 = maximally separated
🔧 Hardening Pass (review-driven)

A review-driven hardening patch was applied after initial implementation.

This patch improves single-source metric consistency, verification robustness, and round-trip integrity validation.

1) Single source of truth hardening

The correspondence engine no longer recomputes divergence metrics inline.

Internal result computation now delegates directly to public primitives:

compute_kl_divergence
compute_total_variation_distance
compute_bregman_alignment

This removes drift risk between internal engine logic and public API behavior.

Future mathematical updates now automatically remain consistent.

2) Validation flow simplification

Removed redundant distribution revalidation inside the wrapper flow.

Validation now follows clean deterministic layering:

distribution build → validation
public primitive → shared validation
engine wrapper → no duplicate pass

This reduces duplicated work while preserving fail-fast guarantees.

3) Robust round-trip self-verification

Self-verification now recomputes report identity from the report object's canonical exported form, not from the original construction payload.

Previous:

hash(original_payload)

Now:

hash(report.to_dict() without report_hash)

This is a materially stronger integrity check because it validates:

construction → dataclass → canonical export → hash

instead of merely rehashing the initial payload.

This catches serialization drift and field-export mismatch.

Verification status

Post-hardening battery:

32 / 32 passing

Includes:

13 correspondence engine tests
19 regression tests from prior geometry layers

title: v137.14.1 — Fisher–Rao Geometry Approximation Layer
body:
v137.14.1 — Fisher–Rao Geometry Approximation Layer

Additive Layer-4 information-geometry module providing a deterministic geometric distance complement to the v137.14.0 Jensen–Shannon signal divergence kernel.

The layer approximates the Fisher–Rao manifold distance between two SignalDistribution artifacts using the spherical Bhattacharyya geodesic:

d = 2 · arccos( Σ √(pᵢ · qᵢ) )

clamped to [0, π] and normalized into bounded replay-safe metrics.

title: v137.12.0 — Neuromorphic Substrate Simulator
body:
v137.12.0 — Neuromorphic Substrate Simulator

This release introduces the deterministic Neuromorphic Substrate Simulator, the first release in the v137.12.x research arc.

This line is governed by:

simulation-first only
no biological claims without evidence receipts

This release remains strictly a deterministic signal-substrate simulation layer.

No biological, medical, or neuroscience claims are made.

Added
Neuromorphic Substrate Simulator

New module:

validate_substrate_input

compile_substrate_report
Deterministic simulation model

Implements deterministic spike-reset substrate evolution using quantized Decimal arithmetic.

State update rule:

next_signal = floor(previous_signal * decay_factor + input_signal[t])

Threshold event:

threshold_crossed = next_signal >= threshold

Reset rule:

if threshold_crossed:
    next_signal = 0

This provides replay-safe threshold-crossing behavior.

Deterministic guarantees

Added canonical artifact serialization:

canonical JSON export
canonical bytes export
stable SHA-256 report identity
deterministic node ordering
stable spike counts
replay-safe receipts

Hard law:

same input = same simulated state bytes
Review hardening pass

Addressed all Copilot review comments.

Key fixes
Direct-instance normalization hardening

Previously:

if isinstance(raw_input, SubstrateInput):
    return raw_input

This allowed direct dataclass instances to bypass canonicalization.

Now:

SubstrateInput → to_dict() → normalize

This enforces callable-leakage protection and canonical ID checks on all paths.

Float-format stability

SubstrateInput.to_dict() now serializes:

input_signal
decay_factor

as quantized Decimal strings.

This removes replay fragility caused by raw Python float formatting differences.

ID invariant hardening

Added explicit direct-instance validation for:

simulation_id
epoch_id

Whitespace-only and blank IDs now fail fast.

Schema normalization hardening

schema_version now uses strict non-empty normalization with strip semantics.

stable receipts

Meaningful compression ratio

compression_ratio is now computed against the original payload size:

len(canonical_merged_latent) / original_payload_size

This replaces the previous self-referential ratio that always collapsed to 1.0.

canonical_merged_latent = merge_latent_blocks(ordered)

block_hashes_match
and

This prevents callers from passing arbitrary merged bytes while still receiving a “valid” receipt.

This guarantees replay-safe integrity.

Test hardening

The brittle assertion:

compression_ratio == 1.0

was replaced with:

compression_ratio > 0.0

which correctly preserves stability without encoding a false invariant.

title: v137.10.3 — Claim Audit Kernel
body:
# v137.10.3 — Claim Audit Kernel

This release introduces a deterministic **Claim Audit Kernel** for Layer-4 scientific reasoning workflows.

The kernel performs bounded, replay-safe claim evaluation using explicit audit rules and canonical scientific artifacts.

Supported verdict space:

* `supported`
* `contradicted`
* `inconclusive`

---

## Added

### Claim Audit Kernel

New module:

* `src/qec/analysis/claim_audit.py`

Introduces frozen dataclasses for:

* `ClaimAuditInput`
* `ClaimAuditFinding`
* `ClaimAuditDecision`
* `ClaimAuditReceipt`

Implements the full public pipeline:

* `normalize_claim_audit_input`
* `validate_claim_audit_input`
* `run_claim_audit`
* `stable_claim_audit_hash`
* `build_claim_audit_receipt`
* `compile_claim_audit`

---

## Deterministic Audit Engine

Added a bounded deterministic rule engine that evaluates claims using explicit audit findings only.

Supported finding types include:

* `criterion_satisfied`
* `criterion_failed`
* `measurement_missing`
* `evidence_missing`
* `lineage_gap`
* `evidence_conflict`

Supported severities:

* `info`
* `warning`
* `error`

No probabilistic reasoning, inference heuristics, or stochastic scoring.

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

Added canonical audit serialization:

* canonical JSON export
* canonical bytes export
* stable SHA-256 audit identity
* deterministic finding ordering
* deterministic related ID ordering
* replay-safe receipt generation

All hashes derive strictly from canonical bytes.

No timestamps, UUIDs, salts, or randomness.

---

## Review Hardening Pass

Addressed all review comments from Codex / Copilot thread.

### Key Fixes

### Measurement Availability Semantics

Audit now correctly treats non-available measurement statuses as missing.

Examples now handled correctly:

* `"missing"`
* `"unavailable"`
* `"not_found"`
* `"absent"`
* status-bearing mappings

This prevents false `supported` verdicts when upstream explicitly reports unavailable measurements.

### Strict Schema Version Input

Explicit empty `schema_version` values now fail validation.

Previously, empty strings could silently default to the current version.

This now correctly raises fail-fast validation errors.

### JSON Round-Trip Integrity

Added explicit empty object / array distinction using sentinel marker logic.

Prevents `{}` from round-tripping as `[]`.

### Duplicate Finding Protection

Added fail-fast duplicate `finding_id` detection during receipt construction.

---

## Tests

Expanded regression coverage now includes:

* supported verdict path
* contradicted verdict path
* inconclusive verdict path
* ordering independence
* hash stability
* receipt stability
* lineage gap emission
* conflict emission
* unavailable measurement handling
* empty schema rejection
* duplicate finding rejection
* deterministic rationale generation


pytest -q tests/test_claim_audit.py
PASS (17 tests)`

---

## Architecture

Preserved invariants:

* `src/qec/decoder/` untouched
* Layer 4 only
* additive change only
* deterministic architecture preserved
* replay-safe audit artifacts guaranteed

claim → experiment → measurement → criterion → result → receipt

with stable serialization, strict validation, and replay-safe graph identity.

Strict fail-fast validation now enforces:

* supported node types only
* supported edge types only
* duplicate node rejection
* duplicate edge rejection
* empty node ID rejection
* empty edge endpoint rejection
* referential integrity checks
* non-empty `claim_id`
* non-empty `experiment_hash`

Receipt-only self loops remain valid.

## Review Hardening Pass

Addressed all automated review feedback from:

* Codex review
* Copilot
* PR thread comments

### Key Fixes

### Schema Version Integrity

Auto-generated `graph_id` now uses the **effective normalized schema version**.

This fixes:

* cross-version hash collisions
* graph/version drift
* replay ambiguity

### Empty Identity Rejection

Validation now explicitly rejects:

* empty `node_id`
* empty `from_node_id`
* empty `to_node_id`

### Validation Optimization

Replaced repeated linear node scans with a single `node_by_id` map for O(1) self-loop and integrity checks.

### Metadata Serialization

Removed redundant metadata re-canonicalization from `EvidenceNode.to_dict()`.

Internal metadata state is now treated as already canonicalized.

## Tests

Now rejects:

Post-review tamper-detection hardening

Following review, a critical verification weakness was identified and resolved.

Issue

verify_drift_provenance_roundtrip() previously compared:

exported["stable_hash"]
== ledger.stable_hash

Because exported is derived directly from:

ledger.to_dict()

this comparison was self-referential.

That meant:

forged stable_hash
→ still passes verification

which is clearly invalid for provenance integrity.

Hardening fix

3887174

the verification path was rebuilt to perform a real canonical hash recomputation.

Excludes:

stable_hash
replay_identity

matching the exact payload semantics used in:

Now performs:

_stable_hash_dict(rebuilt_payload)

and compares against:

ledger.stable_hash

This is now non-self-referential and genuinely validates integrity.

New regression test

Added:

test_provenance_fail_fast_tampered_stable_hash

This explicitly verifies that a forged ledger raises:

ValueError("malformed provenance bundle")

This materially strengthens the replay governance line.

v137.1.2 completes the replay-audit verification line for the unified physics simulation orchestrator and hardens cross-cycle integrity guarantees across composition, simulation, synchronization, and orchestration layers.

Core hardening changes
1) Symbolic trace validation now runs on every replay cycle

Previously, symbolic trace consistency was validated only on the first cycle.

This introduced a blind spot where later replay cycles could diverge without detection.

This release removes the:

if cycle_index == 0:

guard.

Now:

validate_symbolic_memory_trace(...)

is executed for every replay cycle.

Result

Any divergence in:

symbolic tokens
synchronization timestamp map
cross-cycle trace consistency

now fails immediately.

This is a major replay-audit correctness upgrade.

2) Real drift-detection test logic

The previous mismatch test only asserted:

altered != base

which did not exercise real production logic.

This has now been replaced with proper drift verification.

New test behavior

The test now:

A) verifies deterministic replay

All cycle hashes are collected and asserted:

compute_orchestrator_drift_score(...) == 0.0

This proves byte-identical replay across cycles.

B) injects real mismatch

A single-hash divergence is intentionally introduced.

The test now asserts:

compute_orchestrator_drift_score(...) > 0.0

This directly verifies production drift detection.

3) Artifact dataclass finalized

Previously resolved during rebase and confirmed stable.

UnifiedPhysicsOrchestratorArtifact now includes:

stable_hash
replay_identity

This aligns runtime construction with the dataclass schema.

Prevents runtime TypeError.

Duplicate _stable_hash_dict() definition removed.
Single canonical deterministic hashing helper retained.
This prevents drift in hashing semantics across future patches.

cycles in recovery-family routes,
mandatory escalation occurs.

ALTERNATE → EMERGENCY

This now fires at the exact threshold.

Hardening fixed the previous timeout off-by-one.

Critical Hardening Included

This release includes a significant supervisory hardening pass.

Fixed
1. Exact Hysteresis Threshold

Release now occurs at exactly N cycles.

2. Exact Timeout Threshold

Escalation now occurs at exactly M cycles.

3. Recovery Family Counter Reset

Counter now resets on:

RECOVERY ↔ ALTERNATE

route transitions.

This prevents false immediate escalation to EMERGENCY.

This was the most important control-law fix.

eliminating mismatch risk.

Inspired by the MOS Technology 6581 SID architecture, this layer provides replay-stable three-voice synthesis for QEC state, governance alerts, and drift-monitor overlays.

This extends the control chain to:

→ cognition
→ governance
→ drift history
→ SID sonification
→ stable spectral fingerprint

This is a major governance milestone.

title: v136.8.6 - Deterministic Promotion & Rollback Gate
body:
Program Theme: Governance, Promotion, and Safe Rollback Control

This release introduces the deterministic promotion and rollback governance layer for the QEC control stack.

This layer sits directly above the self-evolving loop and provides explicit rule-based decisioning for:

promotion
hold
rollback
invariant blocking
insufficient evidence gating

The control flow is now:

observe
→ route
→ evolve
→ judge
→ promote / rollback

This is the first release where evolved actions are formally governed before becoming canonical routing paths.

Scope
New modules

no decoder modifications

    action: str
    confidence: float
    improvement_score: float
    invariant_passed: bool
    cognition_confidence: float
    evidence_score: float
    snapshot_hash: str

Canonical immutable input artifact for gate evaluation.

This bundles the full cross-stack evidence surface.

GateDecision

class GateDecision:
    verdict: str
    promoted_action: str
    rollback_action: str
    rationale: str
    confidence: float

Canonical immutable governance decision.

Possible verdicts include:

PROMOTE
HOLD
ROLLBACK
BLOCKED_BY_INVARIANT
INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE
GateLedger
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class GateLedger:
    decisions: Tuple[GateDecision, ...]
    cumulative_promotions: int
    cumulative_rollbacks: int
    stable_hash: str

Immutable decision ledger with deterministic SHA-256 audit hash.

Core Functions Added

Builds deterministic governance input from:

evolution result
orchestration decision
cognition result
controller snapshot
Gate evaluation

evaluate_rollback_gate()

These implement deterministic thresholding and rollback logic.

Full governance cycle

This is the canonical governance entry point.

Combines:

candidate assembly
promotion evaluation
rollback logic
ledger append
stable export
Ledger + hashing
record_gate_decision()
compute_gate_hash()
validate_gate_ledger()

Provides stable replay-safe governance audit trail.

Export
export_gate_bundle()

Deterministic JSON-safe export artifact.

Governance Law
Promotion

Promotion occurs only when all required evidence thresholds are satisfied.

This includes:

invariant pass
sufficient improvement
strong confidence
strong cognition confidence
sufficient evidence score

This is fully deterministic.

Rollback

Rollback triggers deterministically under:

invariant failure
dual low-confidence regime
repeated weak-improvement streak
prior-decision-aware downgrade logic

This creates a robust control barrier.

title: v136.8.5 - Deterministic Self-Evolving QEC Loop
body:
This release introduces the deterministic self-evolving control loop for QEC routing and recovery.

The system now supports stable closed-loop progression:

State
→ Observe
→ Fingerprint
→ Route

→ Persist
→ Improve
→ Re-route

This is the first release in the v136 line where prior cycle actions and outcomes directly influence subsequent routing decisions.

The new action_taken field enables true cross-cycle memory propagation.

Subsequent cycles now derive their prior action from the previous cycle’s actual selected action.

This is the core self-evolution mechanism.

Immutable ordered ledger with canonical SHA-256 hash.

Primary deterministic loop entry point.

prior action
prior decision

across chained cycles.

Appends step to immutable ledger.

branch.

Structural invariant validation.

and validates within tolerance:

1e-12

This is a major correctness improvement.

build_next_action()

This is now the single canonical action engine.

Duplicate rule engines were removed.

This eliminates divergence risk.

Excellent hardening.

Family-Specific Adaptation Rules

title: v136.8.3 - QEC Audio Cognition Engine
body:
Program Theme: Machine Hearing + Deterministic Failure Recall

This release introduces the QEC Audio Cognition Engine, a deterministic machine-hearing subsystem for registry-based failure recognition and policy action routing.

This subsystem formalizes a closed-loop observability path:

QEC State
→ Render
→ Fingerprint
→ Registry Match
→ Action Hook

This is the first release in the v136 stack where QEC states become internally recognizable audio signatures.

Scope

no decoder modifications
deterministic by construction
Core Data Model
Frozen dataclasses

CognitionRegistryEntry
CognitionRegistry
CognitionCycleResult

All dataclasses are frozen and replay-safe.

Core Architecture
Triality signal law
Audio Signature =
Carrier(Code Family)
+ Modulation(Error Type)
+ Overlay(Topology State)

This is now implemented as a deterministic render engine.

Same input yields identical waveform.

Core Functions Added
Signal engine
render_qec_audio_signature()
compute_spectral_fingerprint()

These provide deterministic waveform synthesis and spectral feature extraction.

Registry engine
match_registry_signature()
register_cognition_entry()
recall_similar_failure_state()

Matching is performed via cosine similarity on spectral feature vectors:

centroid
rolloff
peak_bins
Cognition cycle
run_cognition_cycle()
export_cognition_bundle()

This formalizes render → fingerprint → match → action.

Determinism Hardening
Strict hash float invariant

Fixed:

digest / (2**32)

This guarantees:

0.0 <= x < 1.0

and prevents edge-case replay drift.

Stable spectral ordering

Peak bin selection now uses:

np.argsort(psd, kind="stable")

This guarantees deterministic tie resolution for identical PSD bins.

Critical for replay-safe DSP.

Confidence normalization

Negative cosine similarity values are now clamped:

confidence = max(0.0, similarity)

This guarantees exported confidence remains in:

[0.0, 1.0]

title: v136.7.1 - Surface Feedback Engine
body:
This release introduces the deterministic evidence and feedback accumulation layer for the v136 controller stack.

The Surface Feedback Engine formalizes how movement episodes, environmental events, and recovery signals are converted into immutable evidence ledgers that can be:

replay-audited
canonically hashed
merged deterministically
exported into shared topology space
consumed by future policy orchestrators

This is the canonical evidence substrate for the next controller / snapshot releases.

Scope
New modules
src/qec/ai/surface_feedback_engine.py
tests/test_surface_feedback_engine.py
Change profile
additive only
deterministic by construction
no decoder modifications
no new heavy dependencies

Fully compliant with architecture law.

Core Data Model
FeedbackEvent

Canonical immutable feedback event.

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class FeedbackEvent:
    source: str
    magnitude: float
    event_type: str
    timestamp_index: int
    confidence: float

Represents a single evidence contribution from:

movement sandbox
recovery engine
hazard surface
future controller policies
FeedbackLedger

Immutable evidence ledger.

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class FeedbackLedger:
    events: Tuple[FeedbackEvent, ...]
    cumulative_score: float
    stability_score: float
    hazard_pressure: float
    classification: str

This becomes the canonical evidence artifact for policy promotion and rollback logic.

Core Functions
record_feedback(event, ledger=None)

Appends a validated event into a new immutable ledger.

Deterministic update rules are applied to:

cumulative_score
stability_score
hazard_pressure

All scores remain bounded in:

[
0.0
,
 
1.0
]
[0.0, 1.0]
Hardening — Input Validation

Validation was substantially strengthened after review.

_validate_event() now enforces:

valid event_type
magnitude ∈ [0,1]
confidence ∈ [0,1]
timestamp_index ≥ 0

Invalid inputs now raise deterministic ValueError.

This is a major semantic correctness improvement.

It prevents:

inverted weights
amplified evidence deltas
invalid temporal ordering

Excellent fix.

score_feedback(ledger)

Returns normalized evidence score in:

[
0.0
,
 
1.0
]
[0.0, 1.0]

This becomes the scalar evidence metric for future:

promotion
rollback
controller ranking
snapshot scoring
apply_feedback_to_policy(policy, ledger)

Injects normalized evidence metrics into policy dictionaries.

This creates the first direct bridge from:

observed behavior → policy evidence

Very important systems step.

merge_feedback_ledgers(ledgers)

Deterministically merges multiple ledgers.

This was significantly hardened after review.

Major Determinism Fix — Merge Tie-Breaking

This was the most important hardening patch.

Previous sort key:

(timestamp_index, source, event_type)

could tie when events differed only in:

magnitude
confidence

This made merge ordering dependent on input sequence.

That violated deterministic merge invariants.

Final fix

Sort key extended to total ordering:

(
    timestamp_index,
    source,
    event_type,
    magnitude,
    confidence
)

This makes merges fully permutation-invariant.

This is the correct fix.

Excellent systems engineering.

classify_feedback_ledger(ledger)

Returns one of:

stable_feedback
hazard_pressure
collapse_recovery
drifting_signal
chaotic_feedback

This extends shared topology language into evidence space.

export_feedback_state_space(ledger)

Exports evidence ledgers into bridge-compatible state-space traces.

Directly compatible with:

build_movement_state_space()
validator layer
canonical hashing
replay audit

Critical for future controller orchestration.

episode_to_feedback_ledger(episode)

Direct integration from:

MovementEpisode

to evidence ledger.

This is the first direct bridge from:

controller movement → evidence memory

Very important architectural milestone.

PASS

Movement episodes now cleanly flow into evidence space.

PASS

→ build_movement_state_space()

Excellent interface compatibility.

validate_state_space_report() returns:

overall_passed = True

All invariants preserved.

bounds enforcement
invalid input rejection
deterministic scoring
bridge export

hash stability

Excellent coverage.

This specifically validates:

when primary keys collide.

This was exactly the right hardening test.

Very high signal.

100 / 100 identical runs

classification
export
hash

Fully compliant.

System progression now:

title: v136.6.4 - State-Space Invariant Validation + Replay Audit Layer
body:
This release introduces a dedicated state-space validation and replay audit layer for the v136 topology substrate.

Built on top of v136.6.3 — 2D State-Space Bridge Layer, this release formalizes:

title: v136.6.2 — Temporal Lineage Analysis of Audio Topology
body:
This release extends the Quantum Audio Logic Lab with a temporal topology lineage engine for ordered spectral state analysis.

Audio artifacts are now modeled as state-space trajectories with support for:

topology nodes
ordered transitions
basin switch detection
attractor clustering
recovery path detection
lineage classification
Files Changed
src/qec/audio/audio_topology_lineage.py
tests/test_audio_topology_lineage.py
New Dataclasses
AudioTopologyNode

Represents a single spectral state.

Fields:

filename
coherence_score
spectral_entropy
harmonic_density
cluster_tightness
mapping_2d
topology_label
AudioTopologyTransition

Represents an ordered state transition.

Fields:

from_node
to_node
coherence_delta
entropy_delta
distance_2d
psd_similarity
transition_label
AudioLineageReport

Represents the full temporal lineage.

Fields:

nodes
transitions
basin_switch_count
attractor_count
recovery_path_detected
lineage_label
Core Functions
build_audio_lineage(paths: List[str])

Builds a lineage report from real spectral outputs produced by:

src/qec/audio/quantum_audio_logic_lab.py

Consumes actual computed topology values.

Preserves exact input ordering.

build_lineage_from_nodes()

Builds a lineage report from precomputed topology nodes.

Delegates to the shared lineage assembly pipeline.

detect_basin_switches(report)

Detects basin switches using deterministic threshold rules.

Current thresholds:

distance_2d > 0.25
OR
|coherence_delta| > 0.05
OR
psd_similarity < 0.7

Module constants:

BASIN_SWITCH_DISTANCE_THRESHOLD
BASIN_SWITCH_COHERENCE_THRESHOLD
BASIN_SWITCH_PSD_THRESHOLD
detect_recovery_path(report)

Detects stable → collapse → recovery arcs.

Conditions:

coherence decreases then increases
cluster tightness decreases
final coherence > initial coherence
classify_lineage(report)

Returns one of:

stable_basin
collapse_recovery
drifting
multi_attractor
chaotic
Attractor Detection

Attractors are identified as stable proximity basins.

Threshold:

distance <= 0.05

Module constant:

ATTRACTOR_PROXIMITY_THRESHOLD
Transition Classification

Transition labels now incorporate entropy drift.

Rule:

|entropy_delta| > 0.3
→ chaotic

Module constant:

CHAOTIC_SWITCH_RATIO

Other labels remain based on coherence delta and topology distance.

Refactor / Hardening Changes
Shared Assembly Pipeline

Extracted:

_assemble_lineage()

title: v136.6.1 — Legacy Audio Sequence Regression & Spectral Continuity Engine
body:
This release extends the v136.6.0 Quantum Audio Logic Lab with a legacy regression engine, cross-era spectral continuity analysis, and decode-path hardening.

The system now compares newly uploaded spectral artifacts against the historic v74.2.0 sonic state chain, enabling longitudinal topology analysis across generations of QSOL audio research.

🎵 Core Release Theme

This release introduces:

legacy MP3 chain replay
transition drift regression
cross-generation topology comparison
frequency-aligned PSD similarity
dependency stack verification
lazy fallback decode optimization

This effectively turns the audio branch into a spectral state evolution framework.

🧠 Legacy Sequence Regression Engine

The historical 5-state sequence is now fully formalized:

Stable
→ Near-Boundary Instability
→ Structural Transition
→ Constraint Failure / Collapse
→ Recovery / Re-stabilization

Each adjacent transition is now deterministically analyzed.

Transition metrics

For every state pair:

centroid delta
entropy delta
harmonic density delta
subharmonic delta
coherence delta
cluster tightness drift
PSD cosine similarity

This is directly regressed against:

artifacts/sonic/sequence_analysis.json

allowing exact drift comparison against the historical baseline.

🌌 Cross-Generation Topology Continuity

New comparison layer:

legacy 5-state chain
vs
Quantum Coherence Threshold (v1 / v2)

The system now computes:

best legacy analog for v1
best legacy analog for v2
collapse similarity
recovery similarity
topology alignment score

Most importantly:

v2_resembles_recovery

now provides a direct answer to whether the newer artifact behaves like re-stabilization / recovery topology.

That’s honestly a beautiful research primitive.

📈 Spectral Similarity Upgrade

Major hardening applied to PSD comparison.

Previous implementation:

truncate to min length

This was index-based and could mismatch physical frequencies.

New law

PSD vectors are now aligned by shared frequency domain interpolation:

overlap frequency range
→ shared linspace grid
→ np.interp
→ cosine similarity

This means:

44.1 kHz vs 48 kHz comparisons are now physically meaningful
frequency bins correspond correctly
cross-SR similarity is robust

Excellent fix.

⚡ Decode Path Optimization

Audio loading path is now lazy.

Previous flow:

always read raw bytes
then try decode

Now:

try decode first
fallback to raw byte read only if needed

This significantly reduces unnecessary I/O for waveform-capable environments.

Very nice QoL + performance improvement.

🔍 Dependency Stack Hardening

Dependency detection has now been centralized.

New shared probe layer:

_probe_dependencies()

Used by:

dependency tuple reporting
health verification
decode path introspection

This removes duplicated logic and keeps stack reporting consistent.

Current supported stack:

NumPy
SciPy
SciPy Signal
optional soundfile
optional audioread
pseudo-spectrum fallback
🧪 Test Expansion

Test suite increased again:

Previous
84 tests
Current
86 tests

All passing.

New critical tests
cross-sample-rate PSD alignment
legacy chain ordering
dependency probe consistency
state count guard
transition topology ordering

Particularly strong addition:

same 440Hz tone
44100 vs 48000
similarity > 0.9

That’s a genuinely meaningful spectral correctness test.

🔒 Architectural Integrity

Decoder untouched

Confirmed:

src/qec/decoder/
NOT modified

Audio branch remains fully isolated.

Determinism preserved.

title: QEC v136.4.1 Test Baseline & Fast Regression Harness — Security Hardening
body:
QEC v136.4.1
Test Baseline & Fast Regression Harness — Security Hardening

This release hardens the new fast regression harness with improved security, robust summary parsing, and path-validation safeguards.

It is a quality-of-life infrastructure release focused on dramatically reducing redundant compute while preserving full regression rigor.

⚡ Tiered Test Strategy

Introduces three test modes:

Mode	Purpose
Fast	smoke + impacted tests
Smoke	ultra-fast invariant checks
Full	complete suite + baseline validation

This dramatically reduces routine validation cost.

Typical runtime improvement:

smoke: ~0.3s
fast: 1–5s
full: ~80s

Huge workflow improvement.

🧱 Frozen Baseline

Updated baseline registry:

9935 passed
11 skipped
0 failed

This becomes the canonical known-good release baseline.

Any future drop below baseline triggers regression failure.

Excellent release discipline.

🔐 Security Hardening

Major security blocker resolved.

Safe subprocess execution

Now explicitly uses:

shell=False

with static argv:

[sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", ...]

No user-controlled shell execution path remains.

Path escape protection

New validation layer:

_validate_test_path()

Prevents path traversal outside repo root.

Examples rejected:

../../etc/passwd

This is an excellent hardening improvement.

🧠 Parser Rewrite

Removed brittle stdout token parser entirely.

Previous risk:

implicit last_num
format sensitivity
unbound variable failure

Now replaced with strict regex parsing:

(\d+)\s+passed
(\d+)\s+failed
(\d+)\s+skipped
(\d+)\s+errors?

Malformed output safely returns zeros.

This is a much stronger design.

🧪 Harness Test Expansion

Expanded harness validation to:

65 total tests

including 18 new hardening tests

Coverage now includes:

regex parser correctness
malformed summary output
subprocess security audit
repo-root normalization
path traversal rejection
centralized root helper

This is excellent infrastructure test coverage.

🗺 Architectural Impact

This release significantly improves development velocity.

Instead of rerunning ~10k tests every patch, the system now supports:

fast impacted validation by default
full-suite validation on release

title: v136.2.0 Quantum Ecosystem / Emergence Sandbox
body:
Quantum Ecosystem / Emergence Sandbox

This release introduces the first deterministic ecosystem-scale emergence engine in the QEC simulation roadmap.

Built on top of the lattice life and civilization layers, the system now supports:

particles
resonant clusters
attractors
decay fields
tunnel fields
civilization interaction zones
ecosystem topology analysis

This is the first full world-scale emergence sandbox in the framework.

🌌 Seven-State Ecosystem Law

The ecosystem now operates over 7 deterministic states:

State	Meaning
0	vacuum
1	particle
2	resonant cluster
3	attractor
4	decay field
5	tunnel field
6	civilization interaction zone

All updates use deterministic Von Neumann 4-neighbor rules.

⚙️ Evolution Rules
Sparse spawning
0 → 1

vacuum with exactly one adjacent particle

Cluster formation
1 → 2

particle with two or more adjacent particles

Attractor emergence
2 → 3

cluster with sufficient cluster / attractor density

Decay overload
2/3 → 4

strict majority decay law under overload

This was significantly hardened in the finalization pass.

Tunnel formation
2/3 → 5

component lies on path connecting two or more attractors

Now precomputed in a single pass.

Civilization interaction
3/5 → 6

adjacent to existing interaction zones or injected civilization influence

Recovery
4 → 0

isolated decay fields recover to vacuum

🛠 Major Hardening Improvements

This release received a substantial correctness and performance pass.

🧠 Exact rule priority semantics

The evolution priority is now formally fixed and documented.

For state 2:

decay → tunnel → attractor

For state 3:

decay → tunnel → interaction

All tests now assert single exact outcomes.

No more ambiguous (3, 5) style assertions.

This is excellent deterministic rigor.

☣️ True decay-majority law

Decay semantics were significantly corrected.

New strict majority logic:

n_decay > (non_vacuum - n_decay)

This now properly reflects:

decay dominates local active ecosystem

Applied consistently to both clusters and attractors.

This is one of the most important semantic fixes in the release.

⚡ Single-pass tunnel precompute

Previous implementation used per-cell BFS, which was expensive.

Now replaced with:

_precompute_tunnel_eligible(grid)

This performs a single connected-component labeling pass per evolution step.

Benefits:

deterministic
much faster on dense grids
O(1) lookup during updates
mirrors v136.1.0 corridor design

Excellent engine-quality improvement.

🌉 Civilization Bridge

inject_civilization_influence() now fully validates both:

ecosystem grid
civilization grid

including:

rectangularity
non-empty rows
shape compatibility

This cleanly bridges v136.1.0 → v136.2.0.

Very nice architectural continuity.

📊 New Topology Metric

Added new frozen report field:

attractor_network_count

This measures the number of connected attractor systems.

This is a fantastic ecology / complexity metric.

Especially useful for future paper figures.

🧪 Test Suite Expanded — 43 Tests

Expanded from 28 → 43 tests

New coverage includes:

exact priority ordering
strict decay-majority law
steps=0 semantics
tunnel precompute determinism
attractor network topology
civ-grid validation
runtime decoder isolation

Total:

43 tests passing

Excellent release quality.

🔒 Architectural Integrity

frozen reports

deterministic evolution

title: v136.1.0 Lattice Civilization Engine
body:
Lattice Civilization Engine

This release introduces the first deterministic civilization-growth automaton for the QEC simulation stack.

Built on top of the qutrit life world substrate, the system now supports:

settlement emergence
infrastructure growth
energy hubs
transport corridors
connected city-region analysis
corridor network topology metrics

This is effectively the first deterministic lattice SimCity engine in the roadmap.

🏙 Core Civilization Law

The civilization lattice now operates over five deterministic states:

State	Meaning
0	empty terrain
1	settlement node
2	infrastructure
3	energy hub
4	transport corridor

All transitions use Von Neumann 4-neighbor connectivity.

⚙️ Evolution Rules
Settlement birth
0 → 1

if exactly 2 adjacent settlements or hubs

Infrastructure emergence
1 → 2

if local settlement density satisfies threshold

Energy hub formation
2 → 3

for sufficiently dense infrastructure clusters

Corridor formation
2 → 4

if the infrastructure component touches ≥ 2 hubs

This rule was significantly improved during hardening.

Collapse
1/2/3/4 → 0

if the cell becomes isolated

This preserves bounded city fragmentation dynamics.

🛠 Major Hardening Improvements

This merge-hardening pass substantially improved semantics and performance.

🚇 Corridor semantics fixed

Previously corridor promotion required direct local hub adjacency.

That was too strict.

Now corridor eligibility depends only on whether the infrastructure connected component touches at least two hubs.

This perfectly matches the documented law:

infrastructure links hubs through path topology

This is the most important behavior fix in the release.

⚡ Single-pass component precompute

Previous implementation performed BFS per cell.

Now replaced with:

_precompute_corridor_eligible()

This performs a single BFS pass per evolution step.

Benefits:

deterministic
faster on dense grids
no repeated component scans
identical semantics

Excellent performance hardening.

📊 Corridor topology metric added

Added new report field:

corridor_network_count

This now reports the number of connected transport corridor systems.

This is a fantastic civilization metric.

Examples now validated in tests:

zero networks
single network
disjoint dual networks

This will be extremely useful for future ecology / city papers.

🧠 Connectivity Analysis

The civilization analyzer now reports:

connected regions
entropy
growth rate

title: 🚀 QSOL-IMC QEC v136.0.0 - Qutrit Life Automata
body:
v136.0.0
Qutrit Life Automata

This release introduces the first deterministic three-state cellular automaton engine for QEC.

QEC now supports a replay-safe qutrit lattice world with:

three-state cell evolution
resonant excitation dynamics
oscillator detection
cluster analysis
entropy scoring
deterministic replay histories

This is the first full emergent world simulation layer in the roadmap.

🌌 Core System — Three-State Qutrit Automata

Each lattice cell now evolves in one of three states:

State	Meaning
0	vacuum / dead
1	active / alive
2	resonant / excited

This is not binary Life.

The qutrit state law enables:

excitation
overload decay
de-excitation cycles
stable oscillators
emergent resonant clusters

This is the scientific core of the release.

⚙️ Evolution Law

The automaton uses deterministic Moore-neighborhood update rules.

Birth
0 → 1

if exactly 3 active / resonant neighbors

Excitation
1 → 2

if local energy threshold is exceeded

Death
1 → 0

for underpopulation or overcrowding

Resonant Overload
2 → 0

for local resonance collapse

Stable De-excitation
2 → 1

for surviving resonant clusters

This produces extremely interesting oscillator behavior.

🧠 Cluster & Stability Analysis

Added:

analyze_qutrit_life()

This returns frozen QutritLifeReport with:

grid shape
steps evolved
state counts
resonant cluster count
detected period
stability label
entropy score

Stability classes:

fixed
oscillatory
emergent
chaotic

This is where the system becomes genuinely research-grade.

📈 Performance + API Hardening

The finalization pass made several important improvements.

Single validation evolution loop

Grid validation now occurs once at entry.

Inner evolution uses:

_step_grid_unchecked()

This removes repeated validation overhead for long runs.

Excellent performance cleanup.

O(n) entropy variance

Variance calculation was reduced from O(n²) → O(n)

Mean entropy is now computed once and reused.

Very important for larger replay histories.

Strict public API validation

Public boundaries now enforce:

positive grid dimensions
non-empty histories
rectangular grids
consistent shapes
valid qutrit state values

This is excellent paper-grade rigor.

🧪 Test Suite Expanded — 35 Tests

Expanded from 25 → 35 tests

New hardening coverage includes:

invalid dimension rejection
ragged history rejection
mixed-shape rejection
oscillator period enforcement
replay determinism
decoder import isolation
API boundary validation

Total:

35 tests passing

This is a very strong release-quality test surface.

🔒 Architectural Integrity

Preserved:

tuple-only grids
frozen reports
bounded state space
replay-safe history
deterministic evolution
decoder untouched

All invariants fully hold.

title: v135.5.0 Mission Control Sandbox + Space Anomalies
body:
Mission Control Sandbox + Space Anomalies

This release introduces the first playable deterministic mission sandbox for the QEC universe engine.

QEC now supports reusable mission scenarios enriched with anomaly fields, replay-safe scoring, deterministic traces, and strict anomaly interaction laws.

This is the first true space simulation gameplay layer in the v135 arc.

🌌 New Capability — Mission Sandbox Engine

Added:

mission_control_sandbox.py
deterministic scenario execution
replay-safe mission traces
anomaly-aware route scoring
mission-grade sandbox reports

This layer combines:

constrained flight law
objective field routing
anomaly effects
mission trace generation

into a unified simulation framework.

🪐 Space Anomaly Law

The sandbox now supports deterministic anomaly types:

Type	Effect
Black Hole	lethal / slingshot bonus
Wormhole	deterministic teleport bonus
Radiation Storm	fuel + stability penalties
Decay Field	propulsion damping
Resonance Zone	reward amplification

This is a major gameplay and systems upgrade.

⚫ Black Hole Physics Hardening

This was the most important finalization fix.

Previous behavior

Only final craft position was checked.

New behavior

The full trajectory_history is now evaluated.

If any route point enters lethal radius, mission fails.

This means:

fly-throughs are lethal
slingshot near misses survive
endpoint-only loopholes are gone

This makes black holes behave like real mission hazards.

Excellent sandbox correctness improvement.

🛰 Deterministic Failure Selection

When all candidate routes are lethal, the sandbox now still applies full deterministic tie-break law.

Order:

lower propulsion sum
lexicographic schedule

This replaces previous “first schedule wins” behavior.

Very important for replay stability.

🎯 Unified Scenario Score

Final scoring law:

scenario_score =
    objective_score
    + gravity_assist_bonus
    - anomaly_penalty
    + fuel_efficiency_score
    + anomaly_bonus

Documentation now exactly matches runtime behavior.

This is the correct final sandbox scoring model.

🧠 Objective Evaluation Refactor

A very strong architectural cleanup.

Previous behavior

Objective evaluation was performed twice per schedule.

New behavior

Exactly one evaluation per candidate

The single evaluation now drives:

reward
required completion
completed objective IDs
mission trace data

This improves both performance and semantic consistency.

Excellent refactor.

🔒 Private Coupling Removed

Removed dependency on private objective helper:

_check_objective_completed

Now uses only public objective evaluation API.

This is a major maintainability win.

Very important for future v136 worldbuilding layers.

🧪 Test Suite Expanded — 39 Tests

The sandbox suite now includes:

lethal black hole crossing
black hole slingshot bonus
wormhole teleport
radiation penalty
decay damping
resonance amplification
longer-route reward victory
all-lethal tie-break
100 replay determinism
no private symbol regression

decoder isolation

title: v135.3.0 Constrained Flight & Mandatory Gravity Assist
body:
v135.3.0
Constrained Flight & Mandatory Gravity Assist

This release introduces the first law-bound mission simulation layer for the universe navigation stack.

QEC now moves from deterministic route selection into mission execution under strict onboard constraints, transforming the system into a replay-safe flight computer with explicit failure semantics.

This is a major systems milestone.

🌌 New Capability — Constrained Flight Engine

Added a new mission-layer simulator:

constrained_flight.py
search_constrained_return_policy()
ConstrainedFlightReport

Mission success now requires all four conditions:

depart from base
perform a valid gravity assist
return to base
remain within resource budgets

This creates a strict deterministic mission law rather than best-effort routing.

🛰 Mandatory Gravity Assist Law

A direct return is now explicitly invalid by design.

Even if the craft physically returns to base, the mission fails unless a real gravity assist maneuver occurred.

Failure reason is now preserved exactly:

gravity_assist_required

This semantic tightening is a major correctness improvement.

The finalization pass removed prior fallback overrides such as:

no_candidate_succeeds

so failure reports now remain scientifically meaningful.

⛽ Explicit Resource Budgets

Mission execution now operates under ferrite-core-era constraints:

Resource	Budget
RAM	1 MB
CPU	7 MHz modeled cycle budget
Fuel	0.5 initial tank

Budget laws:

Resource	Formula
Fuel	idle=0.0, thrust=0.02, warp=0.08
CPU	steps * 5000 + 2000 + candidates * 2000
Memory	trajectory_len * 16 + schedule_len * 4 + 256

Fuel is now sourced from a single authoritative mapping table, eliminating drift between constants and runtime logic.

This was an excellent hardening fix.

🔒 Pre-Evolution Failure Semantics Fixed

For failure cases that occur before route evolution:

fuel failure
CPU failure
memory failure

steps_taken now correctly reports:

0

instead of requested step count.

This keeps report semantics consistent and replay-safe.

🧪 Test Suite Expanded — 32 Mission Tests

The constrained-flight suite now includes:

frozen dataclass immutability
gravity-assist detection
direct-return law
exact failure reason enforcement
out-of-fuel
CPU budget failure
end-to-end memory budget failure
best valid mission selection
100x replay determinism
decoder isolation
phase-boundary regression

32 tests

9623 passed, 0 failed

🛠 Discovery Engine Hardening

Included opportunistic fix in:

src/qec/discovery/discovery_engine.py

Phase-boundary unwrap logic now fails explicitly with:

KeyError

if "phase_boundaries" is missing.

This is much better than silently masking schema drift.

Excellent repository hygiene improvement.

🧱 Architectural Integrity

Preserved:

deterministic execution
replay-safe mission traces
strict failure semantics
bounded state

title: v135.2.0 Autopilot & Route Policy Search
body:
Autopilot & Route Policy Search

This release introduces the first deterministic autopilot route-selection layer for the universe navigation stack.

QEC now moves beyond route simulation and post-flight analysis into:

candidate schedule evaluation

🛰 New Capability — Deterministic Autopilot Search

search_best_route_policy()
AutopilotPolicyReport

The autopilot evaluates candidate propulsion schedules against a universe snapshot and deterministically selects the best route.

Core scoring law:

score =
    net_distance
    + stability_bonus
    + slingshot_bonus
    - energy_penalty

Scoring weights:

Component	Value
stable	+5.0
slingshot	+8.0
oscillatory	-4.0
divergent	-10.0

Energy penalty remains explicit and deterministic.

This keeps the system fully replay-safe and architecture-law compliant.

🎯 Deterministic Tie-Break Law

Tie-break behavior has now been fully hardened.

Order of resolution:

highest total score
lowest propulsion sum
lexicographic schedule order

This guarantees:

deterministic winner selection
order-independent candidate evaluation
byte-identical replay outcomes

A key step toward flight-computer semantics.

🧪 Test Suite Hardening

title: v134.7.0— Adaptive Policy Search & Meta-Control
body:
This release introduces the first deterministic meta-control and adaptive policy search layer for the QEC simulation framework.

v134.7.0 extends the v134 line from comparative control benchmarking into self-improving supervisory policy design.

This is the first release where the simulator can deterministically select and refine better control policies.

Overview

The purpose of this release is to provide deterministic search over supervisory control policies.

This includes:

best-policy selection
deterministic tie-breaking
refinement factor generation
replay-safe search output
policy improvement tracking

This is the first meta-adaptive control release.

New Search Module
Added

src/qec/sims/adaptive_policy_search.py

Introduces deterministic policy search and refinement.

Core Artifact
AdaptivePolicySearchResult

Frozen dataclass representing the result of one search iteration.

Fields include:

best_policy_name
best_score
candidate_damping_factor
search_iteration
improvement_delta

This is the canonical meta-control artifact.

Deterministic Search Logic

The search layer selects the best policy from benchmark results using:

score descending
name ascending

This guarantees deterministic tie-breaking.

Refinement Mapping

The selected policy is refined using exact deterministic mappings:

aggressive_damping -> × 0.9
nominal -> × 0.95
recovery_first -> × 0.8
unknown -> × 1.0

This generates the next candidate damping factor.

Search Metrics

The module tracks:

best policy
score gap vs second best
improvement delta
search iteration

This makes it immediately useful for iterative control experiments.

Finalization Pass (Integrated)

This release includes a repository consistency hardening pass.

SPDX header alignment

Added standard repository header:

# SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

This now matches the convention used across src/qec/sims.

title: v134.6.0 — Policy Sweep & Control Strategy Benchmarking
body:
This release introduces the first deterministic policy benchmarking framework for supervisory control strategies.

v134.6.0 extends the v134 simulation stack from closed-loop control into comparative policy science.

This is the first release to support replay-safe benchmarking and scoring of multiple control policies.

Overview

The purpose of this release is to benchmark supervisory control laws against shared lattice dynamics.

This includes:

multi-policy benchmarking
deterministic scoring
oscillation-aware penalties
stability timing metrics
replay-safe ranking

This is the first comparative control research layer in the QEC simulation platform.

New Benchmarking Module
Added

src/qec/sims/control_policy_benchmark.py

Introduces deterministic policy benchmarking and summary rendering.

Core Artifact
PolicyBenchmarkResult

Frozen dataclass representing a benchmark result.

Fields include:

policy_name
mean_final_amplitude
steps_to_first_stability
recovery_count
oscillation_count
score

This is the canonical comparative benchmarking artifact.

Built-in Policies

Three deterministic policies are benchmarked by default.

nominal

Baseline controller behavior.

aggressive_damping

Higher damping under oscillatory / divergent conditions.

recovery_first

Biases toward recovery-first intervention.

Scoring Formula

Deterministic composite score:

score =
stability_ratio
- 0.5 * recovery_ratio
- 0.25 * oscillation_ratio

Higher scores indicate better control performance.

Major Hardening Pass (Integrated)

This release includes a significant semantics and test-quality hardening pass.

Live oscillation detection

Previously, oscillation_count was effectively dormant.

This has now been fixed.

The benchmark runner now tracks deterministic discrete-state fingerprints using:

seen_states: dict[Tuple[int, ...], int]

Repeated fingerprints now correctly classify as:

oscillatory

This makes oscillation penalties fully active.

This logic now mirrors the attractor semantics from v134.4.0.

Stability field rename

Renamed:

mean_steps_to_stability

to:

steps_to_first_stability

This now correctly reflects the actual deterministic semantics.

This is a substantial clarity improvement.

Stability timing hardening

First stability is now only recorded under a genuine fixed-point condition.

This prevents transient “stable-looking” states from being incorrectly scored as stable.

This materially improves policy discrimination.

Test tightening

Finite-score validation now uses:

math.isfinite(score)

This is much stronger than the prior NaN-only check.

Dead code cleanup

Removed unused imports from both:

source module
test module

No dead imports remain.

Validation

Full suite
9430 tests passing

Excellent integration signal.

title: v134.5.0 — Supervisory Control Feedback Loop
body:
v134.5.0 — Supervisory Control Feedback Loop

This release introduces the first deterministic adaptive control layer for the QEC simulation framework.

v134.5.0 extends the v134 simulation stack from passive diagnostics into closed-loop supervisory control.

This is the first release where the simulator actively responds to its own dynamics.

Overview

The purpose of this release is to create a deterministic supervisory feedback controller driven by propagation stability analysis.

This includes:

control decision synthesis
deterministic control law mapping
replay-safe amplitude damping
recovery signaling
closed-loop pipeline integration

This is the first true adaptive-control release in the v134 line.

New Supervisory Feedback Module
Added

src/qec/sims/supervisory_feedback_controller.py

Introduces deterministic supervisory control logic.

Core Artifact
SupervisoryControlDecision

Frozen dataclass representing a control decision.

Fields include:

input_label
action
damping_factor
recovery_required
next_supervisory_state

This is the canonical control decision artifact.

Deterministic Control Law

The supervisory mapping is now:

Stability label	Action	Damping	Recovery	Next state
fixed_point	maintain	1.0	No	nominal
stable	observe	1.0	No	nominal
oscillatory	damp	0.5	No	damping
divergent	recover	0.1	Yes	recovering

This is deterministic and replay-safe.

Public APIs
decide_supervisory_action(report)

Converts a PropagationStabilityReport into a deterministic control decision.

apply_supervisory_feedback(snapshot, decision)

Applies uniform amplitude damping based on the decision.

Discrete states remain unchanged.

Geometry and epoch structure are preserved.

Closed-Loop Behavior

The simulation stack now supports:

propagate
-> analyze
-> decide
-> feedback
-> propagate

This is the first adaptive simulation feedback loop.

Performance Hardening (Integrated)

This release includes a final optimization pass.

Previous mean update
sum(c.field_amplitude for c in cells) / n
New mean update
snapshot.mean_field_amplitude * factor

Because damping is uniform, this is mathematically exact.

Complexity improvement
O(2N) -> O(N)

This removes the redundant second traversal.

Validation
Tests passing
20 / 20 passing

Includes:

mapping correctness
damping behavior
deterministic replay
divergent recovery
oscillatory damping
end-to-end pipeline tests
Deterministic Guarantees
frozen dataclasses
no randomness
replay-safe control decisions
single-pass amplitude update
exact behavior preservation
decoder untouched
