title: v134.4.0 — Propagation Stability & Attractor Analysis
body:
v134.4.0 — Propagation Stability & Attractor Analysis

This release introduces the first deterministic stability diagnostics and attractor analysis layer for coupled qudit lattice propagation.

v134.4.0 extends the v134 simulation stack from propagation dynamics into trajectory classification and attractor detection.

This is the first release to formally analyze whether lattice propagation:

converges
cycles
diverges
remains bounded
Overview

The purpose of this release is to provide deterministic dynamical systems diagnostics for coupled qudit lattices.

This includes:

fixed-point detection
oscillatory attractor detection
divergence early exit
bounded stability classification
deterministic replay-safe trajectory analysis

This release transforms the simulator into a true dynamical systems research platform.

New Stability Analysis Module
Added

src/qec/sims/propagation_stability_analysis.py

Introduces deterministic propagation diagnostics.

Core Artifact
PropagationStabilityReport

Frozen dataclass representing a trajectory analysis result.

Fields include:

total_steps
final_mean_amplitude
max_state_change
stability_label
attractor_period

This is the canonical analysis artifact for propagation behavior.

Public API
analyze_propagation_stability(initial_snapshot, steps=10)

Deterministically evolves the coupled lattice and classifies the trajectory.

Possible labels:

fixed_point
oscillatory
stable
divergent
Classification Semantics
Fixed point

No cells ever change state.

This corresponds to a period-1 attractor.

Oscillatory

Discrete local-state configuration repeats.

Attractor period is computed as:

second_occurrence - first_occurrence

This now uses explicit state-only fingerprinting.

Stable

Bounded, non-repeating trajectory within the analysis window.

This classification now has clear documented semantics.

Divergent

Amplitude exceeds deterministic divergence threshold:

1e6

Early exit is performed immediately.

Attractor Detection

Attractor analysis uses deterministic state-only fingerprinting:

Tuple[int, ...]

based on ordered local_state values.

Amplitude is intentionally excluded from attractor classification.

This behavior is now explicitly documented.

Post-Review Hardening (Integrated)

This release includes a full cleanup and deterministic test-tightening pass.

Dead code removal

Removed unused:

_STABILITY_THRESHOLD
_lattice_state_fingerprint()
initial_snapshot parameter

This significantly improves internal clarity.

Contract alignment

Module documentation now explicitly defines:

bounded, non-repeating
state-only attractor detection
Test tightening

Three permissive assertions have been upgraded to exact deterministic expectations.

Examples now require:

== "fixed_point"
== "oscillatory"
== "stable"

This materially improves regression detection.

Fixed-point amplitude semantics

Corrected invariance semantics.

Uniform amplitude fixed points now explicitly assert:

pytest.approx(1.0)

This matches the true propagation law.

Validation
Tests passing
30 / 30 passing

All deterministic replay, attractor, divergence, and boundary tests pass.

Deterministic Guarantees
frozen dataclasses
tuple-only collections
no randomness
replay-safe trajectory classification
exact attractor period detection
no dead code
decoder untouched
Architectural Significance

This release introduces the first true trajectory analysis subsystem in the QEC simulation platform.

The simulator now supports:

local field evolution
neighbor propagation
recovery dynamics
attractor classification
stability diagnostics

This is a major research milestone.

Forward Direction

Planned continuation:

v134.5.0 — Supervisory Control Feedback Loop

This will allow stability reports to actively influence supervisory recovery and propagation control.

title: v134.3.0 — Local Coupling & Propagation Dynamics
body:
v134.3.0 — Local Coupling & Propagation Dynamics

This release introduces the first deterministic nearest-neighbor propagation layer for the qudit lattice framework.

v134.3.0 extends the v134 simulation stack from independent local field evolution into interacting lattice dynamics.

This is the first release to model cell-to-cell propagation across the qudit lattice.

Overview

The purpose of this release is to introduce deterministic local coupling laws between neighboring lattice cells.

This includes:

nearest-neighbor state propagation
amplitude mixing dynamics
boundary-safe neighborhood logic
multi-step coupling evolution
structural lattice validation

This release transforms the qudit lattice into an interacting dynamical system.

New Coupling Engine
Added

src/qec/sims/qudit_coupling_dynamics.py

Introduces deterministic local propagation dynamics.

Core Dynamics
State coupling law

Each cell evolves according to the von Neumann neighborhood:

up
down
left
right

The new deterministic state law is:

new_state = (old_state + neighbor_sum) % qudit_dimension

where:

neighbor_sum = Σ(neighbor.local_state)

This is fully deterministic and boundary-safe.

Amplitude propagation law

Each cell amplitude evolves using self-decay and neighbor mixing:

new_amp = old_amp * 0.999 + 0.001 * neighbor_mean_amp

This creates smooth local propagation behavior while preserving stability.

For 1×1 lattices, neighbor fallback uses the cell’s own amplitude.

Public APIs
coupled_evolve_step()

Applies a single deterministic coupling step.

coupled_evolve()

Deterministic multi-step wrapper.

Supports repeated propagation across epochs.

Structural Hardening (Integrated)

This release includes a major defensive validation pass.

Grid lookup validation

_build_grid_lookup() now explicitly validates:

cell count completeness
coordinate bounds
duplicate coordinates

This prevents malformed lattices from silently corrupting propagation dynamics.

Validation guarantees

The coupling engine now guarantees:

len(cells) == width * height
0 <= x < width
0 <= y < height
no duplicate (x,y)

All failures raise explicit ValueError.

Early public API failure

coupled_evolve_step() now guarantees all validation occurs before any coupling math begins.

Malformed snapshots fail immediately.

Post-Review Hardening (Integrated)
Duplicate coordinate rejection

Silent coordinate overwrite has been eliminated.

Out-of-bounds rejection

All invalid coordinates now fail fast.

Incomplete lattice rejection

Partial lattices are now rejected before lookup construction.

Validation
Tests passing
63 / 63 passing

Includes:

35 coupling dynamics tests
28 lattice engine tests
New malformed snapshot coverage

Added 6 new defensive tests:

duplicate coordinate via lookup
duplicate coordinate via evolve
out-of-bounds via lookup
out-of-bounds via evolve
incomplete lattice via lookup
incomplete lattice via evolve
Deterministic Guarantees
frozen dataclasses
tuple-only collections
no randomness
replay-safe propagation
structural validation before math
decoder untouched
Architectural Significance

This release introduces the first interactive lattice propagation layer in the QEC simulation framework.

The system now supports:

local field evolution
nearest-neighbor propagation
deterministic wave-like coupling

This is the first true lattice dynamics release.

Forward Direction

This will introduce stability classification, propagation basins, and attractor diagnostics.

title: v134.2.0 — Generalized Qudit Lattice Field Engine
body:
This release introduces the first deterministic local qudit field dynamics engine for the spatiotemporal lattice framework.

v134.2.0 extends the v134 simulation stack from supervisory lattice evolution into cell-level field-state dynamics.

This is the first release to model generalized qudit state evolution across lattice cells.

Overview

The purpose of this release is to provide a deterministic local field engine for:

qutrit (dim = 3)
generic qudit (dim >= 2)

Each lattice cell now carries its own local field state and amplitude.

This release introduces:

qudit lattice construction
deterministic cyclic evolution
amplitude decay law
validated ASCII rendering
optimized step evolution

This is the first local field dynamics layer in the simulation platform.

New Field Engine
Added

src/qec/sims/qudit_lattice_engine.py

Introduces the deterministic qudit field engine.

Core Artifacts

Frozen dataclass representing a single local qudit field site.

Fields include:

y_index

Frozen dataclass representing a complete qudit lattice epoch.

Contains:

width

This is the canonical field-state snapshot artifact.

Lattice Builder
build_qudit_lattice()

Constructs an initial deterministic qudit lattice.

Supports:

arbitrary width / height
arbitrary qudit dimension ≥ 2
configurable initial state
configurable initial amplitude

Now includes strict validation:

epoch_index >= 0

Negative epochs are explicitly rejected.

Deterministic Evolution Law
evolve_qudit_lattice()

Implements deterministic cyclic field evolution:

new_state = (old_state + steps) % qudit_dimension

and deterministic amplitude decay:

new_amplitude = old_amplitude * 0.999 ** steps

Epoch advances deterministically:

new_epoch = old_epoch + steps
Performance Hardening (Integrated)

This release includes a major optimization pass.

Previous complexity
O(steps × cells)
New complexity
O(cells)

Evolution is now step-count independent.

This significantly improves performance for long qudit evolution sweeps.

Sequential replay equivalence remains fully preserved.

ASCII Renderer
render_qudit_lattice_ascii()

Canonical deterministic integer-grid renderer.

Example:

0 1 2
1 2 0
Renderer Hardening

This release adds strong defensive validation:

coordinate bounds checking
duplicate coordinate rejection
incomplete lattice rejection

Explicit ValueError now replaces raw IndexError.

This aligns with the defensive style of the broader lattice framework.

Post-Review Hardening (Integrated)
Negative epoch validation

Added explicit rejection for:

Single-pass evolution optimization

Replaced iterative evolution loop with mathematically equivalent single-pass evolution.

Render safety

Added validation for:

incomplete grids

title: v134.1.0 — Deterministic Recovery Tunnel Framework
body:
This release introduces the first deterministic supervisory recovery layer for the spatiotemporal lattice framework introduced in v134.0.0.

v134.1.0 extends the simulation platform from state representation into state recovery and controlled supervisory progression.

This is the first release in the v134 line to model deterministic lattice healing and supervisory reentry.

Overview

The purpose of this release is to enable deterministic supervisory recovery transitions across lattice snapshots.

This includes:

supervisory state machine transitions
per-cell regime recovery
divergence reduction
replay-safe recovery summaries
cross-module supervisory vocabulary alignment

This release formally introduces recovery semantics into the simulation layer.

New Recovery Layer
Added

src/qec/sims/recovery_tunnel_engine.py

Introduces the deterministic recovery tunnel engine.

This module performs one-step deterministic recovery transitions for both:

supervisory state
cell-level regime state
Core Artifacts
RecoveryTunnelReport

Frozen dataclass representing a single deterministic recovery step.

Fields include:

previous_state
next_state
epoch_index
recovered_cells
total_cells
recovery_ratio
max_divergence_delta

This is the canonical recovery-step artifact.

Supervisory State Machine

The recovery tunnel now implements the deterministic supervisory FSM:

locked      -> recovering
elevated    -> recovering
recovering  -> normal
normal      -> normal

No randomness is involved.

All transitions are deterministic and replay-safe.

Cell-Level Recovery Semantics

Each recovery pass applies one-step regime healing:

divergent -> critical
critical  -> stable
stable    -> stable

This allows repeated deterministic application across epochs.

Divergence Reduction

Per-cell divergence is deterministically reduced on each pass.

The current law applies a stable multiplicative reduction:

new_divergence = old_divergence * 0.95

with non-negative clamping.

This ensures monotonic recovery.

Summary Renderer
render_recovery_summary()

Canonical deterministic summary format:

epoch=4 locked->recovering recovered=3/9 ratio=0.333

Uses canonical float formatting for stable summaries.

Post-Review Hardening (Integrated)
Shared supervisory vocabulary alignment

The lattice layer now accepts:

normal
elevated
locked
recovering

This resolves the previous cross-module contract mismatch.

The supervisory vocabulary is now fully aligned across:

future supervisory extensions

supervisory transitions
regime recovery transitions
deterministic semantics
Regression coverage

ensuring lattice snapshots accept recovery-generated states.

Validation

FSM correctness

title: # v133.9.2 — Decoder Layer Isolation Remediation & Core Metrics Hardening
body:
This patch release resolves a pre-existing architectural law violation in the decoder layer and hardens the newly introduced shared core metrics contract.

`v133.9.2` is a structural cleanup and numerical robustness release.

No feature semantics were changed.

This release restores clean layer boundaries and stabilizes shared metric contracts prior to `v134.0.0`.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this patch is to eliminate the decoder’s upward dependency on analysis modules and strengthen the new core-layer numerical and API guarantees.

This release includes:

* decoder isolation remediation
* shared core metrics layer
* tolerance-safe resonance computation
* public scalar-key contract export
* regression validation

This is the final constitutional cleanup patch of the `v133` line.

---

## Architectural Violation (Resolved)

### Previous issue

The decoder layer previously imported upward into analysis:

```text id="v1m8pk"
src/qec/decoder/law_driven_decoder.py
```

imported from:

```text id="r6n2wd"
qec.analysis.field_metrics
qec.analysis.multiscale_metrics
```

This violated decoder isolation law.

---

## Remediation

### Added

`src/qec/core/__init__.py`

Introduces the new shared core layer.

---

### Added

`src/qec/core/metrics.py`

Pure decoder-safe metric helpers extracted into shared core.

This module now serves as the canonical lower-layer metrics substrate.

---

### Updated

`src/qec/decoder/law_driven_decoder.py`

Illegal upward imports removed.

Now imports from:

```text id="c8m4rt"
qec.core.metrics
```

This restores proper dependency direction.

---

### Updated

`src/qec/analysis/field_metrics.py`

Now re-exports from core.

Public API preserved.

---

### Updated

`src/qec/analysis/multiscale_metrics.py`

Now re-exports from core.

Public API preserved.

---

## Core Metrics Hardening (Integrated)

### Tolerance-safe resonance

`compute_resonance()` now uses tolerance-based float comparison instead of exact `==`.

This improves numerical robustness for non-integer or derived floating-point inputs.

Comparison now uses deterministic tolerance semantics:

```text id="g7n1px"
np.isclose
```

with explicit tolerances.

This preserves discrete behavior while avoiding brittle equality checks.

---

### Public contract constant

The former private:

```text id="k3m9du"
_SCALAR_KEYS
```

has been promoted to:

```text id="p5v2rq"
SCALAR_KEYS
```

This now acts as the canonical public cross-layer scalar metric contract.

Tuple ordering is unchanged.

This reduces drift risk between:

* core
* analysis
* decoder
* simulation layers

---

## New Layer Graph

### Before

```text id="u4m8zx"
decoder
↑
analysis
```

Violation.

---

### After

```text id="t9n3qp"
decoder
↓
core
↑
analysis
```

Architecturally correct.

---

## Validation

### Decoder tests

```text id="j2m7rt"
46 / 46 passing
```

Includes:

```text id="f8v1pk"
test_full_run_bitwise_deterministic
```

---

### Analysis tests

```text id="w3m9dx"
59 / 59 passing
```

---

### Simulation tests

```text id="q7n2ru"
162 / 162 passing
```

---

### Hardening tests

Added:

* tolerance comparison regression
* `SCALAR_KEYS` contract test
* import-path continuity checks

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Bitwise behavior unchanged

Decoder outputs remain byte-identical.

Confirmed by deterministic regression tests.

### Semantic behavior unchanged

No algorithmic logic was modified.

Only import paths and numerical comparison hardening were introduced.

### Stable contract

`SCALAR_KEYS` ordering remains fixed and public.

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder semantics unchanged
* no new dependencies
* no simulation changes
* no timeline changes
* no TUI changes
* no bootstrap changes

This is a pure architecture remediation and hardening patch.

---

## Forward Direction

This patch completes the `v133` cleanup series and establishes a clean baseline for:

```text id="v1340launch"
v134.0.0
```

The codebase now enters the next major version with:

* clean layer boundaries
* decoder isolation restored
* shared core substrate introduced
* numerical robustness improved
* public scalar metric contract stabilized
* public API continuity preserved

`v133.9.2` is the final constitutional and numerical hardening patch before the `v134` major-version era.

title: # v133.9.1 — Codebase Audit, Invariant Check & Fast Test Bootstrap
body:

`v133.9.1` is an operational hardening release.

title: # v133.9.0 — Phase Timeline & Epoch Evolution Engine
body:
This release introduces deterministic multi-epoch timeline analysis for phase-map artifacts.

`v133.9.0` extends temporal analysis beyond pairwise drift, enabling full timeline evolution across successive simulation epochs.

This release includes the final ergonomic hardening pass prior to tagging.

This is the first epoch-aware history engine in the v133 simulation series.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to track how universes evolve across full timelines.

The new system supports:

* ordered epoch history
* pairwise drift composition
* cumulative drift tracking
* max epoch drift detection
* deterministic ASCII timeline summaries
* replay-safe temporal histories
* list/sequence-friendly APIs

This is the first release where the simulation platform can study:

```text id="g1m8qx"
universe aging
long-term phase stability
multi-epoch drift
```

---

## Finalization Pass (Integrated)

### Sequence API ergonomics

Public-facing snapshot inputs now accept:

```text id="u8n4lp"
Sequence[PhaseMap]
```

instead of only:

```text id="x4q7de"
Tuple[PhaseMap, ...]
```

This allows callers to pass:

* tuples
* lists
* other sequence-like collections

Internally all inputs are immediately normalized to tuple form.

Deterministic storage semantics remain unchanged.

---

### Tuple-only report contract preserved

All report dataclasses remain tuple-only.

This preserves:

```text id="t2v9mr"
replay determinism
immutability
byte-identical equality
```

---

### Future memory note

A lightweight drift-only timeline report variant is explicitly deferred to a future release.

No memory-model behavior changes were introduced in `v133.9.0`.

---

## Validation

### Tests

## Deterministic Guarantees

## Architectural Integrity

This is a clean additive temporal history release.
