`v133.9.0` is the first finalized epoch-history release in the v133 simulation era.

title: # v133.8.0 — Temporal Phase Evolution & Drift Analysis
body:
This release introduces deterministic temporal drift analysis for phase-map artifacts.

`v133.8.0` enables cell-aligned comparison of phase maps across successive simulation sweeps, providing regime-transition analysis and replay-safe temporal drift artifacts.

This release includes the full post-review rendering hardening pass.

This is the first time-aware simulation artifact layer in the v133 series.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to analyze how phase regimes evolve across time / successive sweeps.

The new system supports:

* cell-aligned drift analysis
* strict dimension validation
* exact ordering enforcement
* regime transition tracking
* divergence delta analysis
* deterministic ASCII drift matrices
* explicit render-grid validation
* replay-safe temporal artifacts

This is the first release where the simulation platform can quantify:

```text id="k2m8dr"
how universes drift over time
```

---

## Added

### `src/qec/sims/phase_drift_analysis.py`

Deterministic temporal drift module.

Introduces:

* `PhaseDriftCell`
* `PhaseDriftReport`
* `analyze_phase_drift()`
* `render_drift_ascii()`

---

## Drift Cell

`PhaseDriftCell` tracks per-cell transitions:

* `decay`
* `coupling_profile`
* `from_regime`
* `to_regime`
* `changed`

Frozen and tuple-safe.

---

## Drift Report

`PhaseDriftReport` aggregates:

* `cells`
* `num_changed`
* `num_unchanged`
* `drift_ratio`
* `max_divergence_delta`

This forms the canonical temporal-drift artifact.

---

## Transition Symbols

ASCII drift matrix symbols:

```text id="m5u1px"
.  unchanged
↑  worsening
↓  improving
*  lateral / other change
```

Example:

```text id="p4z7vn"
. . ↑
. * *
↓ . .
```

---

## Hardening Pass (Integrated)

### Render-grid validation

A rendering contract issue was identified during review:

if:

```text id="r8n2wd"
len(report.cells) % num_cols != 0
```

the previous implementation could silently emit a truncated final row.

This has now been hardened.

`render_drift_ascii()` now explicitly validates exact grid alignment.

Invalid layouts now fail with:

```python id="j6q9ts"
ValueError
```

This prevents silent truncation and makes matrix rendering behavior explicit.

---

### Dead-code cleanup

The unreachable mapping entry:

```text id="g3f0yb"
"unchanged"
```

was removed from `_DRIFT_SYMBOLS`.

Unchanged cells are already handled directly by `_drift_symbol()`.

This removes dead code and aligns the mapping with the documented key scheme:

```text id="u7l3ke"
from_regime_to_to_regime
```

---

## Validation

### Tests

* **18 / 18 passing**
* deterministic replay confirmed
* alignment rejection confirmed
* render-grid rejection confirmed
* ASCII rendering confirmed
* temporal drift correctness confirmed

New coverage includes:

* non-multiple `num_cols` rejection
* explicit render-contract enforcement

---

## Audit Invariants Confirmed

### Cell alignment integrity

* exact dimension match required
* exact cell count required
* row-major ordering preserved
* no silent zip truncation
* indexed mismatch errors

---

### Drift semantics

Confirmed:

```text id="y1p8gm"
changed =
from_regime != to_regime
```

Counts and ratios verified.

Zero-cell edge safe.

---

### Divergence delta

Confirmed:

```text id="c9m5re"
abs(new - old)
```

with deterministic max selection.

---

### ASCII rendering

Confirmed:

* row-major rendering
* exact column alignment required
* no truncated final rows
* safe column guards
* full symbol semantics

---

### Replay safety

Repeated runs produce:

```text id="x2d4uk"
byte-identical reports
byte-identical ASCII output
```

---

### Architecture

Confirmed:

* no upward imports
* no decoder imports
* stdlib only
* frozen dataclasses
* tuple-only collections

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Stable comparison

Same pair of phase maps always yields identical drift report.

### Stable rendering

ASCII drift matrices are byte-identical.

### Safe mismatch handling

Dimension, ordering, and render-grid mismatches fail explicitly.

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* phase map generator untouched
* codec untouched
* no TUI changes
* no verification changes
* stdlib only

This is a clean additive temporal analysis release.

---

## Forward Direction

This release opens the door to:

* drift timelines
* temporal sweep chains
* universe aging studies
* attractor drift analysis
* multi-epoch phase transitions
* simulation evolution movies / SVG timelines

`v133.8.0` is the first fully hardened temporal drift release in the v133 simulation era.

title: # v133.7.0 — Phase Map Export & Replay Bundle
body:
# v133.7.0 — Phase Map Export & Replay Bundle

This release introduces canonical export bundles for deterministic phase-map artifacts.

`v133.7.0` enables replay-safe archival, hashing, and JSON round-trip reconstruction of phase-space experiment outputs.

This release includes the full post-audit schema hardening pass and final micro-pass validation fixes.

This is a simulation-layer artifact infrastructure release.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to convert phase maps into canonical research-grade artifacts.

The new system supports:

* frozen export bundles
* canonical JSON serialization
* idempotent SHA-256 hashing
* replay-safe round trips
* strict schema validation
* explicit malformed-artifact rejection
* cell-level key validation
* schema-version enforcement

This is the first long-lived experiment artifact layer for the v133 simulation series.

---

## Added

### `src/qec/sims/phase_map_codec.py`

Deterministic artifact codec.

Introduces:

* `PhaseMapExportMetadata`
* `PhaseMapExportBundle`
* `export_phase_map_to_json()`
* `load_phase_map_from_json()`
* `compute_phase_map_hash()`
* `with_computed_phase_hash()`

---

## Export Metadata

`PhaseMapExportMetadata` includes:

* `schema_version`
* `created_by_release`
* `trace_hash`

Frozen and replay-safe.

---

## Export Bundle

`PhaseMapExportBundle` contains:

* `phase_map`
* `metadata`
* `ascii_render`

This forms the canonical portable artifact.

---

## Canonical JSON

Serialization uses:

```text id="g4t1pe"
sort_keys=True
compact separators
12 decimal-place float normalization
```

Repeated export is byte-identical.

---

## Hashing

Hashing uses:

```text id="f8k3rm"
SHA-256
```

over canonical JSON.

Trace hash normalization prevents circular drift.

Behavior is fully idempotent.

---

## Hardening Passes (Integrated)

### Cell-level schema validation

A defect was identified during verify-only audit:

cell-level keys inside:

```text id="k3v6xp"
phase_map.cells
```

were not explicitly validated.

Malformed cell artifacts could raise raw:

```text id="x0n2jd"
KeyError
```

instead of explicit schema errors.

This has been corrected.

Each cell is now validated with:

```python id="n5m7qw"
_require_keys(...)
```

before reconstruction.

Indexed path context is included, e.g.:

```text id="j1z4rw"
phase_map.cells[0]
```

---

### Schema-version enforcement

`_SCHEMA_VERSION` is now actively enforced during load.

Behavior:

```python id="b8u2nm"
ValueError(
    f"unsupported schema_version: {schema_version}"
)
```

This prevents silent acceptance of incompatible or future artifact schemas.

This is now a formal artifact-law invariant.

---

### Float docstring correction

Documentation now correctly states:

```text id="r2k7cf"
12 decimal places
```

instead of the previous inaccurate wording:

```text id="p4d8vj"
12 significant decimal places
```

Implementation remains unchanged.

This improves semantic clarity of canonical export behavior.

---

## Validation

### Codec tests

* **22 / 22 passing**
* canonical round-trip confirmed
* hash idempotency confirmed
* malformed JSON rejection confirmed
* missing-key rejection confirmed
* missing cell-subkey rejection confirmed
* schema-version mismatch rejection confirmed
* replay determinism confirmed

---

## Audit Invariants Confirmed

### Hashing integrity

* no circular drift
* double-finalize stable
* repeated hash equality

### Canonical JSON

* stable field ordering
* byte-identical replay
* stable float normalization

### Replay safety

```text id="c9m4wr"
export → load → export
```

is byte-identical

### Architecture

* no upward imports
* no decoder imports
* stdlib only
* frozen dataclasses
* tuple-only collections

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Stable export

Same phase map always produces identical JSON bytes.

### Stable hash

Hash survives full round trip.

### Explicit schema rejection

Malformed or incompatible artifacts now fail predictably with `ValueError`.

### Version law

Unsupported schema versions are now explicitly rejected.

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* sweep engine untouched
* phase map generator untouched
* no TUI changes
* no verification changes
* stdlib only

This is a clean additive artifact release.

---

## Forward Direction

This release opens the door to:

* Zenodo-ready phase bundles
* CSV / JSONL exports
* paper-ready supplementary artifacts
* cross-release experiment diffs
* simulation provenance bundles
* schema migration tooling

`v133.7.0` is the first fully hardened research-artifact codec release in the v133 simulation era.

title: # v133.6.0 — Phase Map & Regime Matrix Generator
body:
This release introduces deterministic phase-map artifact generation for the v133 simulation stack.

`v133.6.0` converts law-sweep outputs into structured phase matrices and replay-safe ASCII regime maps.

This release includes the full post-review hardening pass and permanent development-environment dependency improvements.

This is a simulation-layer + environment reproducibility release.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to convert sweep results into structured, readable phase artifacts.

The new system supports:

* deterministic phase cells
* regime matrix construction
* stable row-major ordering
* ASCII regime rendering
* replay-safe matrix artifacts
* rectangular-grid validation
* explicit regime-label validation
* permanent allowed dependency bootstrapping

This is the first visualization-adjacent release without plotting libraries.

---

## Added

### `src/qec/sims/phase_map_generator.py`

Deterministic phase artifact generator.

Introduces:

* `PhaseCell`
* `PhaseMap`
* `build_phase_map()`
* `render_phase_matrix_ascii()`

---

## Phase Cell

`PhaseCell` is a frozen dataclass with:

* `decay`
* `coupling_profile`
* `regime_label`
* `divergence_score`

Tuple-only and immutable.

---

## Phase Map

`PhaseMap` provides:

* `cells`
* `num_rows`
* `num_cols`
* `stable_count`
* `critical_count`
* `divergent_count`
* `max_divergence`

This forms the canonical matrix artifact layer.

---

## ASCII Matrix Rendering

`render_phase_matrix_ascii()` converts the phase map into deterministic matrix output.

Regime symbols:

```text id="q9o4m2"
stable    → S
critical  → C
divergent → D
```

Example:

```text id="mj3n2d"
S S C
S D D
C D D
```

This is now a stable experiment artifact.

---

## Hardening Pass (Integrated)

### Rectangular grid invariant

`build_phase_map()` now explicitly validates:

```text id="n4h1w8"
len(cells) == num_rows * num_cols
```

If violated, it now fails early with:

```python id="g2x7pw"
ValueError(
    "phase map results must form a full rectangular grid"
)
```

This prevents downstream index errors and protects against partial or malformed sweep outputs.

---

### Explicit regime-label validation

`render_phase_matrix_ascii()` now validates labels before symbol mapping.

Invalid labels now fail with:

```python id="j0r5lu"
ValueError(
    f"unsupported regime_label: {label}"
)
```

instead of raw `KeyError`.

This makes future sweep-regime evolution much safer.

---

## Permanent Dependency Hardening

### Updated

`dev-requirements.txt`

Added:

```text id="d7s2ym"
networkx
```

Approved default dependency set now includes:

```text id="f3u8nx"
numpy
scipy
networkx
```

This permanently aligns the bootstrap environment with `CLAUDE.md` allowed dependencies.

---

## Validation

### Phase map tests

* **14 / 14 passing**
* deterministic replay confirmed
* empty edge cases confirmed
* ordering preserved
* ASCII rendering verified
* partial-grid rejection verified
* invalid-label rejection verified

### Coverage

Includes:

* immutability
* count correctness
* max divergence tracking
* stable ordering
* deterministic replay
* rectangular-grid invariant
* explicit label validation

---

## Full Repository

Current suite baseline remains:

```text id="p8t0oz"
9168 passed
9 failed
7 errors
```

Remaining failures are pre-existing optional external dependencies:

* `stim`
* `pymatching`
* `qec_qldpc_codes`

These remain intentionally non-default.

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Stable ordering

Sweep ordering preserved exactly.

### Stable matrix rendering

ASCII output remains byte-identical.

### Structural safety

Malformed phase maps now fail predictably.

### Environment consistency

Approved dependencies auto-install in fresh environments.

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* sweep engine untouched
* kernel untouched
* no TUI changes
* no verification changes
* no PyPI-only dependency additions

This is a clean additive release.

---

## Forward Direction

This release opens the door to:

* CSV phase exports
* SVG matrix rendering
* heatmap generation
* ternary phase tables
* regime transition studies
* lattice bifurcation maps

`v133.6.0` is the first hardened deterministic phase-artifact release in the v133 simulation era.

title: # v133.5.0 — Law Sweep & Phase Transition Experiment Engine
body:
This release introduces the first parameter sweep and phase-transition framework for the v133 simulation stack.

`v133.5.0` enables deterministic law-space exploration across decay and coupling grids, allowing large-scale comparative universe experiments and regime classification.

This release includes the full post-review hardening pass.

This is the first release where the simulation platform becomes a **phase-space research instrument**.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to scale the comparative experiment framework into a sweep engine.

The new system supports:

* law parameter sweeps
* decay × coupling grids
* deterministic regime classification
* divergence basin analysis
* aggregate phase summaries
* strict step validation
* stable result ordering
* explicit qutrit validation
* full replay-safe grid exploration

This allows experiments such as:

* stability basin mapping
* divergence threshold detection
* ternary-law phase studies
* lawful vs anti-law parameter sweeps

---

## Added

### `src/qec/sims/law_sweep_engine.py`

Deterministic sweep framework.

Introduces:

* `LawSweepConfig`
* `run_law_sweep()`
* `LawSweepResult`
* `LawSweepSummary`
* `summarize_sweep()`

---

## Sweep Configuration

`LawSweepConfig` is a frozen dataclass with tuple-only fields:

* `decay_values`
* `coupling_profiles`
* `steps`
* `label`

This defines a deterministic parameter-space experiment.

---

## Sweep Runner

`run_law_sweep()` iterates:

```text
decay × coupling profile
```

across the full grid.

For each run it computes:

* final energy
* divergence score
* regime classification

Returns tuple-only results.

Ordering is explicitly guaranteed as:

```text
decay-major
→ coupling-minor
```

This is now part of the tested sweep contract.

---

## Phase Classification

Each sweep result is labeled deterministically as:

```text
stable
critical
divergent
```

using:

```text
stable:
    final < initial

critical:
    |final - initial| <= 1e-9

divergent:
    final > initial
```

This is the first explicit phase-regime classification layer in the simulation stack.

---

## Aggregate Summary

`LawSweepSummary` provides sweep-wide metrics:

* `num_stable`
* `num_critical`
* `num_divergent`
* `max_divergence`
* `total_runs`

This enables basin-level phase analysis.

---

## Hardening Pass (Integrated)

### Explicit qutrit validation

`_evolve_with_params()` now validates qutrit states before coupling.

Invalid values now fail fast with:

```python
ValueError
```

This prevents silent out-of-range state behavior and protects future qudit / ternary experiments.

---

### Negative-step guard

`run_law_sweep()` now explicitly rejects invalid step counts.

Behavior:

```python
ValueError("steps must be >= 0")
```

Negative values no longer silently behave like zero-step runs.

This is an important experiment-config safety improvement.

---

### Stable ordering contract

The sweep result order is now explicitly tested against:

```python
itertools.product(...)
```

This guarantees:

```text
decay-major
then coupling-minor
```

ordering.

This is critical for future:

* heatmaps
* matrix visualizations
* reproducible CSV exports
* phase tables

---

## Validation

### Sweep Tests

* **13 / 13 passing**
* deterministic replay confirmed
* phase classification verified
* zero-step edge verified
* negative-step rejection verified
* ordering contract verified
* invalid qutrit rejection verified

Coverage includes:

* frozen dataclasses
* regime classification
* deterministic replay
* tuple-only collections
* summary correctness
* zero-step behavior
* negative-step guard
* parameter grid coverage
* stable ordering

---

## Full Repository Validation

Full suite remains green after dependency hardening:

```text
9170 passed
14 skipped
0 failed
```

This includes previously restored graph-topology tests.

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Sweep Replay

Same config always produces byte-identical sweep outputs.

### Stable Classification

Phase labels remain deterministic.

### Stable Ordering

Result ordering is now part of the formal test contract.

### Explicit Failure Paths

Invalid qutrit states and negative steps now fail predictably.

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* simulation kernel untouched
* comparative framework untouched
* adapter layer untouched
* no TUI changes
* no verification changes
* no PyPI dependency introduction

This is a purely additive experimental sweep release.

---

## Forward Direction

This release opens the door to:

* ternary logic universes
* qudit / qutrit law-space sweeps
* phase-transition heatmaps
* anti-law bifurcation studies
* lattice stability basin mapping
* resonance regime classification

`v133.5.0` is the first full parameter-space experiment release in the v133 simulation era.

title: # v133.4.0 — Comparative Universe Experiment Framework
body:
This release introduces the first comparative experiment framework for the v133 simulation stack.

`v133.4.0` enables deterministic side-by-side universe experiments, allowing lawful and anti-law universes to evolve from identical initial conditions and quantify divergence over time.

This release includes the full post-review hardening pass.

This is a simulation-layer only release.

No decoder, TUI, verification, kernel, coupling, or adapter semantics were modified.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to move the v133 series from simulation infrastructure into actual experimental science.

The new framework supports:

* lawful universe evolution
* anti-law universe evolution
* deterministic comparative replay
* divergence scoring
* energy ratio metrics
* zero-energy edge handling
* long-run reproducibility

This is the first release where the platform can ask:

> *what happens when the laws of reality are inverted?*

That is exactly as fun as it sounds 😄

---

## Added

### `src/qec/sims/comparative_experiments.py`

Deterministic comparative simulation module.

Introduces:

* `run_lawful_universe()`
* `run_anti_universe()`
* `compare_universes()`
* `UniverseComparison`

---

## Lawful Universe

`run_lawful_universe()` runs the standard coupled simulation law:

```text id="t9r61j"
baseline decay = 0.999
+
standard qutrit coupling
```

using existing:

```text id="p63ijw"
evolve_universe_coupled()
```

Returns the full deterministic state history as a tuple.

---

## Anti-Law Universe

`run_anti_universe()` applies inverted semantics:

```text id="v6ij7s"
state 0 → 1.000
state 1 → 0.998
state 2 → 1.001
```

with amplifying decay:

```text id="n9w4qj"
1.001
```

This intentionally inverts the stabilizing law set.

The anti-law universe therefore exhibits energy growth under the same initial conditions.

---

## Divergence Metrics

Introduces frozen dataclass:

```text id="k0m1rt"
UniverseComparison
```

with:

* `lawful_final_energy`
* `anti_final_energy`
* `divergence_score`
* `energy_ratio`
* `steps`

Computed as:

```text id="7g0qzx"
divergence_score =
abs(lawful - anti)

energy_ratio =
anti / lawful
```

with explicit zero-lawful-energy handling.

---

## Hardening Pass (Integrated)

### Approximate float assertions

All floating-point tests now use:

```python
pytest.approx(...)
```

This eliminates brittle exact-equality comparisons and improves long-run numerical stability.

---

### Explicit anti-law qutrit validation

`_apply_anti_coupling()` now uses the shared validator from:

```text id="m3r0hj"
qutrit_coupling.py
```

Invalid qutrit states now fail deterministically with clear:

```python
ValueError
```

instead of opaque `IndexError`.

This preserves scientific correctness and improves debugging.

---

### Zero-energy edge coverage

Added explicit coverage for:

```text id="r4l8hv"
lawful_energy == 0.0
```

The `energy_ratio` branch now has dedicated regression tests.

Behavior:

```text id="v1okn6"
energy_ratio = 0.0
```

when lawful energy is zero.

This was an important correctness branch.

---

## Divergence Behavior

### Lawful universe

Energy decays over time.

Stable / convergent behavior.

---

### Anti-law universe

Energy amplifies over time.

Divergent / destabilizing behavior.

---

### Comparative outcome

Divergence grows with step count.

Typical result:

```text id="w9w0u1"
energy_ratio > 1.0
```

confirming anti-law amplification.

---

## Validation

### Test Status

* **22+ tests passing**
* all prior simulation tests remain green
* deterministic replay confirmed
* zero-energy edge confirmed

### Coverage

Includes:

* lawful evolution
* anti-law evolution
* tuple history
* timestep correctness
* deterministic replay
* divergence metrics
* default / zero steps
* zero-energy ratio branch
* frozen dataclass integrity

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Comparative Replay

Same initial state always produces identical lawful vs anti-law trajectories.

### Stable Metrics

Divergence and ratio calculations remain deterministic.

### Explicit Edge Handling

Zero-energy branches and invalid qutrit states now fail predictably.

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* kernel untouched
* coupling untouched
* adapter untouched
* no TUI changes
* no verification changes
* no new dependencies

This is a purely additive experimental simulation release.

---

## Forward Direction

This release opens the door to:

* ternary logic universes
* qudit / qutrit lattice experiments
* lawful vs anti-law sweeps
* parameterized reality models
* alternate physics-rule experiments
* resonance / attractor universe studies

`v133.4.0` is the first true comparative experiment release in the v133 simulation era.

title: # v133.3.0 — Simulation Adapter Interface Layer
body:
This release introduces the external simulation adapter boundary for the v133 simulation stack.

`v133.3.0` creates a deterministic interface layer that converts internal simulation state into backend-ready payloads for future QuTiP and Qiskit integrations.

This release includes the full post-review hardening pass.

This release adds **no heavy dependencies**.

No QuTiP or Qiskit libraries are imported.

This is a pure simulation-layer infrastructure release.

No decoder, TUI, verification, or kernel semantics were modified.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to establish a clean handoff layer between the internal micro-universe runtime and future external simulation engines.

The new layer supports:

* immutable adapter payloads
* backend tagging
* centralized backend validation
* deterministic normalization
* qutrit register sanitization
* replay-safe handoff artifacts
* schema-drift-safe reconstruction

This is the final major interface release before optional external backend integrations.

---

## Added

### `src/qec/sims/adapter_interface.py`

Deterministic simulation adapter module.

Introduces:

* `SimulationAdapterPayload`
* `to_adapter_payload()`
* `normalize_for_backend()`
* `_validate_backend_target()`

---

## Adapter Payload

`SimulationAdapterPayload` is a frozen dataclass with tuple-only fields:

* `state_vector`
* `qutrit_register`
* `timestep`
* `backend_target`
* `schema_version`

This ensures all backend handoff artifacts remain immutable and replay-safe.

---

## Supported Backends

Current supported backend targets:

```text id="95rwj4"
generic
qutip
qiskit
```

Invalid targets are explicitly rejected.

Validation is now centralized through:

```python id="v7l5zm"
_validate_backend_target()
```

This is now the single source of truth for backend validation and error messages.

Invalid targets fail with consistent error text:

```text id="w2ifmj"
unsupported backend_target: ...
```

---

## Normalization Semantics

### Generic

Passthrough.

Returns the identical payload object.

---

### QuTiP-style

State vector is normalized to unit L2 norm.

```text id="4gguw2"
||state_vector||₂ = 1
```

Zero vectors remain unchanged to preserve determinism and avoid divide-by-zero semantics.

---

### Qiskit-style

Qutrit register values are clipped to:

```text id="9r2v1g"
{0, 1, 2}
```

This guarantees valid qutrit-state semantics for future backend adapters.

---

## Hardening Pass (Integrated)

### Centralized backend validation

Backend lookup and validation logic previously duplicated across:

* `to_adapter_payload()`
* `normalize_for_backend()`

This is now centralized in:

```python id="s29hh8"
_validate_backend_target()
```

This ensures consistent validation semantics and simplifies future backend additions.

---

### Safe payload reconstruction

Manual payload reconstruction has been replaced with:

```python id="t3mjkl"
dataclasses.replace(...)
```

This is a major maintainability improvement.

Benefits:

* prevents schema drift
* preserves unchanged fields automatically
* reduces reconstruction duplication
* future-proofs payload evolution

All backend normalization paths now use `replace()`.

---

## Validation

### Test Status

* **28 / 28 passing**
* replay determinism confirmed
* backend validation confirmed
* normalization correctness confirmed
* hardening semantics verified

### New Hardening Coverage

Includes:

* identical invalid-backend rejection across both APIs
* case-sensitive invalid target rejection
* whitespace / empty string rejection
* replace-field preservation
* repeated normalization equality

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Immutable Handoff

All adapter payloads are frozen and tuple-only.

### Replay Safety

Repeated export yields byte-identical payloads.

### Stable Normalization

Backend-specific normalization remains deterministic.

### Strict Validation

Unsupported backend targets fail fast with centralized validation.

### Schema Safety

Payload normalization now preserves all unchanged fields automatically.

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* kernel untouched
* coupling semantics untouched
* no TUI changes
* no verification changes
* no new dependencies
* no external backend imports

This is a purely additive simulation-layer release.

---

## Forward Direction

This release prepares the v133 stack for:

* optional QuTiP backend implementation
* optional Qiskit benchmark harness
* notebook simulation workflows
* parameter sweep pipelines
* lawful vs anti-law universe experiments
* ternary logic universe models
* qutrit / qudit lattice studies

`v133.3.0` establishes the hardened external simulation boundary for the micro-universe platform.

title: v133.2.0 — Qutrit Field Coupling Layer
body:
# v133.2.0 — Qutrit Field Coupling Layer

This release extends the micro-universe simulation stack with the first interactive coupling law.

`v133.2.0` introduces deterministic qutrit → field coupling, allowing qutrit state channels to influence field evolution in a bounded, replay-safe manner.

This release includes the full post-review hardening pass.

This release is fully additive.

The baseline universe kernel remains untouched.

No decoder, TUI, verification, or export-bridge changes were made.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to move the simulation stack from passive field decay into interactive state evolution.

The new layer introduces:

* deterministic qutrit coupling
* bounded per-lane amplification / damping
* cyclic qutrit motifs
* additive coupled evolution
* coupling observables
* explicit state validation
* shared coupling semantics
* long-run replay validation

This is the first release where the simulation layer supports **state-driven field dynamics**.

---

## Added

### `src/qec/sims/qutrit_coupling.py`

Deterministic qutrit coupling layer.

Introduces:

* `apply_qutrit_coupling()`
* `evolve_universe_coupled()`
* `CouplingObservation`
* `observe_coupling()`
* `_lane_multipliers()` (shared internal helper)

---

## Coupling Law

Per-lane deterministic multipliers:

```text id="r09vgf"
state 0 → 1.000
state 1 → 1.001
state 2 → 0.998
```

Applied as:

```text id="r7xszq"
new_field[i] = old_field[i] * multiplier
```

If qutrit channels are shorter than field lanes, states repeat cyclically.

This provides stable repeating coupling motifs.

---

## Shared Coupling Semantics

A new internal helper:

```python id="e0crnn"
_lane_multipliers()
```

now serves as the single source of truth for:

* state validation
* cyclic repeat logic
* per-lane multiplier mapping

Both:

* `apply_qutrit_coupling()`
* `observe_coupling()`

consume this helper.

This eliminates semantic duplication and future drift risk.

---

## Explicit State Validation

Qutrit states are now explicitly validated before multiplier lookup.

Allowed values:

```text id="b0s48p"
0
1
2
```

Invalid states now raise:

```python id="99bq9r"
ValueError("qutrit_states must contain only values {0, 1, 2}")
```

This replaces fragile `IndexError` failure paths with deterministic and scientifically clearer diagnostics.

No silent clamping is performed.

---

## Coupled Evolution

`evolve_universe_coupled()` performs:

```text id="i1t94m"
baseline decay (0.999)
→ qutrit coupling
→ timestep increment
```

This is fully additive.

The original:

```text id="pvzw34"
evolve_universe()
```

remains unchanged.

This preserves baseline-kernel truth and enables direct comparative experiments.

---

## Coupling Observables

`observe_coupling()` returns frozen dataclass:

```text id="29zrcg"
CouplingObservation
```

with:

* `mean_coupling_gain`
* `amplified_lanes`
* `damped_lanes`
* `timestep`

This enables deterministic monitoring of coupling behavior over time.

---

## Validation

### Test Status

* **28+ tests passing**
* **21 existing kernel tests still passing**
* full replay stability confirmed
* invalid-state validation confirmed

### Coverage

Includes:

* neutral states
* amplification states
* damping states
* mixed-state lanes
* cyclic repeat behavior
* tuple-only outputs
* list-input ergonomics
* 100-step replay determinism
* frozen immutability
* invalid qutrit values
* helper consistency
* repeated exception determinism

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Pure Coupling

No randomness, no mutation, no hidden state.

### Replay Safety

100-step replay confirmed byte-identical.

### Explicit Failure Paths

Invalid qutrit states now fail deterministically with explicit `ValueError`.

### Additive Architecture

Baseline kernel semantics preserved.

### Stable Cyclic Behavior

Short qutrit vectors deterministically repeat across field lanes.

### Shared Semantics

All cyclic lane mapping logic now resolves from a single helper.

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* universe kernel untouched
* no TUI changes
* no verification changes
* no export bridge changes
* no new dependencies

This is a purely additive simulation-layer release.

---

## Forward Direction

This release prepares the simulation stack for the next major step:

* QuTiP adapter layer
* Qiskit benchmarking hooks
* qutrit field motif studies
* resonance / attractor experiments
* micro-universe lattice expansion

`v133.2.0` is the first truly interactive field-dynamics release in the v133 simulation era.

title: v133.1.0 — UFF Observable Probe & Rotation-Curve Law
body:
# v133.1.0 — UFF Observable Probe & Rotation-Curve Law

This release extends the micro-universe simulation kernel with the first physically interpretable observable law.

`v133.1.0` introduces a deterministic UFF-style circular velocity model and rotation-curve observation layer, enabling bounded simulation experiments with stable physical observables.

This release includes the full post-review hardening pass.

This is a simulation-layer only release.

No decoder, TUI, verification, or export-bridge semantics were modified.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to make the simulation layer scientifically expressive.

The new observable law supports:

* deterministic circular velocity curves
* stable non-negative clipping
* tuple-only immutable observations
* replay-safe float behavior
* robust edge-case handling
* ergonomic sequence-based public APIs

This creates the foundation for future:

* SPARC-style fitting workflows
* UFF parameter sweeps
* posterior experiments
* small-universe cosmology sandboxing
* later QuTiP / Qiskit adapters

---

## Added

### `src/qec/sims/uff_observable.py`

Deterministic UFF observable law and rotation-curve probe.

Introduces:

* `v_circ_uff()`
* `observe_rotation_curve()`
* `RotationCurveObservation`

The circular velocity law uses the deterministic form:

```text
base = V0 * sqrt(1 - Rc / sqrt(R² + Rc² + eps))
tweak = safe_base ^ beta
v = base * (1 + 0.2 * tweak)
```

with:

```text
safe_base = max(raw_base, 0.0)
```

to guarantee stable exponentiation.

All outputs are clipped non-negative.

---

### `tests/test_uff_observable_probe.py`

Expanded deterministic validation suite.

Coverage includes:

* deterministic replay
* non-negative clipping
* peak / mean correctness
* curve flatness scoring
* tuple-only storage
* frozen immutability
* invalid theta validation
* list input support
* negative Rc edge cases

---

## Observable Probe Layer

`observe_rotation_curve()` returns a frozen immutable dataclass:

```text
RotationCurveObservation
```

with:

* `radii_kpc`
* `velocities_kms`
* `peak_velocity`
* `mean_velocity`
* `curve_flatness_score`
* `timestep`

All collections remain tuple-only.

---

## Hardening Pass (Integrated)

### Fail-fast parameter validation

The public UFF law now validates:

```text
theta length == 3
```

before unpacking.

Invalid inputs now raise:

```python
ValueError("theta must contain exactly 3 values: (V0, Rc, beta)")
```

This significantly improves misuse diagnostics.

---

### More ergonomic API

Public function signatures now accept:

```python
Sequence[float]
```

for:

* radii
* theta

Inputs are immediately normalized internally to tuples.

This improves usability while preserving immutable internal state.

---

### Safe exponent-base fix

Critical domain-error hardening applied.

Previously:

```text
(R / (R + Rc + eps)) ** beta
```

could raise errors for negative bases with non-integer `beta`.

This is now safely clamped:

```python
safe_base = max(raw_base, 0.0)
```

This guarantees deterministic behavior across all tested edge cases.

---

### Edge-case validation

New tests cover:

* empty theta
* too-short theta
* too-long theta
* list-based inputs
* negative `Rc`
* repeated deterministic replay

---

## Validation

### Test Status

* **25 / 25 passing**
* deterministic replay confirmed
* negative-base safety confirmed
* sequence API verified
* edge-case hardening verified

### New Test Coverage

* parameter validation: 3
* list inputs: 3
* negative Rc edge cases: 2

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Stable UFF Law

Same input parameters always yield identical velocity curves.

### Replay Safety

Repeated calls produce byte-identical observable outputs.

### Immutable Observations

All returned observation structures remain frozen and tuple-only.

### Safe Edge Handling

Negative-base exponent risks now resolve deterministically without runtime exceptions.

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* no TUI changes
* no verification changes
* no export bridge changes
* no new dependencies
* simulation layer only

---

## Forward Direction

This release prepares the next phase of the v133 simulation era.

Likely next steps:

* SPARC-style fit harness
* parameter sweep engine
* UFF posterior probes
* qutrit field coupling
* optional QuTiP integration

`v133.1.0` is the first physically interpretable simulation-law release in the QEC simulation stack.

title: v133.0.0 — Micro Universe Simulation Kernel
body:
# v133.0.0 — Micro Universe Simulation Kernel

This release begins the QEC simulation era.

`v133.0.0` introduces the first native bounded simulation runtime: a deterministic micro-universe kernel designed for replay-safe state evolution, stable observable probes, and clean integration with the v132 simulation export bridge.

This release includes the full post-review hardening pass.

This is a Python-engine only release.

No decoder, control semantics, verification infrastructure, or Rust TUI components were modified.

---

## Overview

The purpose of this release is to establish a minimal but scientifically meaningful simulation substrate.

The new kernel supports:

* immutable universe state
* deterministic time evolution
* qutrit / ternary state channels
* stable observable probes
* byte-identical export compatibility
* runtime-stable float serialization

This creates the foundational runtime for future v133 work including:

* UFF observable laws
* SPARC-style fitting workflows
* qutrit field dynamics
* QuTiP / Qiskit adapters
* micro-universe sandbox experiments

---

## Added

### `src/qec/sims/universe_kernel.py`

Deterministic bounded simulation kernel.

Introduces:

* `UniverseState`
* `evolve_universe()`
* `to_simulation_export()`

`UniverseState` is a frozen dataclass containing:

* `field_amplitudes`
* `qutrit_states`
* `timestep`
* `law_name`

The evolution kernel is intentionally minimal and deterministic:

```text
new_field[i] = old_field[i] * 0.999
new_qutrit[i] = old_qutrit[i]
timestep += 1
```

The public API was simplified during review by removing the unused `dt` parameter.

This keeps the evolution interface minimal and semantically precise.

---

### `src/qec/sims/observable_probe.py`

Deterministic observable layer.

Introduces:

* `UniverseObservation`
* `observe_universe()`

`UniverseObservation` is a frozen dataclass containing:

* `mean_field_energy`
* `active_qutrit_count`
* `stability_score`
* `timestep`

Computed observables include:

```text
mean_field_energy = mean(x*x for x in field_amplitudes)
active_qutrit_count = count(qutrit_states != 0)
stability_score = mean_field_energy / (1 + active_qutrit_count)
```

This provides a stable observation interface for future simulation experiments.

---

### `src/qec/sims/__init__.py`

Simulation module initialization and stable package surface.

---

### `tests/test_micro_universe_kernel.py`

Deterministic simulation kernel validation suite.

Coverage includes:

* frozen immutability
* evolution correctness
* timestep increment behavior
* field decay correctness
* qutrit persistence
* observation correctness
* replay determinism
* export bridge compatibility

---

## Hardening Pass (Integrated)

### Removed unused API surface

The unused `dt` parameter was removed from:

```python
evolve_universe()
```

This eliminates dead public API surface and avoids ambiguity around timestep semantics.

---

### Centralized release constants

Version metadata is now defined as module-level constants:

```python
EXPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION = "132.5.0"
CREATED_BY_RELEASE = "133.0.0"
```

This prevents drift between tests, export artifacts, and release metadata.

Tests now reference these constants directly.

---

### Canonical float serialization

Export traces now use stable 17-significant-digit formatting:

```python
f"{value:.17g}"
```

This is a major determinism hardening improvement.

Previously:

```text
raw float stringification
```

could vary subtly across Python runtimes or versions.

Now control traces are guaranteed to remain byte-identical across environments.

This materially improves:

* replay determinism
* export artifact stability
* hash consistency
* cross-runtime reproducibility

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Immutable State

All simulation state and observations are represented using frozen dataclasses with tuple-only collections.

### Pure Evolution

`evolve_universe()` is a pure deterministic function with:

* no randomness
* no hidden state
* no mutation
* no system clock dependence

### Stable Export Artifacts

Simulation export traces now use canonical float formatting and stable release constants.

This guarantees byte-identical exports across supported Python runtimes.

### Replay Safety

Repeated execution with identical input state yields byte-identical behavior.

### Export Compatibility

Simulation states convert cleanly into v132.5-compatible export artifacts without modifying the export bridge itself.

---

## Validation

### Test Status

* **21 / 21 passing**
* deterministic replay confirmed
* observation layer verified
* export compatibility verified
* float serialization stability verified

### Coverage Areas

* frozen immutability: 2 tests
* evolution correctness: 7 tests
* observable probes: 6 tests
* replay determinism: 1 test
* export bridge compatibility: 5 tests

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* no TUI changes
* no verification module changes
* no new dependencies
* stdlib / existing stack only
* additive Python-engine release

---

## Forward Direction

This release establishes the simulation runtime foundation for the v133 series.

Planned next steps include:

* richer local field laws
* UFF-aligned observable kernels
* SPARC / rotation-curve probe integration
* qutrit field coupling experiments
* optional QuTiP / Qiskit simulation adapters

`v133.0.0` is the first step toward a deterministic control + simulation digital twin stack.

title: # v132.5.0 — Deterministic Simulation Export Bridge
body:
This release introduces the deterministic export infrastructure that bridges the QEC control engine to the upcoming v133 simulation suite.

The purpose of this release is to create a stable, immutable, replay-safe artifact layer for external simulation systems such as QuTiP and Qiskit.

This release includes the full CI hardening and code-review fixes.

No decoder, control semantics, or TUI changes were made.

---

## Overview

This release establishes the canonical simulation handoff boundary.

Future simulation systems will consume deterministic exported artifacts rather than internal control-layer state.

This cleanly separates:

```text id="l0i9fi"
control truth
→ export artifact
→ simulation adapter
→ physics validation
```

This is the final bridge release before v133 simulation work begins.

---

## Added

### `src/qec/simulation/export_schema.py`

Immutable simulation artifact schema.

Includes frozen dataclasses:

* `SimulationExport`
* `TransitionEvent`
* `FailSafeEvent`
* `ExportMetadata`

All structures use tuple-only collections and immutable state.

---

### `src/qec/simulation/export_codec.py`

Deterministic serialization and replay utilities.

Public functions:

```python id="7u2vkk"
export_to_json()
load_from_json()
compute_trace_hash()
with_computed_trace_hash()
validate_export_replay()
```

Features:

* canonical JSON
* stable key ordering
* tuple reconstruction
* replay validation
* deterministic SHA-256 hashing
* schema validation
* explicit export contract errors

---

### `tests/test_simulation_export_bridge.py`

Expanded deterministic replay and artifact validation suite.

Coverage includes:

* frozen immutability
* round-trip equality
* tuple reconstruction
* stable hashing
* replay byte identity
* invalid JSON
* missing key validation
* finalized export idempotency
* placeholder independence

---

## Major Hardening Fixes (Integrated)

### Fixed — Circular trace hash risk

Critical hashing issue fully resolved.

Previously:

```text id="6m5v7x"
hash included metadata.trace_hash
```

which created circular dependency and non-idempotent behavior.

This is now fixed.

### New deterministic behavior

`compute_trace_hash()` now normalizes:

```text id="6d5yr6"
metadata.trace_hash
```

to a stable sentinel value before hashing.

This guarantees:

```text id="mjlwmh"
same export
→ same hash
→ same finalized artifact
```

regardless of prior placeholder state.

---

### Added `with_computed_trace_hash()`

New canonical finalization helper:

```python id="fj4f34"
with_computed_trace_hash(export)
```

This removes the need for callers to manually manage placeholder reconstruction.

This is now the recommended artifact finalization path.

---

### Added explicit schema validation

`load_from_json()` now performs deterministic contract validation.

Malformed artifacts now raise:

```python id="27w23j"
ExportSchemaError
```

instead of generic `KeyError` / `TypeError`.

Examples:

```text id="9y6mjt"
missing metadata keys
invalid nested dict shape
malformed fail-safe events
invalid transition payloads
```

This significantly improves debugging and long-term schema evolution safety.

---

### Centralized schema keys

Required key sets are now centralized using `frozenset` constants.

Examples:

```python id="2txwn4"
_EXPORT_REQUIRED_KEYS
_METADATA_REQUIRED_KEYS
_TRANSITION_REQUIRED_KEYS
_FAILSAFE_REQUIRED_KEYS
```

This reduces schema drift risk while preserving explicit field mapping.

---

## Deterministic Guarantees

### Canonical JSON

Serialization guarantees:

```text id="y2qptw"
sort_keys=True
compact separators
ASCII-only
```

Byte-identical replay preserved.

---

### Stable Hashing

Trace hashing is now fully idempotent.

The following now always holds:

```text id="v6f6yt"
compute_trace_hash(export)
==
compute_trace_hash(with_computed_trace_hash(export))
```

This is a major correctness improvement.

---

### Replay Validation

Replay validation flow:

```text id="9md2yj"
export
→ JSON
→ load
→ JSON
→ byte identity check
```

100-iteration repeated replay stability confirmed.

---

## Validation

### Test Status

* **24 / 24 passing**
* CI green
* deterministic replay confirmed
* finalized hash idempotency confirmed
* schema validation confirmed

New tests added:

* hash idempotency
* double-finalize stability
* placeholder independence
* invalid JSON handling
* missing key failures
* non-dict metadata validation

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* no control-layer changes
* no TUI changes
* no new dependencies
* stdlib only
* additive Python-engine release

---

## Forward Direction

This release prepares the start of **v133 simulation infrastructure**.

Planned consumers include:

* QuTiP dynamics suite
* Qiskit benchmark harness
* micro-universe sandbox simulations
* bosonic / qutrit replay experiments
* physics validation workflows

The export layer is now considered stable infrastructure for all future simulation work.

title: v132.4.0 — Release Integrity & TUI Installer Verification
body:
# v132.4.0 — Release Integrity & TUI Installer Verification

This release finalizes the Rust TUI deployment surface and introduces a deterministic release-verification framework for installer integrity, version alignment, and artifact consistency.

The Rust TUI is now considered **feature-stable infrastructure** with independent versioning and a verified install flow.

No TUI feature logic changes were introduced.

---

## Overview

This release focuses on:

* deterministic installer validation
* release artifact synchronization
* version integrity tooling
* shared validation constants
* long-term TUI maintenance freeze preparation

This is the final major Rust TUI infrastructure touchpoint before shifting focus fully back to Python-engine research and simulation work.

---

## Added

### `src/qec/verification/release_integrity.py`

Production-grade stdlib-only verification module.

Provides deterministic integrity checks for release artifacts and TUI deployment surfaces.

### Public API

```python id="1jrcv0"
resolve_repo_root()
verify_install_path_consistency()
verify_binary_version_consistency()
verify_release_tag_alignment()
```

### Shared constants

The following constants are now centralized and reused across verification code, scripts, and tests:

```python id="nrxn3j"
REPO_SLUG
BINARY_NAME
INSTALL_DIR
ASSET_NAME
CANONICAL_CURL_URL
```

This eliminates drift across validation surfaces.

---

### `scripts/validate_tui_release_sync.py`

CLI validator for release alignment.

Validates:

* Cargo metadata
* installer script constants
* README curl command
* binary naming
* release tag alignment
* Cargo tag expectations

Supports independent TUI version validation:

```bash id="5bncmu"
python scripts/validate_tui_release_sync.py --expected-tag v106.0.0
```

---

### `tests/test_tui_installer_flow.py`

Comprehensive deterministic verification suite.

Coverage includes:

* install script presence
* shebang validation
* `set -eu` shell safety
* canonical curl path verification
* pipe-to-sh install flow
* idempotent config parsing
* release tag checks
* deterministic replay validation
* explicit Cargo file existence cases

---

## Hardening Pass (Integrated)

Full Sourcery + Claude Code review pass integrated.

### Fixed

#### Repo-root discovery deduplicated

Promoted:

```python id="h3uhr3"
resolve_repo_root()
```

to shared public helper.

Both:

* verification module
* sync validator script

now use a single implementation.

This prevents future layout drift.

---

#### Type safety improved

`IntegrityReport.results` now uses:

```python id="c2jlwm"
Tuple[IntegrityResult, ...]
```

instead of untyped `tuple`.

This significantly improves:

* static analysis
* IDE hints
* type checking clarity

---

#### Shared constants extracted

Installer / release constants no longer duplicated across tests and scripts.

All key values now resolve from a single source of truth.

---

#### Explicit missing Cargo.toml handling

When `expected_tag` is provided, missing Cargo metadata now emits explicit result:

```text id="agjxtq"
cargo_toml_exists_for_tag
```

This allows callers to distinguish:

* missing file
* version mismatch
* semver mismatch

This materially improves diagnostic clarity.

---

## Validation

### Test Status

* **28 passed**
* **5 skipped**
* deterministic verification only

Skipped tests are intentional runtime smoke checks:

* binary launch
* `--help`
* `--version`

These skip safely when `qec-tui` is not installed.

---

## Verified Install Flow

Canonical installer path confirmed valid:

```bash id="w01rj4"
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/QSOLKCB/QEC/main/tui/install.sh | sh
```

Verified alignment between:

* `README.md`
* `install.sh`
* `Cargo.toml`
* binary naming
* release sync tooling

---

## TUI Versioning Policy

The Rust TUI remains intentionally **independently versioned**.

Example:

```text id="p2uwix"
QEC core: v132.4.0
Rust TUI: v106.0.0
```

This decoupling is permanent and intentional.

Core repository releases do **not** require TUI version bumps unless the UI layer itself changes.

---

## Architectural Status

Rust TUI is now formally considered:

**feature-stable infrastructure**

Future changes are restricted to:

* critical bug fixes
* installer compatibility
* terminal rendering issues
* security updates
* version sync maintenance

All future intelligence, simulation, supervisory logic, and proof systems remain Python-engine side.

---

## Forward Direction

This release closes the Rust TUI infrastructure workstream.

Future roadmap focus shifts to:

* v132.x proof scaffolding
* deterministic trace export
* simulation adapter preparation
* v133 QuTiP / Qiskit simulation suite

title: v132.3.0 — Deterministic Dwell-Time & Mode Switching Layer
body:
# v132.3.0 — Deterministic Dwell-Time & Mode Switching Layer

QEC advances its formal supervisory control stack with a deterministic time-domain switching and safety layer.

This release introduces explicit supervisory mode guards designed to prevent oscillation, rapid unsafe switching, rollback chatter, and repeated mode thrashing while preserving byte-identical replay guarantees.

This version also includes the full post-review hardening pass from Sourcery + Claude Code.

---

## Overview

This release extends the completed supervisory foundation:

* **v132.1.0** — DFA supervisory synthesis
* **v132.2.0** — DPDA supervisory stack specifications
* **v132.3.0** — deterministic dwell-time + mode switching safeguards

The supervisory control engine now includes explicit time-domain safety laws and anti-chatter logic for stable mode transitions.

---

## Added

### `src/qec/control/dwell_time_guard.py`

Deterministic dwell-time validation layer.

Features:

* explicit integer arithmetic
* 64-bit unsigned wrap-safe monotonic timing
* non-negative timestamp validation
* fail-fast invalid input handling
* pure deterministic function semantics

Core API:

```python
can_switch_now(
    last_switch_time_ms: int,
    current_time_ms: int,
    dwell_time_ms: int
) -> bool
```

Switches are only permitted when:

```text
elapsed >= dwell_time_ms
```

---

### `src/qec/control/hysteresis_filter.py`

Deterministic anti-chatter dead-band logic.

Features:

* fixed threshold hysteresis
* `enter_threshold >= exit_threshold`
* explicit validation
* `Literal["active", "inactive"]` typing
* runtime mode validation
* stable no-oscillation dead-band

Core API:

```python
passes_hysteresis(
    current_metric,
    enter_threshold,
    exit_threshold,
    current_mode
)
```

This prevents rapid oscillation between supervisory states.

---

### `src/qec/control/mode_switcher.py`

Atomic deterministic switching pipeline.

Features:

* frozen `ModeSwitchResult`
* frozen `FailSafeState`
* class-level ring capacity constant
* latch-safe repeated block detection
* A↔B chatter suppression
* explicit reason-code outputs

Core pipeline:

```text
fail-safe
→ no-op
→ dwell guard
→ hysteresis
→ switch
```

---

### `src/qec/control/__init__.py`

Stable supervisory-control package exports.

---

## Switching Semantics

The deterministic switching pipeline now guarantees:

```text
fail-safe
→ no-op short-circuit
→ dwell validation
→ hysteresis validation
→ atomic mode transition
```

This ensures stable supervisory control behavior under repeated replay.

---

## Safety Guarantees

### Dwell-Time Protection

Prevents rapid mode switching before dwell-time expiry.

### Hysteresis Dead-Band

Prevents threshold chatter and mode oscillation.

### Fail-Safe Latch

Triggers under:

* ≥ 3 blocked switch attempts
* repeated rollback loops
* A↔B chatter pattern detection

Latch remains permanent until explicit reset.

---

## Hardening Pass (Integrated)

The full Sourcery + Claude Code patch pass is included in this release.

### Fixed

* closed unclosed fenced code block in `README.md`
* aligned hysteresis documentation with `>=` semantics
* converted `_RING_CAP` to `ClassVar[int]`
* added non-negative timestamp validation
* added `Literal["active", "inactive"]` typing
* added runtime invalid-mode validation
* strengthened latch-flip reason assertions

### Hardening

* improved fail-fast clock validation
* improved frozen dataclass identity semantics
* strengthened edge-case regression coverage
* improved latch transition correctness checks

---

## Validation

### Test Status

* **42 tests passing**
* deterministic replay verified
* byte-identical `pickle.dumps` replay checks
* wrap-safe timing validated
* latch-flip semantics validated
* frozen dataclass immutability validated

### Determinism Guarantee

Same input sequence now guarantees:

```text
same input
→ same switching trace
→ same output artifact
→ byte-identical replay
```

---

## Architectural Integrity

* decoder untouched
* `src/qec/decoder/` unchanged
* no dependency additions
* stdlib only
* additive supervisory-layer release
* no UI logic introduced

---

## System Evolution

This release prepares the end-of-v132 supervisory layer for:

* proof objects
* deterministic trace export
* replay artifact schemas
* v133 simulation adapters
* QuTiP / Qiskit simulation suite integration

The control engine is now ready for simulation-bridge preparation at the tail end of the v132 series.

title: v132.1.0 Deterministic DFA Supervisor Synthesizer
body:
Deterministic DFA Supervisor Synthesizer

Release Date: April 1, 2026
Author: Trent Slade — QSOL-IMC
ORCID: 0009-0002-4515-9237

Following the foundational temporal verification layer introduced in v132.0.0, this release marks the first full supervisory-control synthesis milestone in the v132.x roadmap.

v132.1.0 introduces a deterministic DFA-based supervisory synthesizer inspired by Ramadge–Wonham supervisory control theory, enabling the QEC framework to automatically derive a maximally permissive legal supervisor over discrete plant behavior.

This is the point where QEC evolves from verifying transitions to synthesizing legal control policy structure.

✨ Highlights
Deterministic DFA Plant Model

Introduced a new immutable plant model:

DFAStateMachine

Implemented as a frozen dataclass to guarantee:

immutability
deterministic replay
stable state definitions
safe transition schemas

The model includes:

states
transitions
initial state
safe states
controllable events
uncontrollable events

This now serves as the canonical discrete supervisory plant representation.

🧠 Supervisor Synthesis Engine

Added:

src/qec/analysis/dfa_supervisor_synthesizer.py

Core API:

synthesize_supervisor()
run_dfa_supervisor_synthesizer()

The synthesis pipeline performs deterministic fixed-point pruning over the plant.

Reachability Pruning

Unreachable states are removed deterministically from the initial state.

This ensures supervisory reasoning only operates on executable plant states.

Safe-Reachability / Nonblocking Pruning

States that cannot reach any declared safe state are removed.

This guarantees:

nonblocking legal behavior

for all retained states.

Uncontrollable Transition Law

Critical supervisory invariant introduced:

uncontrollable unsafe transitions
cannot be blocked

Instead:

source state becomes illegal

This correctly follows Ramadge–Wonham supervisory synthesis law.

Controllable Transition Blocking

Only controllable events may be blocked.

Blocked transitions are now emitted in stable deterministic schema form.

This preserves the minimum necessary intervention set.

Maximal Permissiveness

The supervisor preserves all legal behavior not required to be removed.

This release guarantees:

minimum pruning
maximum legal behavior

which is a major theoretical milestone for the framework.

📊 Stable Output Schema

Stable synthesis schema:

legal_states
blocked_transitions
maximally_permissive
nonblocking_verified
synthesis_score
synthesis_label

Top-level runner schema:

plant
synthesis
supervisory_ready

All schemas are deterministic and regression-tested.

🧪 Test Coverage

Added:

tests/test_dfa_supervisor_synthesizer.py

Coverage includes:

simple safe DFA
dead-end pruning
uncontrollable unsafe edge pruning
controllable blocking
maximal permissiveness
nonblocking verification
deterministic repeatability
schema stability
frozen immutability
empty DFA boundary behavior

Final test status:

24 / 24 passing

including temporal verifier regression tests.

🔧 Hardening Pass

Final cosmetic hardening applied before release:

removed overwritten dead code
removed unused imports
zero semantic changes
zero schema drift

Diff:

+1 / -9
🎯 Why this release matters

This is the first release in QEC that performs:

supervisory controller synthesis

rather than only analysis or verification.

This is a major architectural evolution.

QEC is now firmly operating as a:

deterministic supervisory control framework

for QLDPC systems.

title: v132.0.0 — Temporal Transition Verifier
body:
v132.0.0 introduces the Temporal Transition Verifier, adding deterministic runtime validation for supervisory state-transition histories and temporal safety invariants.

This release marks the first formal temporal legality and sequence verification layer in the QEC control stack.

✨ New
Temporal Verification Module

Added:

src/qec/analysis/temporal_transition_verifier.py

This new additive module introduces deterministic temporal verification primitives.

Core capabilities include:

legal transition-table validation
absorbing state enforcement
safe-mode latch verification
escalation-lock legality checks
oscillation detection
stable verification schemas
🧠 Public API

Implemented stable public components:

TransitionHistory
validate_transition_sequence
run_temporal_transition_verifier

All APIs remain fully additive and isolated to analysis layer.

No decoder edits.

🔒 Legal Transition Enforcement

Implemented exact legal transition table:

normal -> recovery
recovery -> normal
recovery -> safe_mode
recovery -> escalation_lock
safe_mode -> safe_mode
escalation_lock -> escalation_lock

Any edge outside this set is deterministically classified as illegal.

Examples now explicitly rejected:

normal -> safe_mode
normal -> escalation_lock
safe_mode -> normal
escalation_lock -> recovery

Illegal transitions are surfaced as:

illegal_transition_detected = True
verification_score = 1.0
verification_label = critical
🚨 Safe-Mode Latch Verification

Added strict safe-mode temporal enforcement.

Once safe_mode appears in history:

all following states must remain safe_mode

Any exit is treated as a critical violation.

This provides explicit temporal safety enforcement for fail-safe supervisory states.

🔐 Escalation Lock Absorption

Implemented absorbing legality checks for:

escalation_lock

Once entered, all subsequent states must remain:

escalation_lock

Illegal exits are deterministically classified as critical violations.

🔄 Oscillation Detection

Added deterministic oscillation detection for pathological two-state alternation.

Pattern:

normal
recovery
normal
recovery
normal

Now triggers:

oscillation_detected = True
verification_score = 0.5
verification_label = warning

Threshold requires at least 4 transitions / 5 states.

Shorter alternation runs remain non-triggering.

⚖️ Severity Mapping

Implemented exact temporal severity semantics:

valid + stable -> 0.0 / safe
oscillation -> 0.5 / warning
illegal / latch violation -> 1.0 / critical

Priority ordering:

critical > warning > safe
🧪 Tests

Added comprehensive regression coverage for:

valid legal sequence
illegal transition detection
safe-mode absorbing legality
safe-mode latch violation
escalation-lock absorbing legality
oscillation detection
deterministic repeatability
exact schema stability
frozen immutability
empty history behavior

CI status:

✅ Green

🚀 Architectural Significance

This release advances QEC from:

single-step supervisory control

to:

temporal transition legality verification

QEC now supports explicit runtime temporal safety verification across supervisory histories.

risk_delta =

title: v125.0.0 Threshold Hysteresis Controller
body:
v125.0.0 introduces the Threshold Hysteresis Controller, adding deterministic anti-flap control using explicit threshold windows and counter-driven persistence.

This release marks the first formal threshold stabilization and hysteresis orchestration layer in the QEC stack.

✨ New
Threshold Hysteresis Module

Added:

src/qec/analysis/threshold_hysteresis_controller.py

This new additive module introduces deterministic threshold-band control primitives.

Core capabilities include:

immutable HysteresisState dataclass
threshold-band classification
explicit anti-flap persistence
deterministic escalation / de-escalation
stable transition labels
controller-owned counter evolution
🧠 Threshold Bands

Implemented stable bands:

nominal
warning
critical
recovery

Bands are determined by explicit thresholds.

📈 Threshold Classification

Implemented deterministic score bands:

score < 0.40 -> nominal
0.40 <= score < 0.70 -> warning
score >= 0.70 -> critical

Recovery thresholds are explicitly handled in hysteresis logic.

🔄 Anti-Flap Hysteresis

Implemented explicit two-cycle persistence rules.

Examples include:

nominal -> warning

requires 2 consecutive warning-band observations.

warning -> critical

requires 2 consecutive critical-band observations.

Single-cycle transitions are blocked.

This prevents oscillation and control-state flapping.

🚑 Recovery Logic

Implemented explicit recovery precedence:

critical -> recovery

when score remains below recovery threshold for 2 consecutive cycles.

Recovery retains precedence over warning de-escalation.

🔒 Final Hardening Pass

Completed final controller-ownership hardening before tag.

Added internal additive helper:

next_state(state, score) -> HysteresisState

This moves deterministic counter progression and stale-counter reset semantics into the controller itself.

The controller now:

increments only relevant counters
resets stale counters
owns anti-flap state progression

This closes the release-hardening gap around externally managed counters.

🧪 Tests

Expanded regression coverage for:

no single-cycle escalation
nominal → warning after 2 cycles
warning → critical after 2 cycles
no single-cycle de-escalation
critical → warning after 2 cycles
critical → recovery after 2 cycles
recovery → nominal after 2 cycles
stale warning reset
stale critical reset
stale recovery reset
deterministic repeatability
transition label correctness
frozen dataclass immutability

CI status:

✅ Green

🚀 Architectural Significance

All public interfaces remain pure Python and dependency-free.

No external graph frameworks are required.

🔄 Deterministic SCC Analysis

Implemented fully deterministic SCC decomposition using an iterative internal traversal model.

Public API remains:

tarjan_scc(graph)

Internal traversal is now fully iterative to eliminate recursion-depth risk on deep graphs.

Deterministic guarantees include:

lexicographic node traversal
stable adjacency ordering
lexicographically normalized SCC tuples
stable final SCC ordering

This enables reproducible basin and topology analysis at scale.

🛣️ Escape Routing

Implemented deterministic shortest-path BFS routing from any start node to a safe node.

Escape routing guarantees:

shortest-path semantics
lexicographic tie-break ordering
stable tuple output
predecessor-map memory optimization

This provides explicit safe-basin reachability analysis.

critical basin → escape route exists?
⚠️ SCC Risk Classification

safe
warning
critical

sink SCC
+
no safe node

This formalizes topological control-risk semantics for basin lock and escape failure analysis.

🌐 Condensation DAG

This produces explicit component-level reachability structure over the SCC graph.

Used for:

basin reachability
sink SCC detection
escape-route planning
future safety automata integration
🔒 Hardening Passes

This release includes the full performance + robustness hardening passes before tag.

Graph Construction Optimization

Optimized build_state_graph() using:

setdefault()

while preserving deterministic sorted adjacency output.

Traversal Optimization

Removed redundant neighbor sorting inside SCC traversal.

Deterministic ordering is now guaranteed by pre-sorted adjacency lists.

This reduces repeated per-step allocation and sorting overhead.

BFS Memory Optimization

Refactored escape routing to use:

node queue
predecessor map

instead of storing full path tuples in the BFS queue.

This preserves exact shortest-path semantics while dramatically reducing allocation pressure.

Deep-Graph Robustness

Resolved release-blocking deep-graph recursion issue.

Recursive SCC traversal was replaced with a fully iterative Kosaraju-style internal implementation, eliminating:

RecursionError

for large or deep control-state graphs.

title: v122.0.0 — Hybrid Automata Layer
body:
v122.0.0 introduces the Hybrid Automata Layer, adding deterministic coupling between discrete control modes and continuous bounded system dynamics.

This release marks the first formal hybrid dynamical systems layer in the QEC control stack.

✨ New

Hybrid Automata Module

Added:

src/qec/analysis/hybrid_automata.py

This new additive module introduces deterministic mode transitions over bounded continuous values.

Core components include:

immutable HybridState dataclass

explicit hybrid modes

continuous-value evolution
deterministic mode switching
stable transition labels
typed step output schema
🧠 Hybrid Modes

Implemented stable discrete modes:

nominal
warning
critical
recovery

These modes form the discrete control state space for the hybrid automaton.

📈 Continuous Dynamics

Implemented deterministic bounded update rule:

current + drift_rate + 0.25 * risk_score

All outputs are clamped to:

[0.0, 1.0]

No randomness or async behavior is introduced.

🔄 Mode Transition Semantics

Implemented explicit threshold-based transitions:

< 0.3 -> nominal
< 0.7 -> warning
>= 0.7 -> critical

Recovery transition semantics:

critical -> recovery

occurs only on deterministic crossing below:

0.5

within step().

This logic remains unchanged through hardening.

🔒 Hardening Pass

This release includes the full semantic hardening pass before tag.

Non-Finite Clamp Safety

Resolved release-blocking non-finite trajectory issue.

_clamp01() now explicitly handles:

NaN  -> 0.0
+inf -> 1.0
-inf -> 0.0

This prevents silent corruption of hybrid trajectories during invalid arithmetic propagation.

Finite values remain bounded and unchanged when already in range.

Typed Output Schema

Added module-level:

HybridStepResult(TypedDict)

This formalizes the stable step output schema while preserving field names exactly.

Fields include:

previous_mode
next_mode
continuous_value
mode_transition
hybrid_stable
Performance / Structural Cleanup

Added internal:

_transition_mode_unchecked()

to eliminate redundant per-step validation.

Transition labels are now lifted into stable module-level constant:

TRANSITION_LABELS

to avoid repeated mapping reconstruction.

nominal remains nominal

warning → critical

recovery → nominal
clamp bounds

title: v121.0.0 — Deterministic Law Engine
body:
v121.0.0 introduces the Deterministic Law Engine, establishing the policy and governance layer above QEC’s proof and control stack.

This release provides deterministic rule evaluation, priority-based law execution, and explicit policy action synthesis.

✨ New
Law Engine Module

src/qec/analysis/law_engine.py

The Law structure includes:

law_id
priority
condition

enabled
🧠 Deterministic Rule Ordering

Implemented fully deterministic law ordering:

descending priority
then lexicographic law_id

Sort key:

(-priority, law_id)

This guarantees stable rule execution across repeated runs and CI environments.

⚖️ First-Match Forward Chaining

Implemented deterministic first-match short-circuit evaluation.

The engine:

evaluates enabled laws in deterministic order
executes only the first matching law
stops immediately after execution

This preserves bounded, explicit policy semantics.

🔒 Purity Safeguards

Added input-state purity protection.

State is deep-copied before:

condition evaluation
action execution

This ensures law evaluation does not mutate upstream control snapshots.

🔧 Hardening Pass

This release includes the full semantic hardening pass before tag.

Output Schema Clarification

Renamed output field:

matched_laws

to:

first_matched_law_id

This aligns the output schema with actual engine semantics.

Because execution uses first-match short-circuiting, only a single matching law can be reported.

The new field explicitly reflects this behavior.

Execution Logic Stability

No control-flow or execution semantics were changed.

The following remain unchanged:

deterministic sorting
enabled-law filtering
first-match short-circuit
evaluation trace stability

title: v119.0.0 — Timed Controller Synthesis

v119.0.0 introduces the deterministic Timed Controller Synthesis layer, extending QEC’s supervisory control stack with bounded persistence timing, guard evaluation, explicit control-action synthesis, and finite-state timed transition semantics.

This release marks the transition from instantaneous supervisory analysis into time-aware deterministic control behavior.

✨ New
Timed Controller Synthesis Module

src/qec/analysis/timed_controller_synthesis.py

RECOVERY_PERSISTENCE_THRESHOLD

All counters are bounded by:

MAX_TIMER_VALUE

This ensures constant-memory deterministic timing behavior.

Example control semantics:

warning persists -> stabilize
critical persists -> intervene
post-escalation stabilization -> recover

This establishes the first formal time-aware control law in the QEC supervisory stack.

🧠 Control Action Synthesis

Implemented deterministic action precedence:

critical > warning > recovery > observe

This ensures explicit, unambiguous controller behavior.

Actions are synthesized solely from:

bounded persistence counters
guard thresholds

No async, scheduler, or thread dependencies are introduced.

🔄 Finite-State Transition Semantics

TIMED_TRANSITIONS

remain_observe

This continues the explicit FSM discipline established in prior releases.

🔒 Hardening Pass

Recovery Timer Gating

Resolved release-blocking recovery timing semantics.

Recovery persistence is now correctly gated to post-escalation states only, preventing healthy steady-state operation from drifting into recovery mode.

This preserves correct normal behavior:

healthy stream -> remain_observe

and ensures recovery semantics only activate following prior warning or critical episodes.

Dead Branch Cleanup

from the controller action set.
