Only repairs that produce positive threshold improvements are recorded.

Mutation Operator Integration

Atlas lookup and repair selection now occur inside:

NBEigenvectorFlowMutator.mutate(...)

The mutator emits metadata for each candidate:

defect_signature
atlas_hit
atlas_pattern_index
repair_action

This keeps mutation decisions inside the mutation operator and simplifies threshold_search.

Mutation Context Interface

Mutation operators now accept:

context: dict[str, Any] | None

This lightweight interface allows mutation features to be added without modifying method signatures, reducing merge conflicts and improving extensibility.

Testing

New determinism regression tests validate:

atlas signature generation

deterministic lookup and repair reuse

canonical JSON persistence

mutator metadata emission

Integration tests confirm correct behavior within the spectral threshold search pipeline.

Impact

With this release, the discovery engine gains a persistent memory of spectral defect patterns, enabling deterministic reuse of successful repairs and improving Tanner graph discovery efficiency.

title: v32.0.0 — Spectral Surrogate Phase-Diagram Predictor
body:
release notes
v32.0.0 — Spectral Surrogate Phase-Diagram Predictor

This release introduces a deterministic surrogate model that predicts the full BP phase-diagram curve from spectral diagnostics.

Instead of estimating only a single BP threshold, the surrogate infers decoding behavior across a fixed error-rate grid and derives a threshold from the predicted curve.

Added

New module:

src/qec/analysis/spectral_phase_diagram_surrogate.py

Implements:

fixed PHASE_GRID

SpectralPhaseDiagramSurrogate.predict()

spectral_feature_vector(metrics)

surrogate_threshold(grid, curve)

Threshold Search Integration

The surrogate is available as an opt-in search mode through:

--enable-phase-diagram-surrogate

When enabled, threshold search computes:

spectral_phase_curve

surrogate_predicted_threshold

and uses the surrogate-derived threshold for candidate ranking and rejection.

Determinism

The surrogate is fully deterministic:

float64 arithmetic

fixed linear inference

deterministic rounding

deterministic threshold extraction

Testing

Focused tests verify:

deterministic surrogate behavior

correct threshold extraction

threshold-search pipeline compatibility

artifact serialization

title: v31.0.0 — Spectral Phase Predictor for BP Threshold Estimation
body:
release notes
v31.0.0 — Spectral Phase Predictor

This release introduces a deterministic spectral predictor that estimates BP decoding thresholds directly from spectral diagnostics.

The predictor reduces the number of BP simulations required during Tanner graph discovery by ranking candidates using spectral features.

Spectral Phase Predictor

New module:

src/qec/analysis/spectral_phase_predictor.py

Implements:

SpectralPhasePredictor
spectral_feature_vector(metrics)

The predictor uses fixed float64 coefficients and deterministic rounding to estimate the BP threshold from spectral diagnostics.

Threshold Search Integration

The predictor can be enabled using:

--enable-spectral-phase-predictor
--bp-evaluation-budget

When enabled:

spectral diagnostics
        ↓
spectral predictor
        ↓
candidate ranking
        ↓
BP evaluation (top-K only)

This significantly reduces the number of expensive BP simulations required during discovery.

Metadata

Candidate artifacts now include:

spectral_predicted_threshold
bp_evaluated

allowing analysis of predictor accuracy and BP evaluation coverage.

Determinism

The predictor is fully deterministic:

• float64 arithmetic
• fixed coefficients
• deterministic rounding
• no stochastic training

This preserves the reproducibility guarantees of the QSOL QEC discovery engine.

title: v30.0.0 — Spectral Trapping-Set Repair in Mutation Operator
body:
release notes
v30.0.0 — Spectral Trapping-Set Repair in Mutation Operator

This release integrates deterministic trapping-set repair directly into the spectral mutation pipeline.

Mutation Operator Integration

Spectral trapping repair is now executed inside:

NBEigenvectorFlowMutator.mutate(...)

instead of inside the threshold-search loop.

This aligns with the mutation-interface architecture introduced in v29 and centralizes all mutation logic within mutation operators.

Deterministic Repair

A new module:

src/qec/analysis/spectral_trapping_sets.py

implements deterministic trapping-set repair including:

• localization cluster detection
• stable induced subgraph extraction
• deterministic degree-preserving 2×2 rewiring

Metadata

Repair metadata is now produced inside the mutation operator:

spectral_cluster_size
trapping_repair_applied

Threshold search now consumes this metadata rather than orchestrating repairs directly.

Architecture Impact

Mutation pipeline now executes as:

mutation_operator
    ↓
    spectral trapping repair
    ↓
    NB flow mutation

This change simplifies threshold_search.py and reduces future merge conflicts.

title: v29.0.0 — Spectral Mutation Interface & Memory
body:
Release Notes
v29.0.0 — Spectral Mutation Interface & Memory

This release introduces a modular mutation architecture and deterministic mutation memory system for spectral Tanner graph discovery.

Mutation Interface

Added a minimal mutation interface:

src/qec/discovery/mutation_interface.py

with operator implementations:

SpectralMutationOperator
BeamMutation
NBEigenvectorFlowMutation

This isolates mutation logic from the threshold-search engine and significantly reduces future merge conflicts.

Spectral Mutation Memory

New module:

src/qec/analysis/spectral_mutation_memory.py

Implements deterministic mutation memory that:

• stores only positive threshold improvements
• maintains bounded FIFO history
• computes normalized float64 mode weights
• produces deterministic JSON artifacts

Artifact format:

spectral_mutation_memory.json
Threshold Search Integration

Mutation generation is now routed through mutation operators:

threshold_search
    ↓
mutation_operator.mutate(...)

This modularizes mutation behavior while preserving deterministic metadata and float64 flow computations.

Stability Fixes

During refactor stabilization several nearby issues were corrected:

• fixed signature mapping in nb_eigenmode_flow
• restored Bethe–Hessian helper wiring in spectral_frustration
• ensured CLI phase-diagram artifact directories are created before writing

Testing

Targeted tests confirm deterministic behavior and correct integration:

test_spectral_mutation_memory.py
test_multi_mode_nb_mutation.py
test_nb_spectral_annealing.py
test_spectral_threshold_search.py
test_qec_exp_cli.py
test_nb_eigenmode_flow.py
test_bethe_hessian_cache.py

All spectral mutation features pass their targeted tests.

Impact

This release establishes the foundation for advanced mutation systems added in later versions:

• trapping-set repair
• spectral defect atlas
• spectral phase-diagram predictors

title: v28.0.0 — Multi-Mode Spectral Mutation
body:
Release Notes
v28.0.0 — Multi-Mode Spectral Mutation

This release introduces multi-mode spectral mutation, allowing the NB-flow mutation operator to combine several unstable spectral modes instead of relying solely on the dominant NB eigenvector.

By combining multiple eigenmodes, the discovery engine can explore a broader set of structural mutation directions and avoid spectral local minima during Tanner graph discovery.

Added
Spectral Mode Selection

New helper module:

src/qec/spectral/nb_spectrum.py

Function added:

select_unstable_nb_modes(...)

This deterministically selects the largest-magnitude NB eigenmodes using stable lexicographic ordering.

Multi-Mode Flow Field

NB flow mutation now supports combining multiple eigenvectors using:

compute_multi_mode_flow(...)

Flow is constructed as a normalized sum of eigenvector magnitudes:

flow = Σ |v_i|

Normalization and rounding remain deterministic.

Spectral Search Configuration

New configuration options:

enable_multi_mode_nb_mutation: bool = False
nb_mutation_modes: int = 3

Defaults preserve existing behavior.

CLI Support

New CLI flags:

--enable-multi-mode-nb-mutation
--nb-mutation-modes

Example:

qec-exp spectral-search \
  --enable-nb-flow-mutation \
  --enable-multi-mode-nb-mutation
Mutation Metadata

Candidate artifacts now include:

nb_mutation_modes
multi_mode_flow_strength

These fields help analyze spectral mutation behavior during discovery.

Testing

New test module:

tests/test_multi_mode_nb_mutation.py

Coverage includes:

unstable eigenmode selection

multi-mode flow normalization

mutation determinism

compatibility with threshold search pipeline

Targeted tests pass successfully.

Impact

Benefits:

improved exploration of Tanner graph search space

reduced likelihood of spectral local minima

deterministic multi-directional mutation

enhanced observability of spectral mutation dynamics

title: v27.0.0 — NB Spectral Annealing Mutation Control
body:
Release Notes
v27.0.0 — NB Spectral Annealing Mutation Control

This release introduces spectral annealing for NB-flow mutation, allowing mutation strength to adapt dynamically to the spectral stability of the Tanner graph.

The mutation operator now scales the number of structural modifications according to the non-backtracking spectral gap, providing a deterministic analogue of simulated annealing.

Added
NB Spectral Gap Helper

New helper:

compute_nb_spectral_gap

located in:

src/qec/spectral/nb_spectrum.py

The function computes the difference between the two largest NB eigenvalue magnitudes using float64 precision and deterministic rounding.

Spectral Annealing Mutation Control

NB-flow mutation now supports adaptive mutation strength based on the spectral gap.

New helper:

spectral_annealing_strength(gap)

Scaling:

strength = 1 / (1 + gap)

This produces stronger mutation near spectral instability and smaller mutation for stable graphs.

Adaptive Mutation Size

Mutation size is now computed as:

mutation_size = base_mutation_size * annealing_strength

Edges are selected using deterministic ordering of NB-flow strengths.

Configuration Options

New SpectralSearchConfig parameters:

enable_nb_spectral_annealing: bool = False
annealing_base_mutation_size: int = 4

Defaults preserve existing behavior.

CLI Support

New command-line flags:

--enable-nb-spectral-annealing
--annealing-base-mutation-size

Example:

qec-exp spectral-search \
  --enable-nb-flow-mutation \
  --enable-nb-spectral-annealing
Mutation Metadata

Candidate artifacts now include:

nb_spectral_gap
annealing_strength
mutation_size

These metrics allow analysis of mutation dynamics during discovery runs.

Testing

New test module:

tests/test_nb_spectral_annealing.py

Coverage includes:

NB spectral-gap computation

annealing monotonicity

deterministic mutation size

artifact metadata verification

Integration tests with the spectral threshold search pipeline pass.

Impact

Benefits:

adaptive mutation strength based on spectral stability

improved search efficiency

deterministic annealing without randomness

enhanced mutation observability

title: v26.0.0 — IPR-Localized NB Flow Mutation
body:
Release Notes
v26.0.0 — IPR-Localized NB Flow Mutation

This release introduces IPR-localized NB eigenvector flow mutation, allowing the discovery engine to focus structural mutations on spectral localization clusters associated with trapping-set structures.

Instead of applying flow mutation across the entire Tanner graph, the new operator identifies regions where the non-backtracking eigenvector is strongly localized and restricts mutation candidates to those edges.

This improves search efficiency and provides stronger alignment between spectral instability and structural repair.

Added
IPR Localization Utilities

New deterministic helpers in:

src/qec/discovery/nb_eigenvector_flow_mutation.py

Functions added:

compute_ipr_localization
select_localized_edges

These compute the inverse participation ratio (IPR) and identify localized edge clusters using stable lexicographic ordering.

Localized NB Flow Mutation

The NB eigenvector flow mutator now supports localized mutation mode, restricting candidate edges to the spectral localization cluster.

New parameters:

use_ipr_localization
localization_fraction

When enabled, mutations occur only within the strongest eigenvector support region.

Spectral Search Configuration

New configuration options:

enable_ipr_localized_nb_flow: bool = False
ipr_localization_fraction: float = 0.1

Defaults preserve existing behavior.

CLI Support

New CLI flags:

--enable-ipr-localized-nb-flow
--ipr-localization-fraction

Example usage:

qec-exp spectral-search \
  --enable-nb-flow-mutation \
  --enable-ipr-localized-nb-flow
Artifact Metadata

Candidate artifacts now include:

ipr_localization_score
localization_edge_count

These fields provide insight into spectral localization during mutation.

Testing

New test module:

tests/test_ipr_localized_nb_flow.py

Coverage includes:

IPR computation correctness

deterministic localization ranking

mutation reproducibility

artifact metadata verification

Targeted integration tests with the spectral search pipeline pass.

Impact

Benefits:

improved detection of spectral instability regions

more targeted Tanner graph mutations

improved search efficiency

enhanced observability of spectral localization

title: v25.0.0 — Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Flow Mutation
body:
Release Notes
v25.0.0 — Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Flow Mutation

This release introduces a spectral-guided Tanner graph mutation operator based on the dominant eigenvector of the non-backtracking (NB) operator.

The new operator mutates Tanner graphs along unstable spectral modes, enabling physics-informed structural exploration of the code space.

This approach provides a deterministic alternative to random mutation strategies commonly used in LDPC discovery engines.

Added
Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Flow Mutation

New module:

src/qec/discovery/nb_eigenvector_flow_mutation.py

Implements a deterministic mutation operator that:

Computes the dominant NB eigenvector

Derives edge flow strengths

Selects edges via deterministic ranking

Performs degree-preserving rewiring via a stable swap pattern

The resulting Tanner graph mutation aligns structural changes with spectral instability directions.

Flow Diagnostics

The mutator returns diagnostic metadata:

flow_edge_index
flow_strength

These metrics enable experiment analysis, visualization, and potential integration with spectral predictors.

Flow strengths are stored with deterministic rounding for artifact stability.

Optional Search Integration

The operator is integrated into the discovery pipeline as an opt-in mutation path.

Configuration option added:

enable_nb_flow_mutation: bool = False

When enabled, the spectral search pipeline mutates Tanner graphs using NB-flow guidance before evaluating candidates.

CLI Support

New CLI flag:

--enable-nb-flow-mutation

Example:

qec-exp spectral-search \
  --rank-by-prediction \
  --enable-nb-flow-mutation
Testing

New tests added:

tests/test_nb_flow_mutation.py

Coverage includes:

flow normalization correctness

deterministic tie-breaking

mutation reproducibility

candidate artifact logging

The full discovery pipeline test suite passes with the new operator enabled.

Architecture Context

The discovery pipeline now supports both traditional and spectral-guided mutation strategies:

mutation (random / PEG / NB-flow)
→ spectral diagnostics
→ threshold prediction
→ candidate ranking
→ BP evaluation
→ Pareto archive update
→ predictor recalibration

NB-flow mutation provides a physics-guided structural search direction, improving exploration efficiency in Tanner graph discovery.

Impact

Benefits:

faster exploration of high-quality Tanner graph structures

spectral-guided mutation aligned with decoding stability

deterministic structural optimization

improved discovery efficiency without stochastic operators

title: v24.2.0 — Predictor Auto-Recalibration
body:
Release Notes
v24.2.0 — Predictor Auto-Recalibration

This release introduces automatic predictor bias correction, allowing the spectral threshold predictor to improve its accuracy using results from completed experiments.

The recalibration step learns the systematic difference between predicted thresholds and measured BP phase-diagram thresholds, applying a deterministic bias correction during search.

The feature is fully optional and disabled by default, ensuring existing experiments remain byte-identical and reproducible.

Added
Predictor Recalibration

New deterministic helper module:

src/qec/analysis/predictor_recalibration.py

Provides:

compute_recalibration_bias()
apply_recalibration()

The bias estimator computes the mean difference between measured and predicted thresholds using float64 precision and deterministic rounding.

Automatic Bias Correction

The spectral search loop can now periodically update the threshold predictor bias using accumulated experiment results.

Configuration parameters added to SpectralSearchConfig:

enable_predictor_recalibration: bool = False
recalibration_interval: int = 20

When enabled, the system:

Tracks predicted vs measured thresholds

Computes deterministic bias correction

Applies correction to future predictions

Recalibration Artifacts

Search runs now emit:

predictor_recalibration.json

Example:

{
  "bias_correction": -0.0034,
  "samples": 42
}

This artifact allows post-experiment analysis of predictor accuracy.

CLI Support

New command-line options:

--enable-predictor-recalibration
--recalibration-interval

Example usage:

qec-exp spectral-search \
  --rank-by-prediction \
  --enable-predictor-recalibration
Testing

New tests added:

tests/test_predictor_recalibration.py

Coverage includes:

deterministic bias computation

rounding stability

predictor correction behavior

artifact serialization

All tests pass with the full discovery test suite.

Architecture Context

The discovery pipeline is now:

mutation
→ spectral diagnostics
→ threshold prediction
→ candidate ranking
→ BP evaluation
→ Pareto archive update
→ predictor recalibration

The recalibration step allows the predictor to self-correct using experimental data, improving ranking accuracy over time.

Impact

Benefits:

improved threshold prediction accuracy

faster convergence to high-quality Tanner graphs

deterministic learning from experiment archives

zero impact on reproducibility

title: v24.1.0 — Predictor-Guided Candidate Ranking
body:
v24.1.0 introduces predictor-guided candidate ranking for spectral
Tanner graph discovery.

New

• Optional predictor-guided pruning
  - candidates ranked by predicted threshold quality
  - only top-K candidates evaluated by BP phase diagram

• Deterministic ranking keys
  - predicted_threshold
  - spectral_radius
  - candidate_index

• Artifact metadata
  - predicted_threshold
  - prediction_score
  - prediction_rank

• CLI extensions
  - --rank-by-prediction
  - --max-bp-candidates

Testing

New deterministic tests added for ranking stability,
ordering correctness, and BP evaluation limiting.

Note

Earlier versions of the repository reported test failures related to a
TrapMemory compatibility issue in NBGradientMutator. This issue was
resolved in v23.2.1:

https://github.com/QSOLKCB/QEC/releases/tag/v23.2.1

Any remaining test failures are unrelated to this change and originate
from pre-existing issues elsewhere in the repository.

title: v24.0.0 — Pareto Frontier Search and Spectral Threshold Learning
body:
v24.0.0 introduces multi-objective discovery and learning-assisted
threshold prediction for Tanner graph search.

New

• Pareto frontier archive
  - deterministic non-dominated graph storage
  - Pareto optimization across threshold, spectral stability,
    and convergence speed
  - canonical artifact: pareto_frontier.json

• Non-backtracking spectral threshold predictor
  - lightweight predictor based on spectral diagnostics
  - outputs predicted_threshold and prediction_score

• Spectral regression model
  - deterministic least-squares model trained from experiment history
  - learns mapping from spectral metrics to measured thresholds
  - artifact: spectral_threshold_model.json

• Spectral search integration
  - optional predictor filtering
  - optional Pareto tracking
  - optional regression-based learning

• CLI extensions
  - --enable-pareto
  - --enable-nb-predictor
  - --enable-learning
  - --min-predicted-threshold

Testing

New deterministic tests added for Pareto archive logic,
predictor stability, and regression determinism.

Note

Some existing tests in the repository fail due to
pre-existing issues unrelated to this patch.

title: v23.2.1 — Restore TrapMemory compatibility in NBGradientMutator
body:
v23.2.1 fixes a compatibility regression in NBGradientMutator
introduced by earlier trap-memory refactoring.

Changes

• Replaced legacy TrapMemory constructor usage with
  TrapSubspaceMemory initialized from TrapMemoryConfig.

• Updated trap-memory gating checks to use the correct
  configuration object.

• Replaced obsolete TrapMemory.canonicalize(...) calls
  with deterministic canonicalize_subspace(...).

• Added compatibility alias:
  TrapMemory = TrapSubspaceMemory
  to prevent future regressions.

Testing

• Verified imports for TrapMemory / TrapSubspaceMemory.
• mutation_nb_gradient module compiles successfully.
• pytest --collect-only succeeds.

title: v23.2.0 — Spectral Threshold Predictor and Fast Test Selection
body:

and fast deterministic test selection.

title: v23.0.0 — Spectral-Guided Tanner Graph Search Engine

title: v20.0.0 — Phase Diagram Experiment Orchestrator

title: v19.0.0 — Deterministic Experiment Registry & CLI
body:
v19.0.0 introduces a deterministic experiment registry and CLI,
turning experiments into first-class commands.

New

• Experiment registry
  - register_experiment decorator
  - deterministic experiment discovery
  - duplicate registration detection

• CLI runner
  - qec-exp list
  - qec-exp run <experiment>
  - qec-exp sweep <experiment>

• Built-in experiments
  - bp-threshold
  - spectral-heatmap
  - ldpc-search

• Metadata improvements
  - experiment_name included in experiment artifacts

• Developer UX
  - --dry-run support
  - deterministic registry ordering
  - script wrapper (scripts/qec_exp.py)

Testing

• Added registry tests for registration, ordering,
  duplicate detection, and module discovery.

Note

Full pytest suite currently blocked by a pre-existing
syntax error in tests/conftest.py unrelated to this release.

title: v18.2.0 — Deterministic Parameter Sweep & Hash Provenance
body:
v18.2.0 improves experiment reproducibility and introduces a
deterministic parameter sweep engine.

New

• Parameter sweep engine
  - deterministic Cartesian parameter expansion
  - sorted key ordering for stable sweep generation
  - seamless integration with hash-based experiment cache

• Improved experiment hash payload
  - includes experiment_callable
  - includes dirty_repo flag derived from git status
  - prevents incorrect cache reuse across experiments

• Metadata improvements
  - experiment_hash
  - experiment_callable
  - dirty_repo
  - canonical runtime environment fields

• CLI integration
  - scripts/run_experiment.py now supports sweep execution
  - backward compatibility with legacy experiment configs

Testing

• Added deterministic tests for sweep generation
• Added hash sensitivity tests for callable + dirty repo
• Verified cache reuse across sweep runs

Note

Full pytest suite currently blocked by a pre-existing syntax error
in tests/conftest.py unrelated to this release.

• Verified config sensitivity

Note

The baseline suite currently fails during collection due to

title: v18.0.0 — Deterministic Experiment Runner
body:
v18.0.0 introduces a deterministic experiment execution framework
for reproducible research workflows.

New

• Experiment metadata collector
  - deterministic metadata capture
  - git commit detection with fallback
  - repo version detection from pyproject.toml
  - seed, timestamp, platform, and dependency fields
  - deterministic JSON serialization

• ExperimentRunner
  - explicit NumPy seeding

• Artifact structure

title: v17.2.0 — Coverage-Aware Test Selection

This release further improves developer workflow while
preserving deterministic behavior across the repository.

title: v16.5.0 — Deterministic Spectral Entropy Annealing
body:
v16.5.0 — Deterministic Spectral Entropy Annealing

This release introduces deterministic temperature annealing for
spectral entropy scoring in the NBGradientMutator.

New

• Optional entropy annealing parameters:

    enable_entropy_annealing
    entropy_temperature_start
    entropy_temperature_decay

• Deterministic mutation-step tracking ensures reproducible
temperature schedules across runs.

• Entropy guidance now supports temperature scaling:

    T(t) = T0 * exp(-t / tau)

• Mutation logs now include entropy_temperature and delta_entropy.

Default Behavior

Entropy annealing is disabled by default, preserving the scoring
behavior introduced in v16.4.0.

Testing

Added deterministic regression tests verifying:

• entropy decay ordering
• disabled annealing behavior
• repeated-run determinism
• stable mutation metadata logging

All tests pass under pytest.

Entropy signals are applied during candidate evaluation and recorded

title: v16.3.0 — Deterministic Trap Subspace Memory
body:
v16.3.0 — Deterministic Trap Subspace Memory

This release introduces a deterministic trap subspace memory system
for the NB-gradient mutation engine.

New

• TrapSubspaceMemory module with canonicalized float64 vectors,
  sign-stable normalization, and bounded FIFO storage.

• Subspace-overlap similarity metric enabling detection of rotated
  trapping modes caused by near-degenerate eigenvectors.

• Trap-memory penalty integrated into NBGradientMutator candidate
  scoring pipeline.

Scoring Order

frustration guidance
→ trap-memory similarity penalty
→ spectral diversity guidance

Compatibility

• SpectralSignature now exposes compatibility aliases:
  nb_spectrum_magnitudes
  bh_negative_mass
  flow_ipr

This preserves compatibility with existing spectral analysis tools.

Determinism

All trap similarity calculations are deterministic and
rounded using the repository precision configuration.

Testing

Added regression tests covering:

• trap subspace rotation stability
• deterministic trap similarity
• FIFO eviction behavior
• spectral diversity compatibility

All tests pass:

pytest -q

title: v16.2.0 — Trap Memory Stabilization & Deterministic Flow Repair
body:
v16.2.0 — Trap Memory Stabilization & Deterministic Flow Repair

This release improves trap-memory robustness and fixes a fragile
deterministic comparison in the Non-Backtracking eigenvector flow tests.

Changes

• Trap-memory similarity upgraded to subspace-overlap metric |dot(v,u)|²
  improving stability for near-degenerate eigenmodes.

• Deterministic NB-flow aggregation test hardened using
  np.allclose(atol=1e-12, rtol=0.0) instead of exact equality.

• Analyzer instances now isolated per computation to eliminate
  stateful reuse artifacts.

• Canonical float64 normalization and deterministic sign conventions
  preserved for stored trap vectors.

• Added regression test verifying deterministic subspace similarity
  behavior for near-identical trap vectors.

Testing

All deterministic spectral tests pass:

tests/test_nonbacktracking_eigenvector_flow.py
tests/test_trap_memory.py
tests/test_nb_gradient_mutation.py
tests/test_spectral_frustration.py
tests/test_bethe_hessian_cache.py

Full pytest suite passes.

title: v16.1.0 — Adaptive Spectral Mutation Controller
body:
v16.1.0 — Adaptive Spectral Mutation Controller

This release introduces an adaptive mutation controller that dynamically switches between non-backtracking eigenvector flow mutation and beam-based search depending on the detected basin state of the optimization process.

The goal is to improve escape from deep trapping basins while preserving fast spectral descent when the optimizer is already making progress.

Highlights
Adaptive Mutation Controller

A new discovery controller automatically selects the mutation strategy based on basin diagnostics.

The controller routes mutation steps according to detected basin state:

free_descent        → Non-Backtracking Flow Mutation
metastable_plateau  → Non-Backtracking Flow Mutation
localized_trap      → Beam Search
converged           → terminate

This significantly improves exploration of Tanner-graph space by avoiding repeated local oscillations in trapping basins.

Deterministic Strategy Switching

The controller operates entirely deterministically using existing diagnostics signals:

basin state classification

spectral flow localization

trap persistence indicators

This ensures runs remain bit-reproducible across executions.

Integrated NB Flow Mutator Wrapper

The adaptive controller integrates the previously introduced NonBacktrackingFlowMutator while preserving compatibility with the existing NBGradientMutator engine.

Both mutation engines remain available independently.

New Modules
src/qec/discovery/adaptive_mutation_controller.py

Introduces:

AdaptiveMutationConfig

AdaptiveMutationController

NonBacktrackingFlowMutator wrapper integration

The controller selects mutation strategies based on basin state and records trajectory metadata for analysis.

Metadata Logging

Mutation trajectories now include adaptive-controller metadata:

mutation_strategy
basin_state
beam_width
flow_mode_index

This allows post-analysis of strategy transitions during discovery runs.

API Updates

The discovery API now exports:

AdaptiveMutationConfig
AdaptiveMutationController
NBFlowMutationConfig
NonBacktrackingFlowMutator
NBGradientMutator

These exports allow pipelines to enable adaptive mutation control without changing existing discovery code.

Tests

New deterministic regression coverage:

tests/test_adaptive_mutation_controller.py

Test cases verify:

correct strategy routing

deterministic repeatability

trap-triggered beam activation

preservation of Tanner graph invariants

consistent metadata logging

Determinism Guarantees

The adaptive controller preserves all repository invariants:

deterministic behavior

float64 numerical precision

canonical ordering of swaps

row/column degree preservation

binary Tanner matrix structure

No randomness is introduced.

Compatibility

This release is fully backward compatible:

Existing discovery engines remain unchanged.

The adaptive controller is opt-in via configuration.

Default behavior remains identical to previous versions.

Summary

v16.1.0 introduces a basin-aware mutation controller that dynamically combines spectral flow mutation and beam search.

This significantly strengthens the discovery engine’s ability to escape trapping structures while maintaining deterministic, reproducible optimization behavior.

title: v16.0.0 — Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Flow Mutation
body:
v16.0.0 — Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Flow Mutation

This release introduces a new discovery operator based on non-backtracking eigenvector flow, enabling Tanner graph mutations guided by unstable spectral transport modes.

NonBacktrackingFlowMutator

Added a new opt-in discovery mutator:

NonBacktrackingFlowMutator

controlled via:

NBFlowMutationConfig(enabled=False)

The mutator constructs a flow field from unstable NB eigenmodes and performs deterministic swap mutations aligned with this flow.

Mutation pipeline:

NB operator
↓
unstable eigenvectors
↓
edge flow field
↓
flow-aligned swaps

This effectively turns the discovery engine into a spectral gradient descent mechanism over Tanner graphs.

NB Mode Stabilization

Flow construction is now stabilized for near-degenerate eigenvalues.

When the top two NB eigenvalues are nearly equal, their eigenvectors are deterministically blended:

v = normalize(v1 + v2)

followed by canonical phase normalization.

This prevents eigenvector flips across runs and ensures deterministic flow fields.

Spectral Frustration API Compatibility

Restored the class-based interface expected by existing modules:

SpectralFrustrationConfig
SpectralFrustrationResult
SpectralFrustrationAnalyzer

The compatibility wrapper delegates to the current functional implementation while preserving deterministic float64 behavior.

Deterministic Result Comparison

Added an equality implementation for SpectralFrustrationResult to prevent ambiguous NumPy comparisons in regression tests.

Discovery API

The new mutator and configuration are exported via:

src/qec/discovery/api.py

Existing discovery engines remain unchanged unless explicitly enabled.

Testing

Added deterministic regression tests covering:

NB flow mutation determinism

Tanner degree preservation

localized cycle targeting

eigenmode degeneracy stability

Example test runs:

pytest -q tests/test_nb_flow_mutation.py
pytest -q tests/test_nb_flow_degeneracy.py
pytest -q tests/test_nonbacktracking_eigenvector_flow.py

Repository-wide test collection errors related to spectral frustration imports have been resolved.

title: v15.4.0 — Spectral Frustration Engine Optimizations
body:
v15.4.0 — Spectral Frustration Engine Optimizations

This release focuses on performance improvements to spectral frustration evaluation and Bethe–Hessian construction, significantly reducing overhead in swap scoring and beam search.

Optimized Bethe–Hessian Construction

Added dense fast-path support so existing np.ndarray Tanner matrices avoid unnecessary sparse conversion.

Switched Bethe–Hessian assembly to in-place arithmetic to reduce temporary allocations.

Improved builder lifecycle with cached identity and degree state.

BetheHessianBuilder Improvements

The builder now:

caches adjacency and identity matrices

reuses a trial adjacency buffer for swap evaluation

performs incremental degree updates instead of full recomputation

assembles H in-place for reduced memory pressure

These changes significantly reduce overhead during beam search and candidate evaluation loops.

Spectral Frustration Evaluation

spectral_frustration_count now:

supports an optional flow_field=None fast path

preserves deterministic candidate evaluation via sorted swaps

improves performance during repeated swap scoring.

NB Gradient Mutation Improvements

Precomputed per-check non-neighbor sets to avoid repeated membership scans during swap enumeration.

Added trap mode cap (MAX_TRAP_MODES = 32) to prevent unbounded memory growth during long discovery runs.

Determinism Tests

Added new regression tests ensuring:

baseline-only spectral evaluation behaves correctly

empty swap lists produce deterministic results

candidate swap order does not affect scoring

Testing
pytest -q tests/test_bethe_hessian_fast.py
pytest -q tests/test_spectral_frustration.py
pytest -q tests/test_bethe_hessian_cache.py
pytest -q tests/test_frustration_guided_optimizer.py
pytest -q tests/test_nb_gradient_mutation.py
pytest -q

All tests pass.

Why v15.4.0 matters

This release improves the performance of the spectral frustration scoring pipeline, which is one of the hottest paths in Tanner-graph discovery.

These optimizations reduce overhead in:

candidate swap evaluation

beam search scoring

frustration-guided optimization

while preserving full determinism and numerical stability.

title: v15.3.0 — Fast Bethe–Hessian Inertia Evaluation
body:
v15.3.0 — Fast Bethe–Hessian Inertia Evaluation

This release introduces a fast Bethe–Hessian builder for repeated spectral frustration evaluations.

Added

New utility module:

src/qec/analysis/bethe_hessian_fast.py

Includes BetheHessianBuilder, which caches:

adjacency A
scalar r
scalar r²
identity matrix I

allowing efficient Bethe–Hessian reconstruction after candidate swaps.

Fast Swap Evaluation

The builder supports:

build(A)
build_after_swap(ci, vi, cj, vj)

which enables deterministic Bethe–Hessian updates without recreating identity matrices or scaling terms.

Spectral Frustration Integration

spectral_frustration.py now supports a cached candidate evaluation path using BetheHessianBuilder.

Performance

This reduces repeated Bethe–Hessian construction overhead during candidate swap evaluation while preserving deterministic behavior.

Testing

Added tests verifying:

fast vs full Bethe–Hessian equivalence

swap-update correctness

inertia/frustration equivalence

deterministic repeated evaluation

All tests pass:

pytest -q

title: v15.2.0 — Bethe–Hessian Cache Optimization
body:
v15.2.0 — Bethe–Hessian Cache Optimization

This release introduces a deterministic Bethe–Hessian caching system to accelerate spectral frustration evaluation during Tanner graph discovery.

Added

New utility module:

src/qec/analysis/bethe_hessian_utils.py

Includes:

build_bethe_hessian(A, r)

BetheHessianCache

The cache stores reusable components:

I
D
A
r
r²

allowing efficient Bethe–Hessian reconstruction after candidate swaps.

Swap Update Support

Added deterministic swap updates:

update_for_swap(ci, vi, cj, vj)

which updates adjacency and rebuilds the Bethe–Hessian matrix using cached structure.

Spectral Frustration Integration

spectral_frustration.py now supports an optional cached evaluation path:

use_cache=True

while preserving default behavior (use_cache=False).

Performance

For repeated candidate evaluations at fixed r, this removes repeated identity and degree structure setup and reduces Bethe–Hessian construction overhead.

The dominant cost remains LDLᵀ factorization for inertia counting.

Testing

New tests verify:

cache correctness

swap update correctness

cached vs rebuild equivalence

determinism across runs

repeated evaluation stability

All tests pass:

3285 passed, 5 skipped

title: v15.1.0 — Frustration-Guided Spectral Optimization
body:
v15.1.0 — Frustration-Guided Spectral Optimization

This release integrates spectral frustration signals into the discovery optimizer, allowing Tanner graph mutations to directly target topological defect structures.

Added

Frustration-guided swap scoring in NBGradientMutator.

New opt-in configuration parameters:

frustration_guided
eta_frustration
frustration_eval_limit
track_trap_modes

When enabled, candidate swaps are evaluated based on the change in spectral frustration score.

Spectral Frustration Analyzer

Added deterministic analyzer with:

Bethe–Hessian negative mode counting

localization detection via IPR

NB transport imbalance measurement

canonical eigenvector phase normalization

Optional trap localization via:

extract_trap_modes(H)
Optimizer Integration

Swap scoring now supports an additional term:

score += eta_frustration * ΔFrustration

Evaluation is restricted to the top-K candidates using frustration_eval_limit to maintain bounded runtime.

Metadata

frustration_before
frustration_after
delta_frustration
negative_modes
max_ipr

title: v15.0.0 — Spectral Frustration Counting
body:
v15.0.0 — Spectral Frustration Counting

This release introduces Spectral Frustration Counting, a new diagnostic for detecting trapping structures and topological defects in Tanner graphs.

Added

New analysis module:

src/qec/analysis/spectral_frustration.py

Provides:

SpectralFrustrationAnalyzer

SpectralFrustrationResult

Frustration score combines:

Bethe–Hessian negative modes

title: v14.5.1 — Optimizer Stability & Performance Patch
body:
v14.5.1 — Optimizer Stability & Performance Patch

This patch improves numerical stability, candidate evaluation efficiency, and memory usage in the spectral discovery engine.

Fixed

Prevented NaN propagation in basin-depth energy tracking when variable_flow is empty or non-finite.

Corrected Bethe–Hessian acceptance logic to iterate ranked candidates instead of terminating prematurely.

Improved

Reduced memory usage in basin-aware ranking by removing per-candidate storage of full trial matrices.
Trial matrices are now reconstructed only for top-K candidates during IPR refinement.

Optimized swap enumeration by computing non-neighbor variables using deterministic set difference instead of repeated membership filtering.

Consolidated swap application logic via _apply_swap helper to ensure identical behavior between ranking and execution paths.

Reduced redundant NB-alignment map lookups inside mutation hot loops.

Testing

Added deterministic regression tests verifying:

deferred IPR evaluation behavior

basin-depth finite delta handling

BH rejection path

candidate pruning determinism

All tests pass:

pytest -q

title: v14.5.0 — Adaptive Spectral Beam Search
body:
v14.5.0 — Adaptive Spectral Beam Search

This release improves the spectral discovery engine with adaptive search behavior and stronger guidance from non-backtracking spectral flow.

Added

Adaptive Beam Width

Beam search width now scales deterministically with basin depth using a sigmoid mapping.
Shallow regions use narrow greedy search while deep metastable basins automatically trigger broader exploration.

Basin Depth Analysis

New diagnostics module:

src/qec/analysis/basin_depth.py

Provides:

compute_basin_depth

compute_smoothed_energy_slope

Energy slope is smoothed using:

mean(last_k_energy_deltas)

to improve trap detection stability.

Beam Diversity Guard

First-step beam candidates now enforce deterministic edge diversity to prevent redundant swaps exploring the same edge cluster.

NB Eigenvector Alignment Bias

Optional swap-score bias using directed non-backtracking flow:

alignment = |p(u→v) − p(v→u)|

This guides rewiring toward edges carrying strong loop transport imbalance.

Integration

New opt-in configuration parameters in NBGradientMutator:

adaptive_beam
beam_min
beam_max
depth_scale
beam_diversity
nb_alignment_bias
eta_nb

Trajectory metadata now includes:

basin_depth
beam_width
beam_search_used
planned_sequence_score
planner_depth
Algorithm

Discovery pipeline now operates as:

basin diagnostics
      ↓
adaptive beam sizing
      ↓
diversity-filtered candidate beam
      ↓
two-step swap planning
      ↓
deterministic first-swap commit
Testing

New regression tests verify:

basin-depth determinism

adaptive beam scaling bounds

beam diversity non-overlap

NB alignment preference

deterministic planner outcomes

Tanner graph validity invariants

All tests pass:

pytest -q
v14.5.1 Patch Plan

This should address all Sourcery feedback.

Fixes
Swap enumeration explosion

Restrict candidate generation:

vj ∈ complement(check_neighbors[ci])

or apply deterministic cap.

BH acceptance bug

Replace:

if ranked[0] fails → terminate

with:

for candidate in ranked:
    if valid → accept
IPR recomputation bottleneck

Evaluate IPR only on top-K candidates.

NB flow caching

Cache per-graph flow during beam planning.

Dense fallback cleanup

Remove exception-based branching.

title: v14.4.0 — Two-Step Spectral Beam Search
body:
v14.4.0 — Two-Step Spectral Beam Search

This release introduces deterministic two-step beam search planning for Tanner graph rewiring.

The optimizer can now evaluate short sequences of swaps before committing to a move, enabling it to cross shallow spectral barriers that single-step greedy descent cannot escape.

Beam search integrates with the basin-aware controller introduced in v14.3.0, activating primarily in metastable plateau or localized trap states.

Added
Two-Step Spectral Beam Planner

New planning module:

src/qec/discovery/spectral_beam_search.py

Provides a deterministic planner:

plan_two_step_swap()

Algorithm:

enumerate candidate swaps

select top-K swaps

simulate second-step continuations

evaluate combined sequence score

commit the best first swap

Exploration is bounded using:

beam_width
second_step_limit

This keeps the search space small and deterministic.

Beam Search Integration

Beam planning is integrated into the NB gradient mutator.

New configuration options:

enable_spectral_beam_search
beam_width
second_step_limit
beam_activation_states
beam_score_weight

Beam search is disabled by default, preserving existing optimizer behavior unless explicitly enabled.

Basin-Aware Activation

Beam planning activates primarily when the basin diagnostics detect:

metastable_plateau
localized_trap

Normal descent remains unchanged during healthy optimization.

Trajectory Metadata

Mutation trajectories now include beam-planning metadata:

beam_search_used
beam_width
planned_sequence_score

This enables experiment analysis of planning effectiveness.

Testing

Added new deterministic tests:

tests/test_spectral_beam_search.py

Coverage includes:

look-ahead beating greedy swap selection

deterministic tie handling

mutator integration determinism

trajectory metadata correctness

Targeted test suites for changed modules pass successfully.

Implementation Notes

The beam planner:

reuses existing swap validity and girth logic

preserves deterministic execution

avoids duplicate mutation systems

operates on bounded candidate sets to maintain performance

Roadmap

The beam search planner enables further improvements in upcoming versions:

Version	Planned Feature
v14.5	Adaptive beam width based on basin depth
v14.5.1	Beam scoring cache + performance improvements
v15.0	Spectral frustration counting
Summary

v14.4 adds deterministic planning capability to the spectral Tanner graph optimizer.

The engine can now look ahead one additional step when navigating difficult regions of the optimization landscape, improving its ability to escape shallow barriers while preserving deterministic behavior.

title: v14.3.0 — Basin-Aware Spectral Flow
body:
v14.3.0 — Basin-Aware Spectral Flow

This release introduces basin-aware control logic for the Tanner graph discovery engine.
The optimizer can now detect metastable regions of the spectral landscape and apply deterministic escape strategies when local descent stalls.

v14.3 builds on the spectral engines introduced in previous versions:

v14.1 — Bethe-Hessian spectral gradient flow

v14.2 — Non-backtracking eigenvector flow

and adds state-aware optimization control.

Added
Basin Diagnostics Module

New deterministic diagnostics for tracking optimizer state:

src/qec/analysis/basin_diagnostics.py

Provides:

rolling energy slope estimation

non-backtracking flow localization (IPR)

edge reuse persistence

unstable eigenmode persistence

These signals are combined into a deterministic basin classification.

Basin State Classification

Optimization states are now classified as:

free_descent
metastable_plateau
localized_trap
converged

This allows the discovery engine to distinguish between:

healthy descent

stagnation

localized trapping regions

true convergence.

Basin-Aware Spectral Controller

New opt-in controller:

src/qec/discovery/basin_aware_flow.py

Wraps the spectral optimizer and adapts behavior based on basin state:

Basin State	Action
free_descent	standard spectral descent
metastable_plateau	exploration step
localized_trap	deterministic escape step
converged	terminate

The controller is disabled by default to preserve existing behavior.

Deterministic Escape Operator

Trap states trigger a deterministic escape strategy including:

canonical swap candidate enumeration

temporary blacklist of recently modified edges

limited candidate exploration

scoring augmented with flow localization penalty (IPR)

Tie-breaking remains deterministic via lexicographic ordering.

Trajectory Metadata

Optimization trajectories now record:

basin_state
trap_score
escape_triggered

This enables detailed analysis of optimizer dynamics during experiments.

API Updates

New public exports:

analysis.api → basin_diagnostics
discovery.api → BasinAwareFlowConfig
                 BasinAwareSpectralFlow

Integration required no changes to existing decoder or core modules.

Testing

Added deterministic regression tests:

tests/test_basin_diagnostics.py
tests/test_basin_aware_flow.py

Test coverage includes:

basin classification paths

deterministic controller behavior

trap-state escape activation

swap validity checks

degree preservation and binary matrix integrity

All tests pass for the new modules.

Implementation Notes

The v14.3 design preserves the repository's core principles:

fully deterministic execution

sparse-safe graph operations

minimal dependency footprint

modular analysis vs discovery separation

The basin controller is implemented as a thin wrapper around existing spectral engines, avoiding invasive changes to the optimization pipeline.

Roadmap

v14.3 enables more advanced planning strategies in upcoming versions:

Version	Planned Feature
v14.4	Two-step spectral beam search
v14.5	Adaptive beam width based on basin depth
v15.0	Spectral frustration counting

These features will further improve the optimizer's ability to navigate rugged spectral landscapes.

Summary

v14.3 introduces state-aware optimization to the spectral Tanner graph discovery engine.

The optimizer can now detect when it is trapped in a metastable basin and deterministically escape, improving robustness when searching complex code structures.

title: v14.2.0 - Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Flow for Deterministic Tanner Graph Optimization
body:
Introduces deterministic non-backtracking eigenvector flow for Tanner graph rewiring.

Key features:
- canonical NB eigenvector phase normalization
- deterministic directed-edge ordering
- multi-mode flow aggregation
- directed-edge → undirected pressure projection
- deterministic 2×2 swap optimization
- optional Bethe–Hessian acceptance check

Includes deterministic regression tests covering:
- phase normalization
- edge pressure projection
- swap determinism
- BH acceptance behavior
- optimizer convergence conditions

title: v14.1.0 - Deterministic Bethe-Hessian Spectral Gradient Flow
body:
Adds a deterministic Bethe-Hessian spectral gradient flow engine for Tanner graph optimization.

Key features:
- spectral instability energy E(G) = sum_{μ<0} μ²
- deterministic eigenspace handling via projector clustering
- deterministic degree-preserving 2×2 swap descent
- girth-aware swap filtering
