Includes full unit test coverage for:
- spectral energy correctness
- degeneracy stability
- deterministic behavior
- monotonic descent
- graph validity constraints

title: v14.0.1 Stability Fixes, API Cleanup, and Spectral Descent Improvements
body:
QSOLKCB/QEC v14.0.1
Stability Fixes, API Cleanup, and Spectral Descent Improvements

This release improves numerical stability, simplifies internal APIs, and fixes several determinism and performance issues introduced during the v14 spectral descent development cycle.

No architectural changes were introduced. The release focuses on making the spectral discovery engine more robust and easier to maintain.

Added
Shared Numerical Stability Constants

A new module:

src/qec/analysis/constants.py

centralizes numerical tolerances used across spectral algorithms, including:

MIN_EIGENVECTOR_NORM
MIN_FOHPE_DENOM

These constants replace scattered magic numbers and ensure consistent numerical behavior across analysis and mutation components.

Stable Analysis Export Layer

A centralized export module was introduced:

src/qec/analysis/api.py

This file now provides public helper wrappers including:

compute_nb_spectrum
compute_bh_spectrum
enumerate_candidate_swaps

The new API layer acts as a stable interface for analysis utilities and significantly reduces merge conflicts by preventing repeated edits to analysis/__init__.py.

Improved
FOHPE Perturbation Stability

The FOHPE perturbation scorer now guards against degenerate eigenvector overlap using the shared constant:

Δλ ≈ (v* ΔB u) / (v* u)

If the overlap |vᵀu| falls below MIN_FOHPE_DENOM, the perturbation is rejected to avoid unstable first-order predictions.

Nishimori Temperature Estimation

The Nishimori root estimator now detects near-linear interpolation cases and falls back to midpoint estimation before Newton refinement, preventing unstable polynomial root guesses.

Vectorized Localization Metrics

compute_edge_energy_map now uses COO sparse iteration and vectorized edge-energy computation, removing Python loops and improving performance on larger Tanner graphs.

Symmetric Sparse Adjacency Handling

Localization diagnostics now normalize sparse adjacency matrices to enforce undirected edge symmetry, preventing missing edge cases in sparse inputs.

Trapping-Set Parity Computation

Parity detection in trapping-set analysis now uses integer arithmetic rather than floating-point modulo operations, eliminating potential numerical precision issues.

Spectral Descent Improvements

Several fixes improve determinism and efficiency in the spectral descent engine:

redundant gradient recomputation removed

deterministic dual-mode scheduling

improved mode deduplication

stabilized swap scoring

A small degeneracy stabilization bias was also introduced:

severity *= (1 + ipr_localization)

This helps guide mutations toward localized defects when multiple Bethe–Hessian eigenvalues become nearly degenerate.

API Cleanup

deprecated use_hybrid_perturbation_scoring now emits an explicit warning

unused mutation parameters removed or clarified

internal experiment code updated to use public API helpers rather than private methods

Tests

New deterministic regression tests cover:

FOHPE degeneracy handling

symmetric adjacency enforcement

Nishimori fallback behavior

defect caching reuse

spectral descent dual-mode determinism

All tests pass successfully.

Determinism

All algorithms remain deterministic:

stable eigenpair ordering

deterministic swap selection

explicit iteration limits

no stochastic components added

Decoder modules remain unchanged.

title: v14.0.0 — Spectral Descent Discovery Engine with Ihara–Bass Dual-Operator Guidance
body:
Release Notes
v14.0.0 — Spectral Discovery Engine

This release introduces a deterministic spectral descent engine for Tanner graph optimization, integrating non-backtracking and Bethe–Hessian spectral diagnostics to guide topology evolution.

The new engine performs iterative spectral analysis and mutation to reduce localized instability in Tanner graphs before decoder experiments are performed.

Added
Eigenmode Diagnostics

New analysis modules provide deterministic extraction of unstable spectral modes:

analysis/eigenmode_mutation.py
analysis/ihara_bass_gradient.py
analysis/defect_scheduler.py

Features include:

Bethe–Hessian and NB spectral analysis

unstable-mode extraction

localization metrics (IPR, participation entropy)

deterministic eigenpair ordering

severity-based mode ranking

Ihara–Bass Gradient Mutation

New gradient-based mutation guidance derived from the Ihara–Bass identity.

The gradient projects unstable eigenmodes onto Tanner graph edges:

g(i,j) = -r v(i)v(j)

Aggregated gradients identify candidate rewires likely to reduce spectral instability.

Dual-Operator Guidance

The mutation engine can optionally combine information from both spectral operators:

Non-backtracking operator
+
Bethe–Hessian operator

Gradients are combined as:

G = w_nb * G_nb + w_bh * G_bh

with default weights:

w_nb = 1.0
w_bh = 0.5

This improves mutation targeting in regions where BP instability and free-energy curvature differ.

Spectral Descent Engine

New module:

discovery/spectral_descent_loop.py

implements iterative topology optimization:

extract unstable modes
→ compute spectral gradients
→ evaluate candidate swaps
→ apply best mutation
→ repeat

Safety guarantees include:

deterministic iteration cap

bounded candidate enumeration

monotonic defect-severity enforcement

Eigenmode-Guided Swap Selection

New module:

discovery/eigenmode_guided_swap.py

Implements deterministic scoring and selection of degree-preserving 2×2 Tanner graph swaps.

Defect Scheduler

The scheduler selects which spectral modes guide mutation using strategies:

aggregate
strongest_first
windowed
iterative_elimination

Dual-operator scheduling is supported when Bethe–Hessian and NB modes are combined.

Tests

New deterministic regression tests verify:

gradient determinism

eigenpair ordering stability

spectral descent monotonicity

deterministic dual-operator behavior

All tests pass under deterministic execution.

Determinism

All spectral descent operations remain deterministic:

stable eigenpair ordering

sorted edge iteration

deterministic swap selection

explicit iteration caps

Decoder modules remain unchanged.

Big Picture

With this release, the repository now contains a full spectral discovery pipeline:

Tanner graph
→ spectral diagnostics
→ eigenmode gradients
→ topology mutation
→ spectral descent

This transforms the project from a Tanner graph generator into a physics-inspired spectral optimization engine for LDPC discovery.

title: QSOLKCB/QEC — v13.3.0 Bethe–Hessian Trapping-Set Classification and Spectral Defect Catalog
body:
QSOLKCB/QEC — v13.3.0
Bethe–Hessian Trapping-Set Classification and Spectral Defect Catalog

This release introduces a deterministic spectral defect detection and classification framework for Tanner graphs based on Bethe–Hessian analysis and localization diagnostics.

The new analysis pipeline detects localized unstable spectral modes, extracts their induced Tanner subgraphs, computes trapping-set parameters, and classifies the resulting structures into interpretable defect categories.

These diagnostics provide a structured view of spectral instability in Tanner graphs and form the final analysis layer needed before the repository’s transition to a full spectral discovery engine in the v14 series.

Added
Stable Analysis Export Layer

A new module:

src/qec/analysis/api.py

provides a stable export surface for analysis utilities.

analysis/__init__.py now imports exclusively from this API layer:

from .api import *

This reduces future merge conflicts and decouples discovery modules from internal analysis implementations.

Spectral Defect Detection Pipeline

New module:

src/qec/analysis/defect_catalog.py

Introduces the SpectralDefect data structure and the deterministic pipeline:

detect_spectral_defects(H_pc)

The detection pipeline performs:

Bethe–Hessian construction
→ negative eigenmode extraction
→ localization-based support detection
→ induced subgraph extraction
→ trapping-set classification
→ deterministic severity sorting

Each detected defect records:

eigenvalue

severity score

support nodes

trapping parameters (a, b)

classification label

Trapping-Set Classification

New classifier module:

src/qec/analysis/trapping_set_classifier.py

Localized spectral defects are categorized into:

SMALL_TRAPPING_SET
CYCLE_CLUSTER
PSEUDO_CODEWORD_BASIN
BENIGN_GLOBAL_MODE

Classification uses induced support subgraphs and trapping-set (a, b) parameters derived from Tanner graph structure.

Support Subgraph Extraction

New module:

src/qec/analysis/subgraph_extractor.py

Provides deterministic extraction of Tanner subgraphs induced by spectral support nodes and computation of trapping-set parameters (a, b).

Optional Defect-Guided Mutation

Mutation scoring can optionally incorporate spectral defect information using:

use_defect_guided_scoring
defect_coupling_alpha
defect_scan_steps

When enabled, mutation scoring prioritizes candidate swaps near localized spectral defects.

Default mutation behavior remains unchanged unless explicitly enabled.

Diagnostic Experiment

New demonstration script:

experiments/trapping_set_classifier_demo.py

The script:

constructs the Bethe–Hessian matrix

extracts unstable eigenmodes

detects localized spectral defects

classifies trapping-set structures

prints a deterministic diagnostic summary

Tests

Added deterministic tests covering:

spectral defect ordering

support subgraph extraction

trapping-set (a, b) computation

classification stability

defect-guided mutation determinism

All tests pass under deterministic execution.

Determinism

All newly introduced diagnostics remain deterministic:

stable eigenvalue ordering

deterministic support extraction

deterministic defect catalog sorting

Decoder modules remain unchanged.

Big Picture

With this release the repository now contains a complete spectral diagnostic stack:

non-backtracking diagnostics
+
Bethe–Hessian stability analysis
+
spectral localization metrics
+
trapping-set classification

These tools enable systematic identification of localized instability structures in Tanner graphs and prepare the codebase for the upcoming spectral discovery engine planned for the v14 series.

title: v13.2.0 — Bethe-Hessian Spectral Diagnostics and Localization Metrics
body:
Release Notes
v13.2.0 — Bethe-Hessian Spectral Diagnostics

This release introduces a new set of deterministic Bethe-Hessian spectral diagnostics and localization metrics for analyzing Tanner graph instability.

The new analysis tools provide deeper insight into trapping-set formation and spectral defect localization, extending the repository’s spectral diagnostic capabilities beyond the non-backtracking operator.

Added
Bethe-Hessian Analysis Module

New module:

src/qec/analysis/bethe_hessian.py

Provides deterministic Bethe-Hessian spectral diagnostics including:

sparse-compatible Bethe-Hessian construction

smallest eigenvalue computation

deterministic Nishimori temperature estimation

The Nishimori parameter is estimated using quadratic interpolation followed by Newton refinement to locate the Bethe-Hessian stability root.

Localization Diagnostics

New module:

src/qec/analysis/localization_metrics.py

Introduces spectral localization metrics:

IPR (Inverse Participation Ratio)
Measures eigenvector localization strength.

Participation Entropy
Entropy-based localization measure.

Spectral Instability Score (SIS)
Combined instability metric derived from eigenvalue magnitude and localization.

Edge Energy Projection
Maps eigenvector energy onto Tanner graph edges to identify localized defect regions.

Bethe-Hessian Diagnostics Experiment

New experiment:

experiments/bethe_hessian_diagnostics.py

Computes:

Bethe-Hessian λ_min
IPR
Participation entropy
Spectral instability score
localized edge counts

and prints a deterministic diagnostic summary.

Tests

Expanded deterministic test coverage for spectral diagnostics:

Bethe-Hessian smallest eigenvalue behavior

sparse adjacency compatibility

deterministic Nishimori root estimation

IPR behavior and bounds

participation entropy stability

deterministic edge-energy projection

spectral instability score correctness and entropy-zero safety

All tests pass under deterministic execution.

Determinism

All newly introduced diagnostics are deterministic:

no random initialization

stable eigenvalue computation

deterministic experiments

Decoder modules remain unchanged.

Big Picture

This release expands the repository’s spectral diagnostic layer with Bethe-Hessian stability analysis and localization metrics.

Combined with earlier releases introducing non-backtracking diagnostics and perturbation-based mutation scoring, the project now contains a growing set of tools for analyzing spectral instability in Tanner graphs before decoding experiments are run.

These diagnostics will support future releases that integrate spectral localization signals into graph mutation and discovery workflows.

title: v13.1.0 FOHPE Perturbation Scoring for Spectral Tanner Graph Discovery
body:
QSOLKCB/QEC — v13.1.0
FOHPE Perturbation Scoring for Spectral Tanner Graph Discovery

This release introduces deterministic first-order spectral perturbation scoring for Tanner graph mutation, allowing the discovery engine to estimate the spectral impact of candidate rewires without recomputing the full non-backtracking spectrum.

The new NBPerturbationScorer implements a FOHPE-style estimator for predicting eigenvalue shifts under small graph perturbations, enabling a hybrid mutation workflow that combines fast perturbation ranking with exact spectral verification.

This significantly improves the efficiency of the spectral mutation engine while preserving deterministic behavior and compatibility with the existing discovery pipeline.

Added
Deterministic FOHPE Perturbation Scorer

New module:

src/qec/analysis/nb_perturbation_scorer.py

Implements a deterministic First-Order Hashimoto Perturbation Estimator (FOHPE) for estimating the spectral impact of candidate Tanner graph rewires.

Features:

first-order eigenvalue shift estimation

correct FOHPE normalization using ⟨v,u⟩ denominator

L2-normalized left/right eigenvectors

explicit invalid perturbation detection

sparse-compatible scoring

deterministic behavior with no randomness

Invalid or degenerate perturbations return None, allowing callers to safely skip unstable predictions.

Hybrid Perturbation-Assisted Mutation

NBEigenmodeMutation now supports optional perturbation-assisted mutation scoring.

When enabled:

NB spectrum
→ FOHPE perturbation scoring
→ shortlist exact recheck
→ deterministic swap selection

Key behaviors:

invalid perturbation predictions are skipped

exact recomputation is used only when no valid perturbation predictions remain

shortlist size is deterministically clamped to avoid mutation-time failures

Default mutation behavior remains unchanged unless perturbation scoring is explicitly enabled.

Perturbation Scoring Experiment

New experiment:

experiments/nb_perturbation_scoring_experiment.py

This experiment compares:

FOHPE-ranked candidate swaps

exact spectral recomputation ranking

and reports ranking agreement between perturbation predictions and full spectrum recomputation.

Improvements
Dense Conversion Performance

The perturbation scorer now avoids unnecessary dense copies when the input matrix is already a float64 ndarray.

Sparse matrices are converted using toarray() rather than todense() to reduce overhead in mutation loops.

Tests

New and updated tests cover:

FOHPE normalization correctness

denominator presence in baseline state

invalid perturbation handling

deterministic hybrid mutation behavior

shortlist clamping behavior

experiment execution validation

All new functionality maintains deterministic behavior.

Determinism

All newly introduced code paths remain deterministic:

no RNG introduced

stable sorting and tie-breaking preserved

hybrid mutation fallback remains deterministic

Decoder modules remain unchanged.

Big Picture

With perturbation scoring integrated, the mutation engine can now estimate spectral effects of candidate graph rewires before performing full spectral recomputation.

This moves the project closer to a spectral discovery engine for Tanner graph construction, combining:

spectral diagnostics
+
first-order perturbation prediction
+
deterministic topology mutation

Future releases will extend this framework with additional spectral diagnostics and instability predictors.

title: v13.0.1 — Determinism Fixes, Review Cleanup, and Eigenmode Mutation Stability
body:
Release Title

v13.0.1 — Determinism Fixes, Review Cleanup, and Eigenmode Mutation Stability

Release Notes
Overview

v13.0.1 is a stability and review-consolidation release following the introduction of non-backtracking eigenmode mutation in v13.0.0.

This release tightens numerical semantics, improves mutation stability, and addresses code review feedback while preserving the deterministic behavior of the discovery engine.

No changes were made to decoder logic or default mutation scheduling.

Fixed
Eigenmode Analyzer Zero-Signal Semantics

Improved zero-signal detection in NBEigenmodeFlowAnalyzer.

The previous change to:

np.isclose(x, 0.0)

introduced a tolerance much larger than the original 1e-15 cutoff.

The implementation now uses:

np.isclose(x, 0.0, atol=1e-15, rtol=0.0)

This restores the intended epsilon-scale detection while retaining clearer numerical semantics.

Early-Exit Mutation Semantics

Improved safety and clarity of the optional early-exit logic in NBEigenmodeMutation.

Changes include:

validation that early_exit_improvement_threshold >= 0

explicit documentation that more negative key[0] values represent stronger improvements

removal of repeated candidate sorting during early-exit checks

incremental best-candidate tracking for improved performance

These changes reduce mutation overhead while preserving deterministic ordering.

Added
Deterministic Basin Switch Detector

New diagnostic module:

src/qec/analysis/basin_switch_detector.py

Implements a lightweight detector for metastable basin switching using non-backtracking spectral diagnostics.

This helps identify situations where mutation trajectories oscillate between nearby spectral basins.

The detector is diagnostic-only and does not modify mutation behavior.

Shared Deterministic Edge-Swap Helpers

New helper module:

src/qec/discovery/swap_helpers.py

Provides deterministic degree-preserving 2-edge swap utilities shared between:

mutation operators

robustness experiments

This removes duplicated swap logic and guarantees consistent mutation semantics across the codebase.

Improved
Mutation Stability

Eigenmode mutation search now uses:

incremental best-candidate tracking

deterministic ranking

consistent swap helper utilities

These improvements reduce unnecessary candidate sorting while preserving deterministic behavior.

Experiment Robustness

The eigenmode robustness experiment now uses deterministic bounded perturbations via shared swap helpers.

This guarantees:

structural invariants

consistent perturbation semantics

reproducible experiment results.

Determinism Guarantees

The deterministic design of the repository remains intact:

no RNG introduced

stable tuple-based ranking keys

deterministic tie-breaking

mutation invariants preserved

Decoder code was not modified.

Sparse compatibility remains unchanged.

Default mutation behavior is unaffected unless optional parameters are explicitly enabled.

Files Added
src/qec/analysis/basin_switch_detector.py
src/qec/discovery/swap_helpers.py
tests/test_basin_switch_detector.py
tests/test_swap_helpers.py
Files Updated
src/qec/analysis/nb_eigenmode_flow.py
src/qec/discovery/mutation_nb_eigenmode.py
src/qec/discovery/mutation_nb_gradient.py
experiments/eigenmode_signature_robustness.py

Additional deterministic regression tests were added.

Tests

All tests pass successfully.

pytest -q

Additional validation:

python experiments/eigenmode_signature_robustness.py
python experiments/eigenmode_mutation_experiment.py
python -m compileall -q src/qec experiments tests
Commit
de531de

PR created via make_pr:

v13.0.1: deterministic review-consolidation fixes for NB mutation/analysis
Context

This release stabilizes the architecture introduced in:

v12.9.0  trapping-set gating + basin steering
v13.0.0  non-backtracking eigenmode mutation

and prepares the repository for the next stage:

v13.1.0  first-order perturbation mutation scoring

which introduces a hybrid spectral mutation workflow.

title: v13.0.0 — Non-Backtracking Eigenmode Mutation
body:
This release introduces a new mutation strategy based on unstable
eigenmodes of the non-backtracking operator.

Key additions:

• NB eigenmode flow analyzer
• deterministic eigenmode mutation operator
• spectral signature diagnostics
• robustness experiment using bounded perturbations
• baseline vs eigenmode mutation comparison

The analyzer now correctly handles zero-signal cases, returning
support_fraction = 0.0 and topk_mass_fraction = 0.0 when no edge
pressure is present.

All changes preserve deterministic execution, sparse compatibility,
and do not modify decoder internals.

title: v12.9.0 — Trapping-Set Gating Fix & Spectral Basin Steering
body:
This release fixes an issue where trapping-set mutation penalties could
still be applied even when no trapping candidate sets were predicted.

The mutation penalty map is now constructed strictly from nodes that
appear in predicted candidate_sets, ensuring that empty candidate sets
produce no penalty steering.

Additional features include:

• deterministic trapping-set ranking
• spectral basin steering diagnostics
• trapping-risk vs FER validation experiment
• focused regression tests for gating behavior

Deterministic execution guarantees remain unchanged.
Decoder code is untouched.

title: QSOLKCB/QEC — v12.8.0  NB-Eigenvector Trapping-Set Prediction & Mutation Steering
body:
QSOLKCB/QEC — v12.8.0

NB-Eigenvector Trapping-Set Prediction & Mutation Steering

This release introduces non-backtracking (NB) eigenvector trapping-set prediction, enabling the QEC discovery engine to identify potentially unstable Tanner graph regions before decoding even runs.

The feature integrates directly with the repository’s NB spectral analysis stack and enables risk-aware mutation steering, phase-space visualization, and deterministic trapping-set analysis workflows.

This significantly expands the repository’s capabilities toward spectral-guided LDPC discovery and Tanner-graph dynamical analysis.

Added
NB Trapping-Set Predictor

New deterministic analysis module:

src/qec/analysis/nb_trapping_set_predictor.py

Computes NB-eigenvector-derived instability signals:

node_scores

edge_scores

candidate_sets

ipr

spectral_radius

risk_score

All floating outputs are rounded to 12 decimals for deterministic reproducibility.

The predictor identifies localized NB eigenvector regions associated with trapping-set formation.

Trapping-Set Visualization Utilities
src/qec/experiments/trapping_set_visualization.py

New visualization helpers:

render_trapping_sets_ascii(H, prediction)
plot_trapping_sets_matplotlib(H, prediction)

Features:

deterministic ASCII summaries

matplotlib plotting

automatic ASCII fallback when matplotlib is unavailable

Trapping-Set Prediction Experiment
experiments/trapping_set_prediction_experiment.py

A deterministic experiment for evaluating predictor performance:

compares predicted trapping risk with observed decoder failure behavior

produces precision/recall-style metrics

useful for validating predictor quality across Tanner graph ensembles

Phase-Diagram Risk Integration

Phase-diagram experiments now include trapping-risk metrics:

trapping_risk

Lower-case metric aliases were added for compatibility:

fer
ipr

Plotting utilities now support metric-selectable rendering.

Risk Heatmap Visualization

Added new helper:

plot_risk_heatmap(...)

Enables visualization of spectral phase-space risk landscapes, turning the phase-diagram system into a Tanner-graph instability explorer.

Changed
Gradient Mutation Steering

NBGradientMutator now supports optional trapping-risk guidance:

avoid_predicted_trapping_sets

When enabled, mutation scoring is adjusted using predicted instability:

score / (1 + node_instability)

Default behavior is unchanged unless the option is explicitly enabled.

Spectral Radius Simplification

NBTrappingSetPredictor now derives spectral_radius directly from the existing NB flow computation (max_flow) rather than invoking full spectrum diagnostics.

Benefits:

simpler implementation

avoids redundant spectral calculations

remains consistent with the repository’s NB eigenpair utilities

Phase-Diagram Plot Validation

Phase-diagram plotting now validates requested metrics and raises:

ValueError("Unknown phase diagram metric: <metric>")

This prevents silent plotting of incorrect or misspelled metric names.

Trapping-Prediction Caching

Gradient mutation now caches trapping-set predictions per Tanner graph instance:

self._trapping_prediction_cache[id(H)]

This prevents repeated NB analysis during multi-step gradient mutation.

Tests

New deterministic test coverage added for:

tests/test_nb_trapping_set_predictor.py
tests/test_nb_gradient_mutation.py
tests/test_spectral_phase_diagram.py

Coverage includes:

predictor determinism

sparse/dense input equivalence

rounding invariants

trapping-risk computation

mutation steering behavior

phase-diagram metric validation

caching behavior

Determinism Guarantees

No RNG introduced in new predictor, visualization, or mutation logic.

Floating outputs rounded to 12 decimal places.

Decoder core untouched (src/qec/decoder/ unchanged).

Existing experiment seed semantics preserved.

Test Results
pytest -q

Result:

3181 passed
5 skipped
0 failed
Summary

v12.8 introduces NB-eigenvector trapping-set prediction and integrates it across:

spectral analysis

mutation steering

phase-diagram exploration

visualization

deterministic experimentation

The repository now supports pre-decoding structural instability analysis, enabling new workflows for spectral-guided LDPC code discovery.

title: v12.7.1 — Patch: Spectral Fallback Consistency, Gradient Mutator Validation & Security Hardening
body:
v12.7.1 — Patch: Spectral Fallback Consistency, Gradient Mutator Validation & Security Hardening

This patch release finalizes the v12.7 series by addressing review feedback, improving numerical robustness, and tightening validation around the NB gradient mutation system.

All changes are backwards compatible.
Decoder logic remains unchanged.

Fixed
Non-Backtracking Eigenpair Fallback Consistency

The dense fallback path in:

src/qec/diagnostics/_spectral_utils.py

previously rebuilt the non-backtracking operator independently from graph structure.

This duplicated logic already implemented in build_nb_operator() and risked divergence if the operator definition changed.

The fallback now derives the dense matrix directly from the existing sparse operator (op), ensuring both sparse and dense spectral paths use the exact same operator definition.

The fallback remains guarded by:

n ≤ 512

to preserve performance characteristics.

Import-Time Validation Side Effects

Metric alias validation in:

experiments/run_nb_mutation_ablation.py

previously ran during module import, which could break tooling that imported the module for utilities.

Validation now runs at execution time inside run_ablation(), preserving the fail-fast safety check while avoiding import-time side effects.

Improved
NB Gradient Mutator Parameter Validation

NBGradientMutator now validates the new parameter:

flow_damping_alpha

Expected range:

0.0 ≤ alpha ≤ 1.0

Invalid values now raise a deterministic:

ValueError

This prevents accidental gradient amplification or inversion due to configuration errors.

File:

src/qec/discovery/mutation_nb_gradient.py
QuTiP Installer Security Hardening

The QuTiP helper script now uses an explicitly defined static command tuple:

scripts/qec_qutip.py

Example:

INSTALL_CMD = (
    sys.executable,
    "-m",
    "pip",
    "install",
    "qutip>=5",
)

The subprocess invocation now specifies:

shell=False

and includes a security comment clarifying that the command contains no user-controlled input.

This resolves static-analysis warnings while preserving behavior.

Added
Additional Gradient Mutator Tests

Extended regression coverage in:

tests/test_nb_gradient_mutation.py

New tests include:

validation of flow_damping_alpha range enforcement

deterministic error behavior for invalid configuration

Testing

Full test suite:

3170 passed
0 failed
5 skipped

Syntax verification:

python -m py_compile

completed successfully for all modified modules.

Determinism

This release preserves all determinism guarantees:

no RNG introduced

rounding precision unchanged (12 decimal places)

spectral computations remain deterministic

decoder core (src/qec/decoder/) untouched

Notes

The v12.7 series completes the introduction of NB gradient mutation and spectral basin visualization tooling, enabling detailed analysis of Tanner graph evolution in spectral phase space.

title: v12.7.0 — Spectral Basin Visualization & Stable Gradient Mutation
body:
v12.7.0 — Spectral Basin Visualization & Stable Gradient Mutation

This release introduces spectral basin tracing and trajectory visualization tools for analyzing Tanner graph evolution under non-backtracking (NB) gradient mutation.

The new tooling makes it possible to observe how Tanner graphs move through spectral phase space during mutation evolution, providing deeper insight into instability-driven graph discovery.

Added
Spectral Basin Tracer

New module:

src/qec/experiments/spectral_basin_visualizer.py

SpectralBasinVisualizer.trace_mutation_trajectory() records mutation evolution across iterations, capturing:

spectral radius

eigenvector localization (IPR)

NB spectral energy

All values are rounded to 12 decimal places to preserve deterministic outputs.

Mutation Trajectory Visualization

New visualization utilities:

src/qec/experiments/mutation_trajectory_plot.py

Provides:

render_trajectory_ascii() — deterministic ASCII trajectory rendering

plot_trajectory_matplotlib() — optional matplotlib visualization with automatic fallback if matplotlib is unavailable

These tools allow mutation trajectories to be inspected without introducing mandatory plotting dependencies.

Centralized Experiment Constants

New module:

src/qec/experiments/constants.py

Defines shared experiment configuration values:

MUTATION_STRATEGIES
ABLATION_METRICS

This prevents drift between experiment scripts and improves maintainability of the ablation framework.

QuTiP Helper Script

New optional helper:

scripts/qec_qutip.py

Provides a simple utility to verify or install QuTiP for quantum experiment modules while keeping the core engine free of heavy dependencies.

Usage:

python scripts/qec_qutip.py
Documentation

New documentation:

docs/spectral_basin_visualization.md

Explains:

spectral basin interpretation

mutation trajectory analysis

relationship to NB instability fields

docs/experiments.md updated with QuTiP helper usage.

Improved
NB Gradient Mutation Stability

NBGradientMutator.mutate_flow() now supports deterministic gradient damping, improving stability during mutation evolution.

The damping feature is opt-in and does not alter default mutator behavior.

4-Cycle Detection Performance

Replaced temporary set allocations with .intersection() in mutation cycle checks, reducing unnecessary allocations during mutation candidate evaluation.

Experiment Script Robustness

Added PYTHONPATH fallback in experiment scripts to prevent:

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src'

when running scripts outside editable installs.

Safety Improvements
NB Flow Consistency Guard

Added validation ensuring NB eigenvector structures remain consistent:

if len(directed_edges) != len(directed_edge_flow):
    raise ValueError("NB flow vector mismatch")

This prevents silent corruption of edge-score calculations if analyzer outputs diverge.

Testing

New deterministic tests added:

tests/test_spectral_basin_visualizer.py

Coverage includes:

trajectory determinism

rounding behavior

visualization output

NB flow mismatch guard

Targeted test suites for mutation operators and ablation experiments pass successfully.

Notes

Decoder core (src/qec/decoder/) remains unchanged.

All new functionality resides in experiment and analysis layers.

Existing registry test expectations will be updated in the next patch release.

title: v12.6.0 — NB Instability Gradient Mutation
body:
v12.6.0 — NB Instability Gradient Mutation

Introduces NB instability gradient mutation, enabling Tanner graph rewiring guided by the instability field of the dominant non-backtracking eigenvector.

This release extends the spectral analysis and mutation framework into a continuous topology evolution model, approximating gradient descent in Tanner graph space.

The result is a new class of mutation operator that follows the instability gradient implied by the NB eigenmode, rather than relying purely on discrete heuristic swaps.

Added
NB Instability Gradient Analyzer

New analysis module:

src/qec/analysis/nb_instability_gradient.py

Provides deterministic instability diagnostics derived from the NB spectral flow.

Computed metrics:

edge instability:
|v_(u→v)| + |v_(v→u)|

node instability:
sum of incident edge instability

gradient(ci,vi):
node_score(ci) − node_score(vi)

Outputs:

edge_scores
node_instability
gradient_direction

Fully deterministic with fixed precision rounding.

NB Gradient Mutation Operator

New discovery operator:

src/qec/discovery/mutation_nb_gradient.py

Implements deterministic Tanner graph rewiring that follows the NB instability gradient.

Capabilities:

mutate(H, steps)
mutate_flow(H, iterations)

Mutation rule:

(ci,vi) + (cj,vj)
→
(ci,vj) + (cj,vi)

Acceptance condition:

gradient(ci,vi) > gradient(ci,vj)

Constraints enforced:

degree preservation
bipartite Tanner structure
no duplicate edges
optional 4-cycle avoidance
deterministic ordering

mutate_flow() performs iterative rewiring steps to simulate topological evolution under the instability field.

Instability Field Visualization

New visualization module:

src/qec/experiments/gradient_flow_plot.py

Provides deterministic visualization of the NB instability field.

Includes:

render_gradient_ascii()
plot_gradient_matplotlib()

ASCII output example:

Edge Instability Field

(c0,v2) 0.830000000000 → v3
(c1,v4) 0.720000000000 → v5
(c3,v1) 0.410000000000 → v2

Matplotlib rendering is optional and only activated if the library is installed.

Deterministic Test Coverage

New test suite:

tests/test_nb_gradient_mutation.py

Tests include:

determinism verification
degree preservation
duplicate edge prevention
gradient monotonicity
sparse/dense equivalence
mutate_flow stability

All mutation invariants are enforced.

Documentation

New documentation:

docs/nb_gradient_mutation.md

Explains:

instability gradient definition
mutation rule
continuous topology evolution
visualization tools
determinism guarantees
Determinism

All new functionality preserves repository determinism guarantees.

Properties maintained:

no RNG usage
stable sorted ordering
no input mutation
fixed 12-decimal rounding
sparse/dense parity
Architectural Compliance

The implementation respects repository layering:

Layer 3: Analysis
NBInstabilityGradientAnalyzer

Layer 5: Discovery
NBGradientMutator

Layer 5+: Experiments
gradient_flow_plot

The decoder core remains untouched.

Research Context

The NB instability gradient operator introduces a physics-style mutation mechanism for Tanner graphs:

NB spectral flow
→ instability field
→ gradient direction
→ topology evolution

This enables experiments exploring spectral basin dynamics and gradient-guided LDPC discovery.

Known Issues

A small follow-up patch (v12.6.1) addresses:

4-cycle detection mutating neighbor sets
trivial flow self-alignment metric
strategy key naming consistency

These fixes do not affect the gradient mutation algorithm.

title: v12.5.0 — Spectral Instability Phase Diagram & Mutation Strategy Explorer
body:
## v12.5.0 — Spectral Instability Phase Diagram & Mutation Strategy Explorer

This release introduces a spectral instability phase diagram generator
and mutation ablation experiment framework for evaluating Tanner graph
mutation strategies.

The new tools map decoder failure behavior across spectral space,
turning the QSOL QEC engine into a Tanner graph phase-space exploration
platform.

All functionality resides in the experiments layer and does not modify
the deterministic decoder core.

---

### Added

#### Spectral Phase Diagram Generator

New module:

src/qec/experiments/spectral_phase_diagram.py

Implements `SpectralPhaseDiagramGenerator`, which evaluates decoder
failure rate (FER) across combinations of:

- spectral radius
- channel error rate

Each point records:

- NB spectral radius
- eigenvector localization (IPR)
- flow alignment
- FER

This produces a spectral instability phase diagram.

---

#### Phase Diagram Visualization

New visualization tools:

src/qec/experiments/phase_diagram_plot.py

Provides:

- ASCII heatmap visualization
- optional matplotlib scatter plots

Diagram axes:

- x-axis → spectral radius
- y-axis → error rate
- color → FER

---

#### FER vs Spectral Radius Analysis

New module:

src/qec/experiments/fer_vs_spectral_radius.py

Plots decoder failure rate against spectral radius for multiple
mutation strategies.

Supports ASCII plots and optional matplotlib overlays.

---

#### Mutation Ablation Experiment

New script:

experiments/run_nb_mutation_ablation.py

Runs controlled comparisons between four mutation strategies:

- baseline (no mutation)
- random degree-preserving swap
- NB-guided mutation
- NB × IPR weighted mutation

Metrics recorded:

- FER
- spectral radius
- girth
- cycle counts
- eigenvector localization

Results are exported as structured JSON.

---

### Determinism

All experiments use deterministic RNG:

- SHA-256 sub-seed derivation
- explicit `np.random.default_rng(seed)`
- 12-decimal rounding of floating-point metrics

Outputs are byte-identical across runs.

---

### Testing

New test suites:

tests/test_spectral_phase_diagram.py  
tests/test_ablation_experiment.py

Test coverage includes:

- determinism validation
- schema correctness
- spectral ordering
- FER range checks
- JSON roundtrip

39 tests pass with no failures.

---

### Architecture

All new code resides in:

src/qec/experiments/  
experiments/

The following systems remain unchanged:

- decoder core
- spectral analyzers
- Tanner graph discovery engine

---

### Summary

v12.5.0 introduces spectral phase diagram analysis and mutation
strategy benchmarking, enabling systematic exploration of Tanner graph
stability across spectral regimes.
