title: v12.3.0 — Experimental Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Guided Mutation
body:
## v12.3.0 — Experimental Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Guided Mutation

This release introduces an experimental mutation operator guided by the
dominant non-backtracking (NB) eigenvector of the Tanner graph.

The NB eigenvector identifies regions of concentrated non-backtracking
walk activity. These regions may correspond to cycle-dense or structurally
fragile subgraphs that can contribute to belief-propagation decoding
instability.

The new mutation operator ranks edges using NB flow magnitude and applies
deterministic degree-preserving rewiring to the highest-scoring edges.

The feature is **experimental and disabled by default**.

---

## Added

### NBGuidedMutator

New mutation operator:

title: v12.2.0 — Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Localization Diagnostics
body:
v12.2.0 — Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Localization Diagnostics

This release introduces a localization diagnostic for the dominant
non-backtracking (NB) eigenvector using the Inverse Participation
Ratio (IPR).

Eigenvector localization is a well-known indicator of structural
instability in graphs. In Tanner graphs, localized NB eigenvectors
often correspond to trapping sets or fragile decoding regions.

This diagnostic exposes localization information for research and
analysis without modifying discovery behavior.

Added
-----

EigenvectorLocalizationAnalyzer

• Computes Inverse Participation Ratio (IPR)

    IPR = sum(v^4) / (sum(v^2))^2

• Deterministic implementation
• Clamped to [0,1]
• Rounded to 12 decimal places

Localization classification:

IPR < 0.05        distributed
0.05 – 0.15       mild_localization
≥ 0.15            strong_localization

Integration with NB Flow Analyzer
---------------------------------

compute_flow() now returns:

nb_localization:
  ipr
  localization_type
  vector_norm

This field is diagnostic-only and does not affect discovery scoring.

Testing
-------

• 11 new tests for eigenvector localization
• Existing NB flow tests unchanged
• Full suite: 3020 tests passing

Determinism
-----------

• No randomness introduced
• No scipy eigensolvers used
• Deterministic rounding
• Stable outputs across runs

Research Impact
---------------

This diagnostic provides a structural signal for detecting localized
NB eigenmodes, which are often associated with trapping sets and
decoder instability.

The feature prepares the engine for future work including:

• NB eigenvector guided mutation
• trapping set prediction
• instability-aware graph search

title: v12.1.0 — Non-Backtracking Flow Alignment Diagnostics
body:
v12.1.0 — Non-Backtracking Flow Alignment Diagnostics

This release introduces a diagnostic that measures the alignment
between decoder residual gradients and the dominant non-backtracking
(NB) flow eigenvector direction.

The metric helps determine whether structural Tanner graph instability
predicted by the NB spectrum corresponds to actual decoder instability
observed during belief propagation.

This feature is diagnostic-only and does not affect discovery fitness
or mutation behavior.

Added
-----

FlowAlignmentAnalyzer

• Computes cosine alignment between:
  - BP residual gradient
  - dominant NB flow eigenvector

• Deterministic implementation
• Alignment clamped to [-1,1]
• Rounded to 12 decimal places

Alignment classification:

≥ 0.6   strong alignment  
≥ 0.3   moderate alignment  
< 0.3   weak alignment

Integration with NonBacktrackingFlowAnalyzer

compute_flow(H, residual_map=None)

When a residual map is provided the analyzer returns:

flow_alignment:
  alignment_score
  residual_norm
  flow_norm
  alignment_type

No behavior change occurs when residual_map is not supplied.

Testing
-------

• 16 unit tests for FlowAlignmentAnalyzer
• 7 integration tests
• All existing NB flow tests pass

Total test suite: 36 tests passing.

Determinism
-----------

• No randomness introduced
• No global RNG state
• No scipy eigs usage
• All outputs deterministic across runs

Architecture Impact
-------------------

This diagnostic lays the groundwork for future research including:

• decoder instability prediction
• trapping set identification
• NB-flow guided mutation
• spectral instability scoring

The discovery engine and mutation operators remain unchanged.

title: v12.0.0 — Non-Backtracking Flow Mutation & Constraint-First Discovery
body:
Release Notes
Summary

v12.0.0 introduces a new discovery operator based on non-backtracking eigenvector flow and a constraint-first mutation architecture.

This release adds a deterministic mechanism to detect instability propagation channels in Tanner graphs and apply tension-gated structural corrections.

The new operator interprets the dominant non-backtracking eigenvector as a structural flow direction and performs shear-minimizing rewires when instability signals exceed a configurable threshold.

This allows the discovery engine to react to instability dynamics, not just static structural metrics.

New Features
Non-Backtracking Flow Mutation

Added a new mutation operator:

nonbacktracking_flow_mutation

The operator works as follows:

graph
→ compute non-backtracking flow
→ compute constraint tension κ
→ if κ exceeds threshold
→ perform deterministic rewire reducing instability flow

Key properties:

detects instability propagation channels

performs shear-minimizing rewires

mutation is tension-gated

preserves all mutation invariants

This is the first operator in the engine that uses directional instability flow, not just magnitude-based diagnostics.

NonBacktrackingFlowAnalyzer

New analysis module:

src/qec/analysis/nonbacktracking_flow.py

This analyzer:

constructs the non-backtracking matrix using sparse CSR format

computes the dominant eigenvector via deterministic power iteration

aggregates flow signals across edges, variables, and checks

Returned diagnostics include:

directed_edge_flow
edge_flow
variable_flow
check_flow
max_flow
mean_flow
flow_localization

All computations are deterministic and sparse-safe.

ConstraintTensionAnalyzer

New analysis module:

src/qec/analysis/constraint_tension.py

Computes a constraint tension functional:

κ = wr·residual² + wf·flow² + wc·cluster²

This metric measures structural instability using:

residual decoder signals

non-backtracking flow localization

cluster risk metrics

Triadic State Classification

Variables are classified into three states:

0 = stable
1 = perturbed
2 = corrective

Mutation targets are chosen by moving edges from perturbed regions toward corrective regions, reducing structural shear in the graph.

Architecture

The new discovery pipeline now includes a flow-aware mutation layer:

cycle / girth repairs
↓
ACE repairs
↓
spectral pressure operators
↓
residual cluster mutation
↓
spectral localization mutation
↓
non-backtracking flow mutation (NEW)

This significantly improves the engine’s ability to escape decoder failure basins.

Determinism

All new components maintain the repository’s determinism guarantees.

deterministic power iteration (no eigs)

sparse CSR NB matrix construction

sorted edge ordering

no global RNG state

floating-point normalization and rounding

Discovery runs remain fully reproducible.

Files Added
src/qec/analysis/nonbacktracking_flow.py
src/qec/analysis/constraint_tension.py
tests/test_nonbacktracking_flow.py
tests/test_constraint_tension.py
tests/test_nonbacktracking_flow_mutation.py
Files Modified
src/qec/discovery/guided_mutations.py
src/qec/analysis/__init__.py

Operator registry updated to include:

nonbacktracking_flow_mutation
Tests

Added:

46 new tests

All tests pass:

~2990 total tests
0 failures

New tests verify:

deterministic flow computation

constraint tension stability

mutation invariants

registry integration

Impact

This release introduces the first flow-aware structural mutation in the QEC discovery engine.

The algorithm improves exploration of Tanner graph space by identifying and correcting propagation-driven instabilities rather than only static structural weaknesses.

v12.0.0 establishes a new foundation for future research in spectral-guided code discovery.

title: v11.6.1 — Audit Remediation, Sparse NB Matrices & Deterministic Spectral Fixes
body:
Release Notes
Summary

v11.6.1 is a remediation and stability patch following a full repository audit.
This release fixes several structural and determinism issues while preserving the behavior of the discovery engine.

Key improvements include:

sparse non-backtracking matrix construction

deterministic spectral computations

mutation invariant fixes

optional QLDPC commutativity validation

architectural cleanup

RNG determinism fixes

The repository now provides a clean deterministic baseline for the upcoming v12 discovery algorithm.

Fixes
ACE Repair Mutation

Fixed an edge-loss bug in ace_repair.py.

The operator now uses a two-phase collect → validate → rewire strategy, guaranteeing:

edge count preservation

no degree violations

no duplicate edges

Sparse Non-Backtracking Matrix Construction

Several modules previously built dense non-backtracking matrices.
These are now constructed using CSR sparse matrices, reducing memory complexity from:

O((2E)²)

to

O(E)

Affected modules:

non_backtracking_spectrum.py
nb_localization.py
sensitivity_map.py
spectral_graph_optimizer.py
tanner_graph_repair.py

Sparse implementations were verified against the previous dense construction:

max spectral difference = 0.0
Deterministic Spectral Computations

ARPACK (eigs) can produce nondeterministic eigenvectors for non-symmetric matrices.

To guarantee reproducibility:

eigenvector computations now use numpy.linalg.eig

sparse matrices remain used for matrix construction

dense eigensolver used only when directed edges ≤ 512

This ensures bit-stable experiment runs.

QLDPC Commutativity Validation

Added utility function:

validate_qldpc_commutativity

Location:

discovery/repair_operators.py

The validation is not automatically invoked, ensuring classical LDPC discovery performance is unchanged.

It is available for:

QLDPC code discovery pipelines

stabilizer code validation

research experiments

Sparse Bethe Hessian

Replaced dense matrix multiplication:

H.T @ H

with sparse-safe computation:

H.T.dot(H)

in:

analysis/bethe_hessian.py
Deterministic ARPACK Initialization

All ARPACK calls now use a fixed initial vector:

v0 = np.ones(n)

This prevents nondeterministic solver initialization.

RNG Seeding Fix

Fixed an unseeded RNG instance in:

qec_qldpc_codes.py

All randomness in the repository is now seeded through the deterministic experiment framework.

Layering Fix

Removed an unintended dependency from:

diagnostics → experiments

Restoring the intended architecture:

analysis
↓
discovery
↓
experiments
Tests

Full test suite status:

2944 tests
0 failures

Sparse NB implementations were verified to produce identical spectra to the previous dense implementation.

Version

Version updated to:

v11.6.1

Updated files:

pyproject.toml
CHANGELOG.md
Impact

This release stabilizes the repository following the audit and prepares the codebase for the next major release:

v12.0.0 — Constraint-First Non-Backtracking Flow Mutation

No behavioral changes were introduced to the discovery engine.

title: v11.6.0 — Spectral Localization Mutation
body:
v11.6.0 — Spectral Localization Mutation

This release introduces spectral-localization-guided mutation, allowing the discovery engine to identify and rewire structurally fragile regions of Tanner graphs using the non-backtracking spectral mode.

Unlike scalar spectral metrics, this operator uses eigenvector localization to detect where instability concentrates in the graph and applies targeted rewiring to reduce structural fragility.

The result is a mutation signal informed by graph spectral structure rather than purely local topology.

Added
Spectral Localization Analyzer

New analysis module:

src/qec/analysis/spectral_localization.py

Implements SpectralLocalizationAnalyzer, which computes:

directed-edge participation from the leading non-backtracking eigenvector

undirected edge pressure

normalized variable pressure

Pressure values highlight Tanner graph regions where spectral instability concentrates.

Key properties:

deterministic power-iteration eigenvector computation

sparse non-backtracking matrix representation

normalized pressure metrics for mutation guidance

Spectral Localization Mutation Operator

New mutation operator:

spectral_localization_mutation

Added to the guided mutation framework.

Workflow:

NB eigenvector
→ directed edge participation
→ variable pressure
→ targeted edge rewiring

The operator:

identifies high-pressure variables

disconnects one edge

reconnects it to a low-pressure region

All graph invariants are preserved.

Sparse Compatibility Improvement

SpectralLocalizationAnalyzer now accepts both:

np.ndarray
scipy.sparse.spmatrix

Sparse inputs are processed without dense conversion using CSR indptr / indices iteration.

Dense and sparse inputs produce byte-identical outputs, verified by new tests.

Determinism

The spectral solver uses deterministic power iteration rather than scipy.sparse.linalg.eigs.

Reasons:

eigs introduces non-deterministic random initialization

power iteration with fixed initialization guarantees identical results across runs

This preserves the repository’s core reproducibility guarantee:

same version + same seed → identical results
Tests

New tests added:

tests/test_spectral_localization.py
tests/test_spectral_localization_mutation.py

Coverage includes:

analyzer determinism

sparse vs dense equivalence

mutation invariants

registry integration

Test suite status:

2944 tests passing
7 skipped
0 failures
Operator Registry

The mutation operator registry now includes 11 operators:

spectral_edge_pressure
cycle_pressure
ace_repair
girth_preserving_rewire
expansion_driven_rewire
ipr_trapping_pressure
trapping_set_pressure
residual_guided
absorbing_set_pressure
residual_cluster
spectral_localization

Population scheduling derives dynamically from the registry.

Notes

The spectral localization operator complements the previously introduced residual-cluster mutation by incorporating spectral structure awareness into the discovery engine.

Together these operators allow the engine to respond to:

decoder dynamics (residual clusters)

graph spectral instability (spectral localization)

Next

The upcoming v12.0.0 release will introduce:

Non-Backtracking Eigenvector Flow Mutation

A directional spectral mutation operator that weakens dominant instability channels in Tanner graphs.

title: v11.5.0 — Residual Cluster Mutation
body:
v11.5.0 — Residual Cluster Mutation

Adds residual-cluster mutation for coordinated rewiring of unstable decoder regions.
The discovery engine can now detect and mutate high-residual variable clusters rather than only individual nodes, improving escape from decoder failure basins.

LocalGraphOptimizer now includes residual cluster smoothing as an additional memetic operator.
Runs with max_steps ≥ 5 may produce different optimization trajectories compared to earlier versions.

title: v11.4.0 — Memetic Local Search Evolution
body:
v11.4.0 — Memetic Local Search Evolution

This release introduces a memetic optimization layer to the QSOL-IMC QEC discovery engine.
The engine now combines evolutionary graph search with deterministic local graph optimization, dramatically improving convergence toward high-quality Tanner graphs.

Instead of relying solely on mutation operators, the discovery process now performs targeted local improvements on promising candidates, allowing the system to repair decoder-relevant structural weaknesses between generations.

The result is a more efficient discovery loop capable of finding stronger LDPC / QLDPC codes faster.

Key Features
Memetic Local Search Layer

A new deterministic LocalGraphOptimizer performs targeted edge rewiring on selected population members.

Local optimization uses decoder-aware signals already present in the engine to guide improvements.

Supported local repair heuristics:

• Absorbing-set repair
Uses AbsorbingSetPredictor to detect candidate absorbing sets and rewires internal edges to improve escape connectivity.

• Residual hot-spot smoothing
Uses BPResidualAnalyzer to locate decoder failure regions and redistribute edges away from high-residual variables.

• Cycle irregularity reduction
Reduces short-cycle degree imbalance detected by CycleTopologyAnalyzer.

• Bethe-Hessian stability improvement
Identifies variables contributing to unstable Bethe-Hessian modes and rewires edges to improve spectral stability.

These repairs are applied through a bounded deterministic local search procedure.

Memetic Evolution Step

The discovery engine now includes a memetic refinement stage during evolution.

Each generation now performs:

mutation
→ memetic local optimization
→ fitness evaluation
→ archive update

Local search is applied only to a subset of the population to keep runtime bounded.

CLI Improvements

New discovery options:

--memetic-optimization
--no-memetic-optimization
--local-steps

Defaults:

memetic optimization: enabled
local steps: 10

This allows users to tune the strength of local search during experiments.

Files Added
File	Purpose
src/qec/discovery/local_optimizer.py	Deterministic local graph optimizer implementing memetic search
tests/test_local_optimizer.py	Test suite for local optimizer operators
tests/test_memetic_evolution.py	Tests for memetic discovery integration
Files Modified
File	Change
src/qec/discovery/population_engine.py	Added apply_memetic_step() and memetic evolution support
src/qec/discovery/run_discovery.py	Added CLI options controlling memetic optimization
Deterministic Behavior

The memetic optimizer fully preserves the repository’s deterministic execution guarantees.

Confirmed properties:

• All randomness derived from np.random.RandomState(seed)
• SHA-256 sub-seed derivation used for deterministic operator selection
• No hidden randomness
• No input mutation
• Matrix shapes preserved
• Edge counts preserved

Replay tests confirm identical outputs for identical seeds.

Test Coverage

New tests introduced:

23 tests — Local optimizer behavior

16 tests — Memetic evolution integration

Total test status:

2891 tests passing
0 new failures
Why This Matters

v11.4.0 moves the discovery engine beyond pure evolutionary search and into memetic optimization, a technique widely used in advanced combinatorial optimization systems.

The engine now leverages:

spectral diagnostics

decoder stability signals

absorbing-set prediction

residual localization

targeted graph repair

to actively improve Tanner graphs during search, not just mutate them.

This significantly improves the ability of the system to discover LDPC / QLDPC codes with stronger decoder performance.

title: v11.3.0 — Absorbing-Set Prediction & Cycle Topology Diagnostics
body:
Release Notes

v11.3.0 introduces absorbing-set prediction and cycle topology diagnostics to the QEC discovery engine.

This release adds a localization-driven absorbing-set predictor based on non-backtracking eigenvector analysis and short-cycle motif detection. The discovery engine can now identify and suppress likely error-floor structures before expensive decoding experiments.

Key additions include:

• AbsorbingSetPredictor — estimates absorbing-set risk using short-cycle motifs and eigenvector localization
• CycleTopologyAnalyzer — detects irregular short cycles using a deterministic degree-twist metric
• absorbing_set_pressure_mutation — targeted mutation operator that breaks predicted absorbing structures
• new decoder-aware fitness components incorporating absorbing-set risk and cycle irregularity
• optional CLI flags for absorbing-set control

These improvements allow the discovery engine to evolve Tanner graphs with reduced error-floor risk while preserving deterministic execution.

Test coverage expanded with 36 new tests validating absorbing-set prediction, cycle topology analysis, mutation safety, and fitness integration.

title: v11.2.1 — Spectral Stability Patch
body:
Fixes a pre-existing ARPACK instability in spectral diagnostics.

Spectral computations in `spectral_metrics.py` now use robust
wrappers (`safe_eigsh`, `safe_eigs`) that gracefully fall back
to deterministic dense eigenvalue computation when ARPACK fails
for small or ill-conditioned matrices.

Changes
• Added safe_eigsh() and safe_eigs() wrappers
• Added dense eigenvalue fallbacks
• Added guard for k >= N-1 condition
• Added 17 unit tests
• Hardened spectral diagnostics against ARPACK convergence failures

This patch improves robustness during long discovery runs
without changing APIs or deterministic behavior.

title: v11.2.0 — Bethe-Hessian Guided Evolution
body:
v11.2.0 — Release Description

v11.2.0 introduces Bethe-Hessian–guided stability analysis and upgrades the residual mutation operator to use top-k residual targeting, significantly improving the discovery engine’s ability to locate and suppress decoder-unstable structures.

This release strengthens the decoder-aware discovery pipeline by incorporating spectral stability theory directly into the fitness function, allowing the evolutionary search to steer Tanner graph evolution toward structures with improved belief-propagation stability.

The mutation system is also improved with top-k residual mutation, allowing the search engine to target multiple decoder failure regions simultaneously rather than focusing on a single node.

All changes preserve the deterministic execution guarantees of the repository.

Key Additions
Bethe-Hessian Stability Analyzer

New module:

src/qec/analysis/bethe_hessian.py

Implements a Bethe-Hessian spectral operator used to predict BP stability directly from graph structure.

The analyzer computes a stability score derived from the smallest Bethe-Hessian eigenvalues and integrates with the decoder-aware fitness evaluation.

This provides a fast structural predictor of decoding instability before running full BP simulations.

Decoder-Aware Fitness Upgrade

The fitness engine now incorporates Bethe-Hessian stability alongside existing structural and decoder-aware metrics.

Composite decoder-aware fitness now includes:

structural_score
+ bp_stability_score
+ bethe_hessian_stability
− trapping_set_penalty

This allows the discovery engine to evolve graphs that improve both structural expansion and decoder convergence properties.

Residual-Guided Mutation Upgrade

residual_guided_mutation now targets the top-k residual nodes rather than a single maximum-residual variable.

This improves mutation effectiveness by addressing multiple decoder failure regions simultaneously.

Centralized Mutation Operator Registry

Mutation operators are now centralized in guided_mutations.py via a shared registry.

The discovery engine now imports the mutation schedule directly from this registry, eliminating duplicate operator lists and preventing configuration drift when new operators are added.

Mutation Operators (v11.2)

spectral_edge_pressure_mutation

cycle_pressure_mutation

ace_repair_mutation

girth_preserving_rewire

expansion_driven_rewire

ipr_trapping_pressure_mutation

trapping_set_pressure_mutation

residual_guided_mutation

Tests

New tests validate Bethe-Hessian analysis and residual mutation upgrades.

47 tests added or updated

Full suite results:

2773 tests passed
1 pre-existing ARPACK failure (unrelated to this release)
7 skipped

All new functionality is fully deterministic and reproducible.

Deterministic Execution

All discovery operations remain fully deterministic:

all randomness derives from np.random.RandomState(seed)

deterministic SHA-256 sub-seed derivation

no input mutation

matrix-hash caching

reproducible discovery runs for identical seeds

title: v11.1.0 — Residual-Guided Mutation
body:
v11.1.0 — Residual-Guided Mutation
Overview

v11.1.0 introduces residual-guided mutation, enabling the discovery engine to locate and target decoder-instability regions during evolutionary search.

Previous versions added decoder-aware evaluation through trapping-set detection and BP stability probes. This release extends that capability by using belief-propagation residual maps to identify parts of the Tanner graph that cause decoding difficulty.

Mutations can now preferentially modify graph regions where BP message updates remain unstable, improving the efficiency of the search process.

Added
BP Residual Analyzer

New module:

src/qec/analysis/bp_residuals.py

Introduces BPResidualAnalyzer, which performs short deterministic BP iterations and measures per-variable message change magnitude.

Residuals are defined as:

|LLR_new − LLR_old|

The analyzer produces:

residual_map
max_residual
mean_residual

These values highlight variable nodes associated with unstable or slow-converging decoding behavior.

Residual-Guided Mutation Operator

New mutation operator:

residual_guided_mutation

Algorithm:

run BP residual analysis

rank variable nodes by residual magnitude

remove an edge from a high-residual variable

reconnect to a low-residual variable

This directs graph evolution away from decoder-unstable regions.

The operator is now part of the guided mutation ensemble.

Mutation Operator Set

The discovery engine now uses eight mutation operators:

spectral_edge_pressure
cycle_pressure
ace_repair
girth_preserving_rewire
expansion_driven_rewire
ipr_trapping_pressure
trapping_set_pressure
residual_guided_mutation

These operators combine structural, spectral, and decoder-behavior signals to guide graph evolution.

Testing

New test suite:

tests/test_residual_guided_mutation.py

Coverage includes:

deterministic residual computation

mutation correctness

matrix shape preservation

edge-count preservation

operator registration

All tests pass with no regressions.

Summary

v11.1.0 strengthens the discovery engine’s decoder awareness by incorporating BP residual dynamics into the mutation stage.

The evolutionary search can now target:

decoder instability regions
slow-converging message paths
high-residual variable nodes

This improves the engine’s ability to evolve Tanner graphs that support stable belief-propagation decoding.

Author
Trent Slade
QSOL-IMC
ORCID: 0009-0002-4515-9237

title: v11.0.0 — Decoder-Aware Discovery Engine
body:
Overview

v11.0.0 introduces decoder-aware evaluation to the QEC LDPC / QLDPC discovery framework.
Previous versions optimized Tanner graph structure using spectral and combinatorial metrics.
This release adds lightweight decoder behavior analysis, allowing the evolutionary search to penalize harmful structures such as trapping sets and unstable belief-propagation dynamics.

The discovery engine can now evolve parity-check matrices that optimize both graph structure and decoder stability.

Added
Trapping Set Detection

New analysis subsystem:

src/qec/analysis/trapping_sets.py

Introduces TrappingSetDetector, which detects small elementary trapping sets (ETS) with limits:

a ≤ 6
b ≤ 4

The detector reports:

min_size
counts by (a,b)
total ETS
variable participation

A configurable enumeration budget prevents combinatorial explosion on larger matrices.

BP Stability Probe

New module:

src/qec/decoder/stability_probe.py

Introduces BPStabilityProbe, a lightweight belief-propagation probe used to estimate decoder stability using short min-sum style iterations.

Metrics extracted:

bp_stability_score
divergence_rate
stagnation_rate
oscillation_score
average_iterations

The probe is deterministic and supports edge cases such as trials=0.

BP Instability Spectral Estimate

Also added:

estimate_bp_instability()

This function estimates decoder instability using a Jacobian spectral-radius approximation computed via power iteration.

Interpretation:

ρ < 1   stable
ρ ≈ 1   marginal
ρ > 1   unstable

This provides a fast decoder instability signal without running full decoding experiments.

Discovery Engine Improvements
Decoder-Aware Fitness

The FitnessEngine now supports decoder-aware evaluation via:

decoder_aware
bp_trials
bp_iterations

Additional fitness components include:

trapping_set_penalty
bp_stability_score
jacobian_stability_term

Structural fitness remains fully supported and unchanged when decoder-aware mode is disabled.

Trapping-Set Mutation Operator

A new mutation operator has been added:

trapping_set_pressure_mutation

The operator:

detects trapping sets

identifies variables participating in harmful structures

removes a contributing edge

reconnects outside the trapping region

This introduces decoder-guided structural pressure during evolutionary search.

CLI Improvements

New CLI options:

--no-decoder-aware
--bp-trials
--bp-iterations

Decoder-aware evaluation is enabled by default and can be disabled for purely structural experiments.

Testing

New test suites validate the decoder-aware functionality:

tests/test_trapping_sets.py
tests/test_bp_stability_probe.py
tests/test_decoder_aware_fitness.py
tests/test_trapping_mutation.py

Coverage includes:

deterministic trapping-set detection

BP stability probe reproducibility

decoder-aware fitness evaluation

mutation operator correctness

All tests pass with no regressions.

Summary

v11.0.0 transforms the repository from a structural Tanner-graph discovery engine into a decoder-aware LDPC / QLDPC discovery platform.

The discovery process now incorporates:

graph structure metrics
trapping-set analysis
belief-propagation stability probes
Jacobian instability estimation

This allows the system to evolve codes that optimize iterative decoder performance, not just graph topology.

Author

Trent Slade
QSOL-IMC
ORCID: 0009-0002-4515-9237

title: v10.2.0 — PEG-Seeded Spectral Population Initialization
body:
v10.2.0 — PEG-Seeded Spectral Population Initialization

This release introduces PEG-seeded population initialization for the QLDPC discovery engine.

Instead of starting evolutionary discovery from random Tanner graphs, the engine can now generate Progressive Edge Growth (PEG)–constructed graphs with spectral quality filtering, producing significantly stronger starting populations.

This dramatically improves the discovery process by reducing early search stagnation and providing higher-quality Tanner graphs for evolutionary refinement.

Added
PEG Tanner Graph Generator

New module:

src/qec/generation/peg_generator.py

Implements a deterministic Progressive Edge Growth (PEG) algorithm for constructing parity-check matrices.

Key functions:

generate_peg_tanner_graph()

Constructs Tanner graphs edge-by-edge using PEG expansion.

Features:

BFS expansion tree to maximize graph distance

preferential connection to check nodes outside the local neighbourhood

degree-balanced edge placement

deterministic execution via seeded RNG

This helps avoid early short cycles and improves graph expansion.

_peg_bfs_select()

Internal BFS-based selection method that chooses candidate check nodes based on maximum graph distance and minimum degree.

This is the core mechanism enabling PEG to increase graph girth.

_passes_spectral_filter()

Spectral quality filter that evaluates newly generated PEG graphs using existing diagnostics.

Checks include:

girth ≥ 4

non-backtracking spectral radius below the Ramanujan-style bound

Graphs failing these criteria are rejected and regenerated deterministically.

generate_peg_population()

Population generator compatible with the discovery engine.

Features:

deterministic seed derivation

spectral filtering with retry logic

candidate format matching discovery engine requirements

This allows PEG-generated Tanner graphs to be used directly as discovery populations.

Discovery Engine Integration

The discovery engine now supports configurable initialization strategies.

New option:

init_strategy = "peg"

When enabled, the discovery engine initializes its population using PEG-constructed Tanner graphs instead of random graphs.

This significantly improves the quality of the initial search population.

CLI Improvements

The discovery CLI now supports selecting the initialization strategy.

New argument:

--init-strategy

Available options:

random
peg

Default:

peg

Example:

python -m qec.discovery.run_discovery \
  --population 50 \
  --generations 500 \
  --init-strategy peg
Generation API

src/qec/generation/api.py now re-exports the PEG generator functions:

generate_peg_tanner_graph
generate_peg_population

This allows PEG construction to be used independently of the discovery engine.

Testing

New test suite:

tests/test_peg_generator.py

Coverage includes:

deterministic generation

degree constraints

matrix shape validation

spectral filtering behavior

population generator correctness

discovery engine integration

Results:

22 new tests added
2685 existing tests passing
2 pre-existing ARPACK failures unchanged

No regressions were introduced.

Summary

v10.2.0 introduces PEG-seeded spectral population initialization, dramatically improving the starting point for evolutionary Tanner graph discovery.

Benefits include:

fewer short cycles in initial populations

stronger graph expansion properties

improved evolutionary convergence

higher probability of discovering high-performance LDPC / QLDPC codes

This release further strengthens the repository as a deterministic LDPC / QLDPC code discovery platform.

Author
Trent Slade
QSOL-IMC
ORCID: 0009-0002-4515-9237

title: v10.1.0 — IPR-Guided Trapping-Set Mutation
body:
v10.1.0 — IPR-Guided Trapping-Set Mutation

This release introduces a new spectral mutation operator that targets proto-trapping structures using eigenvector localization.

The mutation leverages the inverse participation ratio (IPR) signal derived from the dominant eigenvector of 
𝐻
𝑇
𝐻
H
T
H to identify regions of spectral localization that often correspond to trapping sets in belief propagation decoding.

By rewiring edges associated with highly localized variables, the discovery engine can suppress structural patterns that typically lead to decoder instability.

Added
IPR-Guided Trapping-Set Mutation

New guided mutation operator:

ipr_trapping_pressure

Algorithm:

compute dominant eigenvector of 
𝐻
𝑇
𝐻
H
T
H

identify variables with highest eigenvector magnitude

remove one edge connected to the most localized variable

rewire that edge toward a variable with low eigenvector magnitude

This mutation helps reduce localized spectral structures that correlate with trapping sets and error-floor behaviour.

Discovery Engine Impact

The evolutionary search now rotates across six guided mutation operators:

spectral_edge_pressure
cycle_pressure
ace_repair
girth_preserving_rewire
expansion_driven_rewire
ipr_trapping_pressure

This improves structural diversity during discovery and reduces search stagnation caused by localized graph structures.

Testing

New tests:

tests/test_ipr_trapping_mutation.py

Validation includes:

deterministic mutation behavior

input immutability

matrix shape preservation

edge count preservation

valid Tanner graph output

dispatcher registration

Results:

16 new tests added
67 existing tests pass
0 regressions
Summary

v10.1.0 strengthens the discovery engine’s ability to avoid spectral localization patterns associated with trapping sets, improving the quality of evolved Tanner graph structures.

Author
Trent Slade
QSOL-IMC
ORCID: 0009-0002-4515-9237

title: v10.0.0 — Deterministic QLDPC Discovery Engine
body:
v10.0.0 — Deterministic QLDPC Discovery Engine

This release transforms the repository from a Tanner graph generator with diagnostics into a fully automated deterministic LDPC / QLDPC structure discovery platform.

v10 introduces a population-based search engine guided by spectral metrics, structural cycle analysis, and ACE connectivity heuristics. The system automatically evolves parity-check matrices and stores discovered structures in a persistent archive with full reproducibility metadata.

The result is a deterministic research environment for discovering high-quality Tanner graphs using modern coding-theory heuristics.

Major Features
Deterministic Population Discovery Engine

A new evolutionary search engine explores the Tanner graph design space.

Features:

deterministic tournament selection

generation-based mutation scheduling

10% elitism to preserve best candidates

population-level fitness evaluation

generation summaries and elite history tracking

The discovery engine evolves candidate parity-check matrices while preserving full reproducibility.

Example CLI usage:

python -m qec.discovery.run_discovery \
    --population 50 \
    --generations 500 \
    --seed 42
Fitness Engine

A new spectral fitness engine evaluates candidate Tanner graphs using metrics derived from modern LDPC research.

Metrics include:

Non-backtracking spectral radius (NBT)

Girth and short cycle spectrum

ACE (Approximate Cycle Extrinsic) connectivity

Eigenvector localization (IPR)

Neighborhood expansion

Cycle density

Sparsity

A weighted composite score ranks candidates and guides the evolutionary search.

Fitness evaluations are cached using matrix content hashes to avoid recomputation during discovery runs.

Guided Mutation System

Five deterministic mutation operators introduce targeted structural changes:

Spectral Edge Pressure

Rewires edges contributing most to non-backtracking spectral instability.

Cycle Pressure Mutation

Targets variable nodes participating in many short cycles and breaks dense cycle clusters.

ACE Repair

Improves extrinsic message connectivity for low-ACE variable nodes.

Girth-Preserving Rewire

Attempts rewires that never decrease graph girth.

Expansion-Driven Rewire

Improves neighborhood expansion for poorly connected nodes.

These operators combine spectral information and structural heuristics to guide the search toward stronger Tanner graph structures.

Persistent Discovery Archive

v10 introduces a SQLite-backed discovery archive for storing discovered codes.

Each entry records:

parity-check matrix

composite fitness score

structural metrics

genealogy (parent code and mutation operator)

generation number

seed

reproducibility metadata

Codes are stored using content-addressable identifiers derived from the matrix SHA-256 hash, ensuring deduplication and deterministic referencing.

Archive capabilities include:

elite code retrieval

diversity-based sampling

population seeding for future runs

Artifact Metadata & Reproducibility

Every discovery run automatically records reproducibility metadata:

git commit hash

numpy version

scipy version

random seed

ISO-8601 timestamp

Run summaries are exported as JSON artifacts alongside the archive database.

This guarantees fully reproducible discovery experiments.

CLI Discovery Interface

New command:

python -m qec.discovery.run_discovery

Arguments:

--population
--generations
--seed
--archive-path
--num-variables
--num-checks
--variable-degree
--check-degree

Outputs:

best discovered code

fitness summary

archive database

JSON run report

Testing

v10 adds extensive validation for the discovery engine:

New tests:

fitness engine validation

guided mutation operators

discovery engine determinism

archive persistence

artifact metadata

Results:

91 new tests added
2649 total tests passing
0 regressions

Deterministic replay is verified across all major components.

Architecture

New subsystems:

src/qec/fitness/
src/qec/discovery/
src/qec/archive/
src/qec/utils/artifact_metadata.py

The new layers sit above the decoder stack and do not modify existing decoding functionality.

All new components maintain the project’s design constraints:

deterministic execution

minimal dependencies

sparse-matrix compatibility

reproducible experiments

Summary

v10.0.0 introduces a deterministic LDPC / QLDPC structure discovery engine capable of automatically evolving Tanner graphs using spectral and structural heuristics.

The repository now supports:

automated code discovery

persistent research archives

spectral-guided graph evolution

reproducible discovery experiments

This release establishes the foundation for future research on spectral-guided QLDPC construction and automated code search.

Author
Trent Slade
QSOL-IMC
ORCID: 0009-0002-4515-9237

title: v9.0.0 — Deterministic QLDPC Structure Discovery Engine
body:
v9.0.0 Release Notes

Introduces the deterministic QLDPC structure discovery engine, extending the Tanner graph generation framework into a full discovery system capable of generating, analyzing, and evolving parity-check matrices.

The discovery engine integrates spectral diagnostics, guided mutation, structural repair, and elite archiving to explore Tanner graph families in a fully reproducible manner.

title: v8.5.0 — Deterministic Cycle-Avoidant Tanner Graph Construction
body:
v8.5.0 — Deterministic Cycle-Avoidant Tanner Graph Construction

Introduces a deterministic Tanner graph construction heuristic that actively reduces short-cycle pressure and local clustering during generation.

This improves the structural quality of generated parity-check matrices while preserving determinism, sparsity, and reproducibility.

The new constructor supports deterministic candidate diversity via variable ordering variants and cyclic check ordering.

This release strengthens the v8 generation framework and prepares the repository for the v9 structure discovery engine.

title: v8.4.0 — Deterministic Tanner Graph Generation Framework
body:
Release Notes
QSOLKCB/QEC v8.4.0
Deterministic Tanner Graph Generation Framework

This release introduces the Tanner graph generation framework, extending the repository beyond decoder diagnostics into structured generation and evaluation of candidate parity-check matrices.

The system now supports deterministic Tanner graph construction, spectral evaluation, stability-based ranking, and generation trajectory tracking.

This marks the transition of the repository from a decoder analysis toolkit into a Tanner graph generation laboratory, preparing the architecture for future automated QLDPC structure discovery.

Added
Generation Framework

New src/qec/generation/ package providing deterministic candidate graph generation and evaluation.

Tanner Graph Generator

src/qec/generation/tanner_graph_generator.py

Generates deterministic Tanner graph candidates from specification:

{
  "num_variables": ...,
  "num_checks": ...,
  "variable_degree": ...,
  "check_degree": ...,
  "seed_graph": optional
}

Supports two deterministic modes:

Fresh construction

builds regular parity-check matrices satisfying degree constraints

Seed perturbation

performs deterministic edge swaps to explore local structural neighborhoods

Output format:

[
  {
    "candidate_id": ...,
    "H": ...
  }
]

All generation remains deterministic and reproducible.

Candidate Evaluation

src/qec/generation/candidate_evaluation.py

Evaluates Tanner graph candidates using the existing spectral diagnostic infrastructure.

Computed metrics include:

spectral_radius

entropy

spectral_gap

bethe_margin

support_dimension

curvature

cycle_density

stability instability score (SIS)

Candidates are classified into predicted stability regimes using existing BP stability predictors.

Candidate Ranking

Deterministic multi-objective ranking of candidates based on:

lower instability score

lower spectral radius

higher entropy

higher Bethe margin

Tie-breaking remains deterministic.

Candidate Export

{

Public Generation API

export_generated_graph

src/qec/generation/__init__.py follows the diagnostics API pattern.

Experiments

generate → evaluate → rank → perturb

The best candidate at each step seeds the next generation.

export / reload integrity

Architecture Impact

Future Work

stability-driven graph evolution

title: v8.3.0 — Tanner Graph Stability Optimization & Spectral Invariant Discovery
body:
Release Notes

v8.3.0 introduces deterministic Tanner graph stability optimization and automated spectral invariant discovery for belief-propagation (BP) analysis.

Building on the spectral diagnostics (v7), stability phase diagrams (v8.0), spectral stability classifier (v8.1), and learned stability boundary (v8.2), this release adds tools for exploring stability landscapes, proposing structural repairs, and automatically discovering new spectral relationships correlated with BP failure.

These additions transform the repository from a diagnostic framework into a Tanner graph stability optimization laboratory.

New capabilities
Stability landscape exploration

Adds experiments for mapping the local structural stability landscape around a Tanner graph using deterministic perturbations.

Module:

src/qec/experiments/stability_landscape.py

Each sample records spectral diagnostics and predicted BP stability:

spectral metrics → stability prediction

Results saved to:

artifacts/stability_landscape.json
Deterministic stability optimization

Introduces an optimization engine that iteratively improves Tanner graph stability by applying repair candidates derived from spectral diagnostics.

Modules:

repair_candidates.py
repair_scoring.py
stability_optimizer.py

Pipeline:

NB eigenvector localization
        ↓
candidate edge swaps
        ↓
spectral metric scoring
        ↓
deterministic improvement

Optimization trajectories are saved to:

artifacts/stability_optimization_trajectory.json
Automatic spectral invariant discovery

Adds a discovery engine that searches for combinations of spectral metrics correlated with BP failure.

Module:

spectral_invariant_discovery.py

Candidate invariants include deterministic combinations such as:

spectral_radius * entropy
entropy / spectral_gap
curvature * cycle_density
spectral_radius + curvature

Each candidate is evaluated using:

Pearson correlation with BP failure

classification accuracy

Results saved to:

artifacts/spectral_invariants.json

This feature enables automated discovery of potentially useful stability diagnostics.

Optimization benchmark

Adds a benchmark experiment for evaluating the effectiveness of stability optimization.

Module:

src/qec/experiments/stability_optimization_benchmark.py

Outputs metrics such as:

initial_stability_score
final_stability_score
critical_radius_shift
repair_steps

Saved to:

artifacts/stability_optimization_benchmark.json
Visualization tools

title: v8.2.0 — Learned BP Stability Boundary & Spectral Stability Prediction
body:
Release Notes

v8.2.0 introduces empirical stability boundary learning and spectral prediction tools for analyzing belief-propagation (BP) convergence in Tanner graphs.

Building on the phase-diagram experiments introduced in v8.0 and the spectral diagnostics layer added in v8.1, this release enables the repository to estimate BP stability regimes directly from spectral metrics and to compare empirical stability thresholds with predictions derived purely from the non-backtracking (NB) spectrum.

These additions extend the toolkit from structural diagnostics to a full experimental framework for studying Tanner graph stability dynamics.

New capabilities
Learned BP stability boundary

Introduces a dataset builder and deterministic boundary estimator that learns a stability boundary from spectral metrics and BP convergence outcomes.

Pipeline:

Tanner graph
      ↓
spectral metrics
      ↓
BP convergence experiments
      ↓
learned stability boundary

Modules:

stability_dataset.py

stability_boundary.py

stability_predictor.py

Spectral-only critical line prediction

Adds a lightweight estimator that predicts the BP stability threshold directly from the NB spectrum without running BP decoding.

Module:

spectral_critical_line.py

This enables direct comparison between:

empirical critical radius λ_c
vs
spectral predicted λ_c
NB eigenvector sign trapping-set detector

Introduces a fast trapping-set discovery heuristic based on the sign structure of the dominant NB eigenvector.

Module:

nb_sign_trapping_sets.py

The method identifies sign-coherent high-energy clusters that often correspond to trapping-set regions responsible for BP instability.

Critical spectral radius estimation

Adds an empirical estimator for the BP stability threshold derived from phase-diagram experiment data.

Module:

critical_radius.py

Returns:

λ_c  — estimated BP critical spectral radius
Δλ   — transition width
Repair stability trajectories

New experiment tracking how the BP stability threshold shifts during deterministic Tanner graph repair.

Module:

repair_stability_trajectory.py

Produces trajectories:

repair step t → λ_c(t)

revealing how structural repairs move the Tanner graph away from instability regions.

ASCII phase-diagram boundary visualization

The phase diagram renderer now automatically marks the estimated BP stability boundary during experiments.

Example:

radius →

0.9 1.0 1.1 1.2 1.3

SIS ↓

0.02  + + + | -
0.04  + + | - -
0.06  + | - - -
0.08  | - - - -

+  BP converged
-  BP failed
|  estimated stability boundary
Visualization tools

New script for plotting learned stability boundaries and critical spectral radii.

Script:

scripts/plot_stability_boundary.py

Supports matplotlib or ASCII output.

Test coverage

New deterministic tests validate:

stability dataset generation

stability boundary estimation

spectral prediction behavior

trapping-set detection

repair stability trajectory artifacts

title: v8.1.0 — Spectral Stability Diagnostics & Ternary Tanner Graph Classifier
body:
Release Description

v8.1.0 introduces a new spectral diagnostics layer for analyzing Tanner graph stability and predicting belief-propagation (BP) convergence behavior.

This release adds several spectral invariants derived from the non-backtracking spectrum and Bethe-Hessian analysis, along with a deterministic ternary stability classifier for Tanner graphs. These diagnostics provide a compact spectral feature vector that can be used to assess structural instability, identify trapping-set regions, and guide graph repair strategies.

New spectral diagnostics

Spectral entropy — measures localization of the NB eigenvector energy distribution

NB spectral gap — separation between dominant instability modes

Bethe Hessian stability margin — smallest eigenvalue of the Bethe Hessian matrix

Effective support dimension — exponential of spectral entropy, estimating active edge support

Spectral curvature — variance of log-energy distribution indicating instability sensitivity

Cycle-space density — density of short cycles contributing to instability

Metric aggregation

Introduces a unified diagnostics aggregator:

compute_spectral_metrics(H)

which returns a deterministic spectral feature vector:

{
spectral_radius,
entropy,
spectral_gap,
bethe_margin,
support_dimension,
curvature,
cycle_density,
sis
}

The aggregator reuses a single NB eigenpair computation to avoid redundant spectral solves.

Stability classification

Adds a deterministic ternary classifier:

classify_tanner_graph_stability(metrics)

which predicts Tanner graph stability regimes:

+1 → stable BP regime
 0 → metastable regime
-1 → unstable regime

A convenience wrapper is also provided:

classify_from_parity_check(H)
Architecture improvements

NumPy-style diagnostics API layer (src/qec/diagnostics/api.py)

Simplified package export via from .api import *

Unified diagnostics entry point for experiments and CLI tools

Testing

All diagnostics are deterministic and rely only on sparse operators.

2293 tests passed
7 skipped
Relationship to v8.0

v8.1 builds directly on the v8.0 Stability Phase Diagram framework, adding spectral feature extraction and stability classification capabilities that enable automated analysis of Tanner graph stability regions.

title: v8.0.0 — Stability Phase Diagram Experiments
body:
3. Release Notes (Recommended)

You can paste this directly into the GitHub release page.

v8.0.0 — Stability Phase Diagram Experiments

This release introduces a large-scale experimental framework for analyzing belief propagation (BP) stability in QLDPC Tanner graphs using spectral diagnostics.

The new system maps BP convergence behavior across structural perturbations and spectral metrics, enabling empirical investigation of decoding stability regimes.

Major Features
Stability Phase Diagram Experiment

New experiment module:

src/qec/experiments/stability_phase_diagram.py

Provides a 2-D grid scan over:

spectral radius
SIS (spectral instability score)

For each grid cell the experiment:

generates deterministic Tanner graph perturbations

computes spectral diagnostics

runs BP decoding

records convergence behavior

Results are aggregated into a stability phase diagram.

Spectral Trajectory Tracking

The experiment records spectral trajectories during perturbation sequences:

λ_NB(t)
IPR(t)
SIS(t)
decoder convergence

This allows analysis of how instability modes evolve as the graph is modified.

ASCII Phase Diagram Visualization

The experiment produces a live ASCII stability map in the terminal.

Features include:

automatic diagram scaling

BP stability boundary highlighting

periodic snapshot saving

Snapshots are stored in:

artifacts/phase_diagram_snapshots/
Stability Boundary Estimation

Two stability boundary estimators are implemented:

Measured boundary

estimate_bp_stability_boundary()

Derived from BP convergence data.

Predicted boundary

predict_spectral_stability_boundary()

Estimated directly from spectral metrics.

Metastable Oscillation Detection

BP oscillatory regimes are detected using periodicity analysis of residual norms.

detect_metastable_bp_oscillation()

This distinguishes:

stable convergence
metastable oscillation
decoding failure
New Diagnostics

Three structural instability detectors were added.

NB Eigenvector Localization
detect_nb_eigenvector_localization()

Detects localization of the dominant NB eigenvector using IPR and energy concentration.

NB Energy Heatmap
compute_nb_energy_heatmap()

Aggregates directed edge energy to node-level instability scores.

Sign Pattern Detector
detect_nb_sign_pattern_trapping_sets()

Identifies sign-coherent regions of the NB eigenvector that may correspond to trapping sets.

Instability Localization

The experiment identifies and logs the most unstable subgraph:

log_most_unstable_subgraph()

This highlights variable and check nodes most responsible for instability.

Deterministic Experiment Design

All perturbations are generated deterministically using SHA-256 seed derivation.

Experiments are fully reproducible across machines.

Performance Improvements

The experiment uses incremental NB spectral updates introduced in v7.9:

update_nb_eigenpair_incremental()

This allows thousands of structural perturbations to be evaluated efficiently.

CLI Integration

New command-line flag:

--stability-phase-diagram

Runs the full experiment pipeline.

Tests

New test suite:

tests/test_stability_phase_diagram.py

51 deterministic tests verifying:

artifact schema validity

spectral metric recording

BP convergence reproducibility

incremental spectrum consistency

All tests pass.

What This Enables

v8.0 introduces the infrastructure needed to empirically map belief propagation stability regimes for Tanner graphs.

The system now supports:

detect instability
localize instability
experimentally map stability regimes

This lays the groundwork for v8.1, which will introduce automated structural stability classification.

title: v7.9.0 — Incremental Non-Backtracking Spectral Updates
body:
v7.9.0 — Incremental Non-Backtracking Spectral Updates

Accelerates Tanner-graph spectral diagnostics by introducing incremental and localized non-backtracking (NB) eigenpair updates.

This release significantly reduces the cost of evaluating Tanner graph repair candidates by reusing previously computed eigenvectors and updating only the NB edges affected by a structural modification.

The result is a much faster spectral analysis pipeline while preserving the project’s core principles:

• deterministic computation
• sparse operator implementations
• architecture-preserving diagnostics

The decoder core remains untouched.

Highlights
Incremental NB Eigenpair Updates

Introduces a warm-started power iteration solver that updates the dominant NB eigenpair after small Tanner graph modifications.

Key properties:

warm-start from previous eigenvector

Rayleigh quotient acceleration

deterministic convergence

canonical eigenvector sign normalization

This allows spectral updates without recomputing the entire eigensystem.

Localized Spectral Updates

Adds a localized update strategy that recomputes only NB edges affected by Tanner graph repairs.

For swaps of the form:

(a,b) + (c,d) → (a,d) + (c,b)

the solver identifies affected Tanner graph nodes and updates only the corresponding NB edges.

Fallback to full incremental updates occurs automatically when the affected set becomes large.

This dramatically reduces the cost of evaluating graph repairs.

Incremental Repair Scoring

Repair candidate scoring can now optionally use the incremental solver.

New helper:

score_repair_candidate_incremental()

Features:

incremental spectral update

automatic fallback to full recomputation

deterministic scoring metrics

SIS delta computation

Incremental Spectral Benchmark

Adds a benchmark experiment comparing:

full NB recomputation

warm-start incremental updates

localized incremental updates

Recorded metrics include:

runtime_full
runtime_incremental
runtime_localized
speedup_incremental
speedup_localized
spectral_radius_difference
eigenvector_cosine_similarity
SIS Drift Diagnostics

The benchmark now records stability drift metrics:

sis_full
sis_incremental
delta_sis

These diagnostics will support upcoming Tanner graph stability phase diagram experiments.

New Modules
Diagnostics
src/qec/diagnostics/spectral_incremental.py

Provides:

update_nb_eigenpair_incremental()

update_nb_eigenpair_localized()

detect_edge_swap()

identify_affected_nb_edges()

score_repair_candidate_incremental()

All algorithms are sparse, deterministic, and decoder-agnostic.

Experiments
src/qec/experiments/incremental_spectral_benchmark.py

Compares full and incremental spectral solvers and produces deterministic benchmark artifacts.

Visualization
scripts/plot_incremental_convergence.py

Optional script for visualizing power-iteration convergence behavior.

Matplotlib remains an optional dependency.

Test Suite

New test module:

tests/test_spectral_incremental.py

Adds 27 tests validating:

deterministic incremental solver behavior

eigenvector normalization

spectral agreement with full solver

localized update correctness

repair scoring integration

benchmark artifact schema

Total test coverage continues to expand while preserving deterministic reproducibility.

Architectural Guarantees

This release preserves all core project invariants:

decoder core unchanged

no dense NB matrix construction

deterministic artifact generation

sparse LinearOperator spectral routines

Incremental solvers are implemented strictly as diagnostic helpers, leaving the baseline spectral solver unchanged.

Research Impact

v7.9.0 completes the core spectral Tanner-graph analysis pipeline:

v7.6 — spectral instability diagnostics
v7.7 — trapping-set heatmaps
v7.8 — deterministic graph repair
v7.9 — accelerated spectral updates

The system can now efficiently evaluate many structural Tanner graph modifications, enabling larger experimental studies.

This prepares the project for the next major research milestone:

v8.0 — Tanner Graph Stability Phase Diagrams

which will map decoding stability regimes across Tanner graph families using the spectral metrics introduced in the v7 series.

Author

Trent Slade
QSOL-IMC

ORCID
https://orcid.org/0009-0002-4515-9237

title: v7.8.0 — Deterministic Gradient-Guided Tanner Graph Repair
body:
Release Title

v7.8.0 — Deterministic Gradient-Guided Tanner Graph Repair

Release Notes
v7.8.0 — Deterministic Gradient-Guided Tanner Graph Repair

This release introduces the first deterministic Tanner graph repair engine for the QEC toolkit.

Previous releases established the spectral diagnostics needed to analyze Tanner graph instability:

Version	Capability
v7.6.1	Spectral instability detection (NB spectrum, IPR, EEEC, SIS)
v7.7.0	Instability localization via spectral heatmaps
v7.8.0	Deterministic structural repair of unstable Tanner graphs

Using the instability signals from v7.6.1 and the localized trapping-set heatmaps from v7.7.0, this release implements a single-step deterministic repair pipeline:

Tanner graph
     ↓
NB spectral diagnostics
     ↓
instability heatmaps
     ↓
hot edge detection
     ↓
candidate edge swaps
     ↓
spectral scoring
     ↓
best deterministic repair

The repair process attempts to reduce spectral instability by performing degree-preserving edge swaps that improve the Spectral Instability Score (SIS).

Added
Spectral Repair Diagnostics

New module:

src/qec/diagnostics/spectral_repair.py

Implements deterministic Tanner graph repair utilities:

propose_repair_candidates()
Generates candidate degree-preserving edge swaps using the hottest edges identified by spectral heatmaps.

score_repair_candidate()
Evaluates candidates using spectral diagnostics from v7.6.1.

select_best_repair()
Deterministically selects the best candidate based on:

repaired SIS

repaired spectral radius

lexicographic candidate ordering (deterministic tie-break)

apply_repair_candidate()
Applies a repair swap to produce a new parity-check matrix without mutating the input.

rank_repair_candidates()
Debug utility for inspecting candidate repair quality.

Candidate swaps use the canonical degree-preserving Tanner swap pattern:

(a, b) + (c, d)  →  (a, d) + (c, b)

All structural constraints are validated before scoring.

Spectral Repair Experiment

New experiment harness:

src/qec/experiments/spectral_repair_experiment.py

Runs a single deterministic repair step and produces a canonical artifact recording spectral diagnostics before and after repair.

Artifact schema:

{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "original_spectral_radius": float,
  "original_ipr": float,
  "original_eeec": float,
  "original_sis": float,
  "repaired_spectral_radius": float,
  "repaired_ipr": float,
  "repaired_eeec": float,
  "repaired_sis": float,
  "delta_spectral_radius": float,
  "delta_ipr": float,
  "delta_eeec": float,
  "delta_sis": float,
  "selected_candidate": dict | null,
  "num_candidates_considered": int,
  "num_valid_candidates": int,
  "improved": bool
}

Artifacts are serialized using deterministic JSON (sort_keys=True).

Repair Visualization Script

New research visualization:

scripts/plot_repair_comparison.py

Displays before/after spectral heatmaps for Tanner graph edges.

The script:

computes the original instability heatmap

runs a single repair step

recomputes the heatmap

plots both states side-by-side

matplotlib is imported locally and remains optional.

Tests

New test suite:

tests/test_spectral_repair.py

Validates:

deterministic candidate generation

structural correctness of edge swaps

preservation of row and column degrees

binary matrix integrity

deterministic scoring and selection

no mutation of input matrices

correct artifact schema

correct no-op behavior when no improvement exists

All tests pass.

Architectural Notes

This release preserves the project’s core invariants:

decoder core unchanged

no randomness introduced

no dense non-backtracking matrices

deterministic artifact generation

structural diagnostics isolated from decoding logic

The system now supports the full spectral instability workflow:

detect instability   (v7.6)
        ↓
localize instability (v7.7)
        ↓
repair instability   (v7.8)
Research Direction

v7.8.0 introduces the first structural intervention mechanism guided by spectral diagnostics.

Upcoming research stages:

Version	Objective
v7.9.0	Incremental non-backtracking spectral updates
v8.0.0	Tanner graph BP stability phase diagrams
v8.1.0	Adaptive ternary / qutrit decoder experiments

The long-term objective is a deterministic BP stability oracle capable of predicting and mitigating decoding failure directly from Tanner graph structure.

title: v7.7.0 — Spectral Trapping-Set Heatmaps
body:
Release Notes
v7.7.0 — Spectral Trapping-Set Heatmaps

This release introduces deterministic spectral heatmap diagnostics for Tanner graphs, enabling localization of potential trapping-set structures using the non-backtracking (NB) eigenvector computed in v7.6.1.

While v7.6.1 introduced spectral instability metrics (IPR, EEEC, SIS), this release converts those signals into edge and node heatmaps that reveal where instability concentrates in the graph.

The system can now:

• localize unstable edges in Tanner graphs
• identify high-risk variable and check nodes
• produce deterministic instability heatmaps
• generate artifacts describing the most unstable graph regions

This marks the transition from instability detection → instability localization.

Added
Spectral Heatmap Diagnostics

New module:

src/qec/diagnostics/spectral_heatmaps.py

Provides deterministic Tanner graph heatmaps derived from NB eigenvector energy.

Functions:

compute_spectral_heatmaps(H)

rank_variable_nodes_by_heat(H)

rank_check_nodes_by_heat(H)

rank_edges_by_heat(H)

Computed signals:

Metric	Description
directed_edge_heat	NB eigenvector energy per directed edge
undirected_edge_heat	paired directed-edge aggregation
variable_node_heat	aggregated incident edge heat
check_node_heat	aggregated incident edge heat

Node heatmaps are weighted by IPR and then contrast-normalized to emphasize localized trapping-set clusters.

All routines are deterministic and reuse the NB spectrum computed in v7.6.1.

Spectral Heatmap Experiment

{

Artifacts identify the highest-energy nodes and edges in the spectral instability map.

Uses matplotlib but remains isolated from the core library.

• directed edge heat matches NB edge energy

• node heat vector sizes
• deterministic ranking behavior
• artifact schema validity
• non-negative heat values
• deterministic experiment outputs

All tests pass.

Architectural Notes

This release preserves all architectural constraints:

• decoder core untouched
• no dense NB matrices constructed
• spectral computations remain sparse
• algorithms remain deterministic
• visualization dependencies remain optional

The spectral diagnostics pipeline now supports:

Tanner graph
↓
NB spectrum
↓
IPR / EEEC / SIS
↓
edge heat
↓
node heat
↓
instability localization

Version	Objective

title: v7.6.0 — Instability Sensitivity Maps for Tanner Graphs
body:
Release Notes
v7.6.0 — Deterministic Instability Sensitivity Maps

This release introduces edge-level structural instability analysis for Tanner graphs, enabling the QEC framework to identify which graph components contribute most strongly to belief propagation (BP) decoding instability.

The new diagnostics expose structural causes of BP failure, transforming the framework from a purely observational decoder analysis tool into a system capable of explaining why instability occurs.

All features are implemented under the existing deterministic architecture and do not modify decoder behavior.

Highlights
Edge-Level Instability Sensitivity

Edges are scored using a deterministic spectral proxy derived from the dominant non-backtracking (NB) eigenvector:

sensitivity(edge) ≈ |v_i|² · |v_j|²

This proxy highlights graph regions where feedback cycles concentrate spectral energy and are likely to induce BP instability.

Measured Instability Influence

The framework now measures the actual instability contribution of each edge by temporarily removing the edge and recomputing the instability score:

instability_delta(edge)

This produces a structural diagnostic:

edge → instability influence

revealing the specific graph structures responsible for decoder instability.

Instability Sensitivity Maps

Combining spectral proxies with measured deltas produces instability sensitivity maps that identify fragile graph regions and highlight the edges most responsible for decoding instability.

These maps provide a structural explanation for BP failures, allowing the framework to move beyond simple failure detection.

Sensitivity-Weighted Graph Optimization

Graph optimization experiments can now optionally use sensitivity scores to prioritize candidate repairs.

When enabled, candidate modifications are explored in order of predicted instability contribution, focusing optimization effort on the most structurally fragile graph regions.

Default optimizer behavior remains unchanged unless this option is explicitly enabled.

Comparative Experiment Harness

The experiment framework now supports comparisons between:

Method	Description
v7.5 baseline	gradient-based graph repair
v7.6 sensitivity-guided	candidate prioritization via spectral sensitivity

This enables controlled evaluation of the new sensitivity diagnostics.

Architectural Impact

v7.6.0 strengthens the structural analysis layer of the QEC pipeline:

Tanner graph
      ↓
NB spectral diagnostics
      ↓
instability sensitivity maps
      ↓
graph optimization
      ↓
belief propagation decoding

The framework can now explain where instability originates in the graph structure, not just detect when decoding fails.

Deterministic Guarantees

All new functionality preserves the deterministic design principles of the framework.

No decoder modifications

No schema or serialization changes

No new dependencies

Canonical ordering with deterministic tie-breaking

Floating-point outputs rounded to 12 decimal places

Baseline optimizer behavior unchanged by default

Files Changed
File	Action	Layer
src/qec/diagnostics/sensitivity_map.py	Added	Spectral diagnostics
src/qec/experiments/sensitivity_preconditioner.py	Added	Experiment framework
tests/test_sensitivity_map.py	Added	Tests
tests/test_sensitivity_preconditioner.py	Added	Tests
src/qec/diagnostics/__init__.py	Updated	Diagnostics module
src/qec/experiments/__init__.py	Updated	Experiments module
CHANGELOG.md	Updated	Documentation
Test Status

54 new tests added

All existing tests passing

Spectral optimizer tests verified

Deterministic outputs confirmed

Conceptual Advancement

Most decoding frameworks report whether decoding fails.

v7.6.0 enables the framework to explain:

decoding fails
→ because THESE edges introduce instability

This capability establishes the foundation for future work on structural instability fields and gradient-guided Tanner graph optimization planned for upcoming releases.

title: v7.5.0 — Deterministic Spectral Graph Optimization Pipeline
body:
Release Notes
Overview

v7.5.0 introduces a deterministic Tanner graph optimization pipeline that improves predicted BP stability before decoding begins. The optimizer uses spectral diagnostics to identify unstable graph structure and applies degree-preserving repairs while preserving all core QEC architectural invariants.

This release also adds a sanity validation experiment with predictor probe to verify optimizer behavior and predictor alignment with actual decode outcomes.

All functionality is fully opt-in and implemented outside the decoder core.

Added
Spectral Graph Optimizer

src/qec/experiments/spectral_graph_optimizer.py

A deterministic optimizer that modifies Tanner graphs using spectral instability diagnostics.

Core mechanisms:

Non-backtracking spectral instability scoring

Spectral edge-gradient heuristic derived from dominant NB eigenvector

Deterministic candidate repair generation

Degree-preserving edge swaps

Iterative optimization loop with deterministic tie-breaking

Optimization pipeline:

graph
↓
spectral diagnostics
↓
instability predictor
↓
graph optimization
↓
decode

score_after ASC

Architectural Invariants

Research Direction
